--title
The New Believers Hymnbook
--additionalInfo
<p><b>Hymnals</b></p><p><i>The New Believers Hymnbook</i> &copy;2019 is copyrighted by John Ritchie Ltd, 40 Beansburn, Kilmarnock, Scotland. All rights reserved. No part of this publication may be reproduced in any form or by any means without the prior written permission of the copyright owner.</p><p><b>Hymns</b></p><p>Owners of copyright are gratefully thanked for the permission given to use the hymns which are owned by them.</p><p>The family of the late Mr Edmund Ewan are thanked for granting permission to use hymns from <i>Remembrance Hymns</i>.</p><p>Other copyrighted hymns are noted below each hymn.</p><p><b>Disclaimer:</b> Where required, copyright has been sought and given and if in any case copyright has been broken, we apologise and will endeavour to put it right.</p><br><p class="popupHeader"><b>Preface to <i>The New Believers Hymn Book</i></b></p><p><i>The Believers Hymn Book</i> was first published in 1884. In the first pages of the new hymn book, there was a Preface, entitled 'Praise', which commenced with these words: "It is truly wonderful that God should not only delight Himself in saving us, but also take pleasure in the praise of His reconciled children, telling us that in praising Him we are glorifying Him". It continued by observing that praise is "also full of blessing and edification to the saints". Hymns could be "a vehicle of mutual exhortation of great value", able to comfort in sorrow or to confirm a spiritual truth. The powerful point was then made: "If praise occupies so important a place in the Church, how very important it must be that the selection of hymns be varied and scriptural".</p><p>With three hundred and sixty-one "varied and scriptural" hymns, <i>The Believers Hymn Book</i> soon became widely used - and in 1959, a <i>Supplement</i> added more than a hundred hymns, thus enhancing its appeal.</p><p><i>The Believers Hymn Book</i> remains much loved, but the Publishers recognise that there is scope for further improvement.</p><p>We are therefore delighted to introduce <i>The New Believers Hymn Book</i>. Seventy-seven hymns have been removed and more than three hundred have been added, bringing the total to seven hundred. Almost ninety of these come from <i>Remembrance Hymns</i>. These and others from other sources are particularly suitable for the Lord's Supper.</p><p>The introduction to the original edition of <i>The Believers Hymn Book</i> concluded with these words: "No doubt there will be defects, and criticism may find much to lay hold of, but we offer it to the Lord's people as our endeavor to meet a felt need, and we pray and ask all who sympathise with us in this effort, to unite with us in prayer, that God may be pleased to use it for the glory of His name, and for the edification of His saints."</p><p>These are our sentiments too as we recall the summons to praise in the last five Psalms of Israel's Psalter, "Praise ye the Lord".</p><p style="text-align:right;">The Publishers,<br>John Ritchie Ltd,<br>40 Beansburn,<br>Kilmarnock</p>
--index

--lyrics
1
A debtor to mercy alone
Previous BHB #7<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Toplady_Augustus_Montague&#39;,this)">Augustus Montague Toplady  (1771)</a></div>
8.8.8.8.D.

A DEBTOR to mercy alone,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of covenant mercy I sing,
Nor fear, with God’s righteousness on,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My person and offerings to bring.
The terrors of law and of God
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With me can have nothing to do;
My Saviour’s obedience and blood
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hide all my transgressions from view.

The work which His goodness began
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The arm of His strength will complete;
His promise is Yea and Amen,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And never was forfeited yet.
Things future, nor things that are now,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Not all things below or above,
Can make Him His purpose forego,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Or sever my soul from His love.

My name from the palms of His hands
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Eternity will not erase;
Imprest on His heart, it remains
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In marks of indelible grace.
Yes! I to the end shall endure,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As sure as the earnest is given;
More happy, but not more secure,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The souls of the blessèd in heaven.


2
A little while of mingled joy and sorrow

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Bancroft_Charitie_Lee&#39;,this)">Charitie Bancroft</a></div>
11.10.11.10.

“A LITTLE while” of mingled joy and sorrow;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“A little while” to love and serve below,
To wait the dawning of that blissful morrow,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When morn shall break upon this night of woe.

A little longer in this vale of weeping,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of yearning for the sinless home above;
“A little while” of watching, and of keeping
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our garments, by the power of Him we love.

“A little while” for winning souls to Jesus, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Ere yet we see His beauty face to face;
“A little while” for healing soul - diseases,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;By telling others of a Saviour’s grace. 

“A little while” to tell the joyful story
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of Him who made our guilt and curse His own;
“A little while”, ere we behold the glory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To gather jewels for His heavenly crown.

“A little while!” and we shall dwell for ever
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Within our bright, our everlasting home;
Where Jesus and His Bride no  time shall sever,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Nor blight of sin, nor curse of death shall come.


3
A little while! our Lord shall come
Previous BHB #14<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Deck_James_George&#39;,this)">James George Deck  (1841)</a></div>
8.8.8.8.8.8.

“A LITTLE while!” our Lord shall come,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And we shall wander here no more;
He’ll take us to our Father’s home,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Where He for us has gone before,
To dwell with Him, to see His face,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And sing the glories of His grace. 

“A little while!” He’ll come again:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Let us the precious hours redeem;
Our only grief to give Him pain,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our joy to serve and follow Him:
Watching and ready may we be,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As those who long their Lord to see.

“A little while!” ‘twill soon be past:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Why should we shun the shame and Cross?
O let us in His footsteps haste,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Counting for Him all else but loss;
Oh, how will recompense His smile
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The suffering of this little while!

“A little while!” Come, Saviour, come!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For Thee Thy Bride has waited long:
Oh, take Thy wearied pilgrims home,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To sing the new, eternal song,
To see Thy glory, and to be
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In everything conformed to Thee!


4
A mighty fortress is our God

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Luther_Martin&#39;,this)">Martin Luther</a></div>
8.7.8.7.6.6.6.6.7.

A MIGHTY Fortress is our God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A Bulwark never failing;
Our Helper He amid the flood
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of mortal ills prevailing:
For still our ancient foe
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Doth seek to work us woe;
His craft and power are great,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And, armed with cruel hate,
On earth is not his equal.

Did we in our own strength confide,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our striving would be losing;
Were not the right Man on our side,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Man of God’s own choosing:
Dost ask who that may be?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Christ Jesus, it is He;
Lord Sabaoth His Name,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From age to age the same,
And He must win the battle.

And though this world, with devils filled,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Should threaten to undo us,
We will not fear, for God hath willed
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His truth to triumph through us:
The Prince of Darkness grim,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We tremble not for him;
His rage we can endure,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For lo! his doom is sure,
One little word shall fell him.

That word above all earthly powers,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No thanks to them, abideth;
The Spirit and the gifts are ours
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Through Him who with us sideth:
Let goods and kindred go,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;This mortal life also;
The body they may kill:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;God’s truth abideth still,
His Kingdom is forever.


5
A mind at perfect peace with God
Previous BHB #3<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Paget_Catesby&#39;,this)">Catesby Paget</a></div>
C.M.

A MIND at perfect peace with God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Oh! what a word is this!
A sinner reconciled through blood;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;This, this indeed is peace.

By nature and by practice far,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;How very far from God!
Yet now by grace brought nigh to Him,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Through faith in Jesus’ blood.

So near, so very near to God
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I cannot nearer be;
For in the person of His Son
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I am as near as He.

So dear, so very dear to God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;More dear I cannot be;
The love wherewith He loves the Son,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Such is His love to me.

Why should I ever careful be
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Since such a God is mine?
He watches o’er me night and day,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And tells me “Mine is thine”.


6
A perfect path of purest grace

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Wylie_Macleod&#39;,this)">Macleod Wylie</a></div>
C.M.

A PERFECT path of purest grace,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Unblemished and complete,
Was Thine, Thou spotless Nazarite,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Pure, even to the feet!

Thy stainless life, Thy lovely walk,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In every aspect true,
From the defilement all around,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No taint of evil drew.

No broken service, Lord, was Thine,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No change was in Thy way;
Unsullied in Thy holiness,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy strength knew no decay.

The vow was on Thee - Thou didst come,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To yield Thyself to death;
And consecration marked Thy path,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And spoke in every breath.

Morning by morning Thou didst wake,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Amidst this poisoned air;
Yet no contagion touched Thy soul,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No sin disturbed Thy prayer.

Thus, Lord, we love to trace Thy course,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To mark where Thou hast trod,
And follow Thee with loving eye,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Up to the throne of God.


7
A Pilgrim through this lonely world
Previous BHB #11
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Denny_Sir_Edward&#39;,this)">Sir Edward Denny</a> & <a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Bonar_Horatius&#39;,this)">Horatius Bonar</a> (1839)</div>
C.M.

A PILGRIM through this lonely world
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The blessèd Saviour passed;
A mourner all His life was He,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A dying Lamb at last.

That tender heart that felt for all,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For all its life-blood gave;
It found on earth no resting-place,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Save only in the grave.

Such was our Lord, and shall we fear
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Cross with all its scorn?
Or love a faithless, evil world,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That wreathed His brow with thorn?

No, facing all its frowns and smiles,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Like Him, obedient still,
We homeward press, through storm or calm,
To yon celestial hill.

In tents we dwell amid the waste,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Nor turn aside to roam
In folly’s paths, nor seek our rest,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Where Jesus had no home. 

Dead to the world, with Him who died
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To win our hearts, our love;
We, risen with our risen Head,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In spirit dwell above.

By faith His boundless glories there,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our wond’ring eyes behold;
The glories which eternal years
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shall never all unfold.

This fills our hearts with deep desire
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To lose ourselves in love!
Bears all our hopes from earth away,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And fixes them above.


8
A Rock that stands for ever
Previous BHB #9
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Gerhardt_Paulus&#39;,this)">Paul Gerhardt</a></div>
7.6.7.6.D.

A ROCK that stands for ever
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Is Christ my righteousness!
And there I stand unfearing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In everlasting bliss!
No earthly thing is needful
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To this my life from heaven,
And nought of love is worthy
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Save that which Christ has given.

There is no condemnation,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;There is no hell for me!
The torment and the fire
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Mine eyes shall never see!
For me there is no sentence,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For me death has no sting,
Because the Lord who loves me
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shall shield me with His wing!

No hunger, Lord, nor thirsting,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No danger, fear, nor fight,
No foe, no tribulation,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No throne, nor pow’r, nor might,
No height, no depth, no creature
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That has been, or can be,
Can pluck me from Thy bosom -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Can sever me from Thee!

My heart in joy upleapeth -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Grief cannot linger there!
O Jesus, Lord in glory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou art my sunshine fair!
The Source of all my singing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Is Jesus there above;
The Sun that shines upon me
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Is Jesus and His love!


9
A wealth of grace and truth replete

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Munro_M_George&#39;,this)">George M. Munro</a></div>
8.8.6.8.8.6.

A WEALTH of grace and truth replete,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Enriched the path those holy feet
With steadfast purpose trod.
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Each faultless step, with fragrance fraught,
Fresh grace revealed and pleasure brought
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Thee, His Father, God.

The wilderness, the mountain-side,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The wayside well, the stormy tide,
Served to reveal the worth
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of Him Who had not where to lay
His head on earth, as day by day,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He told His Father forth.

All Satan’s wiles, all human power,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;United in that dreadful hour
Of shame, which He foreknew,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Could not impede Him as He passed
In grace to reach the Cross at last
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His Father’s will to do. 

Well may our souls in worship bow
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Thee, His God and Father, now,
And offer Thee our praise
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;While musing on Thine Only Son,
The altogether lovely One,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In all His matchless ways.


10
A Wonderful Saviour is Jesus my Lord

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Alystyne_Frances_Jane_Van&#39;,this)">Frances (Fanny) Jane Crosby</a></div>
P.M.

A WONDERFUL Saviour is Jesus my Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A wonderful Saviour to me;
He hideth my soul in the cleft of the rock,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Where rivers of pleasure I see.

c
He hideth my soul in the cleft of the rock
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That shadows a dry, thirsty land;
He hideth my life in the depths of His love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And covers me there with His hand,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And covers me there with His hand.

A wonderful Saviour is Jesus my Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He taketh my burden away;
He holdeth me up and I shall not be moved,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He giveth me strength as my day.

c
He hideth my soul in the cleft of the rock
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That shadows a dry, thirsty land;
He hideth my life in the depths of His love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And covers me there with His hand,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And covers me there with His hand.

With numberless blessings each moment He crowns,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And, filled with His fulness divine,
I sing in my rapture, Oh, glory to God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For such a Redeemer as mine!

c
He hideth my soul in the cleft of the rock
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That shadows a dry, thirsty land;
He hideth my life in the depths of His love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And covers me there with His hand,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And covers me there with His hand.

When clothed in His brightness, transported I rise
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To meet Him in clouds of the sky,
His perfect salvation, His wonderful love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I’ll shout with the millions on high.

c
He hideth my soul in the cleft of the rock
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That shadows a dry, thirsty land;
He hideth my life in the depths of His love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And covers me there with His hand,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And covers me there with His hand.


11
Abba, Father! we adore Thee
Previous BHB #2<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Hawker_Robert&#39;,this)">Robert Hawker</a></div>
8.7.8.7.D.

ABBA, Father! we adore Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Humbly now our homage pay:
’Tis Thy children’s bliss to know Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Welcomed through the living way:
This high honour we inherit,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy free gift through Jesus’ blood;
God the Spirit, with our spirit,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Witnesseth we’re sons of God.

Thine own purpose gave us being,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When, in Christ, in that vast plan
Thou in Christ didst choose Thy people
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;E’en before the world began:
O what love the Father bore us!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O how precious in Thy sight!
When Thou gav’st Thy Church to Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus, Son of Thy delight.

Though our nature’s fall in Adam
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shut us wholly out from God,
Thine eternal counsel brought us
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Nearer still, through Jesus’ blood;
For in Him we found redemption,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Grace and glory in Thy Son;
Oh, the height and depth of mercy!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Christ and His redeemed are one.

Hence, through all the changing seasons,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Trouble, sickness, sorrow, woe,
Nothing changeth God’s affections,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Love divine shall bring us through;
Soon shall all Thy blood-bought children
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Round the throne their anthems raise,
And, in songs of rich salvation,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shout to God’s eternal praise.


12
Abba, Father! we approach Thee
Previous BHB #1<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Deck_James_George&#39;,this)">James George Deck  (1841)</a></div>
8.7.8.7.D.

ABBA, Father! we approach Thee
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In our Saviour’s precious name;
We, Thy children, here assembling,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Access to Thy presence claim;
From our sin His blood hath washed us;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;’Tis through Him our souls draw near,
And Thy Spirit, too, hath taught us,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Abba, Father! name so dear.

Once as prodigals we wandered
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In our folly, far from Thee;
But Thy grace, o’er sin abounding,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Rescued us from misery:
Thou Thy prodigals hast pardoned,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Loved us with a Father’s love;
Welcomed us with joy o’erflowing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;E’en to dwell with Thee above.

Clothed in garments of salvation,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;At Thy table is our place;
We rejoice, and Thou rejoicest,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In the riches of Thy grace:
It is meet, we hear Thee saying,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We should merry be and glad;
I have found My once lost children,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Now they live who once were dead.

Abba, Father! all adore Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All rejoice in heaven above;
While in us they learn the wonders
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of Thy wisdom, power and love;
Soon, before Thy throne assembled,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All Thy children shall proclaim,
Glory, everlasting glory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Be to God and to the Lamb.


13
Abide in me, my Saviour
Previous BHB #362<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Costello_E&#39;,this)">E. Costello</a></div>
7.6.7.6.D.

ABIDE in me, my Saviour,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And let me in Thee live;
Into Thy holy keeping
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My soul, my life I give:
Without Thy grace to keep me
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I know my steps will slide,
So daily, hourly, alway,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Do Thou in me abide.

I long, my Lord and Master,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To love and serve Thee more,
To live more near to Thee, Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Than e’er I have before:
To keep more sure and steadfast
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Within the narrow way,
In love’s obedience growing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;More perfect day by day.

Then let my life and labours
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Henceforth and ever be,
In fervent love abounding,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Acceptable to Thee;
Upon Thy strength relying
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In weakness and when tried,
And seeking that in all things
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy name be glorified.


14
According to Thy gracious Word
Previous BHB #5
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Montgomery_James&#39;,this)">James Montgomery  (1825)</a></div>
C.M.

ACCORDING to Thy gracious Word,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In meek humility,
This would I do, O Christ my Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I would remember Thee.

Thy body, broken for my sake,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My bread from heaven shall be;
The cup of blessing I will take,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And thus remember Thee.

Gethsemane can I forget?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Or there Thy conflict see,
Thine agony and blood-like sweat,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And not remember Thee?

When to the Cross I turn mine eyes
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And rest on Calvary,
O Lamb of God, my sacrifice,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I must remember Thee.

Remember Thee, and all Thy pains,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And all Thy love to me;
Yea, while a breath, a pulse remains,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Would I remember Thee.

And when, O Lord, Thou com’st again
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And I Thy glory see,
For ever, as the Lamb once slain,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I will remember Thee.


15
Alas! and did my Saviour bleed?
Previous BHB #6
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Watts_Isaac&#39;,this)">Isaac Watts  (1707)</a></div>
C.M.

ALAS! and did my Saviour bleed?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And did my Sovereign die?
Would He devote that sacred head
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For such a worm as I?

Was it for crimes that I have done
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He groaned upon the tree?
Amazing pity! grace unknown!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And love beyond degree.

Well might the sun in darkness hide
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And shut his glories in,
When the incarnate Maker died
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For man, His creature’s sin.

Thus might I hide my blushing face,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;While His dear Cross appears;
Dissolve my heart in thankfulness,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And melt my eyes to tears.

But drops of grief can ne’er repay,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The debt of love I owe;
Here, Lord, I give myself away;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;’Tis all that I can do.


16
All hail the power of Jesus’ name!
Previous BHB #361<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Perronet_Edward&#39;,this)">Edward Perronet</a> & John Rippon  (1779)</div>
C.M.

ALL hail the power of Jesus’ name!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Let angels prostrate fall;
Bring forth the royal diadem
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And crown Him Lord of all!

Crown Him, ye martyrs of your God
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who from His altar call;
Extol the stem of Jesse’s rod
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And crown Him Lord of all!

Ye seed of Israel’s chosen race,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A remnant weak and small,
Hail Him who saves you by His grace,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And crown Him Lord of all!

Sinners, whose love can ne’er forget
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The wormwood and the gall,
Go, spread your trophies at His feet,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And crown Him Lord of all!

Let every kindred, every tribe,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On this terrestrial ball,
To Him all majesty ascribe,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And crown Him Lord of all!

O that, with yonder sacred throng,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We at His feet may fall;
Join in the everlasting song,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And crown Him Lord of all!


17
All people that on earth do dwell

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kethe_William&#39;,this)">William Kethe (verse 5 by Thomas Ken)</a></div>
L.M.

ALL people that on earth do dwell, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sing to the Lord with cheerful voice;
Him serve with fear, His praise  forth tell;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Come ye before Him and rejoice.

Know that the Lord is God indeed;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Without our aid He did us make;
We are His folk, He doth us feed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And for His sheep He doth us take.

O enter then His gates with praise,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Approach with joy His courts unto;
Praise, laud, and bless His name always,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For it is seemly so to do.

For why? the Lord our God is good,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His mercy is for ever sure;
His truth at all times firmly stood,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And shall from age to age endure.

Praise God, from whom all blessings flow;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Praise Him, all creatures here below;
Praise Him above, ye heav'nly host;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Praise Father, Son, and Holy Ghost!


18
All that I was, my sin, my guilt
Previous BHB #8
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Bonar_Horatius&#39;,this)">Horatius Bonar (1845)</a></div>
C.M.

ALL that I was, my sin, my guilt,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My death, was all my own;
All that I am, I owe to Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My gracious God alone.

The evil of my former state,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Was mine, and only mine;
The good in which I now rejoice,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Is Thine, and only Thine.

The darkness of my former state,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The bondage, all was mine;
The light of life in which I walk,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The liberty is Thine.

Thy grace first made me feel my sin,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;It taught me to believe;
Then, in believing, peace I found,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And now I live, I live!

All that I am, e’en here on earth,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All that I hope to be,
When Jesus comes and glory dawns,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I owe it, Lord, to Thee.


19
All the way my Saviour leads me
Previous BHB #364<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Alystyne_Frances_Jane_Van&#39;,this)">Frances (Fanny) Jane Crosby (1875)</a></div>
8.7.8.7.D.

ALL the way my Saviour leads me;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What have I to ask beside?
Can I doubt His tender mercy
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who through life has been my guide?
Heavenly peace, divinest comfort,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Here by faith in Him to dwell;
For I know, whate’er befall me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus doeth all things well.

All the way my Saviour leads me;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Cheers each winding path I tread;
Gives me grace for every trial,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Feeds me with the living bread:
Though my weary steps may falter,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And my soul athirst may be,
Gushing from the rock before me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lo! a spring of joy I see.

All the way my Saviour leads me;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O the fulness of His love!
Perfect rest to me is promised
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In my Father’s house above:
When my spirit, clothed immortal,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Wings its flight to realms of day,
This my song through endless ages,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus led me all the way.


20
Altogether lovely, Jesus Lord art Thou

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Young_J_J&#39;,this)">J. J. Young</a></div>
6.5.6.5.D.

ALTOGETHER lovely,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus, Lord art Thou,
Fairest of ten thousand,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;At Thy feet we bow;
As we view Thee coming,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Saviour from above,
All our hearts are ravished,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With Thy wondrous love. 

Precious to the Father,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All Thy steps below,
Telling out God’s glory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In this world of woe;
For His will and purpose,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Was Thy pleasure here,
In devoted service,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Thy God so dear.

But Thy wondrous beauty,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And Thy matchless grace,
Lord, in all their fulness,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who can fully trace?
Yet our hearts enraptured,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Even here on earth,
Now would sing Thy praises,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And proclaim Thy worth.

Soon in brightest glory
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We shall see Thy face,
Praise Thee, and adore Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lord for all Thy grace;
We Thy glory sharing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Gazing, Lord, on Thee,
Altogether lovely,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Through eternity.


21
Am I a soldier of the Cross?

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Watts_Isaac&#39;,this)">Isaac Watts</a></div>
C.M. with Chorus

AM I a soldier of the Cross,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A follower of the Lamb?
And shall I fear to own His cause,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Or blush to speak His name?

c
In the name, the precious name,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of Him who died for me,
By grace I’ll win the promised crown,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Whate’er my cross shall be.

Must I be carried to the skies,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On flow’ry beds of ease,
While others fought to win the prize,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And sailed through raging seas?

c
In the name, the precious name,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of Him who died for me,
By grace I’ll win the promised crown,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Whate’er my cross shall be.

Are there no foes for me to face?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Must I not stem the flood?
Is this vile world a friend to grace,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To help me on to God?

c
In the name, the precious name,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of Him who died for me,
By grace I’ll win the promised crown,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Whate’er my cross shall be.

Sure I must fight if I would reign;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Increase my courage, Lord;
I’ll bear the toil, endure the pain,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Supported by Thy Word.

c
In the name, the precious name,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of Him who died for me,
By grace I’ll win the promised crown,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Whate’er my cross shall be.

Thy saints in all this glorious war,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shall conquer, though they’re  slain;
They view the triumph from afar,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And shall with Jesus reign.

c
In the name, the precious name,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of Him who died for me,
By grace I’ll win the promised crown,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Whate’er my cross shall be.

When that illustrious day shall rise,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And all Thy armies shine,
In robes of vict’ry through the skies,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The glory shall be Thine.

c
In the name, the precious name,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of Him who died for me,
By grace I’ll win the promised crown,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Whate’er my cross shall be.


22
Amazing grace! (How sweet the sound!)

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Newton_John&#39;,this)">John Newton</a></div>
C.M.

AMAZING grace! (How sweet the sound!)
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That saved a wretch like me!
I once was lost, but now am found;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Was blind, but now I see.

’Twas grace that taught my heart to fear,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And grace my fears relieved;
How precious did that grace appear
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The hour I first believed.

Through many dangers, toils and snares,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I have already come;
’Tis grace hath brought me safe thus far,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And grace will lead me home.

The Lord has promised good to me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His Word my hope secures;
He will my shield and portion be,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As long as life endures.

Yes, when this flesh and heart shall fail,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And mortal life shall cease,
I shall possess, within the veil,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A life of joy and peace.

When we’ve been there ten thousand years,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Bright shining as the sun,
We’ve no less days to sing God’s  praise,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Than when we first begun.


23
Amen! one lasting long Amen!

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Midlane_Albert&#39;,this)">Albert Midlane</a></div>
L.M.

AMEN! one lasting, long Amen!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Blest anthem of eternal days,
The fulness of the rapturous song
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Christ the Saviour’s endless praise!

Amen! one lasting, long Amen!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Heaven’s blissful cadence, deep and loud,
While every heart before the throne
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In holy, solemn awe is bowed.

Amen! Amen! it rolls along,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Re-echoing from the throne again!
Be ours to mingle with the throng
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In that eternal, loud “Amen!”


24
Amidst us our Beloved stands
Previous BHB #363<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Spurgeon_Charles_Haddon&#39;,this)">Charles Haddon Spurgeon  (1866)</a></div>
L.M.

AMIDST us our Belovèd stands,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And bids us view His piercèd hands,
Points to His wounded feet and side,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Blest emblems of the Crucified.

What food luxurious loads the board
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When at His table sits the Lord!
The wine how rich, the bread how sweet,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When Jesus deigns the guests to meet.

If now, with eyes defiled and dim,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We see the signs, but see not Him,
Oh, may His love the scales displace
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And bid us see Him face to face.

Thou glorious Bridegroom of our hearts,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy present smile a heaven imparts;
O lift the veil, if veil there be,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Let every saint Thy beauties see.


25
And can it be that I should gain?
Previous BHB #365<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Wesley_Charles&#39;,this)">Charles Wesley  (1738)</a></div>
8.8.8.8.8.8.

AND can it be that I should gain
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;An interest in the Saviour’s blood?
Died He for me who caused His pain?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For me who Him to death pursued?
Amazing love! how can it be
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That Thou, my God, shouldst die for me? 

’Tis mystery all! the Immortal dies;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who can explore His strange design?
In vain the first-born seraph tries
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To sound the depths of love divine;
’Tis mercy all! let earth adore,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Let angel minds inquire no more.

He left His Father’s throne above,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;So free, so infinite His grace;
Emptied Himself of all but love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And bled for Adam’s helpless race;
’Tis mercy all, immense and free;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For, O my God, it found out me.

Long my imprisoned spirit lay
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Fast bound in sin and nature’s night;
Thine eye diffused a quickening ray -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I woke, the dungeon flamed with  light;
My chains fell off, my heart was free,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I rose, went forth, and followed Thee.

No condemnation now I dread;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus, and all in Him, is mine,
Alive in Him, my living Head,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And clothed in righteousness divine,
Bold I approach the eternal throne,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And claim the crown, through Christ, my own.


26
And did the Holy and the Just?

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Steele_Anne&#39;,this)">Anne Steele  (1760)</a></div>
C.M.

AND did the Holy and the Just,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Sovereign of the skies,
Stoop down to wretchedness and dust,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That guilty worms might rise?

Yes, the Redeemer left His throne -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His radiant throne on high -
(Surprising mercy! love unknown!)
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To suffer, bleed, and die.

He took the ruined sinner’s place,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And suffered in his stead:
For man, (O, miracle of grace!)
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For man the Saviour bled.

Dear Lord, what heavenly wonders dwell
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In Thine atoning blood!
By this are sinners saved from hell,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And rebels brought to God.

Jesus, my soul, adoring, bends,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To love so full, so free;
And may I hope that love extends,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Its sacred power to me?

What glad return can I impart,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For favours so divine?
O take my all - this worthless heart,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And make it only Thine. 


27
And is it so! we shall be like Thy Son
Previous BHB #366<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Darby_John_Nelson&#39;,this)">John Nelson Darby  (1872)</a></div>
10.10.10.10.

AND is it so! we shall be like Thy Son,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Is this the grace which He for us has won? 
Father of glory! (thought beyond all thought)
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In glory to His own blest likeness brought.

O Jesus, Lord, who loved us like to Thee?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Fruit of Thy work, with Thee, too, there to see
Thy glory, Lord, while endless ages roll;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy saints the prize and travail of Thy soul.

Yet it must be, Thy love had not its rest
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Were Thy redeemed not with Thee fully blest;
That love that gives not as the world, but shares
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All it possesses with its loved co-heirs.

Not we alone, Thy loved ones all, complete,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In glory 'round Thee there with joy shall meet,
All like Thee, for Thy glory like Thee, Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Object supreme of all, by all adored.

The heart is satisfied, can ask no more;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All thought of self is now for ever o’er;
Christ, its unmingled object, fills the heart
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In blest adoring love - its endless part.


28
And shall we see Thy face?

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Darby_John_Nelson&#39;,this)">John Nelson Darby</a></div>
S.M.

AND shall we see Thy face,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And hear Thy heavenly voice,
Well known to us in present grace?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Well may our hearts rejoice!

With Thee in garments white,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lord Jesus, we shall walk;
And, spotless in that heavenly light,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of all Thy sufferings talk.

Close to Thy trusted side,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In fellowship divine;
No cloud, no distance, e'er shall hide
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Glories that then shall shine.

Fruit of Thy boundless love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That gave Thyself for us;
For ever we shall with Thee prove
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That Thou still lov'st thus.

And we love Thee, blest Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;E'en now, though feeble here,
Thy sorrow and Thy cross record
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What makes us know Thee near.

We wait to see Thee, Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Yet now within our hearts
Thou dwell'st in love that doth afford
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The joy that love imparts.

Yet still we wait for Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To see Thee as Thou art;
Be with Thee, like Thee, Lord, and free
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To love with all our heart.


29
Arise, my soul, arise!

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Wesley_Charles&#39;,this)">Charles Wesley </a></div>
6.6.6.6.8.8.

ARISE, my soul, arise!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shake off thy guilty fears;
The bleeding sacrifice 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In my behalf appears:
Before the throne my Surety stands,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My name is written on His hands.

He ever lives above, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For me to intercede;
His all-redeeming love, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His precious blood to plead:
His blood atoned for all our race,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And sprinkles now the throne of grace.

Five bleeding wounds He bears;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Received on Calvary;
They pour effectual prayers; 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;They strongly plead for me:
“Forgive him, Oh, forgive”, they cry,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“Nor let that ransomed sinner die!”

The Father hears Him pray, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His dear anointed One;
He cannot turn away, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The presence of His Son;
His Spirit answers to the blood,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And tells me I am born of God.

My God is reconciled; 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His pardoning voice I hear;
He owns me for His child; 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I can no longer fear:
With confidence I now draw nigh,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And “Father, Abba, Father!” cry.


30
Around Thy grave, Lord Jesus
Previous BHB #13
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Deck_James_George&#39;,this)">James George Deck  (1841)</a></div>
7.6.7.6.D.

AROUND Thy grave, Lord Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In spirit here we stand,
With hearts all full of praises,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To keep Thy blest command;
Our souls by faith rejoicing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To trace Thy path of love,
Down through death’s angry billows,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Up to the throne above.

Lord Jesus, we remember
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The travail of Thy soul,
When, in Thy love’s deep pity,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The waves did o’er Thee roll;
Baptized in death’s cold waters,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For us Thy blood was shed:
For us Thou, Lord of Glory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Wast numbered with the dead.

Oh, Lord, Thou now art risen,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy travail all is o’er!
For sin Thou once hast suffered,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou liv’st to die no more;
Sin, death, and hell are vanquished
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;By Thee, the Church’s Head;
And lo! we share Thy triumphs,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou First-born from the dead.

Into Thy death baptizèd,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We own with Thee we died;
With Thee, our life, we’re risen,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And in Thee glorified:
From sin, the world, and Satan,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We’re ransomed by Thy blood,
And now would walk as strangers,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Alive with Thee to God.


31
Around Thy table, Holy Lord
Previous BHB #4
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Peters_Mary&#39;,this)">Mary Bowly Peters</a></div>
C.M.

AROUND Thy table, Holy Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In fellowship we meet,
Obedient to Thy gracious Word; 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;This feast of love to eat.

Here every one that loves Thy name
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our willing hearts embrace;
Our source of life and hope the same,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All debtors to Thy grace.

By faith we take the bread of life,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With which our souls are fed;
And cup, in token of Thy blood,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That was for sinners shed.

Under Thy banner thus we sing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The wonders of Thy love,
While we anticipate by faith,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The heavenly feast above.

However poor, despis’d or few,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We know Thy changeless love,
Dear Lord, is just as warm, as true,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Now on the throne above.

Commune with each at this blest hour,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And, when we hence depart,
May deeds of love and words of power,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Engage each faithful heart.


32
As gathered to Thy precious name

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Russell_Douglas&#39;,this)">Douglas Russell</a></div>
C.M.

AS gathered to Thy precious name,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy table we surround,
Thy death, Lord Jesus, to proclaim,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Oh, may our praise abound!

We give what from Thee we receive,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For all we have is Thine -
Oh, may each heart with joy believe,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And echo “Thine is mine!”

Grace, grace it was that brought Thee down;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Love shone in all Thy ways;
Through death Thine is the Victor’s crown,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And Thine the endless praise.

We, or in silence, or in song,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Together worship Thee;
Before our God we shall ere long
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Give praise eternally.

Lord Jesus Christ, Thou comest soon -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Today Thy death we show;
In light, eclipsing sun at noon,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Its mystery we shall know.


33
As in holy contemplation

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kirkpatrick_William&#39;,this)">William Kirkpatrick</a></div>
8.5.8.3.

AS in holy contemplation, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We review His ways, 
Lowly manhood in perfection
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Fills our gaze.

From His lips in fulness flowing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Words with wisdom fraught,
With God’s will so wholly blended
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Every thought!

Ever “altogether lovely”,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Ever, all, “most sweet”,
Richest fragrance ever rising
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Round His feet!

Contemplating every aspect,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Every feature fair,
Find we precious, peerless beauty
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Everywhere.

Gathered from those holy footsteps,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Incense fresh we bring,
And, to Thee, His Father, offer
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As we sing.


34
As prophets past foretold Him

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Ewan_Y_Isaac&#39;,this)">Isaac Y. Ewan</a></div>
7.7.7.7.8.8.

AS prophets past foretold Him, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Anointed eyes behold Him. 
Together gathered to Him,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His God we worship through Him,
Remembering, remembering,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our Blessèd Lord remembering.

His going forth, obeying,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From that dark place of praying, 
The cup of sorrow taking,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To taste that dread forsaking!
Remembering, remembering;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our Blessèd Lord remembering!

His meek, majestic bearing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The mocking purple wearing,
In lowliness submitting
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His face to shame and spitting!
Remembering, remembering;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our Blessèd Lord remembering!

His patience, unopposing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;’Mid darkness o’er Him closing, 
Those unknown pangs enduring,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Eternal peace procuring! 
Remembering, remembering,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our Blessèd Lord remembering!


35
As sinners saved we gladly praise
Previous BHB #12<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Evans_James_Harrington&#39;,this)">James H. Evans</a></div>
L.M.

AS sinners saved we gladly praise
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Author of redeeming grace;
Father, ’tis Thine almighty power 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Secures us when the tempests lower.

Thy love’s a refuge ever nigh,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy watchfulness a mountain high,
Thy name a rock, which winds above
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And waves below can never move.

Thy faithfulness, for ever sure,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Through endless ages shall endure;
Thy perfect work shall ever prove
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The depth of Thine unceasing love.

While all things change, Thou changest not,
Forgetting ne’er, though oft forgot;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy love, immutably the same,
Displays the glory of Thy Name.

Lord, we would then rejoice and praise
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The source of all this wondrous grace;
Father, Thine everlasting power
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Will keep us safe in danger’s hour.


36
Assembled, Lord, at Thy behest
Previous BHB #367<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Beattie_David_Johnstone&#39;,this)">David J. Beattie</a></div>
C.M.

ASSEMBLED, Lord, at Thy behest,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We wait Thy voice to hear; 
O come and bless this hallowed feast -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In spirit now draw near.

Draw near, yea, linger in our midst;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy waiting saints inspire
With thoughts of Thine unfailing love;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Grant this our soul’s desire.

Such love! surpassing human thought,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That Thou, blest Lamb of God,
Should’st bear sin’s curse, and interpose
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thine own atoning blood.

With hearts adoring thus we view
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy table fitly spread;
Love’s sweet memorial feast to Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our risen, glorious Head.

O help us, Lord, while gathered here,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That we none else may see;
Keep Thou our thoughts graced with Thy love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And wholly stayed on Thee.


37
At rest through Jesus’ blood

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Ewan_Y_Isaac&#39;,this)">Isaac Y. Ewan</a></div>
S.M.

AT rest through Jesus’ blood, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;At rest from self and sin,
Saved and at peace with Thee, O God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We boldly enter in.

At rest and unafraid,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Yet in befitting fear,
Calm in the perfect peace He made,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We rev'rently draw near.

Our thoughts are backward cast
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O'er all the path He trod, 
The blest imperishable past
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That filled Thy heart, O God.

The silent loaf and cup
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Speak volumes to the soul, 
And heart and voice are lifted up
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And praises heavenward roll.

For Thou of Him to us
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hast spoken wondrously, 
And in responding worship thus
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We speak of Him to Thee.


38
At the name of Jesus every knee shall bow
Previous BHB #397
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Noel_Caroline_Maria&#39;,this)">Caroline Maria Noel  (1870)</a></div>
6.5.6.5.D.

AT the name of Jesus
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Every knee shall bow,
Every tongue confess Him
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;King of glory now.
’Tis the Father’s pleasure
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We should call Him Lord,
Who from the beginning
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Was the mighty Word.

Mighty and mysterious,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In the highest height,
God from everlasting,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Very Light of light:
In the Father’s bosom,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With the Spirit blest,
Love, in love eternal,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Rest, in perfect rest.

At His voice creation
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sprang at once to sight,
All the angel faces,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All the hosts of light,
Thrones and dominations,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Stars upon their way,
All the heavenly orders
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In their great array.

Humbled for a season,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To receive a name
From the lips of sinners
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Unto whom He came,
Faithfully He bore it
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Spotless to the last,
Brought it back victorious,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When from death He passed.

Bore it up triumphant,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With its human light,
Through all ranks of creatures,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To the central height:
To the throne of Godhead,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To the Father’s breast,
Filled it with the glory
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of that perfect rest.

In your hearts enthrone Him;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;There let Him subdue
All that is not holy,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All that is not true;
Crown Him as your Captain
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In temptation’s hour;
Let His will enfold you
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In its light and power.

Brethren, this Lord Jesus
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shall return again,
With His Father’s glory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With His angel train;
For all wreaths of empire
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Meet upon His brow,
And our hearts confess Him
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;King of glory now.


39
Awake, my soul, in joyful lays
Previous BHB #10
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Medley_Samuel&#39;,this)">Samuel Medley</a></div>
L.M.

AWAKE, my soul, in joyful lays,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And sing thy great Redeemer’s praise;
He justly claims a song from thee;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His loving-kindness - Oh, how free!

He saw me ruined in the fall,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Yet loved me, notwithstanding all;
He saved me from my lost estate;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His loving-kindness - Oh, how great!

When I was Satan’s easy prey
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And deep in debt and bondage lay,
He paid His life for my discharge -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His loving-kindness - Oh, how large!

Though numerous hosts of mighty foes
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Though earth and hell my way oppose,
He safely leads my soul along -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His loving-kindness - Oh, how strong! 

When trouble, like a gloomy cloud,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Has gathered thick and thundered  loud,
He near my soul has ever stood;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His loving-kindness - Oh, how good!

Soon shall we mount and soar away
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To the bright realms of endless day,
And sing, with rapture and surprise,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His loving-kindness, in the skies.


40
Be not dismayed whate’er betide

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Martin_Civilla&#39;,this)">Civilla Martin</a></div>
P.M.

BE not dismayed whate’er betide,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;God will take care of you!
Beneath His wings of love abide,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;God will take care of you!

c
God will take care of you,
Through ev’ry day, o’er all the way;
He will take care of you;
God will take care of you!

Through days of toil when heart doth fail,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;God will take care of you!
When dangers fierce your path  assail,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;God will take care of you!

c
God will take care of you,
Through ev’ry day, o’er all the way;
He will take care of you;
God will take care of you!

All you may need He will provide,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;God will take care of you!
Trust Him, and you will be satisfied,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;God will take care of you!

c
God will take care of you,
Through ev’ry day, o’er all the way;
He will take care of you;
God will take care of you!

Lonely and sad, from friends apart,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;God will take care of you!
He will give peace to your aching heart,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;God will take care of you!

c
God will take care of you,
Through ev’ry day, o’er all the way;
He will take care of you;
God will take care of you!

No matter what may be the test,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;God will take care of you!
Lean, weary one, upon His breast,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;God will take care of you!

c
God will take care of you,
Through ev’ry day, o’er all the way;
He will take care of you;
God will take care of you!


41
Be still, my soul, the Lord is on thy side

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Schlegel_von_Katharina&#39;,this)">Katharina von Schlegel and Jane Borthwick</a></div>
10.10.10.10.10.10.

BE still, my soul: the Lord is on thy side;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Bear patiently the cross of grief or pain;
Leave to thy God to order and provide;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In every change, He faithful will remain.
Be still, my soul: thy best, thy heavenly Friend
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Through thorny ways leads to a joyful end.

Be still, my soul: thy God doth undertake
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To guide the future, as He has the past.
Thy hope, thy confidence let nothing shake;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All now mysterious shall be bright at last.
Be still, my soul: the waves and winds still know
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His voice who ruled them while He dwelt below.

Be still, my soul: when dearest friends depart,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And all is darkened in the veil of tears,
Then shalt thou better know His love, His heart,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who comes to soothe thy sorrow and thy fears.
Be still, my soul: thy Jesus can repay
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From His own fulness all He  takes away.

Be still, my soul: the hour is hast'ning on
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When we shall be forever with the Lord,
When disappointment, grief and fear are gone,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sorrow forgot, love's purest joys restored.
Be still, my soul: when change and tears are past,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All safe and blessèd we shall meet at last.

Be still, my soul: begin the song of praise
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On earth, believing, to Thy Lord on high;
Acknowledge Him in all thy words and ways,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;So shall He view thee with a well-pleased eye.
Be still, my soul: the Sun of life divine
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Through passing clouds shall but more brightly shine.


42
Be Thou my vision, O Lord of my heart

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Byrne_Mary&#39;,this)">Mary Byrne and Eleanor Hull</a></div>
10.10.9.10.

BE Thou my vision, O Lord of my heart;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Naught be all else to me, save that Thou art;
Thou my best thought, by day or by night;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Waking or sleeping, Thy presence my light.

Be Thou my wisdom, and Thou my true Word;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I ever with Thee and Thou with me, Lord;
Thou my great Father and I, Thy true son;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou in me dwelling, and I with Thee one.

Riches I heed not, nor man’s empty praise;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou mine inheritance, now and always;
Thou and Thou only, first in my heart;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O King of glory, my treasure Thou art.

O King of glory, my victory won;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Rule and reign in me ‘til Thy will be done;
Heart of my own heart, whatever befall;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Still be my vision, O Ruler of all.


43
Before the throne of God above
Previous BHB #15
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Bancroft_Charitie_Lee&#39;,this)">Charatie de Cheney Lees Smith Bancroft  (1863)</a></div><p>&copy; 1997 Sovereign Grace Praise (BMI) (adm at IntegratedRights.com)</p>
8.8.8.8.D.

BEFORE the throne of God above
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I have a strong, a perfect plea,
A great High Priest, whose name is Love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who ever lives and pleads for me.
My name is graven on His hands,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My name is written on His heart;
I know that while in heav’n He stands
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No tongue can bid me thence depart.
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No tongue can bid me thence depart.

When Satan tempts me to despair,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Telling of evil yet within,
Upward I look, and see Him there
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who made an end of all my sin.
Because the sinless Saviour died,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My sinful soul is counted free;
For God, the Just, is satisfied
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To look on Him and pardon me.
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To look on Him and pardon me.

Behold Him there! the once slain Lamb!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My perfect, spotless Righteousness,
The great unchangeable I AM,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The King of glory and of grace!
One with Himself, I cannot die;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My soul is purchased by His blood;
My life is hid with Christ on high,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With Christ, my Saviour and my God.
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With Christ, my Saviour and my God.


44
Before Thine open ear

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Ewan_Y_Isaac&#39;,this)">Isaac Y. Ewan</a></div>
S.M.

BEFORE Thine open ear,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Beneath Thy watching eye, 
All clean and full we would appear,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And thus, O God, draw nigh.

With willing heart and mind
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Without the camp we come, 
With eyes for Him no longer blind,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And lips no longer dumb.

Here saints may meet their Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Amid reproach and shame, 
Gathered according to His Word -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Into His holy Name.

For where are two or three
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Simply assembled so, 
He in the midst is sure to be,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As they may surely know.

Before Thine open ear
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We, in His Name, draw nigh 
To lift our hearts in worship here,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Beneath Thy watching eye.


45
Behold! a spotless Victim dies
Previous BHB #16<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Anonymous_Hymn_Writers&#39;,this)">Anonymous</a></div>
C.M.

BEHOLD! a spotless Victim dies,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My surety on the tree;
The Lamb of God, the Sacrifice,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He gave Himself for me!

Whatever curse was mine, He bore;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The wormwood and the gall,
There, in that lone mysterious hour,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My cup - He drained it all!

Lord Jesus! Thou, and none beside,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Its bitterness could know,
Nor other tell Thy joy’s full tide
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That from that cup shall flow.

Thine is the joy, but yet ’tis mine.
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;’Tis ours as one with Thee;
My joy flows from that grief of Thine;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy death brings life to me!

And while the ages roll along,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;This shall my glory be;
And this the new and endless song,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy love to us - to me.
<div class="addedChorus">[optional chorus]
Alone, alone, He bore it all alone,
He gave Himself to save his own
He suffered bled and died
Alone, alone.</div>


46
Behold! behold the Lamb of God
Previous BHB #30
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Hoskins_Joseph&#39;,this)">Joseph Hoskins</a> (BHB Companion) / <a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Jukes_Richard&#39;,this)">Richard Jukes</a> (Primitive Methodist Magazine)</div>
8.3.8.3.8.8.8.3.

BEHOLD! behold the Lamb of God
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On the Cross! On the Cross!
For us He shed His precious blood 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On the Cross! On the Cross!
O hear His all-important cry,
“Eli, lama sabachthani?”
Draw near and see the Saviour die 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On the Cross! On the Cross!

Behold His arms extended wide  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On the Cross! On the Cross!
Behold His bleeding hands and side  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On the Cross! On the Cross!
The sun withholds its rays of light,
The heav’ns are clothed in shades of night,
While Jesus wins the glorious fight  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On the Cross! On the Cross!

By faith we see Him lifted up  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On the Cross! On the Cross!
He drinks for us the bitter cup  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On the Cross! On the Cross!
The rocks do rend, the mountains quake,
While Jesus doth atonement make,
While Jesus suffers for our sake  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On the Cross! On the Cross!

And now the mighty deed is done  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On the Cross! On the Cross!
The battle fought, the vict’ry won  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On the Cross! On the Cross!
To heav’n He turns His languid eyes,
“’Tis finished” now, the Conqueror cries,
Then bows His sacred head and dies  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On the Cross! On the Cross!

Where’er I go I’ll tell the story  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of the Cross! Of the Cross!
Of nothing else my soul shall glory  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Save the Cross! Save the Cross!
Yea, this my constant theme shall be
Through time and in eternity,
That Jesus tasted death for me  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On the Cross! On the Cross!

Let every mourner rise and cling,  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To the Cross! To the Cross!
Let every Christian come and sing,  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Round the Cross! Round the Cross!
There let the preacher take his stand,
And, with the Bible in his hand,
Declare the triumphs through the land,  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of the Cross! Of the Cross!


47
Behold the amazing sight

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Doddridge_Philip&#39;,this)">Philip Doddridge</a></div>
S.M.

BEHOLD the amazing sight,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Saviour lifted high!
The Son of God, His soul's delight,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Expires in agony!

For whom, for whom, my heart,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Were all those sorrows borne?
Why did He feel that piercing smart,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And wear that crown of thorn?

For us in love He bled,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For us in anguish died,
’Twas love that bowed His sacred head,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And pierced His bleeding side.

We see, and we adore,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy deep, Thy dying love,
We feel its strong attractive power
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To lift our souls above.

In Thee our hearts unite,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Nor share Thy grief alone
But from Thy Cross pursue our flight
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Thy triumphant throne.


48
Behold the Lamb with glory crowned!
Previous BHB #17
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kelly_Thomas&#39;,this)">Thomas Kelly</a></div>
C.M.

BEHOLD the Lamb with glory crowned!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Him all power is given;
No place too high for Him is found,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No place too high in heaven.

He fills the throne - the throne above,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Its rights to Him belong;
The object of His Father’s love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The theme of angels’ song.

Though high, yet He accepts the praise
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His people offer here;
The faintest, feeblest lay they raise
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Will reach the Saviour’s ear.

This song be ours, and this alone,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That celebrates the Name
Of Him who sits upon the throne,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And that exalts the Lamb.

To Him whom men despise and slight,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Him be glory given;
The crown is His, and His by right
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The highest place in heaven.


49
Behold the Man by God approved

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Munro_Alexander&#39;,this)">Alexander Munro</a></div>
L.M.

BEHOLD the Man by God approved
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As with a steadfast face He moved
Toward Jerusalem to die, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His Father God to glorify.

His perfect love expressed we see,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To those who, in their enmity,
His kindness and His grace ignored,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And in return their rage outpoured.

O wondrous sight that meets our gaze -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Son of God, in sore amaze,
Lies prostrate in Gethsemane
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And weeps and prays in agony!

Submissive to His Father's will,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He shows His deep devotion still
In yonder judgment hall displayed, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thorn crowned and purple robe arrayed.

God's Holy One upon the tree 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Laid down His life triumphantly;
The depth of sorrow there entailed
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His full perfection has unveiled.

And thus with hearts that overflow,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Although we cannot fully know
His preciousness, for Him we raise
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Thee, O God, our hymn of praise.


50
Behold the Throne of grace!
Previous BHB #18
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Newton_John&#39;,this)">John Newton  (1779)</a></div>
S.M.

BEHOLD the throne of grace!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The promise calls us near,
To seek our God and Father’s face
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who loves to answer prayer.

That rich, atoning blood,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Which sprinkled round we see,
Provides for those who come to God
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;An all-prevailing plea.

My soul, ask what thou wilt,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou canst not be too bold;
Since His own blood for Thee was spilt,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What else can He withhold?

Beyond our utmost wants
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His love and power can bless;
To praying souls He always grants
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;More than they can express.

Since ’tis the Lord’s command,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our mouth we’ll open wide:
Lord, open Thou Thy bounteous hand,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That we may be supplied.

Thine image, Lord, bestow,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy presence and Thy love;
We seek to serve Thee here below,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And reign with Thee above.


51
Behold, what love, what boundless love
Previous BHB #26<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Boswell_Robert&#39;,this)">Robert Boswell</a> Alt. Margaret S. Sullivan</div>
P.M.

BEHOLD, what love, what boundless love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Father hath bestowed
On sinners lost, that we should be
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Now called the sons of God.

c
Behold…what manner of love…
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What manner of love the Father hath bestowed upon us, 
that we…that we should be called
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;…should be called the sons of God.

No longer far from Him, but now
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;By precious blood made nigh;
Accepted in the Well-beloved,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Near to God’s heart we lie.

c
Behold…what manner of love…
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What manner of love the Father hath bestowed upon us, 
that we…that we should be called
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;…should be called the sons of God.

What we in glory soon shall be,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;It doth not yet appear;
But when our precious Lord we see,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We shall His image bear.

c
Behold…what manner of love…
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What manner of love the Father hath bestowed upon us, 
that we…that we should be called
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;…should be called the sons of God.

With such a blessèd hope in view,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We would more holy be,
More like our risen, glorious Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Whose face we soon shall see.

c
Behold…what manner of love…
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What manner of love the Father hath bestowed upon us, 
that we…that we should be called
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;…should be called the sons of God.


52
Beneath an eastern sky, amid a rabble’s cry

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Long_John&#39;,this)">John Long</a></div>
6.6.6.4.

BENEATH an eastern sky,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Amid a rabble’s cry,
A Man went forth to die,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For me! for me!

Thorn-crowned His blessèd head,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Blood-stained His every tread;
Cross-laden He was led,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For me! for me!

Piercèd His hands and feet,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Three hours o’er Him beat
Fierce rays of noon-tide heat,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For me! for me!

Thus wast Thou made all mine;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lord, make me wholly Thine;
Grant grace and strength divine
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To me! to me!

In thought and word and deed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy will to do, O lead
My soul, e’en though it bleed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Thee, to Thee.


53
Beneath the Cross of Jesus
Previous BHB #23
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Clephane_Elizabeth_Cecilia&#39;,this)">Elizabeth Cecilia Douglas Clephane  (1868)</a></div>
7.6.7.6.D.

BENEATH the Cross of Jesus
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I fain would take my stand,
The shadow of a mighty Rock
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Within a weary land;
A home within the wilderness,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A rest upon the way,
From the burning of the noontide heat
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And the burden of the day.

O safe and happy shelter!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O refuge tried and sweet!
O trysting-place, where heaven’s love
And heaven’s justice meet!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As to the pilgrim patriarch
That wondrous dream was given,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;So seems my Saviour’s Cross to me
A ladder up to heaven.

There lies beneath its shadow,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But on the farther side,
The darkness of an awful grave
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That gapes both deep and wide;
And there between us stands the Cross,
Two arms outstretched to save,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Like a watchman set to guard  the way
From that eternal grave.

Upon the Cross of Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Mine eye at times can see
The very dying form of One
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who suffered there for me;
And from my smitten heart with tears,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Two wonders I confess,
The wonder of His glorious love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And my own worthlessness.

I take the Cross of Jesus
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For my abiding place;
I ask no other sunshine than
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The sunshine of His face;
Content to let the world go by,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To know no gain nor loss:
My sinful self my only shame,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My glory all the Cross.


54
Beyond the scenes of darkest night

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Munro_M_George&#39;,this)">George M. Munro</a></div>
8.6.8.8.6.

BEYOND the scenes of darkest night,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of death and agony,
We now, in resurrection light,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Behold the Conqu'ror in the fight 
Ascend triumphantly.

In conflict knew He no retreat
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From sorrow, shame and woe.
In victory o'er death complete,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He bruised the serpent 'neath His feet 
And laid the tyrant low.

And now He sits upon the throne
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;At God's right hand above,
In grace to care for all His own, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And thus to them make fully known
His changeless, boundless love. 

But O, what rapture will it be
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When we no more shall roam, 
When, from all sin and sorrow free, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We dwell with Him eternally,
For evermore at home!


55
Blessed assurance, Jesus is mine!

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Alystyne_Frances_Jane_Van&#39;,this)">Frances (Fanny) Jane Crosby</a></div>
P.M.

Blessèd assurance, Jesus is mine!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Oh, what a foretaste of glory divine!
Heir of salvation, purchase of God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Born of His Spirit, washed in His blood.

c
This is my story, this is my song,
Praising my Saviour all the day long;
This is my story, this is my song,
Praising my Saviour all the day long.

Perfect submission, perfect delight,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Visions of rapture now burst on my sight;
Angels descending, bring from above
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Echoes of mercy, whispers of love.

c
This is my story, this is my song,
Praising my Saviour all the day long;
This is my story, this is my song,
Praising my Saviour all the day long.

Perfect submission, all is at rest,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I in my Saviour am happy and blest;
Watching and waiting, looking above,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Filled with His goodness, lost in His love.

c
This is my story, this is my song,
Praising my Saviour all the day long;
This is my story, this is my song,
Praising my Saviour all the day long.


56
Blessed be God, our God!
Previous BHB #24
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Bonar_Horatius&#39;,this)">Horatius Bonar (1845)</a></div>
6.10.10.6.

Blessèd be God, our God!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who gave for us His well - belovèd Son,
His Gift of gifts, all other gifts in one;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Blessèd be God, our God!

What will He not bestow?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who freely gave this mighty Gift unbought,
Unmerited, unheeded, and unsought;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What will He not bestow?

He sparèd not His Son!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;’Tis this that silences each rising fear,
’Tis this that bids the hard thought disappear;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He sparèd not His Son!

Who shall condemn us now?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Since Christ has died, and  risen, and gone above,
For us to plead at the right hand of Love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who shall condemn us now?

’Tis God that justifies!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who shall recall His pardon or His grace?
Or who the broken chain of guilt replace? 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;’Tis God that justifies!

The victory is ours!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For us in might came forth the Mighty One; 
For us He fought the fight, the triumph won;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The victory is ours!


57
Blessed God of Glory

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Harrison_William&#39;,this)">William Harrison</a></div>
6.5.6.5.D.

Blessèd God of Glory, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Here our hearts would bow; 
Grateful, we adore Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thankful, praise Thee now 
For Thy Son eternal,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who, revealed in time, 
Showed Thy Love supernal, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In His grace sublime.

Here, that holy Stranger
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We would contemplate, 
Who, in yonder manger,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lay in low estate, 
Earthly pomp and splendour
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From Him far removed: 
What He did surrender 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Mightiest Love has proved.

Living for Thy pleasure,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Telling out Thy grace, 
Love, in boundless measure,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Reached the lowest place. 
That, with Him victorious,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We should thus be found 
In this service glorious 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Where our joys abound.

Hearts and voices blending,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Joyfully we raise 
Through Thy Son ascending
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sacrifice of praise.
He Thy heart’s chief treasure,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Theme of themes so sweet;
Here Thou findest pleasure, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Perfect and complete.


58
Blessed God, our hearts are bringing

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Ewan_Y_Isaac&#39;,this)">Isaac Y. Ewan</a></div>
8.5.8.3.

Blessèd God, our hearts are bringing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Unto Thee, as one,
Sacrifice of praise with singing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of Thy Son.

Thou hast told us all the story
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;How in grace He came 
Manifesting all the glory
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of Thy Name.

And, by faith upon Him gazing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We to Thee draw near, 
Thus His holy Person praising
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In Thine ear.

Of Thy heart's most precious treasure
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Praise we raise on high -
Him, Whose path brought perfect pleasure
To Thine eye.

Who, in holy manhood living,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Set Himself apart,
Perfect satisfaction giving
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Thy heart.

Who, at last, in holy suff'ring,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Dying willingly,
Gave Himself, a whole burnt off'ring
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All for Thee.


59
Blessed God, we come before Thee

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kirkpatrick_William&#39;,this)">William Kirkpatrick</a></div>
8.7.8.7.7.7.

Blessèd God, we come before Thee, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And with holy rev'rence bow, 
While in worship we adore Thee
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As His God and Father now; 
Musing on Thy Son today
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In Thine Own appointed way.

We remember Him as being
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Found on earth in manhood true, 
How He said, His path fore-seeing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“Lo, I come, Thy will to do”. 
Man of single purpose, He
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;This accomplished perfectly.

In that holy body dwelling,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Which for Him Thou didst prepare, 
Unto men Thy heart forth-telling,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For Thine interests He would care, 
Drawing, binding to Him still, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Those who do the Father's will!

Such a steadfast path reviewing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Filled throughout with nought but good, 
Finding ever in the doing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of Thy will and work His food!
We His preciousness proclaim, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Making mention of His Name.


60
Blessed God, we meditate

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Hannaford_H&#39;,this)">H. Hannaford</a></div>
7.7.7.7.

Blessèd God, we meditate 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On Thy Son in low estate, 
Glory seeking not, nor fame, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Moving to a Cross of shame.

Perfect Man to Thee, His God, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Beautiful the path He trod! 
His delight to do Thy will, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And the Scriptures to fulfil!

Pleasing not Himself on earth, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Manifesting rarest worth, 
Ever doing nought but good, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All Thy will His pleasant food!

Clothed with meekness and with might, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Turning not to left or right.
Thus the One Whom Thou did'st send 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Journeyed onward to the end.

Thee, O Blessèd God, we praise, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Meditating on His ways
In the perfect path He trod, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Glorifying Thee, His God.


61
Blessed Lord, our souls are longing
Previous BHB #19<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Peters_Mary&#39;,this)">Mary Bowly Peters</a></div>
8.7.8.7.4.7.

Blessèd Lord, our souls are longing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thee, our risen Head, to see;
And the cloudless morning’s dawning,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When Thy saints shall gathered be:
Grace and glory,
All our well-springs are in Thee.

All the sorrow we are tasting
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Is but as the dream of night:
To the day of God we’re hasting,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Looking for it with delight;
Thou art coming,
This will satisfy our sight.

True, the silent grave is keeping
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Many a seed in weakness sown:
But the saints, in Thee now sleeping,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Raised in power, shall share Thy throne;
Resurrection!
Lord of Glory! ’tis Thine own.

As we sing, our hearts grow lighter;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We are children of the day;
Sorrow makes our hope the brighter;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Faith regards not the delay;
Sure the promise!
We shall meet Thee on the way.


62
Blessed retrospection, thus His steps to trace!

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Ewan_Y_Isaac&#39;,this)">Isaac Y. Ewan</a></div>
6.5.6.5.D.

Blessèd retrospection,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thus His steps to trace!
Tasting His rejection
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In the outside place,
Raptured hearts are raising
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Unto Thee, His God,
Praise beyond all praising
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For the path He trod.

In a world without Him,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Gathered thus apart,
As we think about Him,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In each musing heart,
Thoughts arise demanding
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Songs of praise anew
With the understanding
