&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And the spirit too.

Thus we contemplate Him,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Moving on and through:
Sorrows that await Him
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Ever all in view!
Though in full foreknowing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What He’d reach at length 
Comely in His going,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Steadfast in His strength!

Touched with heavenly wonder,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We, with one accord,
Reverently ponder
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our Belovèd Lord:
And to Thee, His Father,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Here in praise express
All our hearts can gather
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of His preciousness. 


63
Blest morning, whose first dawning rays
Previous BHB #370<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Watts_Isaac&#39;,this)">Isaac Watts 1709</a></div>
C.M.

BLEST morning, whose first dawning rays
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Beheld the Son of God
Arise triumphant from the grave,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And leave His dark abode.

Wrapt in the silence of the tomb
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The great Redeemer lay,
Till the revolving skies had brought
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The third, the appointed day.

Hell and the grave combined their force
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To hold our Lord, in vain;
Sudden the Conqueror arose
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And burst their feeble chain.

To Thy great name, almighty Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We sacred honours pay,
And loud hosannahs shall proclaim
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The triumphs of the day.

Salvation and immortal praise
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To our victorious King!
Let heaven and earth, and rocks and seas,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With glad hosannas ring.


64
Break every barrier down
Previous BHB #371<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Mullen_Bertha&#39;,this)">Bertha Mullen</a></div>
S.M.D.

BREAK every barrier down,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou Lamb of Calvary;
Show me the awfulness of sin,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The thing which grieveth Thee:
Purge Thou my soul from dross,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Cleanse me from every sin,
Wash me in Thine atoning blood,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And make me pure within.

Break every barrier down
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Till Christ be formed in me;
Till of the travail of Thy soul
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou, satisfied, shalt see:
May earth’s success seem nought,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;May self be lost to sight,
Reproach for Thee be counted joy
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And weakness turn to might.

Break every barrier down,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou risen Son of God;
Take Thou possession of my heart,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I crown Thee now as Lord!
O for a closer walk,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A greater love for Thee,
A fuller knowledge of Thyself,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A life of victory.

Break every barrier down
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And reign triumphant, Lord;
May every breathing of my heart
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With Thine be in accord:
Grant me to enter in
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The secret place with Thee,
To walk with Thee, as Enoch walked,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Into eternity.


65
Break Thou the bread of life
Previous BHB #373<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Lathbury_Mary_Artemisia&#39;,this)">Mary Artemisia Lathbury</a> & Alexander Groves  (1877)</div>
6.4.6.4.D.

BREAK Thou the bread of life,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Dear Lord, to me,
As Thou didst break the loaves
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Beside the sea;
Beyond the sacred page
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I seek Thee, Lord;
My spirit pants for Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O living Word.

Break Thou the bread of life,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O Lord, to me,
That hid within my heart
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy word may be:
Mould Thou each inward thought,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From self set free,
And let my steps be all
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Controlled by Thee.

Open Thy word of truth
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That I may see
Thy message written clear
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And plain for me;
Then, in sweet fellowship,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Walking with Thee,
Thine image on my life
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Engraved will be.

Bless Thou the truth, dear Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To me, to me,
As Thou didst bless the bread
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;By Galilee;
Then shall all bondage cease,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All fetters fall;
And I shall find my peace,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My all in all.


66
Brethren, let us join to bless
Previous BHB #20
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Cennick_John&#39;,this)">John Cennick</a></div>
7.7.7.7.

BRETHREN, let us join to bless
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus Christ, our joy and peace;
Him, who bowed His head so low
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Underneath our load of woe.

His the curse, the wounds, the gall,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His the stripes - He bore them all;
His the dying cry of pain
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When our sins He did sustain.

He, the accepted Sacrifice,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From the vanquished grave did rise;
Free Himself, He set us free
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In His perfect liberty.

Ransomed now, accepted, free,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Safe from judgment, Lord, in Thee,
We rejoice that God can bless
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All who do Thy name confess.

Praise our God who willed it thus;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Praise the Lamb who died for us;
Praise the Father, through the Son,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who so vast a work hath done.

Thee, our Saviour, we adore,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And would serve Thee more and  more,
Till with joy Thy face we see,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And for ever dwell with Thee.


67
Bride of the Lamb, there is for thee
Previous BHB #29<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Thompson_Boethia&#39;,this)">Boethia Thompson</a></div>
C.M.

BRIDE of the Lamb, there is for thee
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;One only safe retreat;
Where Jesus is thy heart should be,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy home at His dear feet.

When Satan tracks thy lonely way,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;There his temptations meet;
In Jesus’ presence watch and pray,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Yea, conquer at His feet.

Since thou hast much to learn, e’en though
Thou art in Christ complete,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In grace and knowledge seek to grow
By sitting at His feet.

Through tribulation hasten on,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With Christ the cross is sweet;
The little while will soon be gone,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Keep only at His feet.

Bride of the Lamb, forget the past,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Prepare the Lord to greet;
’Tis thine to share His throne, and cast
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy wreath before His feet.


68
Bright with all His crowns of glory
Previous BHB #31<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Denny_Sir_Edward&#39;,this)">Sir Edward Denny</a></div>
8.7.8.7.4.7.

BRIGHT with all His crowns of glory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;See the Royal Victor’s brow;
Once for sinners marr’d and gory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;See the Lamb exalted now,
While before Him
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All His ransomed brethren bow.

Blessèd morning! long expected,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lo! they fill the peopled air,
Mourners once, by man rejected,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;They, with Him, exalted there,
Sing His praises,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And His throne of glory share.

King of kings! let earth adore Him,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;High on His exalted throne;
Fall, ye nations, fall before Him,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And His righteous sceptre own;
All the glory
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Be to Him, and Him alone.


69
Brightness of God’s glory He

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Anonymous_Hymn_Writers&#39;,this)">Author unknown</a></div>
7.7.7.8.

BRIGHTNESS of God’s glory He,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Full essential deity,
Heir enthroned in majesty,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hallelujah, what a Saviour!

Willing lower to become
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Learned obedience though a Son,
Perfected, He fills the throne,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hallelujah, what a Saviour!

Holy, harmless, undefiled,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Yet by wicked men reviled,
Us to God He reconciled,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hallelujah, what a Saviour!

Offered once to bear our sin,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Through His blood He entered in,
Seated there we honour Him,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hallelujah, what a Saviour!

Sin remembered now no more,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Bold to enter, near we draw,
Him we worship, Him adore,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hallelujah, what a Saviour!


70
Brightness of the eternal glory

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Robinson_Robert&#39;,this)">Robert Robinson</a></div>
8.7.8.7.

BRIGHTNESS of the eternal glory
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shall Thy praise unuttered lie?
Who would hush the heaven-sent story
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of the Lamb who came to die?

Came from Godhead’s fullest glory
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Down to Calvary’s depth of woe;
Now on high, we bow before Thee;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Streams of praises ceaseless flow!

Sing His blest triumphant rising;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sing Him on the Father’s throne; 
Sing - till heaven and earth surprising,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Reigns the Nazarene alone.


71
By Christ redeemed, in Christ restored
Previous BHB #372<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Rawson_George&#39;,this)">George Rawson  (1857)</a></div>
8.8.8.4.

BY Christ redeemed, in Christ restored,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We keep the memory adored,
And show the death of our dear Lord
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Until He come.

His body, broken in our stead,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Is seen in this memorial bread,
And so our feeble love is fed
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Until He come.

The drops of His dread agony,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His life-blood shed for us, we see;
The wine shall tell the mystery
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Until He come.

Until the trump of God be heard,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Until the ancient graves be stirred,
And with the great commanding word
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Lord shall come.

O blessèd hope! with this elate,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Let not our hearts be desolate,
But, strong in faith, in patience wait
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Until He come.


72
Came He to Bethlehem’s manger

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;MacGill_Wakefield_J&#39;,this)">J. Wakefield MacGill</a></div>
8.7.8.7.D. with Chorus

CAME He to Bethlehem’s manger 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Infant, yet glorious Lord?
Stood He in temple and uttered
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Wondrous and mystical word?
Toiled He in workshop, and lowly
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Bore He the burden of life?
Calmly, and sweetly, and purely
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Mixed He in anger or strife?

c
Love brought Him down from the glory,
Love made Him come from the sky,
Love in His heart for the sinner
Led Him to suffer and die.

Passed He the fiery temptation?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Was He the homeless and lone?
Was He despised and rejected?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Claimed He dark sorrow His own?
Came He to Olivet’s garden?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Drank He the dregs of the cup?
Say, was that holy cheek sullied?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Gave He His precious life up?

c
Love brought Him down from the glory,
Love made Him come from the sky,
Love in His heart for the sinner
Led Him to suffer and die.

Went He to Pilate and Herod?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Bore He the scourge and the thorn?
Prayed He His Father to pardon?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Died He midst mocking and scorn?
Lay He in grave of the rich man?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Rose He in majesty grand?
Went He to claim as a victor
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Trophies as countless as sand?

c
Love brought Him down from the glory,
Love made Him come from the sky,
Love in His heart for the sinner
Led Him to suffer and die.


73
Christ the Lord is risen on high!
Previous BHB #37<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Wesley_Charles&#39;,this)">Charles Wesley  (1745)</a></div>
7.7.7.7.

CHRIST the Lord is risen on high!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sing, ye heavens, and earth reply;
He endured the Cross, the grave,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sinners to redeem and save.

Love’s redeeming work is done,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Fought the fight, the battle won!
He Himself our ransom paid,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Peace with God for ever made.

Vain the stone, the watch, the seal,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Christ has burst the gates of hell;
Vain their efforts to enthral,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He has triumphed over all.

Christ our Lord is risen indeed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Christ is now the Church’s Head;
Loud the song of triumph raise,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Celebrate the Victor’s praise.


74
Christians, go and tell of Jesus
Previous BHB #35<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Hammond_Edward_Payson&#39;,this)">Edward Payson Hammond</a></div>
P.M.

CHRISTIANS, go and tell of Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;How He died to save our souls;
How that He, from sin might free us,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Suffered agonies untold.

c
Yes, we’ll go and tell of Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The pure and holy, meek and lowly Jesus;
Yes, we’ll go and tell of Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who died our souls to save.

Tell the guilty of their danger
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;While they wander far from God;
While they live to Christ a stranger,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And reject His precious Word.

c
Yes, we’ll go and tell of Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The pure and holy, meek and lowly Jesus;
Yes, we’ll go and tell of Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who died our souls to save.

Tell them of the joys of heaven,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Purchased by the Saviour’s blood,
How, that they might be forgiven,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus left His home above.

c
Yes, we’ll go and tell of Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The pure and holy, meek and lowly Jesus;
Yes, we’ll go and tell of Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who died our souls to save.

Tell them how He hath ascended
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To prepare a home on high,
Where all sorrows shall be ended,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Where the saved shall never die.

c
Yes, we’ll go and tell of Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The pure and holy, meek and lowly Jesus;
Yes, we’ll go and tell of Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who died our souls to save.


75
Christ’s grave is vacant now
Previous BHB #32
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Tomkins_W_Noel&#39;,this)">W. Noel Tomkins</a></div>
S.M.

CHRIST’S grave is vacant now,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Left for the throne above;
His Cross asserts God’s right to bless
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In His own boundless love.

’Twas there His blood was shed;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;’Twas there His life was poured;
There Mercy gained her diadem,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;While Justice sheathed her sword.

And thence the child of faith
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sees judgment all gone by,
Perceives the sentence fully met:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“The soul that sins shall die!”

Learns how that God in love
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Gave Christ the sins to bear
Of all who own His Lordship now,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That they His place might share;

And cries with wondering joy,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“As He is so am I,
Pure, holy, loved as Christ Himself;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who shall my peace destroy?”

Reach my blest Saviour first;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Take Him from God’s esteem;
Prove Jesus bears one spot of sin;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Then tell me I’m unclean!

Nay! for He purged my guilt
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;By His own precious blood,
And such its virtue not a stain
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;E’er meets the eye of God.


76
Come, all ye saints of God!
Previous BHB #34<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Boden_James&#39;,this)">James Boden</a></div>
6.6.4.6.6.6.4.

COME, all ye saints of God!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Publish through earth abroad 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus’ great fame:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Tell what His love has done;
Trust in His name alone;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shout to His lofty throne,  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Worthy the Lamb!

Hence! gloomy doubts and fears;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Dry up your mournful tears;  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Swell the glad throng:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Christ, the heavenly King,
Strike each melodious string,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From heart and voice to sing,  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Worthy the Lamb!

Hark, how the choirs above,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Filled with the Saviour’s love,  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Dwell on His name!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;There, too, shall we be found,
With light and glory crowned,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;While all the heavens resound  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Worthy the Lamb!


77
Come, every joyful heart
Previous BHB #375
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Stennett_Samuel&#39;,this)">Samuel Stennett</a></div>
6.6.6.6.8.8.

COME, every joyful heart
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That loves the Saviour’s name, 
Your noblest powers exert
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To celebrate His fame;
Tell all above and all below
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The debt of love to Him you owe.

He left His starry crown,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And laid His robes aside;
On wings of love came down,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And wept, and bled, and died:
What He endured no tongue can tell,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To save our souls from death and  hell.

From the dark grave He rose -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The mansion of the dead;
And thence His mighty foes
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In glorious triumph led:
Up through the sky the Conqueror rode,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And reigns on high, the Saviour God.

From thence He’ll quickly come,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His chariot will not stay,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And bear His people home
To realms of endless day:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;There shall we see His lovely  face,
And ever be in His embrace.

Jesus, we ne’er can pay
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The debt of love we owe;
Yet grant us day by day
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our gratitude to show;
Our life, our all, to Thee we give,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Thee, by whom alone we live.


78
Come, let us all unite to sing
Previous BHB #39
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kingsbury_Howard&#39;,this)">Howard Kingbury (?)</a></div>
8.3.8.3.8.8.8.3.

COME, let us all unite to sing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;God is love.
Let heaven and earth their praises bring;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;God is love.
Let every soul from sin awake,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Each in his heart sweet music make,
And sing with us, for Jesus’ sake,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;God is love.

O tell to earth’s remotest bound,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;God is love.
In Christ we have redemption found:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;God is love.
His blood has purged our sins away,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His Spirit turned our night to day;
And now we can rejoice to say,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;God is love.

How happy is our portion here!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;God is love.
His promises our spirits cheer;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;God is love.
He is our sun and shield by day,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our help, our hope, our strength, and stay,
He will be with us all the way:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;God is love.

In glory we shall sing again,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;God is love.
Yes, this shall be our lofty strain,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;God is love.
Whilst endless ages roll along,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In concert with the heavenly throng,
This shall be still our sweetest song,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;God is love.


79
Come, let us join our cheerful songs
Previous BHB #38
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Watts_Isaac&#39;,this)">Isaac Watts  (1707)</a></div>
C.M.

COME, let us join our cheerful songs
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With all around the throne;
Ten thousand thousand are their tongues,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But all their joys are one.

“Worthy the Lamb that died”, they cry,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“To be exalted thus”;
“Worthy the Lamb”, our lips reply,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“For He was slain for us”.

Jesus is worthy to receive
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Honour and power divine;
And blessings, more than we can give,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Be, Lord, for ever Thine!

Let all that dwell above the sky,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And air, and earth, and seas,
Conspire to raise Thy glories high,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And speak Thine endless praise.

Let all creation join in one,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To bless the sacred name
Of Him who sits upon the throne,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And to adore the Lamb.


80
Come, let us sing the matchless worth
Previous BHB #40
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Medley_Samuel&#39;,this)">Samuel Medley</a></div>
8.8.6.8.8.6.

COME, let us sing the matchless worth,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And sweetly sound the glories forth
Which in the Saviour shine:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To God and Christ our praises bring;
The song, with which the heavens ring,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We sing by grace divine.

How rich the precious blood He spilt,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our ransom from the dreadful guilt
Of sin against our God.
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;How perfect is His righteousness,
In which unspotted beauteous dress
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His saints have ever stood.

How rich the character He bears,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And all the forms of love He wears,
Exalted on the throne;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In songs of sweet untiring praise,
We would to everlasting days,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Make all His glories known.

And soon the happy day shall come,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When we shall reach our destined  home,
And see Him face to face;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Then with our Saviour, Master, Friend,
The glad eternity we’ll spend,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And celebrate His grace.


81
Come, let us sing the song of songs

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Montgomery_James&#39;,this)">James Montgomery</a></div>
L.M.

COME, let us sing the song of songs,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And yield in joyful heavenly strain
The homage which to Christ belongs:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“Worthy the Lamb, for He was slain!”

Slain to redeem us by His blood,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To cleanse from every sinful stain,
And make us kings and priests to God:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“Worthy the Lamb, for He was slain!”

To Him who suffered on the tree,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our souls, at His soul's price, to gain,
Blessing, and praise, and glory be:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“Worthy the Lamb, for He was slain!”

To Him, enthroned by filial right,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All power in heaven and earth pertain,
Honour, and majesty, and might:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“Worthy the Lamb, for He was slain!”

Long as we live, and should we die,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And while in heaven with Him we  reign,
This song our song of songs shall be:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“Worthy the Lamb, for He was slain!”


82
Come, let us to the Lord our God, with contrite hearts return

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Morison_John&#39;,this)">John Morrison</a></div>
C.M.

COME, let us to the Lord our God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With contrite hearts return;
Our God is gracious, nor will leave,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The desolate to mourn.

His voice commands the tempest forth,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And stills the stormy wave;
And, though His arm be strong  to smite,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;’Tis also strong to save.

Long hath the night of sorrow reigned;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The dawn shall bring us light:
God shall appear, and we shall rise
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With gladness in His sight.

Our hearts, if God we seek to know,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shall know Him, and rejoice;
His coming like the morn shall be,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Like morning songs His voice.

As dew upon the tender herb,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Diffusing fragrance round,
As showers that usher in the spring,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And cheer the thirsty ground;

So shall His presence bless our souls,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And shed a joyful light;
That hallowed morn shall chase away
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The sorrows of the night.


83
Come, my soul, thy suit prepare
Previous BHB #374
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Newton_John&#39;,this)">John Newton  (1779)</a></div>
7.7.7.7.

COME, my soul, thy suit prepare,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus loves to answer prayer;
He Himself has bid thee pray,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Therefore will not say thee nay.

Thou art coming to a King;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Large petitions with thee bring;
For His grace and power are such,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;None can ever ask too much.

Lord, I come to Thee for rest;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Take possession of my breast;
There Thy blood-bought right
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;maintain,
And without a rival reign.

As the image in the glass
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Answers the beholder's face,
Thus unto my heart appear;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Print Thine own resemblance there.

While I am a pilgrim here,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Let Thy love my spirit cheer;
As my Guide, my Guard, my Friend,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lead me to my journey's end.

Show me what I have to do; 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Every hour my strength renew.
Let me live a life of faith;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Let me die Thy people's death.


84
Come sing, my soul, and praise the Lord
Previous BHB #335
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Whittle_Major_Daniel_Webster&#39;,this)">Daniel Webster Whittle</a></div>
L.M.D.

COME sing, my soul, and praise the Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who hath redeemed thee by His blood,
Delivered thee from chains that bound,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And brought thee to redemption ground.

c
Redemption ground, the ground of peace!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Redemption ground, O wondrous grace!
Here let our praise to God abound,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who saves us on redemption ground! 

Once from my God I wandered far,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And with His holy will made war;
But now my songs to God abound:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I’m standing on redemption ground.

c
Redemption ground, the ground of peace!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Redemption ground, O wondrous grace!
Here let our praise to God abound,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who saves us on redemption ground! 

O joyous hour when God to me
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A vision gave of Calvary!
My bonds were loosed, my soul unbound:
I sang upon redemption ground.

c
Redemption ground, the ground of peace!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Redemption ground, O wondrous grace!
Here let our praise to God abound,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who saves us on redemption ground! 

No works of merit now I plead,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But Jesus take for all my need;
No righteousness in me is found,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Except upon redemption ground.

c
Redemption ground, the ground of peace!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Redemption ground, O wondrous grace!
Here let our praise to God abound,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who saves us on redemption ground! 


85
Come, Thou Fount of every blessing
Previous BHB #43
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Robinson_Robert&#39;,this)">Robert Robinson  (1758)</a></div>
8.7.8.7.

COME, Thou Fount of every blessing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Tune my heart to sing Thy grace;
Streams of mercy, never ceasing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Call for songs of loudest praise.

Teach me, Lord, some rapturous measure,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Meet for blood-bought hosts above;
Let me sing the countless treasure,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of my God’s unchanging love.

Jesus sought me when a stranger,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Wandering from the fold of God;
He, to rescue me from danger,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Interposed His precious blood.

Oh, to grace how great a debtor,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Daily I’m constrained to be!
Let that grace, Lord, like a fetter,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Bind my wandering heart to Thee.

Prone to wander, Lord, I feel it,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Prone to leave the God I love;
Keep my heart from wandering, keep it;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Till I’m perfected above.

Here I’ll raise my Ebenezer,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hither by Thy help I’ve come;
And I hope, by Thy good pleasure,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Safely to arrive at home.


86
Come, ye that know the Saviour’s name
Previous BHB #41<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Burder_George&#39;,this)">George Burder</a></div>
C.M.

COME, ye that know the Saviour’s name,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And raise your thoughts above;
Let every heart and voice unite
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To sing - that God is love!

His Word this precious truth reveals,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And all His mercies prove;
Creation and redemption join
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To show - that God is love!

His patience, bearing much and long
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With those who from Him rove,
His kindness, while He leads them home,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Attest - that God is love!

The work begun is carried on
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;By power from heaven above;
And every step, from first to last,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Declares - that God is love!

O may we all, while here below,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His perfect will approve,
Till nobler songs, in brighter worlds
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Proclaim - that God is love!


87
Crown Him with many crowns
Previous BHB #376
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Bridges_Matthew&#39;,this)">Matthew Bridges</a> & <a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Thring_Godfrey&#39;,this)">Godfrey Thring</a>  (1852)</div>
S.M.D.

CROWN Him with many crowns,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Lamb upon His throne;
Hark! how the heavenly anthem drowns
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All music but its own;
Awake, my soul, and sing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of Him who died for thee,
And hail Him as thy matchless King
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Through all eternity.

Crown Him the Lord of life
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who triumphed o’er the grave,
And rose victorious in the strife
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For those He came to save:
His glories now we sing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who died and rose on high,
Who died eternal life to bring,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And lives that death may die.

Crown Him the Lord of heaven,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Enthroned in worlds above,
The King of kings to whom is given
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The wondrous name of Love:
His reign shall know no end,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And round His piercèd feet
Fair flowers of Paradise extend
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Their fragrance ever sweet.

Crown Him the Lord of lords,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who over all doth reign,
Who once on earth, the incarnate Word,
For ransomed sinners slain,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Now lives in realms of light,
Where saints with angels sing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Their songs before Him day and  night,
Their God, Redeemer, King.

Crown Him the Lord of years,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Potentate of time,
Creator of the rolling spheres,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Ineffably sublime.
All hail, Redeemer, hail!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For Thou has died for me:
Thy praise and glory shall not fail
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Throughout eternity.


88
Crowned with thorns upon the tree
Previous BHB #42<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Guinness_Henry_Grattan&#39;,this)">H. Grattan Guinness</a></div>
7.7.7.7.

CROWNED with thorns upon the tree,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Silent in Thine agony;
Dying, crushed beneath the load
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of the wrath and curse of God.

On Thy pale and suff’ring brow,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Mystery of love and woe;
On Thy grief and sore amaze,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Saviour, I would fix my gaze!

On Thy pierced and bleeding breast
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou dost bid the weary rest;
Rest there from the world’s false ways,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Rest there from its vanities.

Rest in pardon and relief
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From the load of guilt and grief;
Rest in Thy redeeming blood,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Rest in perfect peace with God.

Sin-atoning Sacrifice,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou art precious in mine eyes;
Thou alone my rest shall be,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Now and through eternity.


89
Crowns of glory ever bright
Previous BHB #44<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kelly_Thomas&#39;,this)">Thomas Kelly</a></div>
7.7.7.7.D.

CROWNS of glory ever bright
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Rest upon the Victor’s head;
Crowns of glory are His right,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His, who liveth, and was dead.
Jesus fought and won the day,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Such a day was never fought;
Well His people now may say,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;See what God, our God, has  wrought.

He subdued the powers of hell;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In the fight He stood alone;
All His foes before Him fell,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;By His single arm o’erthrown.
They have fall’n to rise no more;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Final is the foe’s defeat;
Jesus triumphed by His power,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And His triumph is complete.

His the fight, the arduous toil,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His the honours of the day,
His the glory and the spoil,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus bears them all away.
Now proclaim His deeds afar,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Fill the world with His renown;
His alone the Victor’s car,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His the everlasting crown.


90
Darkness veiled Gethsemane

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kirkpatrick_William&#39;,this)">William Kirkpatrick</a></div>
7.7.7.7.7.7.

DARKNESS veiled Gethsemane
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;While He prayed in agony;
Sore amazed, His sweat like blood,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Cries and tears poured forth in flood! 
His th' exceeding sorrow there,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His the cup that none might share.

Undesired, betrayed and bound, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Wicked men beset Him round;
Spat on, scourged, and mocked in scorn,
Robed in purple, wreathed with thorn!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;’Mid such base indignity,
Matchless His tranquillity.

There, upon the tree, He passed 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Into sorrow's depths at last,
He alone, so spotless pure,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Could such anguish sore endure,
Would in love devoted, free, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Unto death obedient be.

Fadeless scenes thus pond'ring o'er,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our adoring thanks we pour 
Unto Thee, O God, today,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For Thy Son in this Thy way;
All His life and death review,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And His praises sing anew.


91
Day by day, and with each passing moment

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Sandell-Berg_Carolina&#39;,this)">Carolina Sandell-Berg</a> tr. A. L. Skoog</div>
P.M. 

DAY by day, and with each passing moment,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Strength I find to meet my trials here;
Trusting in my Father’s wise bestowment,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I’ve no cause for worry or for fear.
He, whose heart is kind beyond all measure,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Gives unto each day what He deems best,
Lovingly, its part of pain and pleasure,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Mingling toil with peace and rest.

Every day the Lord Himself is near me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With a special mercy for each hour;
All my cares He fain would bear and cheer me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He whose name is Counsellor and Pow’r.
The protection of His child and treasure
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Is a charge that on Himself He laid;
“As thy days, thy strength shall be in measure”,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;This the pledge to me He made.

Help me then, in every tribulation,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;So to trust Thy promises, O Lord,
That I lose not faith’s sweet consolation,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Offered me within Thy holy Word.
Help me, Lord, when toil and trouble meeting,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;E’er to take, as from a father’s hand,
One by one, the days, the moments fleeting,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Till I reach the promised land.


92
Burdens are lifted at Calvary

DAYS are filled with sorrow and care, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hearts are lonely and drear;
Burdens are lifted at Calvary,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus is very near.

c

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Calvary, Calvary,
Burdens are lifted at Calvary,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus is very near.

Cast your care on Jesus today,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Leave your worry and fear;
Burdens are lifted at Calvary,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus is very near.

c
