&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Calvary, Calvary,
Burdens are lifted at Calvary,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus is very near.

Troubled soul, the Saviour can see,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Every heartache and tear;
Burdens are lifted at Calvary
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus is very near.

c
Burdens are lifted at Calvary,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Calvary, Calvary,
Burdens are lifted at Calvary,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus is very near.


93
Dear Saviour, Thou Art Mine

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Hudson_Anna&#39;,this)">Anna Hudson</a></div>
P.M.

DEAR Saviour, Thou art mine,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;How sweet the thought to me!
Let me repeat Thy name,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And lift my heart to Thee.

c
Mine! mine! mine! I know Thou art mine;
Saviour, dear Saviour, I know Thou art mine.

Thou art the sinner's Friend,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;So I Thy friendship claim,
A sinner saved by grace,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When Thy sweet message came.

c
Mine! mine! mine! I know Thou art mine;
Saviour, dear Saviour, I know Thou art mine.

My hardened heart was touched;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy pardoning voice I heard;
And joy and peace came in,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;While listening to Thy Word.

c
Mine! mine! mine! I know Thou art mine;
Saviour, dear Saviour, I know Thou art mine.

So let me sing Thy praise,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;So let me call Thee mine;
I cannot doubt Thy word,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I know that I am Thine.

c
Mine! mine! mine! I know Thou art mine;
Saviour, dear Saviour, I know Thou art mine.


94
Does Jesus care when my heart is pained?

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Graeff_Frank&#39;,this)">Frank Graeff</a></div>
P.M.

DOES Jesus care when my heart is pained,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Too deeply for mirth or song;
As the burdens press, And the cares distress,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And the way grows weary and long?

c
Oh, yes, He cares; I know He cares;
His heart is touched with my grief;
When the days are weary, The long nights dreary,
I know my Saviour cares.

Does Jesus care when my way is dark
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With a nameless dread and fear?
As the daylight fades Into deep night shades,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Does He care enough to be near?

c
Oh, yes, He cares; I know He cares;
His heart is touched with my grief;
When the days are weary, The long nights dreary,
I know my Saviour cares.

Does Jesus care when I’ve tried and failed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To resist some temptation strong;
When in my deep grief I find no relief,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Though my tears flow all the night long?

c
Oh, yes, He cares; I know He cares;
His heart is touched with my grief;
When the days are weary, The long nights dreary,
I know my Saviour cares.

Does Jesus care when I’ve said “Goodbye”
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To the dearest on earth to me,
And my sad heart aches Till it nearly breaks - 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Is this aught to Him? Does He see?

c
Oh, yes, He cares; I know He cares;
His heart is touched with my grief;
When the days are weary, The long nights dreary,
I know my Saviour cares.


95
Done is the work that saves
Previous BHB #45<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Bonar_Horatius&#39;,this)">Horatius Bonar (1845)</a></div>
6.6.6.6.8.8.

DONE is the work that saves,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Once and for ever done; 
Finished the righteousness
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That clothes the unrighteous one.
The love that blesses us below
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Is flowing freely to us now.

The sacrifice is o’er,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The veil is rent in twain,
The mercy-seat is red
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With blood of Victim slain.
Why stand we then without, in fear?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The blood of Christ invites us near.

The gate is open wide;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The new and living way
Is clear, and free, and bright
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With love, and peace, and day.
Into the holiest now we come,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our present and our endless home.

Enthroned in majesty
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The High Priest sits within;
His precious blood, once shed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Has made and keeps us clean;
With boldness let us now draw near;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That blood has banished every fear.

Then to the Lamb once slain
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Be glory, praise, and power,
Who died, and lives again,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who liveth evermore;
Who loved us, cleansed us by His blood,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And made us kings and priests to God.


96
Drawing near in full assurance

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Young_J_J&#39;,this)">J. J. Young</a></div>
8.5.8.5.D. 

DRAWING near in full assurance
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We would seek Thy face,
Praise and worship and adore Thee
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;God of matchless grace;
Coming, Lord, with holy boldness
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To the mercy seat,
For the sprinkled blood has made us
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For Thy presence meet.

See the sacrifice appointed
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On the altar laid,
Christ, the sinless, spotless offering
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sin for us was made;
Yet it pleased the Lord to bruise Him
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Blessèd be Thy name
For the wondrous fragrance rising
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From that death of shame.

In the evening hour they laid Him
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In a rich man’s tomb.
Sad indeed, their hearts and heavy,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Filled with deepest gloom;
But the resurrection morning
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Chased away their fears;
‘He is risen’, ‘He is risen’,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Wiped away their tears.

In the morning we shall see Him,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Gaze upon His face;
Fall in worship and adore Him
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For His wondrous grace;
With what bliss our hearts shall greet Him
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In that morning fair.
There to be for ever with Him,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And His joy to share.

Hark that Hallelujah chorus
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Ringing through the sky,
‘Praise the Saviour, praise the Saviour!’
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Countless hosts reply:
‘He is worthy, He is worthy’,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Join the glad refrain,
Giving everlasting glory
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To the Lamb once slain.


97
Dread Golgotha! place of blended love and sorrow

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kirkpatrick_William&#39;,this)">William Kirkpatrick</a></div>
8.7.8.7.4.7.

DREAD Golgotha! place of blended
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Love and sorrow all unknown;
On a Cross of shame suspended, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;See the One Whom men disown! 
Comprehended
By His Father - God alone!

Taunting cries and wicked railing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Rise, His holy ears to greet; 
Anguish sore, and thirst assailing, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Piercèd hands and piercèd feet!  
Love prevailing,
Where such love and sorrow meet!

“It is finished!” consummation
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Vast indeed is in that cry;
And a wondrous revelation
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of His worth, Who came to die!  
His vocation -
God alone to glorify.

Thorns around His brow entwining,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Low His head is laid at last, 
On His lifeless breast declining, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All His shame and suff’ring past.  
Love outshining,
Love unfailing, full, and vast!

On Himself thus meditating,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Unto Thee, His God, we pour 
Thanks and praise, appreciating 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy Belovèd Son the more.  
Praise creating,
Is the theme for evermore!


98
Eternal Light! Eternal Light!
Previous BHB #378<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Binney_Thomas&#39;,this)">Thomas Binney  (c.1826)</a></div>
8.6.8.8.6.

ETERNAL Light! Eternal Light!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;How pure the soul must be
When, placed within Thy searching sight,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;It shrinks not, but with calm delight
Can live, and look on Thee.

The spirits that surround Thy throne
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;May bear the burning bliss;
But that is surely theirs alone,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Since they have never, never known
A fallen world like this.

O how shall I, whose native sphere
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Is dark, whose mind is dim,
Before the Ineffable appear,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And on my naked spirit bear
The uncreated beam?

There is a way for man to rise
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To that sublime abode;
An offering and a sacrifice,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A Holy Spirit’s energies,
An advocate with God.

These, these prepare us for the sight
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of holiness above;
The sons of ignorance and night
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;May dwell in the eternal Light,
Through the eternal Love.


99
Eternal Word, eternal Son!

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Anonymous_Hymn_Writers&#39;,this)">Author unknown</a></div>
8.6.8.6.8.8.

ETERNAL Word, eternal Son!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Father's constant joy,
What Thou hast done and what Thou art
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shall all our tongues employ;
Our Life, our Lord, we Thee adore;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Worthy art Thou for evermore!

The eternal glory's living light,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of God the image Thou,
Creator of the universe,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Upholding all things now;
Our Peace, our Strength, we Thee adore;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Worthy art Thou for evermore!

The Son, in whom all fulness dwells,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Through whom all glories flow,
Thou didst the human form assume
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That we our God might know;
Our Life, our Head, we Thee adore,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Worthy art Thou for evermore!

Declarer of the Father's name,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Expression of His grace,
The Word of life, the Light of men,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Lord with unveiled face;
Our Joy, our Hope, we Thee adore;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Worthy art Thou for evermore!


100
Face to face with Christ, my Saviour

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Breck_Carrie&#39;,this)">Carrie Breck</a></div>
8.7.8.7.D.

FACE to face with Christ, my Saviour,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Face to face - what will it be?
When with rapture I behold Him,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus Christ, who died for me.

c
Face to face shall I behold Him,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Far beyond the starry sky;
Face to face in all His glory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I shall see Him by and by!

Only faintly now I see Him,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With the darkling veil between,
But a blessèd day is coming,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When His glory shall be seen.

c
Face to face shall I behold Him,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Far beyond the starry sky;
Face to face in all His glory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I shall see Him by and by!

What rejoicing in His presence,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When are banished grief and pain;
When the crooked ways are straightened,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And the dark things shall be plain.

c
Face to face shall I behold Him,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Far beyond the starry sky;
Face to face in all His glory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I shall see Him by and by!

Face to face! O blissful moment!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Face to face - to see and know;
Face to face with my Redeemer,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus Christ, who loves me so.

c
Face to face shall I behold Him,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Far beyond the starry sky;
Face to face in all His glory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I shall see Him by and by!


101
Facing a task unfinished

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Houghton_Frank&#39;,this)">Frank Houghton</a></div>
7.6.7.6.D

FACING a task unfinished,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That drives us to our knees;
A need that, undiminished,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Rebukes our slothful ease;
We, who rejoice to know Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Renew before Thy throne,
The solemn pledge we owe Thee
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To go and make Thee known.

Where other lords beside Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hold their unhindered sway;
Where forces that defied Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Defy Thee still today;
With none to heed their crying,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For life, and love, and light,
Unnumbered souls are dying
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And pass into the night

We bear the torch that flaming,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Fell from the hands of those,
Who gave their lives proclaiming,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That Jesus died and rose.
Ours is the same commission,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The same glad message ours;
Fired by the same ambition,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Thee we yield our powers

O Father who sustained them,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O Spirit who inspired,
Saviour, whose love constrained them
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To toil with zeal untired;
From cowardice defend us,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From lethargy awake!
Forth on Thine errands send us
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To labour for Thy sake.


102
Faint not, Christian! though the road
Previous BHB #47<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Evans_James_Harrington&#39;,this)">James H. Evans</a></div>
7.7.7.7.

FAINT not, Christian! though the road
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Leading to Thy blest abode
Darksome be, and dang’rous too,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Christ, thy Guide, will bring thee through.

Faint not, Christian! though in rage
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Satan doth thy soul engage;
Take thee faith’s anointed shield,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Bear it to the battle-field.

Faint not, Christian! though the world
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hath its hostile flag unfurled;
Hold the Cross of Jesus fast,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou shalt overcome at last.

Faint not, Christian! though within
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;There’s a heart so prone to sin;
Christ, thy Lord, is over all,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He’ll not suffer thee to fall.

Faint not, Christian! though thy God
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Smite thee with the chast’ning rod;
Smite He must with Father’s care,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That He may His love declare.

Faint not, Christian! Christ is near;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Soon in glory He’ll appear;
Then shall end thy toil and strife,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Death be swallowed up of life.


103
Fairer than the fairest

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Irwin_William&#39;,this)">William Irwin</a></div>
6.5.6.5.D.

FAIRER than the fairest
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;’Mong the sons of men, 
Moral glories proved Him
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Great beyond our ken,
In His perfect pathway
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As on earth He trod,
Giving perfect pleasure 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Unto Thee, His God.

Majesty surpassing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Everything below
Crowned Him with a glory
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;More than men could know;
Yet a lowly meekness,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Deep, and sweet, and true, 
Marked Him in His movements
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All His pathway through.

Comeliness excelling
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In our Lord we see,
Purest heavenly fragrance
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Flowing full and free.
He our occupation,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We His praises sing,
And to Thee, His Father,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Raise our offering.


104
Father of mercies! in Thy Word
Previous BHB #337
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Steele_Anne&#39;,this)">Anne Steele  (1760)</a></div>
C.M.

FATHER of mercies! in Thy Word
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What endless glory shines!
For ever be Thy Name adored
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For these celestial lines.

Here may the wretched sons of want 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Exhaustless riches find;
Riches above what earth can grant
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And lasting as the mind.

Here may the blind and hungry come, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And light and food receive;
Here shall the meanest guest have room,
And taste, and see, and live. 

Here the Redeemer’s welcome voice
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Spreads heavenly peace around;
And life and everlasting joys
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Attend the blissful sound.

Oh, may these holy pages be
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our ever new delight!
And still new beauties may we see,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And still increasing light.

Divine Instructor! gracious Lord!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou art for ever near;
Teach us to love Thy sacred Word,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And view a Saviour there.


105
Father, Thy name our souls would bless

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Darby_John_Nelson&#39;,this)">John Nelson Darby</a></div>
C.M.

FATHER, Thy name our souls would bless,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As children taught by grace,
Lift up our hearts in righteousness
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And joy before Thy face.

Sweet is the confidence Thou giv'st,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Though high above our praise,
Our hearts resort to where Thou liv'st
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In heaven's unclouded rays.

There in the purpose of Thy love
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our place is now prepared,
As sons with Him who is above,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who all our sorrows shared.

Eternal ages shall declare
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The riches of Thy grace,
To those who with Thy Son shall share
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A son's eternal place.

Absent as yet, we rest in hope,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Treading the desert path,
Waiting for Him who takes us up
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Beyond the power of death.

We joy in Thee, Thy holy love
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our endless portion is, 
Like Thine own Son, with Him above,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In brightest heavenly bliss.

O holy Father, keep us here
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In that blest name of love,
Walking before Thee without fear
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Till all be joy above. 


106
Father, to seek Thy face

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Deck_James_George&#39;,this)">James George Deck</a></div>
6.6.6.6.8.8.

FATHER, to seek Thy face,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy children now draw near;
Before the throne of grace
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With boldness we appear;
We plead His name, His precious blood
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who loved, and made us priests to God.

No more we shun the light,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No more Thy presence fear;
In robes of spotless white
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Before Thee we appear;
Our great High Priest for us is there,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And He presents our praise and prayer.

No power have we to praise
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy name, O God of Love;
Unless Thy Spirit raise
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our thoughts and hearts above;
His grace avails in all our need -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;May He our priestly worship lead!

Lord, give us faith to plead
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy true and faithful Word -
Grace for each time of need,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And help to us afford:
Thy promises in Christ are yea,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In Him, Amen! to endless day.


107
Father! we, Thy children, bless Thee
Previous BHB #46
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Tregelles_Samuel_Prideaux&#39;,this)">Samuel Prideaux Tregelles</a></div>
8.7.8.7.D.

FATHER! we, Thy children, bless Thee
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For Thy love on us bestowed:
As our Father we address Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Called to be the sons of God.
Wondrous was Thy love in giving
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus for our sins to die!
Wondrous was His grace in leaving,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For our sakes, His home on high.

Now the sprinkled blood has freed us,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Onward go we to our rest;
Through the desert Thou dost lead us,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With Thy constant favour blest -
By Thy truth and Spirit guiding,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Earnest He of joys to come,
And with daily food providing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou dost lead Thy children home.

Though our pilgrimage be dreary,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;This is not our resting-place;
Shall we of the way be weary -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When we see our Master’s face?
Now, by faith anticipating,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In this hope our souls rejoice;
We, His promised advent waiting,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Soon shall hear His welcome voice.

Father, Oh, how vast the blessing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When Thy Son returns again!
Then Thy saints, their rest possessing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O’er the earth with Him shall reign.
For their fathers’ sakes belovèd,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Israel, in Thy grace restored,
Shall on earth, the curse removèd,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Be the people of the Lord.

Then shall countless myriads wearing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Robes made white in Jesus’ blood,
Palms (like rested pilgrims) bearing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Stand around the throne of God.
These, redeemed from every nation,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shall in triumph bless Thy name;
Every voice shall cry, “Salvation
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To our God, and to the Lamb!” 


108
Fight the good fight with all thy might
Previous BHB #381<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Monsell_John_Samuel_Bewley&#39;,this)">John Samuel Bewley Monsell  (1863)</a></div>
L.M.

FIGHT the good fight with all thy might;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Christ is thy strength, and Christ thy right;
Lay hold on life, and it shall be
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy joy and crown eternally.

Run the straight race through God’s good grace,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lift up thine eyes, and seek His face;
Life with its way before thee lies;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Christ is the path, and Christ the prize.

Cast care aside; lean on thy Guide,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His boundless mercy will provide;
Lean, and the trusting soul shall prove
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Christ is its life, and Christ its love.

Faint not, nor fear; His arm is near;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He changeth not, and thou art dear;
Only believe, and thou shalt see
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That Christ is all in all to thee.


109
Fill Thou my life, O Lord my God

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Bonar_Horatius&#39;,this)">Horatius Bonar</a></div>
C.M.D.

FILL Thou my life, O Lord my God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In every part with praise,
That my whole being may proclaim
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy Being and Thy ways;
Not for the lip of praise alone,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Nor e’en the praising heart,
I ask, but for a life made up
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of praise in every part. 

Enduring wrong, reproach, or loss,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With sweet and steadfast will;
Loving and blessing those who hate,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Returning good for ill;
Surrendering my fondest will
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In all things, great or small;
Seeking the good of others still,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Nor pleasing self at all.

So shall Thou, Lord, from me - e’en me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Receive the glory due;
And so shall I begin on earth,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The song for ever new:
So shall no part of day or night
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From sacredness be free,
But all my life, in every step,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Be fellowship with Thee.


110
For ever with the Lord! Amen! so let it be
Previous BHB #48
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Montgomery_James&#39;,this)">James Montgomery</a></div>
S.M.D.

FOR ever with the Lord!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Amen! so let it be;
Life from the dead is in that word,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;’Tis immortality.
Here in the body pent,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Absent from Him I roam,
Yet nightly pitch my moving tent
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A day’s march nearer home.

My Father’s house on high,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Home of my soul, how near
At times to faith’s foreseeing eye
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy golden gates appear!
My thirsty spirit faints
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To reach the land I love,
The bright inheritance of saints,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jerusalem above.

And though there intervene,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Rough seas and stormy skies.
Faith will not suffer aught to screen,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy glory from mine eyes:
There shall all clouds depart,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The wilderness shall cease
And sweetly shall each gladdened heart
Enjoy eternal peace.

For ever with the Lord!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Father, if ’tis Thy will,
The promise of that faithful word,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;E’en here to me fulfil.
Be Thou at my right hand,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Then can I never fail;
Uphold Thou me, and I shall stand,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Fight, and I must prevail.


111
For ever with Thee, Lord

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Darby_John_Nelson&#39;,this)">John Nelson Darby</a></div>
6.5.11.6.5.11.

FOR ever with Thee, Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And like Thee to be,
For ever with Thee, at Thy coming again;
We’ll live in Thy grace, Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We’ll gaze on Thy face,
When finished our race, at Thy coming again.

The traits of that face, Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Once marred through Thy grace,
With joy we shall trace at Thy coming again;
With Thee evermore, Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our hearts will adore,
Our sorrow be o’er at Thy coming again.

We’ll sit on Thy throne, Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Confessed as Thine own,
Of all to be known at Thy coming again.
But glory on high, Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Is not like being nigh,
When all is gone by, at Thy coming again.


112
For the bread and for the wine
Previous BHB #49<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Bonar_Horatius&#39;,this)">Horatius Bonar (1845)</a></div>
7.7.7.6.

FOR the bread and for the wine,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For the pledge that seals Him mine,
For the words of love divine,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We give Thee thanks, O Lord.

Only bread and only wine,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Yet to faith the solemn sign
Of the heavenly and divine!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We give Thee thanks, O Lord.

For the words that turn our eye
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To the Cross of Calvary,
Bidding us in faith draw nigh,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We give Thee thanks, O Lord.

For the words that fragrance breathe,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;These plain symbols underneath,
Words that His own peace bequeath,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We give Thee thanks, O Lord.

For the words that tell of home,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Pointing us beyond the tomb,
“Do ye this, until I come!”
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We give Thee thanks, O Lord.

Till He come we take the bread,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Type of Him on whom we feed,
Him who liveth and was dead!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We give Thee thanks, O Lord.

Till He come we take the cup;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As we at His table sup
Eye and heart are lifted up!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We give Thee thanks, O Lord.

For that coming, here foreshown,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For that day to man unknown,
For the glory and the throne,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We give Thee thanks, O Lord.


113
Forth from Gabbatha see Him led

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kirkpatrick_William&#39;,this)">William Kirkpatrick</a></div>
8.8.8.6.

FORTH from Gabbatha see Him led,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Foes all around and friends all fled,
Wearing the thorns upon His head,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Holy and righteous One!

Knowing the sorrow of the way, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Facing it all unflinchingly,
Moving through all obediently, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Patient, submissive One!

On to Golgotha and the tree,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Uplifted in the midst to be, 
Love there to show beyond degree,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;God's well-belovèd One!

Men all their enmity there show,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Railing and insult fiercer grow; 
Tempest and flood uprise and flow
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Against the sinless One!

Pressure intense reveals anew 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Inner perfections gleaming through;
As in His path, for God so true,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Still the devoted One!

Issues momentous now fulfilled, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Stripped every foe, the tumult stilled,
Thus would He die, as God had willed, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Ever triumphant One!


114
Forth in His Name we go

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Ewan_Y_Isaac&#39;,this)">Isaac Y. Ewan</a></div>
6.6.6.6.8.8.

FORTH in His Name we go,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A royal priesthood now,
His excellence to show,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Whom as Lord we bow; 
From darkness called to wondrous light, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The sons of day amidst the night.

Bearing the torch on high,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We go not forth in vain,
For men in darkness die,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In darkness to remain. 
They know not Him Who is the light, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Their sun goes down in endless night.

Praying for grace to live
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As fits a royal race,
We seek to men to give
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The light of truth and grace: 
His Name to bear, His will obey, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His steps to follow day by day.

Forth in His Name we go,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In royal dignity,
His excellence to show
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In what we do and say : 
To tread by faith the path He trod, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A royal priesthood unto God.


115
From Egypt lately come

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kelly_Thomas&#39;,this)">Thomas Kelly</a></div>
S.M. with Chorus

FROM Egypt lately come,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Where death and darkness reign,
We seek our new, our better home,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Where we our rest shall gain: 

c
Hallelujah!
We are on our way to God!

There sin and sorrow cease,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And every conflict’s o’er;
There shall we dwell in endless peace,
And never hunger more -

c
Hallelujah!
We are on our way to God!

There, in celestial strains,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Enraptured myriads sing;
There love in every bosom reigns,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For God Himself is King -

c
Hallelujah!
We are on our way to God!

We soon shall join the throng,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Their pleasures we shall share,
And sing the everlasting song,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With all the ransomed there -

c
Hallelujah!
We are on our way to God!

How sweet the prospect is!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;It cheers the pilgrim’s breast;
We’re journeying through the wilderness,
But soon shall gain our rest -

c
Hallelujah!
We are on our way to God!


116
From every stormy wind that blows
Previous BHB #50
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Stowell_Hugh&#39;,this)">Hugh Stowell  (1828)</a></div>
L.M.

FROM every stormy wind that blows,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From every swelling tide of woes,
There is a calm, a safe retreat;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;’Tis found beneath the Mercy-seat.

There is a place where Jesus sheds
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The oil of gladness on our heads,
A place than all beside more sweet;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;It is the blood-stained Mercy-seat.

There is a spot where spirits blend,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Where friend holds fellowship  with friend;
Though sundered far, by faith we meet
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Around one common Mercy-seat.

Ah! whither could we flee for aid,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When tempted, desolate, dismayed,
Or how the hosts of hell defeat,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Had suff’ring saints no Mercy-seat?

There, there on eagle-wing we soar,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And time and sense appear no more;
There heav’nly joys our spirits greet,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And glory crowns the Mercy-seat.

Oh, let my hand forget her skill,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My tongue be silent, cold and still,
This bounding heart forget to beat,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;If I forget the Mercy-seat.


117
From heaven He came

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Munro_M_George&#39;,this)">George M. Munro</a></div>
4.6.8.8.4.

FROM heaven He came 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Into this scene of shame,
Thy will to do, Thy claims to own, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy law fulfil, Thyself make known; 
From heaven He came.

To heaven He's gone;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The throne He sits upon.
Death He annulled. His vict'ry won, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His work complete - Triumphant One! 
To heaven He's gone.

In heaven He dwells;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He all our fear dispels;
As Great High Priest appearing there, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As Lord and Christ for us to care
In heaven He dwells.

From heaven He'll come
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To take His people home.
He came; He's gone; He dwells above; 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And soon for us in wondrous love 
From heaven He'll come.


118
From Salem’s gates advancing slow

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Butty_William&#39;,this)">William Butty</a></div>
C.M.

FROM Salem’s gates advancing slow,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What object meets my eyes,
What means that majesty of woe,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What mean those mingled cries?

Who is the man that groans beneath 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The pond’rous Cross of wood,
Whose soul’s oppressed with pangs of death,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And body bathed in blood?

Is this the man! Can this be He
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The prophets have foretold,
Should with transgressors number’d be,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And for my crimes be sold? 

Yes, now I know, ’tis He, ’tis He, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;E’en Jesus, God’s dear Son,
Wrapt in mortality to die, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For crimes that I have done.

Ah, lovely sight! A heavenly form
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For sinful souls to see,
I’ll creep beside Him as a worm,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And see Him die for me.

c
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;<i>[optional chorus]</i>
Oh, the blood of Jesus,
The precious blood of Jesus,
Oh, the blood of Jesus,
It cleanseth from all sin.


119
From whence this fear and unbelief
Previous BHB #51<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Toplady_Augustus_Montague&#39;,this)">Augustus Montague Toplady</a></div>
8.8.6.8.8.6.

FROM whence this fear and unbelief,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;If God, my Father, put to grief
His spotless Son for me?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Can He, the righteous Judge of men,
Condemn me for that debt of sin
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Which, Lord, was charged to Thee?

Complete atonement Thou hast made,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And to the utmost farthing paid
Whate’er Thy people owed;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;How, then, can wrath on me take  place,
If sheltered in God’s righteousness,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And sprinkled by Thy blood?

If Thou hast my discharge procured,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And freely in my place endured
The whole of wrath divine;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Payment God will not twice demand,
First at my bleeding Surety’s hand,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And then again at mine.

Turn then, my soul, unto thy rest;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The merits of thy great High Priest
Speak peace and liberty;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Trust in His efficacious blood,
Nor fear thy banishment from God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Since Jesus died for thee.


120
Garden of gloom appalling

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Ewan_Y_Isaac&#39;,this)">Isaac Y. Ewan</a></div>
7.6.7.6.D.

GARDEN of gloom appalling,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Where, in His sore amaze,
Earthward in anguish falling,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Prostrate, the Saviour prays;
Prays in exceeding sorrow, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Prays, on the ground bowed low 
Facing the dark tomorrow
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Full of unfathomed woe!

Garden of grief amazing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Where, in His agony, 
He, His petition raising, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Prays yet more earnestly,
Prays till His sweat is falling,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Falling like drops of blood!
Deep unto deep is calling,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Calling in flood to flood.

Garden of tears that never
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Mortal could ever weep!
Not of the common river;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Drawn from a deeper deep!
Drawn from a depth unsounded,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Coursing toward the sod,
Telling of love unbounded,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sourced in the heart of God!

Garden of myrrh unfading,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Breathing submissive prayer,
Sweet-flowing myrrh, pervading
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Heaven with its fragrance rare!
Myrrh that perfumes our praises,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Fresh from the tear-wet clod,
Softly the heart upraises
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Thee, His Father, God.


121
Gathered again to break the bread

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Ewan_Y_Isaac&#39;,this)">Isaac Y. Ewan</a></div>
C.M.

GATHERED again to break the bread
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;This first day of the week, 
We, by the Holy Spirit led, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy face, O God, would seek.

No earth-born sentiment inspires,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No fleshly zeal imparts
The touch that stirs the sacred fires 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In musing, priestly hearts.

Here, in the pow'r that ever lives,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The light that never dies, 
The estimate the Spirit gives 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Moves priestly exercise.

Thus, in the Holies, we, O God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Would unto Thee draw near
To give Thee thanks for Him Who trod 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That perfect path while here.

Thus, while our hearts within us burn,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Rememb'ring rev'rently,
We, in His holy Name, return 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thanksgivings unto Thee.


122
Gathered, Lord, around Thy table
Previous BHB #383<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Russell_Douglas&#39;,this)">Douglas Russell</a></div>
8.5.8.3.

GATHERED, Lord, around Thy table,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Now we seek Thy face;
Let us know Thy presence with us,
Lord of grace.

Love divine first drew us to Thee
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In our sin and need;
For our sin, in deep compassion,
Thou didst bleed.

Risen Lord, in glory seated,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We are one with Thee;
Thou hast snapt the chains that bound us:
We are free.

Gratefully we Thee remember
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As we break the bread,
Symbol of Thy body broken
In our stead.

Drink we too the cup of blessing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Which Thy love has filled;
Through Thy blood we have redemption:
Fears are stilled.

Backward look we, drawn to Calvary,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Musing while we sing;
Forward haste we to Thy coming,
Lord and King.


123
Gathered to Thy name, Lord Jesus
Previous BHB #384<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Wellesley_C_Anne&#39;,this)">C. A. Wellesley</a></div>
8.7.8.7.

GATHERED to Thy name, Lord Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Losing sight of all but Thee,
O what joy Thy presence gives us,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Calling up our hearts to Thee.

Loved with love which knows no measure
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Save the Father’s love to Thee,
Blessèd Lord, our hearts would treasure
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All the Father’s thoughts of Thee.

All His joy, His rest, His pleasure,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All His deep delight in Thee;
Lord, Thy heart alone can measure
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What Thy Father found in Thee.

How He set His love upon Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Called Thee His belovèd Son;
Yet for us He did not spare Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;By Thy death our life was won.

O the joy, the wondrous singing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When we see Thee as Thou art;
Thy blest name, Lord Jesus, bringing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sweetest music to God’s heart.

Notes of gladness, songs unceasing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hymns of everlasting praise,
Psalms of glory, joy increasing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Through God’s endless day of days.


124
Gazing on Thee, Lord, in glory
Previous BHB #385
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Thompson_Centra&#39;,this)">Centra Thompson</a></div>
8.7.8.7.

GAZING on Thee, Lord, in glory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;While our hearts in worship bow,
There we read the wondrous story
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of the Cross, its shame and woe.

Every mark of dark dishonour
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Heaped upon Thy thorn-crowned brow,
All the depths of Thy heart’s sorrow
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Told in answering glory now.

On that Cross, alone, forsaken,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Where no pitying eye was found;
Now to God’s right hand exalted,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With Thy praise the heavens  resound.

Did Thy God e’en then forsake Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hide His face from Thy deep need?
In Thy face, once marred and smitten,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All His glory now we read.

Gazing on it we adore Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Blessèd, precious, holy Lord;
Thou, the Lamb, art ever worthy;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;This be earth’s and heaven’s accord.

Rise our hearts, and bless the Father,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Ceaseless song e’en here begun,
Endless praise and adoration
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To the Father and the Son.


125
Gently they took Him down

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Ewan_Y_Isaac&#39;,this)">Isaac Y. Ewan</a></div>
S.M.

GENTLY they took Him down;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Unfixed His hands and feet;
Took from His head the thorny crown;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Brought forth a winding sheet.

Fine linen, fitly made,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Wrapped they around His face; 
Where never man before was laid 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Made they His resting-place.

Spices most sweet they chose;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Aloes they brought, and myrrh;
Wound Him with these in linen clothes;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Gave Him a sepulchre;

Laid Him in hewn rock;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Rolled to the door the stone; 
Watched the world add its waxen lock, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And left Him there alone.

Dead in a garden gay!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Dead, with the wanton bloom 
Giving, in incongruity,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Last touches to His tomb!


126
Give me a sight, O Saviour

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kelly_Katherine&#39;,this)">Katherine Kelly</a></div>
P.M.

GIVE me a sight, O Saviour,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of Thy wondrous love to me,
Of the love that brought Thee down to earth
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To die on Calvary.

c
Oh, make me understand it!
Help me to take it in,
What it meant to Thee, the Holy One,
To bear away my sin.

Was it the nails, O Saviour,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That bound Thee to the tree?
Nay, ’twas Thine everlasting love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy love for me, for me. 

c
Oh, make me understand it!
Help me to take it in,
What it meant to Thee, the Holy One,
To bear away my sin.

Oh, wonder of all wonders,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That through Thy death for me,
My open sins, my secret sins,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Can all forgiven be.

c
Oh, make me understand it!
Help me to take it in,
What it meant to Thee, the Holy One,
To bear away my sin.

Then melt my heart, O Saviour,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Bend me, yea, break me down,
Until I own Thee Conqueror,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And Lord and Sovereign crown.

c
Oh, make me understand it!
Help me to take it in,
What it meant to Thee, the Holy One,
To bear away my sin.


127
Glory, glory everlasting
Previous BHB #52
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kelly_Thomas&#39;,this)">Thomas Kelly</a></div>
8.7.8.7.4.7.

GLORY, glory everlasting,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Be to Him who bore the Cross,
Who redeemed our souls by tasting
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Death, the death deserved by us!
Spread His glory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who redeemed His people thus.

His is love, ’tis love unbounded,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Without measure, without end;
Human thought is here confounded,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;’Tis too vast to comprehend!
Praise the Saviour!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Magnify the sinner’s Friend.

Dwelling on the wondrous story
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of the Saviour’s Cross and shame,
Sing we, “Everlasting glory
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Be to God and to the Lamb!”
Saints and angels,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Give ye glory to His name!


128
Glory, Lord, is Thine for ever

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Williams_E&#39;,this)">E. Williams</a></div>
8.7.8.7.D.

GLORY, Lord, is Thine for ever,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Ever Thine - Thou art the Son!
Great the glory Thou art given,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Great the glory Thou hast won;
Great the glory and the splendour
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of the holy heavenly place;
Greater far the Godhead glory
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shining, Saviour, in Thy face!

Lord of glory, Thou didst enter
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;This dark world of sin and woe;
Veiled Thy glory, yet ’twas witnessed
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;By Thine own while here below.
Thou didst die, and now we praise Thee
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In Thy glory, Lord, above;
For in death Thou hast declarèd
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All the fulness of God's love.

Yes, we see Thee crowned with glory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Highest honour to Thee given;
But the glory of Thy Person
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Is the light that shines in heaven.
Thou art greater, glorious Saviour,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Than the glory Thou hast won;
This the greatness of Thy glory -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Ever blest - Thou art the Son!


129
Glory to God on high!
Previous BHB #56
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kelly_Thomas&#39;,this)">Thomas Kelly</a></div>
6.6.4.6.6.6.4.

GLORY to God on high!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Peace upon earth and joy!
Good will to man!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We who God's blessings prove,
Join with the hosts above
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To praise the Saviour's love, 
Too vast to scan.

Mercy and truth unite;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Oh, ’tis a wondrous sight,
All sights above!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus the curse sustains!
Bitter the cup He drains!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Nothing for us remains,  
Nothing but love.

Love that no tongue can teach,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Love that no thought can reach,
No love like His!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;God is its blessèd source,
Death ne’er can stop its course,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Nothing can stay its force,  
Matchless it is.

Blest in this love we sing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To God our praises bring,
All sins forgiven!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus, our Lord, to Thee
Honour and majesty
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Now and for ever be  
Here, and in heaven.


130
Glory to Thee, Thou Son of God most High
Previous BHB #386<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Quine_Edward_C&#39;,this)">Edward C. Quine</a></div>
10.4.10.4.10.10.

GLORY to Thee; Thou Son of God most High,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All praise to Thee!
Glory to Thee, enthroned above the sky,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who died for me;
High on Thy throne, Thine ear, Lord Jesus, bend
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As grateful hearts now to Thyself ascend.

Deep were Thy sorrows, Lord, when heaven frowned -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Gethsemane!
Bloodlike Thy sweat, Lord, falling  to the ground
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;So heavily;
Dark was the night, but heaven  was darker still,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O Christ my God! - is this the Father’s will?

Thorns wreathed Thy brow when hanging on the tree,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Man’s cruelty!
Why lavish love like this, O Lord,  on me?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou lovest me!
Would that my soul could understand its length,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Its breadth, depth, height, and everlasting strength!

Thy precious blood was freely shed for me
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On Calvary
To save me from a lost eternity;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Glory to Thee!
Nor death, nor hell, nor things below - above
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Can sever me from Thy eternal love.

Like shoreless seas, Thy love can know no bound;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou lovest me!
Deep, vast, immense, unfathomed, Lord - profound,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lord, I love Thee!
And when above, my crown is at Thy feet,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I’ll praise Thee still for Calvary’s mercy seat.


131
Glory unto Jesus be!
Previous BHB #54
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Rutherford_Archibald_J&#39;,this)">Archibald Rutherford</a></div>
7.7.7.7.

GLORY unto Jesus be!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From the curse He set us free;
All our guilt on Him was laid, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He the ransom fully paid.

All the blessèd work is done;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;God’s well pleasèd in His Son;
He has raised Him from the dead, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Set Him over all as Head. 

All should sing His work and worth, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All above, and all on earth;
As they sing around the throne, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“Thou art worthy, Thou alone”.

Ye who love Him, cease to mourn,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He will certainly return; 
All His saints with Him shall reign -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“Come, Lord Jesus, come! Amen.”.


132
Go, labour on; spend, and be spent
Previous BHB #387
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Bonar_Horatius&#39;,this)">Horatius Bonar  (1843)</a></div>
L.M.

GO, labour on; spend, and be spent,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy joy to do the Father’s will;
It is the way the Master went,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Should not the servant tread it still?

Go, labour on; ’tis not for naught;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy earthly loss is heavenly gain;
Men heed thee, love thee, praise thee, not;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Master praises - what are men?

Go, labour on; enough, while here,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;If He shall praise thee, if He deign
The willing heart to mark and cheer;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No toil for Him shall be in vain.

Go, labour on; your hands are weak,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Your knees are faint, your soul cast down;
Yet falter not; the prize you seek
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Is near - a kingdom and a crown!

Go, labour on, while it is day, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The world's dark night is hastening on;
Speed, speed thy work, cast sloth away;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;It is not thus that souls are won.

Men die in darkness at your side,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Without a hope to cheer the tomb;
Take up the torch and wave it wide,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The torch that lights time’s thickest gloom.

Toil on, faint not, keep watch and pray;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Be wise the erring soul to win;
Go forth into the world’s highway,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Compel the wanderer to come in.

Toil on, and in thy toil rejoice;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For toil comes rest, for exile home;
Soon shalt thou hear the Bridegroom’s voice,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The midnight peal, Behold, I come!


133
What God Hath Promised

<p>© 1919, Ren. 1947 Hope Publishing Company , www.hopepublishing.com. All rights reserved. Used by permission.  Words by Annie Johnson Flint, Music by William M. Runyan. Credit: Hope, Agape, Somerset Press, Providence Press, The Hymn Society in the United States and Canada, Stainer & Bell Ltd., Jubilate Hymns Ltd., or Hymns Ancient & Modern</p><div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Flint_Annie_Johnston&#39;,this)">Annie Johnson Flint</a></div>
P.M.

GOD hath not promised skies always blue,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Flower-strewn pathways all our lives through;
God hath not promised sun without rain,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Joy without sorrow, peace without pain.

c
But God hath promised strength for the day,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Rest for the labour, light for the way;
Grace for the trials, help from above,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Unfailing sympathy, undying love.

God hath not promised we shall not know
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Toil and temptation, trouble and woe;
He hath not told us we shall not  bear
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Many a burden, many a care. 

c
But God hath promised strength for the day,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Rest for the labour, light for the way;
Grace for the trials, help from above,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Unfailing sympathy, undying love.

God hath not promised smooth roads and wide,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Swift, easy travel, needing no guide;
Never a mountain, rocky and steep,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Never a river, turbid and deep. 

c
But God hath promised strength for the day,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Rest for the labour, light for the way;
Grace for the trials, help from above,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Unfailing sympathy, undying love.


134
God holds the key of all unknown

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Parker_Joseph&#39;,this)">Joseph Parker</a></div>
8.4.8.8.4.

GOD holds the key of all unknown, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And I am glad;
If other hands should hold the key,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Or if He trusted it to me,
I might be sad, I might be sad.

What if tomorrow’s cares were here
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Without its rest!
I’d rather He unlocked the day;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And, as the hours swing open, say,
My will is best, My will is best.

The very dimness of my sight
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Makes me secure;
For, groping in my misty way,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I feel His hand; I hear Him say,
My help is sure, My help is sure.

I cannot read His future plans;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But this I know;
I have the smiling of His face,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And all the refuge of His grace,
While here below, while here below.

Enough! this covers all my wants,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And so I rest!
For what I cannot, He can see,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And in His care I saved shall be,
Forever blest, forever blest.


135
God moves in a mysterious way
Previous BHB #53<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Cowper_William&#39;,this)">William Cowper  (1774)</a></div>
C.M.

GOD moves in a mysterious way,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His wonders to perform;
He plants His footsteps in the sea,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And rides upon the storm.

Deep in unfathomable mines,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of never-failing skill,
He treasures up His bright designs,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And works His sovereign will.

Ye fearful saints, fresh courage take;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The clouds ye so much dread,
Are big with mercy, and shall break,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In blessings on your head.

Judge not the Lord by feeble sense,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But trust Him for His grace;
Behind a frowning providence,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He hides a smiling face.

His purposes will ripen fast,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Unfolding every hour;
The bud may have a bitter taste,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But sweet will be the flower.

Blind unbelief is sure to err,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And scan His work in vain;
God is His own interpreter,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And He will make it plain.


136
Because He Lives

<p>Copyright © 1971 Hanna Street Music (BMI) (adm. at CapitolCMGPublishing.com) All rights reserved. Used by permission.</p><div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Gaither_William&#39;,this)">William and Gloria Gaither</a></div>
P.M.

GOD sent His Son, they called Him Jesus;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He came to love, heal and forgive;
He lived and died to buy my pardon,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;An empty grave is there to prove my Saviour lives!

c
Because He lives, I can face tomorrow,
Because He lives, All fear is gone;
Because I know, He holds the future
And life is worth the living, Just because He lives!

How sweet to hold a newborn baby
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And feel the pride and joy he brings;
But greater still the calm assurance:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;This child can face uncertain days because He lives.

c
Because He lives, I can face tomorrow,
Because He lives, All fear is gone;
Because I know, He holds the future
And life is worth the living, Just because He lives!

And then one day, I’ll cross the river
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I’ll fight life’s final war with pain
And then, as death gives way to victory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I’ll see the lights of glory and I’ll know He lives.

c
Because He lives, I can face tomorrow,
Because He lives, All fear is gone;
Because I know, He holds the future
And life is worth the living, Just because He lives!


137
God’s almighty arms are round me
Previous BHB #57
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Smith_Joseph_Denham&#39;,this)">Joseph Denham Smith</a></div>
8.4.8.4.8.8.8.4.

GOD’S almighty arms are round me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Peace, peace is mine!
Judgment scenes need not confound me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Peace, peace is mine!
Jesus came Himself and sought me;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Slave of sin, He found and bought me;
Then my blessèd freedom taught me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Peace, peace is mine!

While I hear life’s surging billows,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Peace, peace is mine!
Why suspend my harp on willows?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Peace, peace is mine!
I may sing with Christ beside me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Though a thousand ills betide me:
Safely He hath sworn to guide me, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Peace, peace is mine!

Every trial draws Him nearer,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Peace, peace is mine!
All His strokes but make Him dearer,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Peace, peace is mine!
Bless I then the hand that smiteth
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And in grace to heal delighteth;
’Tis against my sins He fighteth,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Peace, peace is mine!

Welcome! ev’ry morning sunlight,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Peace, peace is mine!
Nearer home each passing midnight,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Peace, peace is mine!
Death and hell cannot appal me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Safe in Christ whate’er befall me,
Calmly wait I, till He call me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Peace, peace is mine!


138
Golgotha’s scene recalling

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kirkpatrick_William&#39;,this)">William Kirkpatrick</a></div>
7.6.7.6.D.

GOLGOTHA’S scene recalling,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With reverence we view
His sorrow so appalling,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His love devoted, true. 
Impressions freshly gleaning
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of matchless worth and grace, 
Some glimpses of its meaning 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We, by the Spirit, trace.

His friends have fled in trembling;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His foes in rage abound, 
Tumultuously assembling,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The evil hosts surround; 
What grace He shows in bearing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Derision, shame, and scorn! 
What meekness His, in wearing 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The crown of piercing thorn!

His heavenly wisdom ever
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In fulness shineth clear; 
His holy zeal was never
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;More manifest than here. 
The grace within Him dwelling,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Revealed at such a time, 
With emphasis most telling, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His character sublime.

Unblemished life's fruition,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Unique and fitting crown
He, of His own volition,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That precious life lays down.
Death of a Cross the measure
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of His obedience still -
To give the Father pleasure 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And do the Father's will!


139
Grace! ’tis a charming sound
Previous BHB #55
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Doddridge_Philip&#39;,this)">Philip Doddridge</a> & <a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Toplady_Augustus_Montague&#39;,this)">Augustus Montague Toplady</a>  (1755)</div>
S.M.

GRACE! ’tis a charming sound,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Harmonious to the ear; 
Heaven with the echo shall resound,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And all the earth shall hear.

Grace first contrived the way 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To save rebellious man;
And all the steps that grace display,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Which drew the wondrous plan.

’Twas grace that wrote my name
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In life’s eternal book;
’Twas grace that gave me to the Lamb,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who all my sorrows took.

Grace taught my wandering feet
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To tread the heavenly road;
And new supplies each hour I meet,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;While pressing on to God.

Grace saved us from the foe
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Grace taught us how to pray
And God will ne’er His grace forego
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Till we have won the day.

Grace taught my soul to pray,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And made my eyes o’erflow; 
’Tis grace has kept me to this day, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And will not let me go. 

O let Thy grace inspire 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My soul with strength divine; 
May all my powers to Thee aspire, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And all my days be Thine.

Grace all the work shall crown
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Through everlasting days;
It lays in heaven the topmost stone,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And well deserves the praise.


140
Gracious God, we worship Thee
Previous BHB #388<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Francis_Samuel_Trevor&#39;,this)">Samuel Trevor Francis</a></div>
7.7.7.6.

GRACIOUS God, we worship Thee, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Reverently we bow the knee;
Jesus Christ our only plea;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Father, we adore Thee.

Vast Thy love, how deep, how wide,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In the gift of Him who died;
Righteous claims all satisfied;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Father, we adore Thee.

Low we bow before Thy face,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sons of God, O wondrous place!
Great the riches of Thy grace;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Father, we adore Thee.

By Thy Spirit grant that we
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Worshippers in truth may be;
Praise, as incense sweet to Thee;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Father, we adore Thee.

Yet again our song we raise,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Note of deep adoring praise;
Now, and soon through endless days;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Father, we adore Thee.


141
Great God of wonders! all Thy ways
Previous BHB #58<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Davies_Samuel&#39;,this)">Samuel Davies  (1769)</a></div>
8.8.8.8.8.8.

GREAT God of wonders! all Thy ways
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Display Thine attributes divine;
But the bright glories of Thy grace
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Above Thine other wonders shine:
Who is a pardoning God like Thee?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Or who has grace so rich and free?

Such deep transgressions to forgive!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Such guilty, daring worms to spare!
This is Thy grand prerogative,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And in this honour none shall share:
Who is a pardoning God like Thee?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Or who has grace so rich and free?

Pardon, from an offended God!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Pardon, for sins of deepest dye!
Pardon, bestowed through Jesus’ blood!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Pardon, that brings the rebel nigh!
Who is a pardoning God like Thee?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Or who has grace so rich and free?


142
Great God, with wonder and with praise

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Watts_Isaac&#39;,this)">Isaac Watts</a></div>
C.M.

GREAT God, with wonder and with praise
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On all Thy works I look;
But still Thy wisdom, power, and grace
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shine brighter in Thy Book. 

The stars, which in their courses roll,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Have much instruction given;
But Thy good Word informs my soul
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;How I may rise to heaven.

The fields provide me food, and show
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The goodness of the Lord;
But fruits of life and glory grow
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In Thy most holy Word.

Here are my choicest treasures hid,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Here my best comfort lies,
Here my desires are satisfied,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And hence my hopes arise.

Lord, make me understand Thy law:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Show what my faults have been;
And from the Gospel let me draw
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Pardon for all my sin.

Here would I learn how Christ has died,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To save my soul from hell;
Not all the books on earth beside
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Such heavenly wonders tell.

Then let me love my Bible more;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And take a fresh delight
By day to read these wonders o’er,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And meditate by night.


143
Great is Thy faithfulness

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Chisholm_Thomas&#39;,this)">Thomas Chisholm</a></div>
P.M.

“GREAT is Thy faithfulness”, O God my Father, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;There is no shadow of turning with Thee;
Thou changest not, Thy compassions, they fail not:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As Thou hast been, Thou forever wilt be.

c
“Great is Thy faithfulness!”
“Great is Thy faithfulness!”
Morning by morning new mercies I see;
All I have needed Thy hand hath provided -
“Great is Thy faithfulness”, Lord, unto me!

Summer and winter, and springtime and harvest,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sun, moon and stars in their courses above,
Join with all nature in manifold witness
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Thy great faithfulness, mercy and love.

c
“Great is Thy faithfulness!”
“Great is Thy faithfulness!”
Morning by morning new mercies I see;
All I have needed Thy hand hath provided -
“Great is Thy faithfulness”, Lord, unto me!

Pardon for sin and a peace that endureth,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thine own dear presence to cheer and to guide;
Strength for today and bright hope for tomorrow,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Blessings all mine, with ten thousand beside!

c
“Great is Thy faithfulness!”
“Great is Thy faithfulness!”
Morning by morning new mercies I see;
All I have needed Thy hand hath provided -
“Great is Thy faithfulness”, Lord, unto me!


144
Great Shepherd of the sheep!

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kirkpatrick_William&#39;,this)">William Kirkpatrick</a></div>
6.6.6.6.8.8.

GREAT Shepherd of the sheep!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Brought forth from death's domain,
He all His flock shall keep,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Their every cause maintain.
This hope imbues their hearts with cheer -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Soon the Chief Shepherd will appear!

On high, within the veil,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hath Jesus entered in; 
His priesthood cannot fail,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;’Tis He preserves from sin. 
Forerunner there - a proof that we 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With Him in glory soon shall be!

“The Lord Himself” - blest word! -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From heaven shall descend;
The dead in Christ bestirred
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With living ones ascend.
Soon shall the quick'ning shout resound,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Gath'ring His own from utmost bound!

Blest moment, when we rise
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From sinful scenes below!
Blest hope that purifies,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That sets our hearts aglow!
“Like Him”, through all-surpassing grace,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“With Him”, before the Father's face!


145
Great Shepherd of Thy chosen flock
Previous BHB #59<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Newton_John&#39;,this)">John Newton  (1779)</a></div>
L.M.

GREAT Shepherd of Thy chosen flock,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy people’s shield, their shadowing rock,
Once more we meet to hear Thy voice,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Once more before Thee to rejoice.

Now may Thy Spirit, by the Word,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Refresh each wearied heart, O Lord,
Wearied of earth’s vain strife and woe,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And longing more Thyself to know.

Thine is the heart our griefs to feel,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And Thine the love each wound  to heal;
Home Thou art gone for us to care,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Returning soon to take us there.


146
Guide us, O Thou great Jehovah
Previous BHB #60<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Williams_William&#39;,this)">William Williams of Pantycelyn</a> tr/ad by Peter Williams  (1745)</div>
8.7.8.7.4.7.

GUIDE us, O Thou great Jehovah,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Pilgrims through this barren land;
We are weak, but Thou art mighty;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hold us by Thy powerful hand:
Bread of heaven!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Feed us now and evermore.

Open wide the living fountain
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Whence the healing waters flow;
Be Thyself our cloudy pillar
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All the dreary desert through:
Strong Deliverer!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Be Thou still our strength and shield.

While we tread this vale of sorrow
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;May we in Thy love abide;
Keep us, O our gracious Saviour!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Cleaving closely to Thy side:
Still relying
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On our Father’s changeless love.

Saviour, come! we long to see Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Long to dwell with Thee above;
And to know, in full communion,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All the sweetness of Thy love:
Come, Lord Jesus!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Take Thy waiting people home.


147
Hail, sovereign love, that first began

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Brewer_Jehoiada&#39;,this)">Jehoiada Brewer</a></div>
L.M.

HAIL, sovereign love, that first began
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The scheme to rescue fallen man;
Hail, matchless, free, eternal grace,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That gave my soul a hiding place.

Against the God who rules the sky, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I fought with hands uplifted high;
Despised the mention of His grace,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Too proud to seek a hiding place.

Enwrapped in thick Egyptian night, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And fond of darkness more than light,
Madly I ran the sinful race,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Secure without a hiding place.

But lo! the eternal counsel rang, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“Almighty love, arrest that man!”
I felt the arrows of distress,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And found I had no hiding place!

Indignant justice stood in view,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Sinai's fiery mount I flew;
But justice cried with frowning face,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“This mountain is no hiding place!”

But lo! a heavenly voice I heard,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And mercy's angel soon appeared:
Who led me on a pleasing pace,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Jesus Christ, my hiding place!

Should storms of sevenfold vengeance roll,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And shake this globe from pole to pole:
No thunder-bolt shall daunt my face,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;While Jesus is my hiding place!

On Him almighty vengeance fell,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Which must have sunk a world to hell
He bore it for a sinful race,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And thus became their hiding place.

A few more rolling suns at most
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shall land me safe on heaven’s coast,
There I shall sing the song of grace
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Jesus Christ, my hiding place!


148
Hail, Thou once despised Jesus!
Previous BHB #61
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Bakewell_John&#39;,this)">John Bakewell</a> & <a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Madan_Martin&#39;,this)">Martin Madan</a>  (1757)</div>
8.7.8.7.D.

HAIL, Thou once despisèd Jesus!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hail, Thou still rejected King!
Thou didst suffer to release us,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou didst free salvation bring;
Through Thy death and resurrection,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Bearer of our sin and shame!
We enjoy divine protection,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Life and glory through Thy name.

Paschal Lamb, by God appointed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All our sins on Thee were laid;
By our Father’s love anointed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou hast full atonement made:
All who trust Thee are forgiven
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Through the virtue of Thy blood;
Rent in Thee the veil of heaven,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Grace shines forth to man from  God.

Saviour, hail! amid the glory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Where for us Thou dost abide;
We, by faith, do now adore Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Seated at Thy Father's side.
There, for us, Thou now art pleading
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;While Thou dost our place prepare;
For Thy saints still interceding,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Till in glory we appear.

Worship, honour, praise and blessing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou shalt then from all receive;
Loudest praises, without ceasing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All that earth or heaven can give:
In that day Thy saints will meet  Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Welcome Thee with grateful song;
Joyful hearts will ever greet Thee, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Source of joy to all the throng.

Soon we shall, with those in glory, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His transcendent grace relate; 
Gladly sing th' amazing story 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of His dying love so great: 
In that blessèd contemplation, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We for evermore shall dwell,
Crown'd with bliss and consolation, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Such as none below can tell.


149
Hail to the Lord’s Anointed
Previous BHB #389
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Montgomery_James&#39;,this)">James Montgomery  (1821)</a></div>
7.6.7.6.D

HAIL to the Lord's Anointed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Great David's greater Son!
Hail, in the time appointed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His reign on earth begun;
He comes to break oppression,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To set the captive free;
To take away transgression
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And rule in equity.

He shall come down like showers
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Upon the fruitful earth:
And love, joy, hope, like flowers,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Spring in His path to birth;
Before Him on the mountains
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shall peace, the herald, go;
And righteousness in fountains
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From hill to valley flow.

Kings shall fall down before Him,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And gold and incense bring;
All nations shall adore Him,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His praise all people sing:
For He shall have dominion
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O'er river, sea, and shore,
Far as the eagle's pinion
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Or dove's light wing can soar.

For Him shall prayer unceasing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And daily vows ascend;
His kingdom still increasing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A kingdom without end:
The mountain-dews shall nourish
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A seed in weakness sown,
Whose fruit shall spread and flourish,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And shake like Lebanon.

O'er every foe victorious
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He on His throne shall rest;
From age to age more glorious,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All-blessing and all-blest:
The tide of time shall never
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His covenant remove;
His Name shall stand for ever,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That Name to us is - Love.


150
Hallelujah! Hallelujah!

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Tait_S_M_J&#39;,this)">J. M. S. Tait</a></div>
8.7.8.7.8.8.8.6.

HALLELUJAH! Hallelujah!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Let the heavens declare His Name; 
Let the highest heights of Glory
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All His majesty proclaim.
Let His angels all adore Him;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Ye His hosts fall down before Him; 
Hallelujah! Hallelujah!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Praise His Name! Praise His Name!

Sun and moon repeat the story,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Tell His praise, ye stars of light; 
Let the heavens in all their splendour
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Evermore extol His might.
He hath 'stablished them for ever; 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His decree it faileth never. 
Hallelujah! Hallelujah!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Praise His Name! Praise His Name!

Let the earth and teeming ocean
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Join their voice the strain to swell;
Let the elements that serve Him
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His supremacy forthtell. 
Lofty peaks of rugged feature;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Fruitful fields, yea, every creature:
Hallelujah! Hallelujah! 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Praise His Name ! Praise His Name!

Kings and peoples, bend before Him; 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Rulers, hail the only Lord; 
Young and old, the youth, the maiden,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To His Name the praise accord.
Name sublime! Alone excelling 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All in earth or heaven dwelling! 
Hallelujah! Hallelujah! 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Praise His Name! Praise His Name!

Mighty chorus, mingling, blending,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hear its cadence rise and fall!
Ye His saints, can ye be silent,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Ye who owe Him most of all?
Ye, of all His creatures nighest,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Well may raise your notes the highest. 
Hallelujah! Hallelujah!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Praise His Name! Praise His Name!


151
Happy they who trust in Jesus!
Previous BHB #62<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kelly_Thomas&#39;,this)">Thomas Kelly</a></div>
8.7.8.7.4.7.

HAPPY they who trust in Jesus!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sweet their portion is, and sure;
When the foe on others seizes,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He will keep His own secure: 
Happy people!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Happy, though despised and poor.

Since His love and mercy found us
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We are precious in His sight;
Thousands now may fall around us,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thousands more be put to flight;  
But His presence
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Keeps us safe by day and night.

Lo! our Saviour never slumbers;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Ever watchful is His care:
Though we cannot boast of numbers,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In His strength secure we are:  
Sweet their portion
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who the Saviour’s kindness share.

As the bird, beneath her feathers,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Guards the objects of her care,
So the Lord His children gathers,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Spreads His wings and hides them there:  
Thus protected
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All their foes they boldly dare.


152
Hark! my soul, it is the Lord
Previous BHB #63<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Cowper_William&#39;,this)">William Cowper  (1768)</a></div>
7.7.7.7.

HARK! my soul, it is the Lord;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;’Tis thy Saviour! hear His word;
Jesus speaks, and speaks to thee:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“Say, poor sinner, lov’st thou Me?”

“I delivered thee when bound,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And when wounded, healed thy wound;
Sought thee wand’ring, set thee right,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Turned thy darkness into light.”

“Can a woman’s tender care
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Cease towards the child she bare?
Yes, she may forgetful be,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Yet will I remember thee.”

“Mine is an unchanging love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Higher than the heights above,
Deeper than the depths beneath,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Free and faithful, strong as death.”

“Thou shalt see My glory soon
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When the work of grace is done;
Partner of My throne shalt be:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Say, poor sinner, lov’st thou Me?”

Lord, it is my chief complaint
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That my love is cold and faint;
Yet I love Thee, and adore;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O for grace to love Thee more.


153
Hark! ten thousand voices crying
Previous BHB #65
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Darby_John_Nelson&#39;,this)">John Nelson Darby  (1837)</a></div>
8.7.8.7.

HARK! ten thousand voices crying,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“Lamb of God!” with one accord;
Thousand, thousand saints replying,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Wake at once the echoing chord.

“Praise the Lamb”, the chorus waking,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All in heaven together throng,
Loud and far, each tongue partaking,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Rolls along the endless song.

Grateful incense this, ascending
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Ever to the Father’s throne;
Every knee to Jesus bending,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All the mind in heaven is one.

All the Father’s counsels claiming
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Equal honours to the Son;
All the Son’s effulgence beaming
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Makes the Father’s glory known.

By the Spirit all pervading,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hosts unnumbered round the Lamb,
Crowned with light and joy unfading,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hail Him as the great I AM.

Joyful now the new creation
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Rests in undisturbed repose;
Blest in Jesus’ full salvation,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sorrow now, nor thraldom knows.

Hark! still louder swells the singing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As the notes are heard again;
Through creation's vault is ringing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Joy's response, “Amen! Amen!”


154
Hark! the herald angels sing

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Wesley_Charles&#39;,this)">Charles Wesley </a></div>
7.7.7.7.D. with Chorus

HARK! the herald angels sing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“Glory to the newborn King,
Peace on earth, and mercy mild,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;God and sinners reconciled!”
Joyful, all ye nations, rise,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Join the triumph of the skies;
With the angelic host proclaim,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“Christ is born in Bethlehem!”

c

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“Glory to the newborn King!”

Christ, by highest heaven adored,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Christ, the everlasting Lord,
Late in time behold Him come,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Offspring of a virgin’s womb;
Veiled in flesh the Godhead see;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hail, the Incarnate Deity,
Pleased as Man with man to dwell,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus our Immanuel.

c

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“Glory to the newborn King!”

Hail, the heavenly Prince of Peace!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hail, the Sun of Righteousness!
