&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Risen with healing in His wings;
Mild, He lays His glory by,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Born that man no more may die,
Born to raise the sons of earth,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Born to give them second birth.

c
Hark! the herald angels sing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“Glory to the newborn King!”


155
Hark! ’tis the watchman’s cry
Previous BHB #64<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Anonymous_Hymn_Writers&#39;,this)">Anonymous</a> & The Revival  (1859)</div>
6.4.6.4.6.7.6.4.

HARK! ’tis the watchman’s cry,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Wake, brethren, wake!
Jesus, our Lord, is nigh;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Wake, brethren, wake!
Sleep is for sons of night;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Ye are children of the light,
Yours is the glory bright;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Wake, brethren, wake!

Call to each waking band,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Watch, brethren, watch!
Clear is our Lord’s command;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Watch, brethren, watch!
Be ye as men that wait
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Always at their Master’s gate,
E’en though the hour seem late;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Watch, brethren, watch!

Heed we the steward’s call,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Work, brethren, work!
There’s room enough for all;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Work, brethren, work!
This vineyard of the Lord
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Constant labour will afford;
Yours is a sure reward;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Work, brethren, work!

Hear we the Shepherd’s voice,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Pray, brethren, pray!
Would ye His heart rejoice?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Pray, brethren, pray!
Sin calls for constant fear,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Weakness needs the strong One near, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Long as ye struggle here; 
Pray, brethren, pray!

Now sound the final chord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Praise, brethren, praise!
Thrice holy is our Lord;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Praise, brethren, praise!
What more befits the tongues
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Soon to lead the eternal songs,
While heaven the note prolongs?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Praise, brethren, praise!


156
Hast Thou heard Him, seen Him, known Him?

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Rowan_Ora&#39;,this)">Ora Rowan</a></div>
8.7.8.7.D.

HAST thou heard Him, seen Him, known Him?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Is not thine a captured heart?
Chief among ten thousand own Him;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Joyful choose the better part.

c
Captivated by His beauty,
Worthy tribute haste to bring;
Let His peerless worth constrain thee,
Crown Him now unrivalled King.

Idols oft they won thee, charmed thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lovely things of time and sense;
Gilded, thus does sin disarm thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Honeyed lest thou turn thee hence.

c
Captivated by His beauty,
Worthy tribute haste to bring;
Let His peerless worth constrain thee,
Crown Him now unrivalled King.

What has stripped the seeming beauty,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From the idols of the earth?
Not a sense of right or duty,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But the sight of peerless worth.

c
Captivated by His beauty,
Worthy tribute haste to bring;
Let His peerless worth constrain thee,
Crown Him now unrivalled King.

Not the crushing of those idols,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With its bitter void and smart,
But the beaming of His beauty,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The unveiling of His heart.

c
Captivated by His beauty,
Worthy tribute haste to bring;
Let His peerless worth constrain thee,
Crown Him now unrivalled King.

Who extinguishes their taper,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Till they hail the rising sun?
Who discards the garb of winter,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Till the summer hath begun?

c
Captivated by His beauty,
Worthy tribute haste to bring;
Let His peerless worth constrain thee,
Crown Him now unrivalled King.

’Tis the look that melted Peter,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;’Tis the face that Stephen saw,
’Tis the heart that wept with Mary,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Can alone from idols draw.

c
Captivated by His beauty,
Worthy tribute haste to bring;
Let His peerless worth constrain thee,
Crown Him now unrivalled King.

Draw and win and fill completely,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Till the cup o’erflow the brim;
What have we to do with idols
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who have companied with Him? 

c
Captivated by His beauty,
Worthy tribute haste to bring;
Let His peerless worth constrain thee,
Crown Him now unrivalled King.


157
Have I an object, Lord, below?

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Fraser_George_West&#39;,this)">George West Fraser</a></div>
8.8.8.8.8.8.

HAVE I an object, Lord, below,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Which would divide my heart from Thee;
Which would divert its even flow
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In answer to Thy constancy?
Oh, may this wandering heart return,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Filled with Thy love afresh to burn!

Have I a hope, however dear,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Which would defer Thy coming, Lord,
Which would detain my spirit here,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Where nought can lasting joy  afford?
From it, my Saviour, set me free,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To look, and long, and wait for Thee.

Be Thou the object, bright and fair,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To fill and satisfy my heart;
My hope to meet Thee in the air,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And nevermore from Thee to part;
That I may undistracted be
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To follow, serve, and wait for Thee.


158
Have Thine own way, Lord

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Pollard_Adelaide&#39;,this)">Adelaide Pollard</a></div>
9.9.9.9.

HAVE Thine own way, Lord! Have Thine own way!
Thou art the Potter; I am the clay.
Mould me and make me After Thy will,
While I am waiting Yielded and still.

Have Thine own way, Lord! Have Thine own way!
Wounded and weary, Help me, I pray!
Power - all power - Surely is Thine!
Touch me and heal me, Saviour divine!

Have Thine own way, Lord! Have Thine own way!
Hold o'er my being Absolute sway!
Fill with Thy Spirit 'til all shall see
Christ only, always, Living in me!


159
He bears the cruel scourge from men

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kirkpatrick_William&#39;,this)">William Kirkpatrick</a></div>
8.6.8.6.8.6.

HE bears the cruel scourge from men
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In meek and lowly grace,
The platted crown of thorns which then
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Upon His head they place: 
Submissive when in hate again
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;They spit upon His face.

God's chosen One men disallow;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Lord they crucify,
While He completes His service now
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Upon a Cross raised high, 
Revealing how He thus would bow 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Obediently to die.

And sitting down they watch Him there,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And mock His holy Name;
And gaze with rude and vulgar stare
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Upon that Cross of shame;
All this they dare, nor know, nor care,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To suffer thus He came.

O God, our praise we offer Thee
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For Thy Belovèd Son,
Whose precious death upon the tree
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Declares Thy will is done,
In love so free, and perfectly,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With endless glory won.


160
He comes! He comes!

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Ewan_Y_Isaac&#39;,this)">Isaac Y. Ewan</a></div>
C.M.

HE comes! He comes! with hearts aglow,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We fill our lips with song;
He comes! He comes! and this we know -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He will not now be long.

He comes! why should the saints be sad?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Why should they weep and wail?
He comes! He comes! O tidings  glad! 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The promise cannot fail.

He comes! no longer shall we roam
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Unwanted and unknown;
He comes! He comes to take us home 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To share His glorious throne.

He comes! let all the saints prepare,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With hearts tuned to the chord -
He comes! He comes! with joyful care 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Make ready for the Lord.

He comes ! let every heart and voice
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Take up the glad refrain -
He comes! He comes! rejoice! rejoice! 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Again and yet again.


161
He dies! He dies! the lowly Man of sorrows
Previous BHB #390

HE dies! He dies! the lowly Man of sorrows, 
On whom were laid our many griefs and woes;
Our sins He bore beneath God’s awful billows,
And He hath triumphed over all our foes.

Exalted at His Father’s own right hand;
He pleads for us, and, by His intercession,
Enables all His saints by grace to stand.

In keeping with His true and faithful word;
To call us to our heavenly consummation -
Caught up, to be for ever with the Lord.

c

And reign in righteousness and equity.

162
He giveth more grace when the burdens grow greater
Previous BHB #391
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Flint_Annie_Johnston&#39;,this)">Annie Johnson Flint</a></div><p>&copy; 1941, Renewed 1969 Lillenas Publishing Company (admin. by Music Services) All Rights Reserved. SESAC</p>
12.11.12.11.

HE giveth more grace when the burdens grow greater,
He sendeth more strength when the labours increase,
To added afflictions He addeth His mercy,
To multiplied trials, His multiplied peace.

When we have exhausted our store of endurance,
When our strength has failed ere the day is half-done,
When we reach the end of our hoarded resources
Our Father’s full giving is only begun.

Fear not that thy need shall exceed His provision, 
Our God ever yearns His resources to share;
Lean hard on the arm everlasting, availing;
The Father both thee and thy load will upbear.

His love has no limit, His grace has no measure,
His power no boundary known unto men;
For out of His infinite riches in Jesus
He giveth and giveth and giveth again.


163
He is coming, coming for us

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Monteith_M_A&#39;,this)">A. M. Monteith</a></div>
8.7.8.7.D. with Chorus

HE is coming, coming for us;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Soon we'll see His light afar,
On the dark horizon rising,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As the Bright and Morning Star, 
Cheering many a waiting watcher,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As the star whose kindly ray
Heralds the approaching morning
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Just before the break of day.
Oh, what joy, as night hangs round us,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;’Tis to think of morning's ray!
Sweet to know He's coming for us,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Just before the break of day.

He is coming, coming for us;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Soon we'll hear His voice on high;
Dead and living, rising, changing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In the twinkling of an eye,
Shall be caught up all together,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For the meeting in the air;
With a shout, the Lord, descending,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shall Himself await us there.
Oh, what joy, that great foregathering,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Trysted meeting in the air!
Sweet to know He's coming for us, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Calling us to join Him there.

He is coming - Oh, how solemn,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When the Judge’s voice is heard,
And in His own light He shows us,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Every thought, and act, and word!
Deeds of merit, as we thought them, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He will show us were but sin;
Little acts, we had forgotten,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He will tell us were for Him.
Oh, what joy, when He imputeth,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Righteousness instead of sin,
Sweet to take the linen garments,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All a gift and all from Him.

He is coming as the Bridegroom, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Coming to unfold at last
The great secret of His purpose,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Mystery of ages past.
And the bride, to her is granted,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In His beauty then to shine,
As in rapture she exclaimeth,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“I am His, and He is mine!”
Oh, what joy that marriage union
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Mystery of love divine!
Sweet to sing in all its fullness,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“I am His, and He is mine”.


164
He is coming! how this gladdens

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Murray_George&#39;,this)">George Murray</a></div>
8.7.8.7.

HE is coming! how this gladdens
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Days of darkness in this vale, 
With so much that tries and saddens, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Causing faith and hope to fail.

He is coming! word unfailing!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All the pains and tears will cease; 
No more weeping, no more wailing, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Nought but joy and calm and peace!

He is coming! we are hasting 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To our place prepared above, 
All His goodness here foretasting, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All His fulness, all His love.

He is coming! long benighted,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Soon we'll hear the wakening cry, 
And, with loved ones reunited, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Reach, at last, our home on high.

He is coming! sweet the keeping
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of the feast until He come,
When, with those through Jesus sleeping, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We shall see Himself, at home.

He is coming! O the glory
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To be with Him where He is,
And to tell the wondrous story 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Through the countless years  of bliss!


165
He is not a disappointment!

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Anonymous_Hymn_Writers&#39;,this)">Author unknown</a></div>
8.7.8.7.D.

HE is not a disappointment!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus is far more to me
Than in all my glowing daydreams
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I had fancied He could be;
And the more I get to know Him,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;So the more I find Him true,
And the more I long that others
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Should be led to know Him too.

He is not a disappointment!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He has saved my soul from sin;
All the guilt and all the anguish,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Which oppressed my heart within,
He has banished by His presence,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And His blessèd kiss of peace
Has assured my heart for ever
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That His love will never cease. 

He is not a disappointment!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He is coming by and by;
In my heart I have the witness
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That His coming draweth nigh.
All the scoffers may despise me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And no change around may see,
But He tells me He is coming,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And that’s quite enough for me.

He is not a disappointment!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He is all in all to me -
Blessèd Saviour, Sanctifier,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The unchanging Christ is He!
He has won my heart’s affections,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And He meets my every need;
He is not a disappointment,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For He satisfies indeed.

He leadeth me! O blessed thought!

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Still 'tis God's hand that leadeth me!

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sometimes where Eden's bowers bloom,

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And never murmur nor repine;

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When, by Thy grace, the victory's won,
E'en death's cold wave I will not flee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;If God through Jordan leadeth me!

He leadeth me! He leadeth me!
By His own hand He leadeth me!
His faithful follower would I be,

167
He lives - the great Redeemer lives
Previous BHB #71
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Steele_Anne&#39;,this)">Anne Steele  (1760)</a></div>
L.M.

HE lives - the great Redeemer lives;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What joy the blest assurance gives!
And now before His Father, God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Pleads the full merits of His blood.

Repeated crimes awake our fears,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And justice arm'd with frowns  appears;
But in the Saviour's lovely face
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sweet mercy smiles, and all is  peace.

Hence then, ye black despairing thoughts;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Above our fears, above our faults,
His powerful intercessions rise,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And guilt recedes, and terror dies.

In every dark, distressful hour,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When sin and Satan join their  power;
Let this blest truth repel each dart,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That Jesus bears us on His heart. 

Great Advocate, Almighty Friend!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On Him our humble hopes depend;
Our cause can never, never fail,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For Jesus pleads, and must prevail.


168
He sitteth o’er the waterfloods
Previous BHB #73<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Whately_Richard&#39;,this)">Richard Whateley</a></div>
C.M.

HE sitteth o’er the waterfloods,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And He is strong to save;
He sitteth o’er the waterfloods,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And guides each drifting wave.

Though loud around the vessel’s prow
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The waves may toss and break;
Yet at His word they sink to rest,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As on a tranquil lake.

He sitteth o’er the waterfloods,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As in the days of old,
When o’er the Saviour’s sinless head
The waves and billows rolled.

Yea, all the billows passed o’er Him; 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our sins - they bore Him down;
For us He met th’ o’erwhelming storm,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He met th’ Almighty’s frown.

He sitteth o’er the waterfloods;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Then doubt and fear no more,
For He who passed through all the storms
Has reached the heavenly shore.

And every tempest-driven barque,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With Jesus for its Guide,
Will soon be moored in harbour calm,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In glory to abide. 


169
He took a loaf, gave thanks, and brake the bread

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Ewan_Y_Isaac&#39;,this)">Isaac Y. Ewan</a></div>
10.10.10.10.

HE took a loaf, gave thanks, and brake the bread.
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“This is My body which is given for you; 
This do in memory of Me”, He said;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And we, in memory of Him, “this do”.

He took a loaf. The simple action showed
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His incarnation and His wondrous birth. 
He took that body, and therein abode, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Lord of Glory as a Man on earth.

And He gave thanks, such was His heart's delight - 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To please His Father in the path He trod,
To be, as Man, a pleasure in His sight,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To glorify His Father and His God.

And then He brake - to make the broken whole,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To turn to gain irreparable loss.
That simple act, O ponder it, my  soul!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Announced the gloom-veiled breakings of the Cross.

O God, thus, as He did, the loaf we take,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And, as He did, return our thanks to Thee, 
And, as He did, the symbol bread we break, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Remembering our Lord adoringly.


170
Here is love, vast as the ocean

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Rees_William&#39;,this)">William Rees</a>, William Williams (last stanza), tr. William Edwards</div>
8.7.8.7.D.

HERE is love, vast as the ocean,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Loving-kindness as the flood,
When the Prince of Life, our Ransom,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shed for us His precious blood.
Who His love will not remember?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who can cease to sing His praise?
He can never be forgotten
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Throughout heav'n's eternal days.

On the Mount of Crucifixion
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Fountains opened deep and wide;
Through the floodgates of God's mercy
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Flowed a vast and gracious tide.
Grace and love, like mighty rivers,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Poured incessant from above,
And heav'n's peace and perfect  justice
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Kissed a guilty world in love.

Let me all Thy love accepting,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Love Thee ever all my days;
Let me seek Thy Kingdom only,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And my life be to Thy praise;
Thou alone shalt be my glory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Nothing in the world I see,
Thou hast cleansed and sanctified me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou Thyself hast set me free.

In Thy truth Thou dost direct me
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;By Thy Spirit through Thy Word;
And Thy grace my need is meeting
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As I trust in Thee, my Lord.
Of Thy fulness Thou art pouring
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy great love and power on me,
Without measure, full and boundless,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Drawing out my heart to Thee.


171
Here, O my Lord, I see Thee face to face
Previous BHB #339
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Bonar_Horatius&#39;,this)">Horatius Bonar</a></div>
10.10.10.10.

HERE, O my Lord, I see Thee face to face;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Here faith can touch and handle things unseen;
Here would I grasp with firmer hand Thy grace,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And all my weariness upon Thee lean.

Here would I feed upon the bread of God;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Here drink with Thee the royal wine of heaven;
Here would I lay aside each earthly load;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Here taste afresh the calm of sin forgiven.

Mine, mine the sin, but Thine the righteousness; 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Mine, mine the guilt, but Thine the cleansing blood; 
Here is my robe, my refuge, and my peace -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy blood, Thy righteousness, O Lord my God!

I have no help but Thine; nor do I need
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Another arm save Thine to lean upon;
It is enough, my Lord, enough  indeed;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My strength is in Thy might, Thy might alone.

Too soon we rise; the symbols disappear;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The feast, though not the love, is past and gone;
The bread and wine remove, but Thou art here -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Nearer than ever - still my Shield and Sun.

But see, the Pillar-cloud is rising now,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And moving onward through the  desert night;
It beckons, and I follow, for I know 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;It leads me to the heritage of light. 

Feast after feast thus comes and passes by;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Yet, passing, points to the glad feast above,
And gives sweet foretastes of the festal joy,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Lamb’s great bridal feast of bliss and love.


172
Here o’er the earth as a stranger I roam
Previous BHB #72<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Ainsworth_C_W&#39;,this)">C. W. Ainsworth</a></div>
P.M.

HERE o’er the earth as a stranger I roam,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Here is no rest - is no rest;
Here as a pilgrim I wander alone,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Yet I am blest - I am blest;
For I look forward to that glorious day
When sin and sorrow shall vanish away;
My heart doth leap while I hear Jesus say,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“There, there is rest - there is rest”.

Here are afflictions and trials severe,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Here is no rest - is no rest;
Here I must part with the friends I hold dear,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Yet I am blest - I am blest;
Sweet is the promise I read in Thy Word;
Blessèd are they who have died in the Lord;
They have been called to receive their reward;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“There, there is rest - there is rest”.

This world of care is a wilderness state,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Here is no rest - is no rest;
But I must bear from the world  all its hate,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Yet I am blest - I am blest.
Soon shall I be from the wicked  released;
Soon shall the weary for ever be blest;
Soon shall I lean upon Jesus’ breast;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“There, there is rest - there is rest”.


173
Here we remember Him

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kirkpatrick_William&#39;,this)">William Kirkpatrick</a></div>
6.10.10.6.

HERE we remember Him,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His walk, and works, and ways afresh review,
Reflect upon His words of grace anew; 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Here we remember Him.

Himself the vision fills
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As all His perfect path again we  trace, 
Appreciate the glory and the grace; 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Himself the vision fills.

Fairer than all is He.
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The sons of men in moral gloom  obscure, 
He, like the sun, in glory clear and pure! 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Fairer than all is He.

Precious and peerless One!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Beyond compare in thought  and word and deed, 
Unique recipient of heaven's meed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Precious and peerless One!

Our praise ascends to Thee
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For Him, the “altogether lovely” One, 
Thy Perfect Servant, Thy Belovèd Son; 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our praise ascends to Thee.


174
High on a cruel cross of shame

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Ewan_Y_Isaac&#39;,this)">Isaac Y. Ewan</a></div>
C.M.

HIGH on a cruel cross of shame,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;'Mid mockery and scorn, 
Nailed with His superscribèd Name, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Wearing a crown of thorn!

Friend of the friendless and the sad,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who wept o'er others' woes, 
Left by the failing friends He had,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Friendless among His foes!

Giver to all of life, and breath,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And riches that endure,
Ranked with the robbers in His death,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Poorest among the poor!

Mocked by the mob that round Him stands
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To view their work complete -
Nails through His holy, healing  hands, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And through His holy feet!

Patient in suffering and shame,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To do His Father's will
Perfect in life, in death the same, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Pleasing His Father still!

Pouring appreciative praise
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From hearts His worth has won, 
To Thee, His God, our hymn we raise, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Remembering Thy Son.


175
Himself He could not save!

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Midlane_Albert&#39;,this)">Albert Midlane</a></div>
6.6.6.6.8.8.

HIMSELF He could not save!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He on the Cross must die,
Or mercy could not come
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To ruined sinners nigh;
Yes, Christ, the Son of God, must bleed
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That sinners might from sin be freed.

Himself He could not save!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For justice must be done;
And sin’s full weight must fall
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Upon the sinless One; 
For nothing less could God accept
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In payment of our fearful debt.

Himself He could not save!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For He as Surety stood
For all who now rely
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Upon His precious blood;
He bore the penalty for thee
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When dying on the accursèd tree.

Himself He could not save!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Yet, now a Saviour, He
Bids sinners to Him come,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And live eternally,
Believing in Him, now, we prove,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His saving power, His  changeless love. 


176
His exodus - the wondrous theme

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kirkpatrick_William&#39;,this)">William Kirkpatrick</a></div>
L.M.

“HIS exodus” - the wondrous theme
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Upon the holy mountain high -
Revealeth all His grace supreme 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Whose precious Name we magnify.

Deep sorrows at the Cross enclose
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Him round, and men revile and  stare, 
While He would thus, beset by foes, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“His exodus” accomplish there!

With all God's will done perfectly,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“’Tis finished” is the Victor's shout;
With calmness then His spirit He 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Dismisses at “His going out.”

Our thoughts around Himself are twined;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On such a life and death we dwell; 
Our privilege in this, we find -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Such thoughts to Thee, O God, to tell.


177
His fame we sing, His victory

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kirkpatrick_William&#39;,this)">William Kirkpatrick</a></div>
8.6.8.8.6.

HIS fame we sing, His victory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Rejoicing, we proclaim,
Who, on the third, th' appointed day, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Swept all the power of death away 
And forth in triumph came.

He ne'er by death could holden be;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His God hath loosed its bands, 
And raised Him up that all may see 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In resurrection victory
The Lord of Glory stands.

Death He annulled effectively,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And life hath brought to light, 
And incorruptibility,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With glory for eternity
As spoil from that dread fight.

Dead He became; alive is He 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Whom all our souls adore,
The First, the Last, eternally 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Living One, triumphantly, 
Alive for evermore.

Together truth and mercy meet, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And righteousness and peace,
With holy kiss, each other greet 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To bear abiding fruit most sweet 
In union ne'er to cease.


178
Hold Thou my hand!

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Alystyne_Frances_Jane_Van&#39;,this)">Frances (Fanny) Jane Crosby</a></div>
11.10.11.10.

HOLD Thou my hand! so weak I am, and helpless,
I dare not take one step without Thy aid;
Hold Thou my hand! for then, O loving Saviour,
No dread of ill shall make my soul afraid.

Hold Thou my hand! and closer, closer draw me
To Thy dear self - my hope, my joy, my all:
Hold Thou my hand, lest haply I should wander;
And, missing Thee, my trembling feet should fall.

Hold Thou my hand! the way is dark before me
Without the sunlight of Thy face divine;
But when by faith I catch its radiant glory,
What heights of joy, what rapturous songs are mine!

Hold Thou my hand! that when I reach the margin
Of that lone river Thou didst cross for me,
A heav'nly light may flash along its waters,
And every wave like crystal bright shall be. 


179
Holy Father, we address Thee

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Peters_Mary&#39;,this)">Mary Bowly Peters</a></div>
8.7.8.7.7.7.

HOLY Father, we address Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Loved in Thy belovèd Son:
Holy Son of God, we bless Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Boundless grace hath made us one;
May the Spirit aid our songs -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;This glad work to Him belongs.

Wondrous was Thy love, O Father!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Wondrous Thine, O Son of God!
Vast the love that bruised and wounded!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Vast the love that bore the rod!
May the Spirit still reveal
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;How those stripes alone could heal.

Gracious Father, Thy good pleasure
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Is to love us as Thy Son,
Meting out the self-same measure,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Since Thou seest us as one:
By Thee, Saviour, loved are we,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As the Father loveth Thee!

Hallelujah! we are hasting
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To our Father’s house above;
By the way our souls are tasting
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Rich and everlasting love:
In Jehovah is our boast -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Father, Son and Holy Ghost!


180
Holy, holy, holy! Lord God Almighty!
Previous BHB #392
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Heber_Reginald&#39;,this)">Reginald Heber  (1826)</a></div>
11.12.12.10.

HOLY, holy, holy! Lord God Almighty!
Early in the morning our song shall rise to Thee;
Holy, holy, holy! merciful and mighty,
God in Three Persons, blessèd Trinity!

Holy, holy, holy! All the saints adore Thee,
Casting down their golden crowns around the glassy sea;
Cherubim and seraphim falling down before Thee,
Who wert, and art, and evermore shalt be.

Holy, holy, holy! though the darkness hide Thee,
Though the eye of sinful man Thy glory may not see;
Only Thou art holy, there is none beside Thee,
Perfect in power, in love, and purity.

Holy, holy, holy! Lord God Almighty!
All Thy works shall praise Thy name, in earth, and sky, and sea;
Holy, holy, holy! merciful and  mighty,
God in Three Persons, blessèd Trinity.


181
Holy Saviour! we adore Thee
Previous BHB #74
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Tregelles_Samuel_Prideaux&#39;,this)">Samuel Prideaux Tregelles  (1838)</a></div>
8.7.8.7.4.7.

HOLY Saviour! we adore Thee
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Seated on the throne of God;
While the heavenly hosts before Thee
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Gladly sing Thy praise aloud.
Thou art worthy! 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We are ransomed by Thy blood.

Saviour! though the world despised Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Though Thou here wast crucified,
Yet the Father’s glory raised Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lord of all creation wide;
Thou art worthy! 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We shall live, for Thou hast died.

And though here on earth rejected,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;'Tis but fellowship with Thee;
Should we not with joy expect it
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Here like Thee, our Lord, to be?
Thou art worthy,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou from earth hast set us free. 

Haste the day of Thy returning
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With Thy ransomed church to  reign;
Then shall end our days of mourning,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We shall sing with rapture then:
Thou art worthy! 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Come, Lord Jesus, come, Amen.


182
Hope of our hearts, O Lord, appear!
Previous BHB #75
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Denny_Sir_Edward&#39;,this)">Sir Edward Denny</a></div>
C.M.

HOPE of our hearts, O Lord, appear!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou glorious Star of day,
Shine forth, and chase the dreary night,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With all our tears, away.

No resting-place we seek on earth,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No loveliness we see;
Our eye is on the royal crown,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Prepared for us and Thee.

But, dearest Lord! however bright
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That crown of joy above,
What is it to the brighter hope
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of dwelling in Thy love?

What to the joy, the deeper joy,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Unmingled, pure, and free,
Of union with our living Head,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of fellowship with Thee?

This joy e’en now on earth is ours;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But only, Lord, above,
Our hearts, without a pang, shall know
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The fulness of Thy love.

There, near Thy heart, upon the throne,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy ransomed Church shall see
What grace was in the bleeding Lamb,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who died to make her free.


183
How bright that blessed hope!
Previous BHB #76<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Anonymous_Hymn_Writers&#39;,this)">Anonymous</a></div>
6.4.6.4.6.6.6.4.

HOW bright that blessèd hope!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus will come!
Let us our heads lift up,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus will come!
Morning so bright and clear,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Mansions of God appear,
Sin shall not enter there,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus will come.

Him every eye shall see
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When He appears;
Bright will the glory be
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When He appears;
Soon shall the trumpet speak,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Each sleeping saint awake,
And the glad morning break
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When He appears.

Raised unto glory then
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;At His return;
Joyous our song shall be
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;At His return;
Gathered around to Him
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All learn the heav’nly hymn;
Jesus our joyful theme
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;At His return.

Full of this blessèd hope
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Till He shall come;
Let us the cross take up
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Till He shall come;
Happy reproach to bear,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shame, for His sake, to share,
Since we the crown shall wear
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When He shall come.


184
How deep the Father’s love for us

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Townend_Stuart&#39;,this)">Stuart Townend</a></div><p>&copy; 1995 Thankyou Music (Adm. by CapitolCMGPublishing.com excl. UK & Europe, Adm at IntegrityRights.com)</p>
8.7.8.7.D.

HOW deep the Father’s love for us,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;How vast beyond all measure,
That He should give His only Son
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To make a wretch His treasure.
How great the pain of searing loss -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Father turns His face away,
As wounds which mar the Chosen One
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Bring many sons to glory.

Behold the man upon a Cross,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My sin upon His shoulders;
Ashamed, I hear my mocking voice
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Call out among the scoffers.
It was my sin that held Him there
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Until it was accomplished;
His dying breath has brought me life -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I know that it is finished.

I will not boast in anything,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No gifts, no power, no wisdom;
But I will boast in Jesus Christ,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His death and resurrection.
Why should I gain from His reward?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I cannot give an answer;
But this I know with all my heart -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His wounds have paid my ransom.


185
How firm a foundation, ye saints of the Lord
Previous BHB #77
<div class="author">From <i>"A Selection of Hymns from the Best Authors"</i> by John Rippon (1787) where it is attributed to <a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Keene_Richard&#39;,this)">"K-"</a> (author unknown)</div>
11.11.11.11.

HOW firm a foundation, ye saints of the Lord,
Is laid for your faith in His excellent Word!
What more can He say, than to you He hath said,
To you who for refuge to Jesus have fled?

“Fear not, I am with thee; Oh, be not dismay'd!
For I am thy God, I will still give thee aid; 
I'll strengthen thee, help thee, and cause thee to stand, 
Upheld by My gracious omnipotent hand.”

“When through the deep waters I call thee to go,
The rivers of sorrow shall not overflow;
For I will be with thee, thy trials to bless,
And sanctify to thee, thy deepest distress.”

“When through fiery trials thy pathway shall lie,
My grace, all-sufficient, shall be thy supply:
The flame shall not hurt thee: I only design
Thy dross to consume, and thy gold to refine.”

“The soul that on Jesus hath leaned for repose,
I will not, I cannot desert to its foes; 
That soul, though all hell should endeavour to shake,
I'll never - no never - no never forsake!”


186
How good is the God we adore
Previous BHB #78<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Hart_Joseph&#39;,this)">Joseph Hart  (1759)</a></div>
8.8.8.8.

HOW good is the God we adore,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our faithful, unchangeable Friend,
Whose love is as great as His power,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And knows neither measure nor end.

’Tis Jesus, the First and the Last,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Whose Spirit shall guide us  safe home;
We’ll praise Him for all that is past,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And trust Him for all that’s to  come.


187
How great, our Father, was Thy love

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Maull_H&#39;,this)">H. Maull</a></div>
C.M.

HOW great, our Father, was Thy love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;How wonderful Thy grace,
In sending forth Thy Well-beloved,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To save our ruined race!

As being in the form of God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Eternally the same,
With Thee, the Father, He, the Son,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Equality could claim.

But, Thy blest counsels to fulfil,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He left His glorious throne;
And made Himself of no repute,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A servant’s form to own.

Thus found in fashion as a man,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All blameless, spotless, pure,
He was obedient unto death,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sin’s judgment to endure.

To Him, above all heavens, dost Thou
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His rightful place accord,
That every knee to Him should  bow,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Each tongue confess Him Lord.

Soon, thro’ those opened heavens again,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Saviour will appear;
With Him in highest glory then,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our praises Thou wilt hear.

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That I might Thy channel be.

c

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Every day and every hour.

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To the thirsty hearts around;

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All Thy loving message sound.

c

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But with all Thy wondrous power
Flowing through us, Thou canst use us
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Every day and every hour.

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Graciously with each command.

Flowing through us, Thou canst use us
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Every day and every hour.

Witnessing Thy power to save me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Setting free from self and sin;

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In Thy fulness, Lord, come in.

c

Flowing through us, Thou canst use us
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Every day and every hour.

Jesus, fill now with Thy Spirit
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hearts that full surrender know;
That the streams of living water
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From our inner man may flow. 

c
Channels only, blessèd Master,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But with all Thy wondrous power
Flowing through us, Thou canst use us
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Every day and every hour.


189
How sweet is the story of God’s boundless love

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Edwards_D_R&#39;,this)">R. D. Edwards</a></div>
11.11.11.11. with Chorus

HOW sweet is the story of God's boundless love,
That brought His own Son from the glory above,
Who died in our stead upon Calvary's tree,
Obtaining redemption that we might be free!

c
Sound His praise! Sound His praise!
All the work has been done;
Praise His name! Praise His name!
God’s own blessèd Son.
We give Him the glory, our Saviour and Friend;
Our song is of Jesus and never will end.

How wondrous the story! The claims of God’s throne
Were met by that blood which for guilt did atone;
The judgment of sin has been borne by the Son,
Who glorified God in the work He has done -

c
Sound His praise! Sound His praise!
All the work has been done;
Praise His name! Praise His name!
God’s own blessèd Son.
We give Him the glory, our Saviour and Friend;
Our song is of Jesus and never will end.

How brilliant the glory where Christ is enthroned!
How rightly His name above all names is owned!
Yes, Jesus, the Saviour, the glory - crowned Lord,
Is worthy by all to be ever adored -

c
Sound His praise! Sound His praise!
All the work has been done;
Praise His name! Praise His name!
God’s own blessèd Son.
We give Him the glory, our Saviour and Friend;
Our song is of Jesus and never will end.

How blessèd the hope of all those who believe,
That Jesus is coming, “His own” to receive!
What rapture, what glory, for ever will be,
When “caught up” to meet Him, their Saviour they see!

c
Sound His praise! Sound His praise!
All the work has been done;
Praise His name! Praise His name!
God’s own blessèd Son.
We give Him the glory, our Saviour and Friend;
Our song is of Jesus and never will end.


190
How sweet the Name of Jesus sounds
Previous BHB #79
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Newton_John&#39;,this)">John Newton  (1779)</a></div>
C.M.

HOW sweet the Name of Jesus sounds
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In a believer’s ear!
It soothes his sorrows, heals his wounds,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And drives away his fear.

It makes the wounded spirit whole,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And calms the troubled breast;
’Tis Manna to the hungry soul,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And to the weary rest.

Dear Name! the Rock on which we build,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our Shield and Hiding-place,
Our never-failing Treasury, filled
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With boundless stores of grace.

Jesus, our Saviour, Shepherd, Friend, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Prophet, and Priest, and King;
Our Lord, our Life, our Way, our End, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Accept the praise we bring.

Weak is the effort of our heart,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And cold our warmest thought;
But when we see Thee as Thou art, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We’ll praise Thee as we ought!

Till then we would Thy love proclaim 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With every fleeting breath;
And may the music of Thy Name
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Refresh my soul in death.


191
How wonderful! that Thou the Son hast come

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kammer_der_von_Albert&#39;,this)">Albert von der Kammer</a></div>
10.10.10.10.D.

HOW wonderful! that Thou the Son hast come,
And here for us as Son of man hast died;
Our sins were laid on Thee, Thou didst become
Salvation’s Rock, when Thou wast crucified;
And faith perceives Thy finished work - the rest
Where love well-known, yet passing human thought,
Has set our feet; as those in Jesus blest,
We praise and worship by Thy Spirit taught.

To Thee, O Lord, we bring our note of praise,
To Thee who bore for us the Cross of shame!
What grief Thou knewest on that day of days,
When curse and death on Thee, the Victim, came!
How great Thy grace! no mind of man can grasp
The love told out in suffering on the tree;
Love that has gathered now within its clasp
Those once far off, but now brought home to Thee.

How wonderful that love made manifest
In Thee - its fulness told! so that the heart,
Touched by Thy kindness, finds in Thee its rest,
And lost in Thee, adoring, knows its part;
There to our hearts Thy rich unmeasured grace,
And love’s full fountain more and more revealed,
Call forth from every mouth Thine endless praise,
And willing lips their heart-felt homage yield.


192
I am redeemed, oh, praise the Lord

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Alystyne_Frances_Jane_Van&#39;,this)">Frances (Fanny) Jane Crosby</a></div>
C.M.D.

I AM redeemed, oh, praise the Lord;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My soul from bondage free,
Has found at last a resting place
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In Him who died for me.

c
I am redeemed, I am redeemed,
I’ll sing it o’er and o’er;
I am redeemed, oh, praise the Lord;
Redeemed forevermore.

I looked, and lo! from Calvary’s Cross
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A healing fountain streamed;
It cleansed my heart, and now I sing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Praise God, I am redeemed. 

c
I am redeemed, I am redeemed,
I’ll sing it o’er and o’er;
I am redeemed, oh, praise the Lord;
Redeemed forevermore.

The debt is paid, my soul is free,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And by His mighty power,
The blood that washed my sins away
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Still cleanseth every hour. 

c
I am redeemed, I am redeemed,
I’ll sing it o’er and o’er;
I am redeemed, oh, praise the Lord;
Redeemed forevermore.

All glory be to Jesus’ name;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I know that He is mine,
For on my heart the Spirit seals
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His pledge of love divine. 

c
I am redeemed, I am redeemed,
I’ll sing it o’er and o’er;
I am redeemed, oh, praise the Lord;
Redeemed forevermore.


193
I am the Lord’s! O joy beyond expression
Previous BHB #394
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Bennett_Lucy_Ann&#39;,this)">Lucy Ann Bennett</a></div>
11.10.11.10.

I AM the Lord’s! O joy beyond expression,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O sweet response to voice of love divine;
Faith’s joyous ‘Yes!’ to the assuring whisper,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Fear not, I have redeemed thee, thou art Mine.

I am the Lord’s! it hushes every murmur,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;It soothes the fevered spirit to its rest;
I am the Lord’s! it is the child’s rejoinder,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who knows and feels the Father’s will is best.

I am the Lord’s! O eagerly and gladly,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Triumphantly and gratefully we sing;
I am the Lord’s! It is the rock  unfailing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To which our storm-tossed souls in danger cling.

I am the Lord’s! yet teach me all it meaneth,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All it involves of love and loyalty,
Of holy service, full and glad  surrender,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And unreserved obedience unto Thee.

I am the Lord’s! yes; body, soul and spirit;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;They’re sealed, and irrecoverably Thine;
As Thou, Belovèd, in Thy grace and fulness
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For ever and for evermore art mine.

I am the Lord's! It is the glad confession
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Wherewith the Bride recalls the happy day,
When love's “I will” accepted Him forever,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“The Lord's”, to love, to honour and obey.


194
I am Thine, O Lord, I have heard Thy voice
Previous BHB #329<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Alystyne_Frances_Jane_Van&#39;,this)">Frances (Fanny) Jane Crosby (1875)</a></div>
P.M.

I AM Thine, O Lord, I have heard Thy voice,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And it told Thy love to me;
But I long to rise in the arms of faith,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And be closer drawn to Thee.

c

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To the Cross where Thou hast died;
Draw me nearer, nearer, nearer, blessèd Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Thy precious, wounded side.

Consecrate me now to Thy service, Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;By the power of grace divine;
Let my soul look up with a steadfast hope,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And my will be lost in Thine. 

c
Draw me nearer, nearer, blessèd Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To the Cross where Thou hast died;
Draw me nearer, nearer, nearer, blessèd Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Thy precious, wounded side.

O the pure delight of a single hour
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That before Thy throne I spend,
When I kneel in prayer, and with Thee, my God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I commune as friend with friend.

c
Draw me nearer, nearer, blessèd Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To the Cross where Thou hast died;
Draw me nearer, nearer, nearer, blessèd Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Thy precious, wounded side.

There are depths of love that I cannot know
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Till I cross the narrow sea;
There are heights of joy that I  may not reach
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Till I rest in peace with Thee.

c
Draw me nearer, nearer, blessèd Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To the Cross where Thou hast died;
Draw me nearer, nearer, nearer, blessèd Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Thy precious, wounded side.


195
I am waiting for the dawning
Previous BHB #81
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Francis_Samuel_Trevor&#39;,this)">Samuel Trevor Francis</a></div>
8.7.8.7.D.

I AM waiting for the dawning
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of that bright and blessèd day
When the darksome night of sorrow
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shall have vanished far away;
When for ever with the Saviour,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Far beyond this vale of tears,
I shall swell the song of worship
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Through the everlasting years.

I am looking at the brightness,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;See, it shineth from afar,
Of the clear and joyous beaming
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of the “Bright and Morning Star”;
Through the dark grey mist of morning
Do I see its glorious light;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Then away with every shadow
Of this sad and weary night.

I am waiting for the coming
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of the Lord who died for me;
Oh, His words have thrilled my spirit,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“I will come again for thee”.
Faith can almost hear His footfall
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On the threshold of the door,
And my heart, my heart is longing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To be with Him evermore.


196
I bow me to Thy will, O God
Previous BHB #87<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Faber_Frederick_William&#39;,this)">Frederick William Faber</a></div>
C.M.

I BOW me to Thy will, O God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And all Thy ways adore,
And every day I live I’d seek
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To please Thee more and more.

Why should I care, O blessèd Lord?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Since all my cares are Thine;
Why not in triumph live, since Thou
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hast made Thy triumphs mine?

Lead on, lead on triumphantly,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O blessèd Lord! lead on;
Faith’s pilgrim sons behind Thee seek
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The road that Thou hast gone.

He always wins who sides with God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To him no chance is lost;
God’s will is sweetest to him when
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;It triumphs at his cost.

Ill that God blesses is our good,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And unblest good is ill;
And all is right that seems most wrong,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;If it be His sweet will.


197
I cannot breathe enough of Thee

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Walton_Spencer_W&#39;,this)">W. Spencer Walton</a></div>
8.6.8.8.6.

I CANNOT breathe enough of Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O gentle breeze of love;
More fragrant than the myrtle tree
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Rose of Sharon is to me,
The Balm of heaven above.

I cannot gaze enough on Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou Fairest of the Fair;
My heart is filled with ecstasy,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As in Thy face of radiancy
I see such beauty there.

I cannot work enough for Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My Saviour, Master, Friend;
I do not wish to go out free,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But ever, always, willingly,
To serve Thee to the end.

I cannot sing enough of Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The sweetest name on earth;
A note so full of melody
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Comes from my heart so joyously,
And fills my soul with mirth.

I cannot speak enough of Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I have so much to tell;
Thy heart it beats so tenderly
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As Thou dost draw me close to Thee,
And whisper, “All is well”.


198
I cannot tell why He, whom angels worship

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Fullerton_Y_William&#39;,this)">William Y. Fullerton</a></div>
11.10.11.10.11.10.11.12.

I CANNOT tell why He, whom angels worship,
Should set His love upon the sons of men,
Or why, as Shepherd, He should seek the wanderers,
To bring them back, they know not how or when.
But this I know, that He was born of Mary
When Bethl'em's manger was His only home,
And that He lived at Nazareth and laboured,
And so the Saviour, Saviour of the world, is come.

I cannot tell how silently He suffered,
As with His peace He graced this place of tears,
Or how His heart upon the Cross was broken,
The crown of pain to three and thirty years.
But this I know, He heals the broken-hearted
And stays our sin and calms our lurking fear
And lifts the burden from the heavy-laden; 
For still the Saviour, Saviour of the world, is here. 

I cannot tell how all the lands shall worship,
When at His bidding every storm is stilled,
Or who can say how great the jubilation
When every heart with love and joy is filled.
But this I know, the skies will thrill with rapture, 
And myriad, myriad human voices sing,
And earth to heav'n, and heav'n to earth, will answer,
“At last the Saviour, Saviour of the world, is King!”


199
I cannot work my soul to save

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Anonymous_Hymn_Writers&#39;,this)">Author Unknown</a></div>
C.M.

I CANNOT work my soul to save,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For that my Lord has done;
But I would work like any slave,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From love to God’s dear Son.

He gave Himself upon the Cross,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A sacrifice for me;
And God accepts what He has done,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To save and pardon me.

His precious blood has cleansed my soul,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My sins are all forgiven;
And now I long to see His face,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And serve Him more in heaven.


200
I know who holds the future

<p>Copyright © 1958 New Spring Publishing Inc. (ASCAP)(adm. at CapitolCMGPublishing.com) All rights reserved. Used by permission.</p><div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Clark_Eugene&#39;,this)">Eugene Clark</a> and <a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Smith_Alfred&#39;,this)">Alfred Smith</a></div>
P.M.

I DO not know what lies ahead,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The way I cannot see,
But One stands near to be my guide,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He'll show the way to me.

c
I know who holds the future
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And He'll guide me with His hand;
With God things don't just happen,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Everything by Him is planned.
So as I face tomorrow,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With its problems large and small,
I'll trust the God of miracles,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Give to Him my all.

I do not know how many days
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of life are mine to spend,
But One who knows and cares for me
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Will keep me to the end.

c
I know who holds the future
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And He'll guide me with His hand;
With God things don't just happen,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Everything by Him is planned.
So as I face tomorrow,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With its problems large and small,
I'll trust the God of miracles,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Give to Him my all.

I do not know the course ahead,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What joys and griefs are there,
But One stands near who fully knows,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I'll trust His loving care.

c
I know who holds the future
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And He'll guide me with His hand;
With God things don't just happen,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Everything by Him is planned.
So as I face tomorrow,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With its problems large and small,
I'll trust the God of miracles,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Give to Him my all.


201
I have a Friend whose faithful love

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Tydeman_A_Ebenezer&#39;,this)">Ebenezer A. Tydeman</a></div>
8.8.8.8.8.8.8.

I HAVE a Friend whose faithful love
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Is more than all the world to me,
’Tis higher than the heights above,
And deeper than the soundless sea:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;So old, so new, So strong, so true;
Before the earth received its frame,
<i>He loved me - Blessèd be His Name!</i>

He held the highest place above,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Adored by all the sons of flame,
Yet, such His self-denying love,
He laid aside His crown and came
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To seek the lost, And, at the cost
Of heav’nly rank and earthly fame,
<i>He sought me - Blessèd be His Name!</i>

It was a lonely path He trod,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From every human soul apart,
Known only to Himself and God
Was all the grief that filled His heart:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Yet from the track, He turned not back,
Till where I lay in want and shame,
<i>He found me - Blessèd be His Name!</i>

Then dawned at last that day of dread
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When, desolate but undismayed,
With wearied frame and thorn - crowned head
He, now forsaken and betrayed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Went up for me To Calvary,
And dying there in grief and shame,
<i>He saved me - Blessèd be His Name!</i>

Long as I live my song shall tell
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The wonders of His matchless love:
And when at last I rise to dwell
In the bright home prepared above,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My joy shall be His face to see,
And bowing then with loud acclaim,
<i>I’ll praise Him - Blessèd be His Name!</i>


202
I have a home above
Previous BHB #89<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Bennett_Henry&#39;,this)">Henry Bennett</a></div>
S.M.

I HAVE a home above
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From sin and sorrow free;
A mansion which eternal love
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Designed and formed for me.

My Father’s gracious hand
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Has built this blest abode;
From everlasting it was planned
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My dwelling-place with God.

My Saviour’s precious blood
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Has made my title sure:
He passed through death’s dark raging flood
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To make my rest secure.

The Comforter is come,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The earnest has been given;
He leads me onward to the home
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Reserved for me in heaven.

Loved ones are gone before
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Whose pilgrim days are done;
I soon shall greet them on that shore
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Where partings are unknown.

But more than all, I long
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His glories to behold,
Whose smile fills all the radiant throng
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With ecstasy untold.

That bright, yet tender smile,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My sweetest welcome there,
Shall cheer me through the “little while”
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I tarry for Him here.

Thy love, most gracious Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My joy and strength shall be,
Till Thou shalt speak the gladd’ning word
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That bids me rise to Thee.

And then, through endless days
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Where all Thy glories shine,
In happier, holier strains I’ll praise
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The grace that made me Thine.


203
I have a Shepherd, One I love so well

P.M.

I HAVE a Shepherd, One I love so well;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;How He has blessed me tongue can never tell;
On the Cross He suffered, shed His blood and died,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That I might ever in His love confide.

c
Following Jesus, ever day by day,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Nothing can harm me when He leads the way;
Darkness or sunshine, whate'er befall,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus, the Shepherd, is my All in All.

Pastures abundant doth His hand provide,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Still waters flowing ever at my side,
Goodness and mercy follow on  my track,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With such a Shepherd nothing can I lack.

c
Following Jesus, ever day by day,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Nothing can harm me when He leads the way;
Darkness or sunshine, whate'er befall,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus, the Shepherd, is my All in All.

When I would wander from the path astray,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Then He will draw me back into the way;
In the darkest valley I need fear no ill,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For He, my Shepherd, will be with  me still.

c
Following Jesus, ever day by day,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Nothing can harm me when He leads the way;
Darkness or sunshine, whate'er befall,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus, the Shepherd, is my All in All.

When labour's ended and the journey done,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Then He will lead me safely to my home;
There I shall dwell in rapture sure and sweet,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With all the loved ones gathered round His feet.

c
Following Jesus, ever day by day,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Nothing can harm me when He leads the way;
Darkness or sunshine, whate'er befall,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus, the Shepherd, is my All in All.


204
I have been at the altar and witnessed the Lamb
Previous BHB #88<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Hull_Amelia_Matilda&#39;,this)">Amelia Matilda Hull</a></div>
P.M.

I HAVE been at the altar and witnessed the Lamb
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Burnt wholly to ashes for me;
And watched its sweet savour ascending on high,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Accepted, O Father, by Thee.

And lo, while I gazed at the glorious sight,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A voice from above reached mine ears:
“By this thine iniquity’s taken away,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And no trace of it on thee appears”.

“An end of thy sin has been made for thee here
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;By Him who its penalty bore;
With blood it is blotted eternally  out,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And I will not remember it more.”

O Lord, I believe it with wonder and joy;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Confirm, Thou, this precious belief;
While daily I learn that I am in  myself
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of sinners the vilest and chief.


205
I have only one life on this earth

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Anonymous_Hymn_Writers&#39;,this)">Author Unknown</a></div>
P.M.

I HAVE only one life on this earth
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And as vapour it's passing away.
I must labour for treasures of worth,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Ere toil ends at the close of the day.

c
Only one life to give!
I must never withhold it from God.
Only one life to live!
I must not miss the “Well done!” of God.

Only one life, and white are the fields,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With compassion this great need I view;
This one life that I have, I will yield;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And the little I can, let me do.

c
Only one life to give!
I must never withhold it from God.
Only one life to live!
I must not miss the “Well done!” of God.

This one life that I have, I may lose,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And, in losing, a hundred-fold  gain!
Then to fall on the Rock, I will choose,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And be broken, God’s best to  obtain.

c
Only one life to give!
I must never withhold it from God.
Only one life to live!
I must not miss the “Well done!” of God.

One poor life, small the offering at best,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Yet the world and the flesh often call;
This my answer must be to each  test:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“I'll not serve God with less than my all!”

c
Only one life to give!
I must never withhold it from God.
Only one life to live!
I must not miss the “Well done!” of God.


206
I hear the accuser roar
Previous BHB #93<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Gandy_Samuel_Whitelock&#39;,this)">Samuel Whitlock Gandy</a></div>
S.M.

I HEAR the accuser roar
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of ills that I have done;
I know them well, and thousands more;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jehovah findeth none.

Sin, Satan, Death, press near
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To harass and appal;
Let but my risen Lord appear,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Backward they go and fall.

Before, behind, around,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;They set their fierce array
To fight and force me from my ground
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Along Immanuel’s way.

I meet them face to face
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Through Jesus’ conquest blest;
March in the triumph of His grace
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Right onward to my rest.

There, in His book I bear
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A more than conq’ror’s name,
A soldier, son, and fellow-heir,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who fought and overcame.

His be the Victor’s name
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who fought our fight alone:
Triumphant saints no honour claim,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Their conquest was His own.

By weakness and defeat
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He won the meed and crown;
Trod all our foes beneath His feet
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;By being trodden down.

He hell in hell laid low;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Made sin, He sin o’erthrew;
Bowed to the grave, destroyed it so,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And death, by dying, slew.

Bless, bless the Conq’ror slain!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Slain in His victory!
Who lived, who died, who lives again,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For thee, His Church, for thee.


207
I hear the words of love
Previous BHB #91
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Bonar_Horatius&#39;,this)">Horatius Bonar  (1846)</a></div>
S.M.

I HEAR the words of love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I gaze upon the blood,
I see the mighty sacrifice,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And I have peace with God.

'Tis everlasting peace!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sure as Jehovah’s Name,
'Tis stable as His steadfast throne,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For evermore the same.

The clouds may go and come,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And storms may sweep my sky;
This blood-sealed friendship changes not,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Cross is ever nigh.

My love is ofttimes low,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My joy still ebbs and flows,
But peace with Him remains the same,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No change Jehovah knows.

I change, He changes not;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Christ can never die:
His love, not mine, the resting-place,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His truth, not mine, the tie.

That which can shake the Cross
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;May shake the peace it gave,
Which tells me Christ has never died,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Or never left the grave!

Till then my peace is sure,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;It will not, cannot yield,
Jesus, I know, has died and lives,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On this firm rock I build.

I know He liveth now
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;At God’s right hand above;
I know the throne on which He sits,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I know His truth and love.


208
I heard the voice of Jesus say
Previous BHB #90<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Bonar_Horatius&#39;,this)">Horatius Bonar  (1846)</a></div>
C.M.

I HEARD the voice of Jesus say,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“Come unto Me and rest;
Lay down, thou weary one, lay down
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy head upon My breast”.

I came to Jesus as I was,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Weary, and worn, and sad;
I found in Him a resting-place,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And He has made me glad.

I heard the voice of Jesus say,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“Behold, I freely give
The living water: Thirsty one
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Stoop down, and drink, and live”.

I came to Jesus, and I drank
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of that life-giving stream;
My thirst was quenched, my soul revived,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And now I live in Him.

I heard the voice of Jesus say,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“I am this dark world’s Light;
Look unto Me, thy morn shall rise,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And all thy day be bright”.

I looked to Jesus, and I found
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In Him my Star, my Sun;
And in that light of life I’ll walk
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Till trav’lling days are done.


209
I journey through a desert drear and wild
Previous BHB #92<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Walker_Mary_Jane_Deck&#39;,this)">Mary Jane Deck Walker</a></div>
10.10.10.10.

I JOURNEY through a desert drear and wild,
Yet is my heart by such sweet thoughts beguiled
Of Him on whom I lean, my Strength, my Stay,
I can forget the sorrows of the way.

Thoughts of His love - the root of ev’ry grace
Which finds in this poor heart a dwelling-place;
The sunshine of my soul, than day more bright,
And my calm pillow of repose by night.

Thoughts of His sojourn in this vale of tears;
The tale of love unfolded in those years
Of sinless suffering and patient grace,
I love again and yet again to trace.

Thoughts of His glory - on the Cross I gaze,
And there behold its sad yet healing rays;
Beacon of hope which, lifted up on high,
Illumes with heavenly light the tear-dimmed eye.

Thoughts of His coming; for that joyful day
In patient hope I watch, and wait, and pray;
The dawn draws nigh, the midnight shadows flee,
O what a sunrise will that advent be!


210
I know not the hour of His coming

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Alystyne_Frances_Jane_Van&#39;,this)">Frances (Fanny) Jane Crosby</a></div>
P.M.

I KNOW not the hour of His coming,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Nor how He will speak to my heart;
Or whether at morning or mid-day,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My spirit to Him will depart.

c
But I know ... I shall wake in the likeness
Of Him ... I am longing to see;
I know ... that mine eyes shall behold Him,
Who died ... for a sinner like me.

I know not the bliss that awaits me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;At rest with my Saviour above;
I know not how soon I shall enter,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And bathe in the ocean of love. 

c

Of Him ... I am longing to see;
I know ... that mine eyes shall behold Him,
Who died ... for a sinner like me.

Perhaps in the midst of my labour,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A voice from my Lord I shall hear;
Perhaps in the slumber of midnight,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Its message may fall on my ear. 

c
