Of Him ... I am longing to see;
I know ... that mine eyes shall behold Him,
Who died ... for a sinner like me.

I know not, but oh I am watching,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My lamp ever burning and bright!
I know not if Jesus will call me
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;At morning, at noon, or at night. 

c
But I know ... I shall wake in the likeness
Of Him ... I am longing to see;
I know ... that mine eyes shall behold Him,
Who died ... for a sinner like me.


211
I know not when, but this I know

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Anonymous_Hymn_Writers&#39;,this)">Author Unknown</a></div>
8.6.8.8.6.

I KNOW not when, but this I know,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That I shall see His face:
I may be called by death to go,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Or wait His coming here below,
But I shall see His face.

That face once spit upon for me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That holy, blessèd face!
And stared at in His agony,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;While hanging on the cursèd tree,
Yes, I shall see that face.

But not a cloud of sorrow now
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Can shade His glorious face,
Eternal gladness crowns His brow,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Where heavenly hosts before  Him bow,
There I shall see His face.

E’en now by faith my soul can say,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I see my Saviour’s face,
Though mine be here a darkened way,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;This cheers me on from day to day,
Until I see His face.


212
I lay my sins on Jesus
Previous BHB #166
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Bonar_Horatius&#39;,this)">Horatius Bonar (1845)</a></div>
7.6.7.6.D.

I LAY my sins on Jesus
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The spotless Lamb of God;
He bears them all, and frees us,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From the accursèd load.
I bring my guilt to Jesus;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To wash my crimson stains
White in His blood most precious,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Till not a spot remains.

I lay my wants on Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All fulness dwells in Him;
He healeth my diseases,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He doth my soul redeem.
I lay my griefs on Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My burdens and my cares;
He from them all releases,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He all my sorrows shares.

I rest my soul on Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;This weary soul of mine;
His right hand me embraces,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I on His breast recline.
I love the Name of Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Immanuel, Christ, the Lord!
Like fragrance on the breezes
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His Name abroad is poured.

I long to be like Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Meek, loving, lowly, mild;
I long to be like Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Father’s holy Child.
I long to be with Jesus
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Amid the heavenly throng,
To sing with saints His praises,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To learn the angels’ song .


213
I left it all with Jesus long ago
Previous BHB #94
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Willis_Ellen_H&#39;,this)">Ellen H. Willis  (1876)</a></div>
P.M.

I LEFT it all with Jesus long ago;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All my sins I brought Him, and my woe:
When by faith I saw Him on the tree,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Heard His still, small whisper, “'Tis for thee”,
From my heart the burden rolled away: Happy day!
From my heart the burden rolled away: Happy day!

I leave it all with Jesus, for He knows
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;How to steal the bitter from life's woes;
How to gild the tear-drop with His smile,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Make the desert garden bloom awhile:
When my weakness leaneth on  His might, All seems light.
When my weakness leaneth on  His might, All seems light.

I leave it all with Jesus day by day;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Faith can firmly trust Him, come what may:
Hope has dropped her anchor, found her rest
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In the calm, sure haven of His breast;
Love esteems it heaven to abide At His side.
Love esteems it heaven to abide At His side.

Oh, leave it all with Jesus, drooping soul!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Tell not half thy story, but the whole.
Worlds on worlds are hanging on His hand,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Life and death are waiting His command;
Yet His tender bosom makes thee room - Oh, come home!
Yet His tender bosom makes thee room - Oh, come home!


214
I need Thee, precious Saviour!
Previous BHB #95<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Whitfield_Frederick&#39;,this)">Frederick Whitfield</a></div>
7.6.7.6.D.

I NEED Thee, precious Saviour!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O Thou art all to me;
Before the throne for ever 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I stand complete in Thee.
Though Satan loud accuses,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Yet I can ever see
The blood of Christ most precious,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The sinner’s perfect plea.

I need Thee, precious Saviour!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For I am very poor;
A stranger and a pilgrim,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I have no earthly store.
I need Thy love, Lord Jesus!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To cheer me on my way,
To guide my doubting footsteps,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To be my strength and stay.

I need Thee, precious Saviour!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I need a friend like Thee;
A friend to soothe and comfort,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A friend to care for me.
I need Thy heart, Lord Jesus!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To feel each anxious care,
To bear my ev’ry burden,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And all my sorrow share.

I need Thee, precious Saviour!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For I am very blind,
A weak and foolish wand’rer,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With dark and evil mind.
I need Thy light, Lord Jesus!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To tread the thorny road,
To guide me safe to glory
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Where I shall see my God.

I need Thee, precious Saviour!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I need Thee day by day
To fill me with Thy fulness,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To lead me on my way.
I need Thy Holy Spirit
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To teach me what I am,
To show me more of Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To point me to the Lamb.

I need Thee, precious Saviour!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And hope to see Thee soon,
Encircled with the rainbow
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And seated on Thy throne;
There, with Thy blood-bought people,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My joy shall ever be
To sing Thy praise, Lord Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And ever gaze on Thee.


215
I once was a stranger to grace and to God
Previous BHB #96
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;McCheyne_Robert_Murray&#39;,this)">Robert Murray McCheyne  (1837)</a></div>
11.11.11.11.

I ONCE was a stranger to grace and to God;
I knew not my danger, I felt not my load;
Though friends spoke in rapture of Christ on the tree,
Jehovah Tsidkenu was nothing to me.

Like tears from the daughters of Zion that roll,
I wept when the waters went over His soul;
Yet thought not that my sins had nailed to the tree
Jehovah Tsidkenu - ’twas nothing to me.

When free grace awoke me by light from on high,
Then legal fears shook me, I trembled to die;
No refuge, no safety in self could I see,
Jehovah Tsidkenu my Saviour must be.

My terrors all vanished before the sweet Name;
My guilty fears banished, with boldness I came
To drink at the fountain, life-giving and free;
Jehovah Tsidkenu is all things to me.

E'en treading the valley, the shadow of death,
This watchword shall rally my faltering breath;
For when from life’s fever my God sets me free,
Jehovah Tsidkenu, my death song shall be.


216
I saw One hanging on a Tree

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Newton_John&#39;,this)">John Newton</a></div>
C.M.D.

I SAW One hanging on a tree,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In agony and blood;
He fixed His languid eyes on me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As near His Cross I stood.

c
Oh, can it be, upon a tree
The Saviour died for me?
My soul is thrilled, My heart is filled,
To think He died for me!

Sure, never, till my latest breath,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Can I forget that look:
It seemed to charge me with His death,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Tho' not a word He spoke.

c
Oh, can it be, upon a tree
The Saviour died for me?
My soul is thrilled, My heart is filled,
To think He died for me!

My conscience felt and owned the guilt,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And plunged me in despair;
I saw my sins His blood had spilt
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And helped to nail Him there. 

c
Oh, can it be, upon a tree
The Saviour died for me?
My soul is thrilled, My heart is filled,
To think He died for me!

Alas! I knew not what I did,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But now my tears are vain:
Where shall my trembling soul be hid?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For I the Lord have slain. 

c
Oh, can it be, upon a tree
The Saviour died for me?
My soul is thrilled, My heart is filled,
To think He died for me!

A second look He gave, which said,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“I freely all forgive;
This blood is for thy ransom paid,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I die that thou may'st live”. 

c
Oh, can it be, upon a tree
The Saviour died for me?
My soul is thrilled, My heart is filled,
To think He died for me!

Thus while His death my sin displays
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In all its blackest hue,
Such is the mystery of grace,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;It seals my pardon too.

c
Oh, can it be, upon a tree
The Saviour died for me?
My soul is thrilled, My heart is filled,
To think He died for me!


217
I saw the Cross of Jesus
Previous BHB #97
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Muller_George&#39;,this)">George Müller</a> & (tr?) <a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Whitfield_Frederick&#39;,this)">Frederick Whitfield</a>  (1875)</div>
7.6.7.6.D.

I SAW the Cross of Jesus
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When burdened with my sin,
I sought the Cross of Jesus
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To give me peace within;
I brought my soul to Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He cleansed it in His blood;
And in the Cross of Jesus
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I found my peace with God.

I love the Cross of Jesus;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;It tells me what I am:
A vile and guilty creature,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Saved only through the Lamb.
No righteousness, no merit,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No beauty can I plead;
Yet in the Cross I glory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My title there I read.

I trust the Cross of Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In every trying hour,
My sure and certain refuge,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My never-failing tower;
In every fear and conflict,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I more than conqueror am;
Living, I'm safe, or dying,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Through Christ, the risen Lamb.

Safe at the Cross of Jesus;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;There let my weary heart
Still rest in peace unshaken
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Till with Him - ne’er to part;
And then in strains of glory
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I’ll sing His wondrous power,
Where sin can never enter,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And death is known no more.


218
He Lives

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Ackley_H_Alfred&#39;,this)">Alfred H. Ackley</a></div><p>&copy; 1933 Renewed 1961 The Rodeheaver Company (Adm Song Solutions www.songsolutions.org) All rights reserved. Used by permission.</p>
P.M.

I SERVE a risen Saviour,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He’s in the world today;
I know that He is living,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Whatever men may say;
I see His hand of mercy;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I hear His voice of cheer;
And just the time I need Him
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He’s always near.

c
He lives, He lives, Christ Jesus lives today!
He walks with me and talks with 
me along life’s narrow way.
He lives, He lives, salvation to impart!
You ask me how I know He lives?
He lives within my heart.

In all the world around me
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I see His loving care,
And though my heart grows weary,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I never will despair;
I know that He is leading,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Through all the stormy blast;
The day of His appearing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Will come at last.

c
He lives, He lives, Christ Jesus lives today!
He walks with me and talks with 
me along life’s narrow way.
He lives, He lives, salvation to impart!
You ask me how I know He lives?
He lives within my heart.

Rejoice, rejoice, O Christian,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lift up your voice and sing
Eternal hallelujahs
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Jesus Christ the King!
The Hope of all who seek Him,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Help of all who find,
None other is so loving,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;So good and kind.

c
He lives, He lives, Christ Jesus lives today!
He walks with me and talks with 
me along life’s narrow way.
He lives, He lives, salvation to impart!
You ask me how I know He lives?
He lives within my heart.


219
I stand all amazed at the love Jesus offers me

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Gabriel_Charles&#39;,this)">Charles Gabriel</a></div>
P.M.

I STAND all amazed at the love Jesus offers me,
Confused at the grace that so fully He proffers me;
I tremble to know that for me He was crucified,
That for me, a sinner, He suffered, He bled, and died.

c
Oh! it is wonderful that He should care for me,
Enough to die for me!
Oh, it is wonderful, wonderful to me!

I marvel that He would descend from His throne divine,
To rescue a soul so rebellious and proud as mine;
That He should extend His great love unto such as I,
Sufficient to own, to redeem, and to justify.

c
Oh! it is wonderful that He should care for me,
Enough to die for me!
Oh, it is wonderful, wonderful to me!

I think of His hands, pierced and bleeding to pay the debt!
Such mercy, such love and devotion can I forget?
No, no! I will praise and adore at the mercy seat,
Until at the glorified throne I kneel at His feet. 

c
Oh! it is wonderful that He should care for me,
Enough to die for me!
Oh, it is wonderful, wonderful to me!


220
I stand amazed in the presence of Jesus the Nazarene

P.M.

I STAND amazed in the presence
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of Jesus the Nazarene,
And wonder how He could love me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A sinner, condemned, unclean.

c
How marvellous! How wonderful!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And my song shall ever be:
How marvellous! How wonderful!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Is my Saviour's love for me!

For me it was in the garden
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He prayed, “Not My will, but Thine”;
He had no tears for His own griefs,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But sweat drops of blood for mine.

c
How marvellous! How wonderful!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And my song shall ever be:
How marvellous! How wonderful!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Is my Saviour's love for me!

In wonder angels beheld Him,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And came from the world of light
To comfort Him in the sorrows
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He bore for my soul that night. 

c
How marvellous! How wonderful!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And my song shall ever be:
How marvellous! How wonderful!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Is my Saviour's love for me!

He took my sins and my sorrows,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He made them His very own;
He bore the burden to Calvary,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And suffered and died alone.

c
How marvellous! How wonderful!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And my song shall ever be:
How marvellous! How wonderful!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Is my Saviour's love for me!

When with the ransomed in glory
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His face I at last shall see,
’Twill be my joy thro' the ages
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To sing of His love for me. 

c
How marvellous! How wonderful!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And my song shall ever be:
How marvellous! How wonderful!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Is my Saviour's love for me!


221
I thirst, but not as once I did
Previous BHB #98
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Cowper_William&#39;,this)">William Cowper</a></div>
L.M.

I THIRST, but not as once I did,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The vain delights of earth to share:
Thy wounds, Emmanuel, all forbid
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That I should seek my pleasure there.

It was the sight of Thy dear Cross
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;First weaned my soul from  earthly things;
And taught me to esteem as dross
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The mirth of fools and pomp of  kings.

I want that grace that springs from Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That quickens all things where it flows,
And makes a wretched thorn, like me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Bloom as the myrtle, or the rose.

Great fountain of delight unknown!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No longer sink below the brim;
But overflow, and pour me down
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A living, and life-giving stream!

For sure, of all the plants that share
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The notice of Thy Father's eye,
None proves less grateful to His care,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Or yields Him meaner fruit than I.


222
I to the hills will lift mine eyes

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Whittingham_William&#39;,this)">William Whittingham</a> and <a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Rous_Francis&#39;,this)">Francis Rous</a></div>
C.M.

I TO the hills will lift mine eyes,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From whence doth come mine aid.
My safety cometh from the Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who heav’n and earth hath made.

Thy foot He’ll not let slide, nor will
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He slumber that thee keeps.
Behold, He that keeps Israel,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He slumbers not, nor sleeps.

The Lord thee keeps, the Lord thy shade
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On thy right hand doth stay:
The moon by night thee shall  not smite,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Nor yet the sun by day.

The Lord shall keep thy soul; He shall
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Preserve thee from all ill.
Henceforth thy going out and in
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;God keep for ever will.


223
I was a wand’ring sheep
Previous BHB #105<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Bonar_Horatius&#39;,this)">Horatius Bonar  (1843)</a></div>
S.M.D.

I WAS a wand’ring sheep,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I did not love the fold;
I did not love my Shepherd’s voice,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I would not be controlled.
I was a wayward child,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I did not love my home;
I did not love my Father’s voice,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I loved afar to roam.

The Shepherd sought His sheep,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Father sought His child,
And followed me o’er vale and hill,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O’er desert waste and wild.
Yea, found me nigh to death,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Famished, and faint, and lone,
And bound me with the bands of love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And saved the wand’ring one.

Jesus my Shepherd is,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;’Twas He that loved my soul,
’Twas He that cleansed me by His blood,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;’Twas He that made me whole.
’Twas He that sought the lost,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That found the wand’ring sheep;
’Twas He that brought me to the fold,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;’Tis He that still doth keep.

No more a wand’ring sheep
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I love to be controlled.
I love my tender Shepherd’s voice,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I love the peaceful fold.
No more a wayward child
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I seek no more to roam;
I love my heavenly Father’s voice,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I love, I love His home.

225
I will never, never leave thee
Previous BHB #330<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Anonymous_Hymn_Writers&#39;,this)">Anonymous</a></div>
8.7.8.7.D.

I WILL never, never leave thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I will never thee forsake!
I will guard, and save, and keep thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For My Name and mercy’s sake!
Fear no evil, fear no evil,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Only all My counsel take,
For I’ll never, never leave thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I will never thee forsake.

When the storm is raging round thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Call on Me in humble prayer,
I will fold my arms about thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Guard thee with the tend’rest care;
In the trial, in the trial
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I will make thy pathway clear.
For I’ll never, never leave thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I will never thee forsake.

When the sky above is glowing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And around thee all is bright,
Pleasure like a river flowing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All things tending to delight,
I’ll be with thee, I’ll be with thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I will guide thy steps aright,
For I’ll never, never leave thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I will never thee forsake.

When thy soul is dark and clouded,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Filled with doubt, and grief, and  care,
Through the mist by which ’tis shrouded,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I will make a light appear;
And the banner, and the banner
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of my love I will uprear.
For I’ll never, never leave thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I will never thee forsake.

227
No one ever cared for me like Jesus

<p>Copyright © 1932 New Spring Publishing Inc. (ASCAP) (adm. at CapitolCMGPublishing.com) All rights reserved. Used by permission.</p><div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Weigle_Charles&#39;,this)">Charles Weigle</a></div>
P.M.

I WOULD love to tell you what I think of Jesus,
Since I found in Him a friend so strong and true;
I would tell you how He changed my life completely,
He did something that no other friend could do.

c
No one ever cared for me like Jesus,
There's no other friend so kind as He;
No one else could take the sin and darkness from me,
O how much He cared for me!

All my life was full of sin when Jesus found me,
All my heart was full of misery and woe;
Jesus placed His strong and loving arms around me,
And He led me in the way I ought to go.

c
No one ever cared for me like Jesus,
There's no other friend so kind as He;
No one else could take the sin and darkness from me,
O how much He cared for me!

Every day He comes to me with new assurance,
More and more I understand His words of love;
But I'll never know just why He came to save me,
Till some day I see His blessèd face above.

c
No one ever cared for me like Jesus,
There's no other friend so kind as He;
No one else could take the sin and darkness from me,
O how much He cared for me!


228
I’d rather have Jesus

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Bernadotte_Oscar&#39;,this)">Oscar Bernadotte and Rhea Miller</a></div><p>&copy; 1966 The Rodeheaver Co/Word Music LLC (Adm Song Solutions www.songsolutions.org). All rights reserved. Used by permission.</p>
P.M.

I'D rather have Jesus than silver or gold;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I'd rather be His than have riches untold;
I'd rather have Jesus than houses or land,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I'd rather be led by His nail-pierced hand

c
Than to be the king of a vast domain
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And be held in sin's dread sway;
I'd rather have Jesus than anything
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;This world affords today.

I'd rather have Jesus than men's applause;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I'd rather be faithful to His dear cause;
I'd rather have Jesus than world- wide fame,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I'd rather be true to His holy name.

c
Than to be the king of a vast domain
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And be held in sin's dread sway;
I'd rather have Jesus than anything
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;This world affords today.

He's fairer than lilies of rarest bloom;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He's sweeter than honey from  out the comb;
He's all that my hungering spirit needs,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I'd rather have Jesus and let Him  lead.

c
Than to be the king of a vast domain
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And be held in sin's dread sway;
I'd rather have Jesus than anything
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;This world affords today.


229
I’m a pilgrim and a stranger
Previous BHB #83<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Dana_Mary_S_B&#39;,this)">Mary S. B. Dana</a></div>
P.M.

I’M a pilgrim and a stranger,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Rough and thorny is the road,
Often in the midst of danger, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But it leads to God.
Clouds and darkness oft distress me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Great and many are my foes;
Anxious cares and thoughts oppress me:
But my Father knows.

O how sweet is this assurance
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;’Midst the conflict and the strife!
Although sorrows past endurance
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Follow me through life.
Home in prospect still can cheer me;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Yes, and give me sweet repose,
While I feel His presence near me: 
For my Father knows.

Yes, He sees and knows me daily;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Watches over me in love;
Sends me help when foes assail me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Bids me look above.
Soon my journey will be ended,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Life is drawing to a close;
I shall then be well attended: 
This my Father knows.

I shall then with joy behold Him,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Face to face my Saviour see;
Fall with rapture and adore Him
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For His love to me.
Nothing more shall then distress me
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In the land of sweet repose;
Jesus stands engaged to bless me: 
This my Father knows.

I’m not ashamed to own my Lord

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Watts_Isaac&#39;,this)">Isaac Watts</a></div>
C.M. with Chorus

I'M not ashamed to own my Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Or to defend His cause;
Maintain the honour of His Word,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The glory of His Cross.

c
At the Cross, at the Cross, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Where I first saw the light,
And the burden of my heart roll’d away;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;It was there by faith I received my sight,
And now I am happy all the day.

Jesus, my Lord! I know His name -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His name is all my trust;
Nor will He put my soul to shame,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Nor let my hope be lost.

c

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Where I first saw the light,
And the burden of my heart roll’d away;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;It was there by faith I received my sight,
And now I am happy all the day.

Firm as His throne His promise stands,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And He can well secure
What I've committed to His hands
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Till the decisive hour.

c

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Where I first saw the light,
And the burden of my heart roll’d away;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;It was there by faith I received my sight,
And now I am happy all the day.

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Before His Father's face;
And in the new Jerusalem
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Appoint my soul a place.

c

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Where I first saw the light,
And the burden of my heart roll’d away;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;It was there by faith I received my sight,
And now I am happy all the day.


231
I’m waiting for Thee, Lord
Previous BHB #84<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Burlingham_Hannah_Killham&#39;,this)">Hannah Kilham Burlingham</a></div>
P.M.

I’M waiting for Thee, Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy beauty to see, Lord,
I’m waiting for Thee, for Thy coming again.
Thou’rt gone over there, Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A place to prepare, Lord,
Thy home I shall share at Thy coming again.

’Mid danger and fear, Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I’m oft weary here, Lord;
The day must be near of Thy coming again.
’Tis all sunshine there, Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No sighing or care, Lord,
But glory so fair at Thy coming again.

Whilst Thou art away, Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I stumble and stray, Lord;
O hasten the day of Thy coming again!
This is not my rest, Lord;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A pilgrim confest, Lord,
I wait to be blest at Thy coming again.

Our loved ones before, Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Their troubles are o’er, Lord,
I’ll meet them once more at Thy coming again.
The blood was the sign, Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That marked them as Thine, Lord,
And brightly they’ll shine at Thy coming again.

E’en now let my ways, Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Be bright with Thy praise, Lord,
For brief are the days ere Thy coming again.
I’m waiting for Thee, Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy beauty to see, Lord;
No triumph for me like Thy coming again.


232
I’ve found a Friend; oh such a Friend!
Previous BHB #103<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Small_James_Grindley&#39;,this)">James Grindlay Small  (1863)</a></div>
8.7.8.7.D.

I’VE found a Friend; oh such a Friend!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He loved me ere I knew Him;
He drew me with the cords of love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And thus He bound me to Him.
And round my heart still closely twine
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Those ties which nought can sever,
For I am Christ’s, and He is mine,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For ever and for ever.

I’ve found a Friend; oh such a Friend!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He bled, He died to save me;
And not alone the gift of life,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But His own self He gave me.
Nought that I have, mine own I’ll call,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I’ll hold it for the Giver:
My heart, my strength, my life, my all,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Are His, and His for ever.

I’ve found a Friend; oh such a Friend!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All power to Him is given
To guard me on my onward course
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And bring me safe to heaven.
Eternal glories gleam afar
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To nerve my faint endeavour;
So now to watch! to work! to war!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And then to rest for ever.

I’ve found a Friend; oh such a Friend!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;So kind, and true, and tender,
So wise a Counsellor and Guide,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;So mighty a Defender.
From Him, who loves me now so well,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What power my soul can sever?
Shall life? or death? shall earth? or hell?
No! I am His for ever.


233
I’ve found a joy in sorrow
Previous BHB #104<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Crewdson_Jane&#39;,this)">Jane Fox Crewdson  (1864)</a></div>
7.6.7.6.D.

I’VE found a joy in sorrow,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A secret balm for pain,
A beautiful tomorrow
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of sunshine after rain.
I’ve found a branch of healing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Near ev’ry bitter spring,
A whispered promise stealing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O’er ev’ry broken string.

I’ve found a glad hosanna
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For every woe and wail;
A handful of sweet manna
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When grapes of Eshcol fail;
I’ve found the Rock of Ages
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When desert wells are dry;
And, after weary stages,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I’ve found an Elim nigh.

An Elim with its coolness,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Its fountains, and its shade;
A blessing in its fulness,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When buds of promise fade.
O’er tears of soft contrition
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I’ve seen a rainbow light,
A glory and fruition,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;So near! yet out of sight.

My Saviour, Thee possessing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I have the joy, the balm,
The healing and the blessing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The sunshine and the psalm;
The promise for the fearful,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Elim for the faint,
The rainbow for the tearful,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The glory for the saint.


234
I’ve found the precious Christ of God
Previous BHB #107<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Mason_John&#39;,this)">John Mason</a></div>
C.M.

I’VE found the precious Christ of God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My heart doth sing for joy;
And sing I must, for Christ I have,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A precious Christ have I.

Christ Jesus is the Lord of lords,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He is the King of kings;
He is the Sun of Righteousness
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With healing in His wings.

Christ is my meat, Christ is my drink,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My med’cine and my health;
My peace, my strength, my joy, my crown,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My glory and my wealth.

Christ is my Shepherd and my Friend,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My Saviour whom I love,
My Head, my Hope, my Counsellor,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My Advocate above.

Christ Jesus is the heaven of heaven;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My Christ what shall I call?
Christ is the First, Christ is the Last,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And Christ is all in all.


235
Immortal, invisible, God only wise
Previous BHB #396<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Smith_Walter_Chalmers&#39;,this)">Walter Chalmers Smith  (1876)</a></div>
11.11.11.11.

IMMORTAL, invisible, God only wise,
In light inaccessible hid from our eyes,
Most blessèd, most gracious, the Ancient of Days,
Almighty, victorious, Thy great name we praise.

Unresting, unhasting, and silent as light,
Nor wanting, nor wasting, Thou rulest in might;
Thy justice like mountains high soaring above
Thy clouds which are fountains of goodness and love.

To all, life Thou givest - to both great and small;
In all life Thou livest, the true life of all;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We blossom and flourish as  leaves on the tree,
And wither and perish - but nought changeth Thee.

Great Father of glory, pure Father of light,
Thine angels adore Thee, all veiling their sight;
All laud we would render: O help us to see
’Tis only the splendour of light hideth Thee.


236
In Christ Alone

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Getty_Keith&#39;,this)">Keith Getty</a> and <a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Townend_Stuart&#39;,this)">Stuart Townend</a></div><p>&copy; 2001 Thankyou Music (Adm. by CapitolCMGPublishing.com excl. UK & Europe, adm. at IntegrityRights.com)</p>
8.8.8.8.D.

IN Christ alone my hope is found;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He is my light, my strength, my song;
This cornerstone, this solid ground,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Firm through the fiercest drought and storm.
What heights of love, what depths of peace,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When fears are stilled, when strivings cease!
My Comforter, my all in all - 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Here in the love of Christ I stand.

In Christ alone, who took on flesh,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Fulness of God in helpless babe!
This gift of love and righteousness,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Scorned by the ones He came  to save.
Till on that Cross as Jesus died,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The wrath of God was satisfied;
For every sin on Him was laid - 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Here in the death of Christ I live.

There in the ground His body lay,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Light of the world by darkness  slain;
Then bursting forth in glorious day
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Up from the grave He rose again!
And as He stands in victory
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sin's curse has lost its grip on me,
For I am His and He is mine -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Bought with the precious blood  of Christ.

No guilt in life, no fear in death - 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;This is the power of Christ in me;
From life's first cry to final breath,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus commands my destiny.
No power of hell, no scheme of man,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Can ever pluck me from His hand;
Till He returns or calls me home -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Here in the power of Christ I'll  stand.


237
In heavenly love abiding
Previous BHB #395<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Waring_Anna_Laetitia&#39;,this)">Anna Laetitia Waring</a> & E. Cronin(?)  (1850)</div>
7.6.7.6.D.

IN heavenly love abiding,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No change my heart shall fear;
And safe is such confiding,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For nothing changes here:
The storm may roar without me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My heart may low be laid,
But God is round about me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And can I be dismayed?

Wherever He may guide me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No want shall turn me back;
My Shepherd is beside me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And nothing can I lack:
His wisdom ever waketh,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His sight is never dim;
He knows the way He taketh,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And I will walk with Him.

Green pastures are before me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Which yet I have not seen;
Bright skies will soon be o'er me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Where dark the clouds have been:
My hope I cannot measure,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My path to life is free;
My Saviour is my treasure
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And He will walk with me.


238
In holy majesty He moved

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Ewan_Y_Isaac&#39;,this)">Isaac Y. Ewan</a></div>
C.M.

IN holy majesty He moved
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Towards a Cross of shame; 
And every step the fuller proved
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The glory of His Name.

Not even Messianic birth
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His honour could enhance; 
The bogus dignity of earth 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Was withered in His glance.

Before the earth on which He trod
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Its circuit had begun,
Forever was He Son of God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Forever God the Son.

Unqualified perfection sealed
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The witness of His Word;
And every touch afresh revealed 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;None other than the Lord.

And yet, O lowliness most high!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For this to earth He came, 
As Man for guilty men to die 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Upon a Cross of shame.


239
In hope we lift our wishful, longing eyes

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Deck_James_George&#39;,this)">James George Deck</a></div>
10.10.10.10.

IN hope we lift our wishful, longing eyes,
Waiting to see the Morning Star arise;
How bright, how gladsome will His advent be,
Before the Sun shines forth in majesty!

How will our eyes to see His face delight,
Whose love has cheered us through the darksome night!
How will our ears drink in His well- known voice,
Whose faintest whisper makes our soul rejoice!

No stain within, no foes or snares around;
No jarring notes shall there discordant sound;
All pure without, all pure within the breast;
No thorns to wound, no toil to mar our rest.

If here on earth the thoughts of Jesus’ love
Lift our poor hearts this weary world above,
If even here the taste of heavenly springs
So cheers the spirit that the pilgrim sings: -

What will the sunshine of His glory prove?
What the unmingled fulness of His love?
What hallelujahs will His presence raise?
What, but one loud eternal burst of praise!


240
In loving kindness Jesus came

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Gabriel_Charles&#39;,this)">Charles Gabriel</a></div>
P.M.

IN loving kindness Jesus came
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My soul in mercy to reclaim,
And from the depths of sin and shame
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Through grace He lifted me.

c
From sinking sand He lifted me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With tender hand He lifted me,
From shades of night to plains of light,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Oh, praise His name, He lifted me!

He called me long before I heard,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Before my sinful heart was stirred,
But when I took Him at His word,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Forgiven, He lifted me. 

c
From sinking sand He lifted me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With tender hand He lifted me,
From shades of night to plains of light,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Oh, praise His name, He lifted me!

His brow was pierced with many a thorn,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His hands by cruel nails were torn,
When from my guilt and grief, forlorn,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In love He lifted me. 

c
From sinking sand He lifted me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With tender hand He lifted me,
From shades of night to plains of light,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Oh, praise His name, He lifted me!

Now on a higher plane I dwell,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And with my soul I know 'tis well;
Yet how or why, I cannot tell,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He should have lifted me.

c
From sinking sand He lifted me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With tender hand He lifted me,
From shades of night to plains of light,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Oh, praise His name, He lifted me!


241
In seasons of grief to my God I’ll repair
Previous BHB #99
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Price_John&#39;,this)">John Price</a></div>
P.M.

IN seasons of grief to my God I’ll repair,
When my heart is o’erwhelmed with sorrow and care;
From the ends of the earth to Thee will I cry,
Lead me to the Rock that is higher than I.

c
Higher than I, higher than I,
Lead me to the Rock that is higher than I.

When Satan, my foe, shall come in like a flood,
To drive my poor soul from the Fountain of good,
I’ll pray to the Saviour who meekly did die;
Lead me to the Rock that is higher than I.

c
Higher than I, higher than I,
Lead me to the Rock that is higher than I.

And while as a stranger I sojourn below,
Thy covenant blessings, Lord, freely bestow;
In affliction’s dark night to Thy throne let me fly,
Lead me to the Rock that is higher than I.

c
Higher than I, higher than I,
Lead me to the Rock that is higher than I.

And when I have ended my pilgrimage here, 
In Jesus' pure righteousness let me appear:
From the swellings of Jordan to Thee will I cry:
Lead me to the Rock that is higher than I.

c
Higher than I, higher than I,
Lead me to the Rock that is higher than I.

And when the last trumpet shall sound through the skies,
And the dead from the dust of the earth shall arise,
With the millions I’ll join, far above yonder sky,
To praise the Great Rock that is higher than I.

c
Higher than I, higher than I,
Lead me to the Rock that is higher than I.


242
In smooth and silken whiteness

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Ewan_Y_Isaac&#39;,this)">Isaac Y. Ewan</a></div>
7.6.7.6.D.

IN smooth and silken whiteness,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Without a rough'ning grain,
In clear, unbroken brightness,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Without a speck or stain,
The fine flour in its beauty
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The perfect man portrays 
In all His path of duty,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In all His heavenly ways.

In softness unresisting
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The rough and ruthless touch, 
In purity consisting
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As not another such,
In every feature flawless,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In every aspect fair,
The search of sinners lawless 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Could find no blemish there.

A wealth of heavenly glory
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Unfolds before our gaze: 
Remembering the story
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of all His wondrous ways, 
We muse in conscious weakness
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On gentleness and power, 
On lowliness and meekness - 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Frankincense and fine flour.

O God, His God and Father,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What shall we say to Thee?
Our hearts would incense gather
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And raise it rev'rently.
In worship thus ascending,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Thee our hymn we sing,
The theme our thoughts transcending,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy Son remembering.


243
In songs of praise, our God, to Thee

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Withy_John&#39;,this)">John Withy</a></div>
C.M.D.

IN songs of praise, our God, to Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We lift our voices high,
For each unfolded mystery,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of grace to us brought nigh:
Wondrous the love that first began,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In counsel deep to lay
Foundation sure for ruined man,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy glory to display.

We praise Thee for that gift divine -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Son, from off Thy throne,
Whose ways of truth and mercy shine
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In perfectness Thine own:
We praise Thee for the Holy Ghost,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sent forth to win a Bride
From out the lost - a ransomed host -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For Jesus glorifed.

Father of mercies, Thee we praise!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The glory Thine alone!
Thy Son, Thy Spirit, sent to raise
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lost sinners to a throne!
And this throughout the hosts above,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The joy and song shall be -
The Father's wondrous work of love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Redemption's mystery!


244
Is it Thy will that I should be?
Previous BHB #340<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Anonymous_Hymn_Writers&#39;,this)">Anonymous  (1888)</a></div>
L.M.

IS it Thy will that I should be
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Buried, in symbol, Lord, with Thee?
Owning Thee by this solemn sign,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Telling the world that I am Thine.

Gladly I yield obedience now;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In all things to Thy will I’d bow;
I’ll follow where my Saviour led,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And humbly in His footsteps tread.

This emblematic, watery grave
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shows forth His love, who  came to save;
And as I enter it, I see
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The price my Saviour paid for me.

Forth from Thy burial, Lord, I come,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For Thou hast triumphed o’er  the tomb;
Thy resurrection life I share,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My portion is no longer here.

O may I count myself to be
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Dead to the sins that wounded Thee,
Dead to the pleasures of this earth
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Unworthy of my heavenly birth.

Lord Jesus, when I gaze on Thee
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And all Thy radiant glory see,
That joy will far exceed the shame
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I bear on earth for Thy loved name.


245
It is a thing most wonderful

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;How_William&#39;,this)">William How</a></div>
L.M.

IT is a thing most wonderful,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Almost too wonderful to be,
That God’s own Son should come from heaven,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And die to save a child like me.

And yet I know that it is true:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He chose a poor and humble lot,
And wept and toiled, and mourned and died,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For love of those who loved Him not.

I cannot tell how He could love
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A child so weak and full of sin;
His love must be most wonderful,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;If He could die my love to win.

I sometimes think about His Cross,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And shut my eyes, and try to see
The cruel nails, and crown of thorns,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And Jesus crucified for me.

But even could I see Him die,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I could but see a little part
Of that great love, which, like a fire,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Is always burning in His heart.

It is most wonderful to know
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His love for me so free and sure;
But ’tis more wonderful to see
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My love for Him so faint and poor.

And yet I want to love Thee, Lord:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O light the flame within my heart,
And I will love Thee more and more
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Until I see Thee as Thou art!


246
It may be at morn
Previous BHB #106<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>

IT may be at morn, When the day is awaking,
When sunlight through darkness And shadow is breaking,
That Jesus will come In the fulness of glory
To receive from the world His own.

c
O Lord Jesus, how long? How long - 
Ere we shout the glad song?
Christ returneth! Hallelujah!

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hallelujah! Amen!

Will burst into light In the blaze of His glory
When Jesus receives His own.

c

Ere we shout the glad song?

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hallelujah! Amen!

With grace on His brow, Like a halo of glory
Will Jesus receive His own.

c

Ere we shout the glad song?
Christ returneth! Hallelujah!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hallelujah! Amen!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hallelujah! Amen!

O joy! O delight! Should we go without dying;
No sickness, no sadness, No dread, and no crying;
Caught up through the clouds With our Lord into glory
When Jesus receives His own.

c
O Lord Jesus, how long? How long - 
Ere we shout the glad song?

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hallelujah! Amen!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hallelujah! Amen!


247
It passeth knowledge, that dear love of Thine
Previous BHB #101
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Shekleton_Mary&#39;,this)">Mary Shekleton  (1863)</a></div>
10.10.10.10.4.

IT passeth knowledge, that dear love of Thine,
Lord Jesus, Saviour; yet this soul of mine
Would of Thy love, in all its breadth and length,
Its height and depth, its everlasting strength
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Know more and more.

It passeth telling, that dear love of Thine,
Lord Jesus, Saviour; yet these lips of mine
Would fain proclaim to sinners, far and near,
A love which can remove all guilt and fear,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And love beget.

It passeth praises, that dear love of Thine,
Lord Jesus, Saviour; yet this heart of mine
Would sing that love, so full, so rich, so free,
Which brings a rebel sinner, even me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Nigh unto God.

But though I cannot sing, or tell, or know
The fulness of Thy love while here below,
My empty vessel I may freely bring;
O Thou, who art of love the living  spring,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My vessel fill.

I am an empty vessel - not one thought,
Or look of love, I ever to Thee brought;
Yet I may come, and come again  to Thee,
With this, the empty sinner’s only plea -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou lovest me.

Then fill me, O my Saviour, with Thy love!
Lead, lead me to the living fount above;
Thither may I in simple faith draw  nigh,
And never to another fountain fly,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But unto Thee.

And when, Lord Jesus, Thine own face I see,
When at Thy lofty throne I bow the knee,
Then of Thy love, in all its breadth and length,
Its height and depth, its everlasting strength,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My soul shall sing.

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In dark Gethsemane;

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And suffered there for me.

c
Alone, alone, 
He bore it all alone;
He gave Himself to save His own,
He suffered, bled and died alone, alone.

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In Pilate’s judgment hall;
Alone the crown of thorns He wore
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Forsaken thus by all.

He gave Himself to save His own,
He suffered, bled and died alone, alone.

Alone upon the Cross He hung
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That others He might save;
Forsaken then by God and man
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Alone, His life He gave. 

c
Alone, alone, 
He bore it all alone;
He gave Himself to save His own,
He suffered, bled and died alone, alone.


249
Jehovah is our strength
Previous BHB #108
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Barnard_Samuel&#39;,this)">Samuel Barnard</a></div>
6.6.6.6.8.8.

JEHOVAH is our strength,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And He shall be our song;
We shall o’ercome at length,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Although our foes be strong: 
In vain then Satan doth oppose,
The Lord is stronger than His foes.

The Lord our refuge is,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And ever will remain;
Since He hath made us His
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He will our cause maintain:
In vain our enemies oppose,
For God is stronger than His foes.

The Lord our portion is;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What can we wish for more?
As long as we are His
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We never can be poor:
In vain do earth and hell oppose,
For God is stronger than His foes.

The Lord our Shepherd is;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He knows our ev’ry need;
And since we now are His,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His care our souls will feed:
In vain do sin and death oppose
For God is stronger than His foes.


250
Jesus calls us! o’er the tumult
Previous BHB #398<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Alexander_Cecil_Frances&#39;,this)">Cecil Frances (Humphreys) Alexander  (1852)</a></div>
8.7.8.7.

JESUS calls us! o’er the tumult
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of our life’s wild restless sea,
Day by day His sweet voice soundeth,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Saying, “Christian, follow Me”.

As of old, apostles heard it
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;By the Galilean lake,
Turned from home and toil and kindred,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Leaving all for His dear sake.

Jesus calls us from the worship
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of the vain world’s golden store,
From each idol that would keep us
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Saying, “Christian, love Me more”.

In our joys and in our sorrows,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Days of toil and hours of ease,
Still He calls, in cares and pleasures,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“Christian, love Me more than these”.

Jesus calls us! by Thy mercies,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Saviour, may we hear Thy call,
Give our hearts to Thine obedience,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Serve and love Thee best of all.


251
Jesus Christ, Thou King of Glory
Previous BHB #110<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Burlingham_Hannah_Killham&#39;,this)">Hannah Kilham Burlingham</a></div>
8.7.8.4.8.4.

JESUS CHRIST, Thou King of Glory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Born a Saviour-Prince to be,
While the angel-hosts adore Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We joy in Thee,
Singing of Thy grace the story,
Praise, praise to Thee.

Thou the bands of death didst sever,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Conflict Thine and victory;
God is for us now and ever;  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We joy in Thee.
We are Thine, Thine own for ever,  
Praise, praise to Thee.

Thou the ransom price hast given,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Setting thus the captive free;
Thou art Lord of earth and heaven;  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We joy in Thee.
Through Thy blood we stand forgiven,  
Praise, praise to Thee.

Risen Lord! at Thy returning
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sweet and full our song shall be;
Hasting to that blissful morning 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We joy in Thee.
Thou hast read our spirits’ yearning,  
Praise, praise to Thee.


252
Jesus, I am resting, resting
Previous BHB #400<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>

8.7.8.5.D.

JESUS, I am resting, resting
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In the joy of what Thou art;
I am finding out the greatness 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of Thy loving heart:
Thou hast bid me gaze upon Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And Thy beauty fills my soul,
For, by Thy transforming power,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou hast made me whole.

c
Jesus, I am resting, resting 
In the joy of what Thou art;
I am finding out the greatness 
Of Thy loving heart.

O how great Thy loving-kindness!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Vaster, broader than the sea:
O how marvellous Thy goodness
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lavished all on me!
Yes, I rest in Thee, Belovèd,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Know what wealth of grace is Thine,
Know Thy certainty of promise,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And have made it mine.

c
Jesus, I am resting, resting 
In the joy of what Thou art;
I am finding out the greatness 
Of Thy loving heart.

Simply trusting Thee, Lord Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I behold Thee as Thou art,
And Thy love, so pure, so changeless,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Satisfies my heart;
Satisfies its deepest longings,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Meets, supplies its every need,
Compasseth me round with blessings;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thine is love indeed.

c
Jesus, I am resting, resting 
In the joy of what Thou art;
I am finding out the greatness 
Of Thy loving heart.

Ever lift Thy face upon me
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As I work and wait for Thee,
Resting ’neath Thy smile, Lord Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Earth’s dark shadows flee;
Brightness of my Father’s glory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sunshine of my Father’s face,
Keep me ever trusting, resting;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Fill me with Thy grace.

c
Jesus, I am resting, resting 
In the joy of what Thou art;
I am finding out the greatness 
Of Thy loving heart.


253
Jesus in His heav’nly temple
Previous BHB #111<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Chapman_Robert_Cleaver&#39;,this)">Robert Cleaver Chapman</a></div>
8.7.8.7.

JESUS in His heav’nly temple,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sits with God upon the throne;
Now no more to be forsaken,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His humiliation gone.

Never more shall God, Jehovah,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Smite the Shepherd with the sword;
Ne’er again shall cruel sinners
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Set at nought our glorious Lord.

Dwelling in eternal sunshine
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of the countenance of God,
Jesus fills all heav’n with incense
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of His reconciling blood.

On His heart our names are graven;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On His shoulders we are borne;
Of our God beloved in Jesus
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We can love Him in return.


254
Jesus! in Thee our eyes behold

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Watts_Isaac&#39;,this)">Isaac Watts</a></div>
C.M.

JESUS! in Thee our eyes behold
A thousand glories more
Than the rich gems and polished gold
The sons of Aaron wore.

They first their own sin-offering brought,
To purge themselves from sin;
Thy life was pure, without a spot,
And all Thy nature clean.

Fresh blood, as constant as the day,
Was on their altar spilt;
But Thy one offering takes away
For ever all our guilt.

Their priesthood ran through several hands,
For mortal was their race;
Thy never-changing office stands,
Eternal as Thy days.

Their range was earth, nor higher soared;
The heaven of heavens is Thine:
Thy majesty and priesthood, Lord,
Through endless ages shine.


255
Jesus! in whom all glories meet

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Anonymous_Hymn_Writers&#39;,this)">Author Unknown</a></div>
L.M.

JESUS! in whom all glories meet,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Whose praise through earth and heaven shall ring,
Thy name, than all beside more sweet,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With hearts adoring would we sing.

Thou as our Paschal Lamb wast slain,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy blood has met the avenger’s eye;
Beneath that shelter we remain,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And keep the feast, nor fear, nor die.

A Priest for ever, Thou art there
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For us, the holiest within;
Our names upon Thy breast to bear,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Absolved from every charge of sin.

There, in the Father’s love we dwell,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Called by His grace His sons to be,
And now in songs adoring tell,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We owe it all, O Lord, to Thee!

257
Jesus is our Shepherd, wiping ev’ry tear
Previous BHB #109<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Stowell_Hugh&#39;,this)">Hugh Stowell</a></div>
11.11.11.11.

JESUS is our Shepherd, wiping ev’ry tear,
Folded in His bosom what have we to fear?
Only let us follow whither He doth lead,
To the thirsty desert, or the dewy mead.

Jesus is our Shepherd, well we know His voice!
How its gentlest whisper makes our hearts rejoice:
Even when He chideth, tender is His tone;
None but He shall guide us; we are His alone.

Jesus is our Shepherd; for the sheep He bled;
Ev’ry lamb is sprinkled with the blood He shed;
Then on each He setteth His own secret sign,
“They that have My Spirit, these”, saith He, “are Mine”.

Jesus is our Shepherd; guarded by His arm,
Though the wolves may ravin none can do us harm;
If we tread death’s valley, dark with fearful gloom,
We shall fear no evil, victors o’er the tomb.

Jesus is our Shepherd; with His goodness now,
And His tender mercy, He doth us endow.
Let us sing His praises with a gladsome heart
Till in heaven we meet Him, nevermore to part.


258
Jesus, keep me near the Cross

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Alystyne_Frances_Jane_Van&#39;,this)">Frances (Fanny) Jane Crosby</a></div>
P.M.

JESUS, keep me near the Cross,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;There a precious fountain
Free to all, a healing stream,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Flows from Calvary’s mountain.

c
In the Cross, in the Cross
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Be my glory ever;
Till my ransomed soul shall find
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Rest beyond the river.

Near the Cross, a trembling soul,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Love and mercy found me;
There the Bright and Morning Star,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shed its beams around me.

c
In the Cross, in the Cross
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Be my glory ever;
Till my ransomed soul shall find
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Rest beyond the river.

Near the Cross! O Lamb of God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Bring its scenes before me;
Help me walk from day to day,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With its shadows o’er me. 

c
In the Cross, in the Cross
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Be my glory ever;
Till my ransomed soul shall find
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Rest beyond the river.

Near the Cross I’ll watch and wait,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hoping, trusting ever,
Till I reach the golden strand
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Just beyond the river. 

c
In the Cross, in the Cross
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Be my glory ever;
Till my ransomed soul shall find
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Rest beyond the river.


259
Jesus, Lord, I need Thy presence
Previous BHB #399<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Goodman_George&#39;,this)">George Goodman</a></div>
8.7.8.7.7.7.

JESUS, Lord, I need Thy presence
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As I journey on my way,
For without Thee I am lonely,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And my feet are apt to stray;
But if Thou wilt walk with me
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Life will calm and holy be.

Jesus, Lord, I need Thy wisdom,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For perplexing problems press,
And without Thee I am foolish,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Nor can bear the strain and stress;
But if Thou wilt counsel me
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I shall true and upright be.

Jesus, Lord, I need Thy power,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For temptations come and go,
And without Thee I am helpless,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With no strength to meet the foe;
But if Thou wilt strengthen me
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Life will all-triumphant be.

Jesus, Lord, I need Thy guidance,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Fire by night, and cloud by day,
For without them I am sightless,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Groping for the proper way;
But if Thou dost lead me on
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I will follow Thee alone.

Jesus, Lord, Thy love so tender
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Is my greatest need of all,
For without Thee pride and anger
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From unguarded lips will fall;
But if Thou Thy love impart
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I shall have a gracious heart.


260
Jesus, Lord, I’m never weary
Previous BHB #113<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Thompson_Boethia&#39;,this)">Boethia Thompson</a></div>
8.7.8.7.4.7.

JESUS, Lord, I’m never weary
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Looking on Thy Cross and shame;
Gazing there I seem so near Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Dear to me each throb of pain.
Ever near Thee,
Ling’ring here I would remain.

Little cared I for the anguish
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of Thy bitter, bitter cry;
Left alone, Lord, there to anguish,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;None to share Thy parting sigh.  
All forsaken!
Left alone, O Lord, to die.

Jesus, Saviour, I have found Thee
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All my utmost need required;
In Thyself, Lord, Thou hast found me
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All Thy loving heart desired.  
I would praise Thee
From my soul by love inspired.

All my sins were laid upon Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All my guilt was on Thee laid,
And the blood of Thine atonement
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All my utmost debt has paid.  
Gracious Saviour,
I believe, for Thou hast said.

Thine almighty arms are round me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And my head is on Thy breast,
For my weary soul has found Thee
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Such a perfect, perfect rest.  
Gracious Saviour,
Now I know that I am blest.
