262
Jesus, Lord, we come together
Previous BHB #401
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Reynolds_Thomas_H&#39;,this)">Thomas Henry Reynolds</a></div>
8.7.8.7.

JESUS, Lord, we come together
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In the bonds of Thine own love;
Thou hast drawn our footsteps hither
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Its deep meaning now to prove.

Here together we recall Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In Thy presence break the bread;
Never more can grief befall Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou art risen from the dead.

But Thy love remains, that entered
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Into death to make us Thine;
In that death all love was centred;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thankful now we drink the wine.

Thou dost make us taste the blessing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Soon to fill a world of bliss;
And we bless Thy name, confessing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thine own love our portion is.

Sweet it is to sit before Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sweet to hear Thy blessèd voice,
Sweet to worship and adore Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;While our hearts in Thee rejoice.


263
Jesus, Lord, we know Thee present
Previous BHB #114
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Thompson_Boethia&#39;,this)">Boethia Thompson</a></div>
8.7.8.7.D.

JESUS, Lord, we know Thee present
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;At Thy table freshly spread,
Seated at Thy priceless banquet
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With Thy banner overhead.
Precious moments at Thy table
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From all fear and doubt set free;
Here to rest, so sweetly able,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Occupied alone with Thee.

Here rejoicing in Thy nearness,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Gladly by Thy Spirit led;
Calmly in the blest remembrance
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of Thy precious blood once shed.
Lord, we take each simple token
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In fond memory of Thee;
Muse upon Thy body broken,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And Thy blood shed on the tree.

Oh, what joy it is to see Thee, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In these chosen emblems here!
In the bread and wine of blessing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Bread to strengthen, wine to cheer!
Lord, behold us met together,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;One in Thee, our risen Head,
Thus we take the cup of blessing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thus we share the broken bread.

Lord, we know how true Thy promise
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To be with us where we meet,
When in Thy loved Name we gather
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To enjoy communion sweet;
Dearer still that looked-for promise,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To each waiting, yearning heart,
That with Thee we soon shall be, Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Yea, “for ever” where Thou art.


264
Jesus, Lover of my soul
Previous BHB #119
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Wesley_Charles&#39;,this)">Charles Wesley  (1740)</a></div>
7.7.7.7.D.

JESUS, Lover of my soul,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Let me to Thy bosom fly,
While the nearer waters roll,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;While the tempest still is high:
Hide me, O my Saviour, hide,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Till the storm of life is past;
Safe into the haven guide,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O receive my soul at last!

Other refuge have I none;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hangs my helpless soul on Thee;
Leave, ah! leave me not alone,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Still support and comfort me.
All my trust on Thee is stayed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All mine help from Thee I bring;
Cover my defenceless head
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With the shadow of Thy wing.

Thou, O Christ, art all I want, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;More than all in Thee I find;
Raise the fallen, cheer the faint,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Heal the sick, and heal the blind.
Just and holy is Thy name,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I am all unrighteousness!
Vile and full of sin I am, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou art full of truth and grace.

Plenteous grace with Thee is found,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Grace to cover all my sin;
Let the healing streams abound;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Make and keep me pure within.
Thou of life the fountain art;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Freely let me take of Thee;
Spring Thou up within my heart
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Now, and to eternity.


265
Now I belong to Jesus

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Clayton_Norman&#39;,this)">Norman Clayton</a></div><p>&copy; 1938 Renewed 1966 Wordspring Music LLC (Adm by Song Solutions www.songsolutions.org) All rights reserved. Used by permission.</p>
P.M.

JESUS, my Lord, will love me forever,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From Him no power of evil can sever,
He gave His life to ransom my soul;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Now I belong to Him.

c
Now I belong to Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus belongs to me,
Not for the years of time alone,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But for eternity.

Once I was lost in sin’s degradation,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus came down to bring me  salvation,
Lifted me up from sorrow and shame,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Now I belong to Him.

c
Now I belong to Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus belongs to me,
Not for the years of time alone,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But for eternity.

Joy floods my soul for Jesus has saved me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Freed me from sin that long had enslaved me;
His precious blood, He gave to redeem,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Now I belong to Him.

c
Now I belong to Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus belongs to me,
Not for the years of time alone,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But for eternity.


266
Jesus, our Lord, Thy worthiness we sing

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Lawrence_W&#39;,this)">W. Lawrence</a></div>
10.4.10.4.10.10

JESUS, our Lord, Thy worthiness we sing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy peerless fame.
Our sacrifice of praise to Thee we bring,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And bless Thy Name.
Thy glories fill our vision with delight,
And heart and voice to honour Thee unite.

Yet wider praise in Zion waits for Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Her Lord and King;
Creation too, in rest and liberty,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shall tribute bring;
Both heav’n and earth shall Thy dominion own,
And every tongue confess Thee Lord alone.

More blessèd still - Thine own peculiar joy
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy saints shall be,
Who then shall find their constant blest employ
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In praising Thee;
And with Thee, Lord, Thy glory shall behold,
While love delights its wonders to unfold.


267
Jesus, our Lord, with what joy we adore Thee
Previous BHB #402<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Champney_Henry_DArcy&#39;,this)">Henry d'Arcy Champney</a></div>
11.10.11.10.

JESUS, our Lord, with what joy we adore Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Chanting our praise to Thyself on the throne,
Blest in Thy presence, we worship before Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Own Thou art worthy, and worthy alone. 

Verily God, yet become truly human -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lower than angels - to die in our  stead;
How hast Thou, long promised Seed of the woman,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Trod on the serpent and bruisèd his head!

How didst Thou humble Thyself to be taken,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Led by Thy creatures, and nailed to the Cross?
Hated of men, and of God, too, forsaken,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shunning not darkness, the curse, and the loss. 

How hast Thou triumphed, and triumphed with glory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Battled death’s forces, rolled back every wave!
Can we refrain, then, from telling the story,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;How Thou art victor o’er death and the grave?

c
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;<i>[Optional chorus for tune "Only Remembered"]</i></i>
Lord, Thou art worthy; Lord, Thou art worthy;
Lord, Thou art worthy, and worthy alone!
Blest in Thy presence, we worship before Thee,
Own Thou art worthy, and worthy alone!


268
Jesus, our Saviour Thou and Lord

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Webley_Daniel&#39;,this)">Daniel Webley</a></div>
C.M.

JESUS, our Saviour Thou and Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;How precious is Thy Word!
To lowly and believing hearts
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What joy it doth afford!

Thy Word of pure, eternal truth
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shall yet unshaken stay,
When all that man has thought or planned,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Like chaff has passed away.

Thy Word, Lord, speaks to us of Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And Thine exceeding grace;
In it Thy thoughts and ways of love
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With wondering joy we trace.

Thy Word upon our daily path,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Its light divine doth shed;
By it our feet through Satan’s snares,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In safety may be led.

Oh, may it richly dwell within,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And mould our every thought;
And be each heart to Thy blest sway,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In full subjection brought!

Lord, by Thy Spirit teach and lead,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;While seated at Thy feet,
That we may in Thy holy will,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Stand perfect and complete. 


269
Jesus shall reign where’er the sun
Previous BHB #403
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Watts_Isaac&#39;,this)">Isaac Watts  (1719)</a></div>
L.M.

JESUS shall reign where’er the sun
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Doth his successive journeys run;
His kingdom stretch from shore to shore
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Till moons shall wax and wane no more.

For Him shall endless prayer be made,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And praises throng to crown His  head;
His name like sweet perfume shall rise
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With every morning sacrifice.

People and realms of every tongue
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Dwell on His love with sweetest  song;
And infant voices shall proclaim
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Their early blessings on His name.

Blessings abound where’er He reigns,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The prisoner leaps to lose his chains,
The weary find eternal rest,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And all the sons of want are blest.

Where He displays His healing power,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Death and the curse are known  no more;
In Him the sons of Adam boast
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;More blessings than their father  lost.

Let every creature rise and bring
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Peculiar honours to our King,
Angels descend with songs again,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And earth repeat the loud Amen.


270
Jesus! Source of life eternal
Previous BHB #115<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Homburg_Ernst_Christoph&#39;,this)">Ernst Christoph Homburg</a> tr/ad by <a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Burlingham_Hannah_Killham&#39;,this)">Hannah Kilham Burlingham</a></div>
P.M.

JESUS! Source of life eternal,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus! Author of our breath,
Victor o’er the hosts infernal
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;By defeat and shame and death.
Thou through deepest tribulation
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Deigned to pass for our salvation:
Thousand, thousand praises be,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lord of Glory, unto Thee.

Thou, O Son of God, wert bearing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Cruel mockings, hatred, scorn;
Thou, the King of Glory, wearing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For our sake, the crown of thorn;
Dying, Thou didst us deliver
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From the chains of sin for ever:
Thousand, thousand praises be,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Precious Saviour, unto Thee.

All the shame men heaped upon Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou didst patiently endure;
Not the pains of death too bitter
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our redemption to procure.
Wondrous Thy humiliation
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To accomplish our salvation:
Thousand, thousand praises be,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Precious Saviour, unto Thee.

Heartfelt praise and adoration,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Saviour, thus to Thee we give;
For Thy life humiliation,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For Thy death, whereby we live.
All the grief Thou wert enduring,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All the bliss Thou wert securing:
Evermore the theme shall be
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of thanksgivings, Lord, to Thee.


271
Jesus, the Lord our Righteousness!
Previous BHB #120
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Zinzendorf_Count_Nicolaus_Ludwig&#39;,this)">Nikolaus Ludwig von Zinzendorf</a><br>Translated by John Wesley</div>
L.M.

JESUS, the Lord our Righteousness!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our beauty Thou, our glorious dress;
Before the throne, when thus arrayed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With joy shall we lift up the head.

When from the dust of death I rise,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To claim my mansion in the skies;
E’en then shall this be all my plea,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“Jesus has lived and died for me”.

Bold shall we stand in that great day,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For who aught to our charge shall lay,
Since by Thy blood absolved we are
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From sin and guilt, from shame  and fear?

Thus Abraham, the friend of God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thus all the saints redeemed  by blood,
Saviour of sinners Thee proclaim,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And all their boast is in Thy Name.

This spotless robe the same appears, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When ruined nature sinks in years; 
No age can change its glorious hue,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The robe of Christ is ever new.

Till we behold Thee on Thy throne,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We boast in Thee, in Thee alone;
Our beauty this, our glorious dress,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus, the Lord our Righteousness!


272
Jesus, the name high over all

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Wesley_Charles&#39;,this)">Charles Wesley </a></div>
C.M.

JESUS, the name high over all,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In hell, or earth, or sky;
Angels and men before it fall,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And devils fear and fly.

Jesus, the name to sinners dear,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The name to sinners given;
It scatters all their guilty fear;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;It turns their hell to heaven.

Jesus the prisoner’s fetters breaks,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And bruises Satan’s head;
Power into strengthless souls He speaks,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And life into the dead.

Oh, that the world might taste and see
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The riches of His grace;
The arms of love that compass  me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Would all mankind embrace.

Thee I shall constantly proclaim,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Though earth and hell oppose;
Bold to confess Thy glorious Name
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Before a world of foes.

His only righteousness I show,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His saving truth proclaim:
’Tis all my business here below
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To cry, “Behold the Lamb!”

Happy, if with my latest breath
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I may but gasp His name;
Preach Him to all, and cry, in death,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“Behold, behold the Lamb!”


273
Jesus! the very thought of Thee
Previous BHB #137
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Bernard_of_Clairvaux&#39;,this)">Bernard of Clairvaux</a></div>
C.M.

JESUS! the very thought of Thee
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With sweetness fills my breast;
But sweeter far Thy face to see,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And in Thy presence rest.

Nor voice can sing, nor heart can frame,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Nor can the mem’ry find
A sweeter sound than Thy blest Name,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O Saviour of mankind.

O hope of every contrite heart,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O joy of all the meek,
To those who fall, how kind Thou art;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;How good to those who seek.

But what to those who find? Ah, this
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Nor tongue, nor pen can show;
The love of Jesus! what it is
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;None but His loved ones know.

Saviour, our only joy be Thou,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As Thou our crown wilt be;
Be Thou, O Lord, our glory now,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And through eternity.


274
Jesus, Thou joy of loving hearts!
Previous BHB #404
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Bernard_of_Clairvaux&#39;,this)">Bernard of Clairvaux</a> tr/ad by <a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Palmer_Dr_Ray&#39;,this)">Ray Palmer</a>  (12 c)</div>
L.M.

JESUS, Thou Joy of loving hearts!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou Fount of life! Thou Light of men!
From the best bliss that earth imparts,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We turn unfilled to Thee again.

Thy truth unchanged hath ever stood;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou savest those that on Thee call;
To them that seek Thee, Thou art good,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To them that find Thee, All in all!

We taste Thee, O Thou living Bread,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And long to feast upon Thee still;
We drink of Thee, the Fountain-head,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And thirst our souls from Thee  to fill.

Our restless spirits yearn for Thee
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Where’er our changeful lot is cast;
Glad, when Thy gracious smile we see;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Blest, when our faith can hold Thee fast.

Lord Jesus, ever with us stay! 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Make all our moments calm  and bright!
Chase the dark night of sin away,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shed o’er the world Thy holy light.


275
Jesus, Thy dying love I own

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Smith_Joseph_Denham&#39;,this)">Joseph Denham Smith</a></div>
L.M.

JESUS, Thy dying love I own - 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A love unfathomed and unknown!
All other love can measured be,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But not Thy dying love to me.

Oh, wonder to myself I am,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou loving, bleeding, suffering Lamb,
That I can scan the mystery o’er,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And not be moved to love Thee  more!

’Tis well, my Lord, that ’twas Thy love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Not mine, that brought Thee from  above;
And well that ’twas Thy bitter grief,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Not mine, that gave my soul relief.

Loved now, and ever on Thy throne,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Adored and loved, Thou timeless  One!
Thou wilt, through one eternal day,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The height and depth of love  display!


276
Jesus, Thy Name I love!
Previous BHB #245
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Deck_James_George&#39;,this)">James George Deck  (1841)</a></div>
6.4.6.4.6.6.6.4.

JESUS, Thy Name I love!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus, my Lord!
All other names above
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus, my Lord!
Thou, Lord, my all must be!
Nothing to please I see,
Nothing apart from Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus, my Lord!

Thou blessèd Son of God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus, my Lord!
Hast bought me with Thy blood,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus, my Lord!
Great was indeed Thy love,
All other loves above,
Love Thou didst clearly prove,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus, my Lord!

When unto Thee I flee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus, my Lord!
Thou wilt my refuge be,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus, my Lord!
Whom, then, have I to fear?
What trouble, grief or care?
Since Thou art ever near,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus, my Lord!

Soon Thou wilt come again,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus, my Lord!
I shall be happy then,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus, my Lord!
When Thine own face I see;
Then I shall like Thee be;
Then evermore with Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus, my Lord!


277
Jesus was slain for me, at Calvary

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Perfect_George&#39;,this)">George Perfect</a></div>
6.4.6.4.6.6.6.4.

JESUS was slain for me, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;At Calvary.
Crownèd with thorns was He,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;At Calvary.
There He in anguish died,
There from His opened side,
Poured forth the crimson tide,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;At Calvary.

Pardoned is all my sin,  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;At Calvary.
Cleansed is my heart within,  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;At Calvary.
Now robes of praise I wear,
Gone are my grief and care,
Christ bore my burdens there,  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;At Calvary.

Wondrous His love for me,  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;At Calvary.
Glorious His victory,  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;At Calvary.
Vanquished are death and hell,
Oh, let His praises swell,
Ever my tongue shall tell,  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of Calvary.


278
Join all the glorious names
Previous BHB #342
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Watts_Isaac&#39;,this)">Isaac Watts  (1709)</a></div>
6.6.6.6.8.8.

JOIN all the glorious names
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of wisdom, love, and power,
That mortals ever knew,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That angels ever bore:
All are too mean to speak Thy worth, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Too mean to set Thee, Saviour, forth. 

Great Prophet of our God!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our tongues would bless Thy Name;
By Thee the joyful news
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of our salvation came -
The joyful news of sins forgiven,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of hell subdued, of peace with  heaven.

But O what gentle terms, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What condescending ways
Doth our Redeemer use
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To teach His heav’nly grace!
Mine eyes with joy and wonder see
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What forms of love He bears  for me.

Be Thou our Counsellor, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our Pattern, and our Guide;
And through this desert land
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Still keep us near Thy side. 
Oh, let our feet ne’er run astray, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Nor rove, nor seek the crooked way!

We love our Shepherd’s voice;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His watchful eyes shall keep
Our wand’ring souls among 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The thousands of His sheep:
He feeds His flock, He calls their names,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His bosom bears the tender lambs.

Should all the hosts of death, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And pow’rs of hell unknown,
Put their most dreadful forms
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of rage and mischief on;
I shall be safe, for Christ displays
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Superior pow’r, and guardian grace.


279
Just as I am, Thine own to be
Previous BHB #405
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Farningham_Marianne&#39;,this)">Marianne "Farningham" Hearn  (1887)</a></div>
8.8.8.6.

JUST as I am, Thine own to be,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Friend of the young, who lovest me,
To consecrate myself to Thee, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O Jesus Christ, I come.

In the glad morning of my day,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My life to give, my vows to pay,
With no reserve and no delay, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With all my heart, I come.

I would live ever in the light,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I would work ever for the right,
I would serve Thee with all my might,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Therefore to Thee, I come.

Just as I am, young, strong, and free,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To be the best that I can be
For truth, and righteousness, and Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lord of my life, I come.

And for Thy sake to win renown,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And then to take the victor’s crown,
And at Thy feet to lay it down,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O Master, Lord, I come.


280
Kept, safely kept
Previous BHB #121<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Blane_William&#39;,this)">William Blane</a></div>
4.6.8.8.4.

KEPT, safely kept;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My fears away are swept;
In weakness to my God I cling,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Though foes be strong I calmly sing,
Kept, safely kept.

Kept by His power,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Whatever dangers lower
The strength of God’s almighty arm
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Doth shield my soul from every harm, 
Kept by His power.

Through simple faith,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Believing what He saith,
Unshaken on my God I lean,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And realise His power unseen, 
But known to faith.

Kept all the way,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;E’en to salvation’s day,
His mighty love ne’er cold shall wax,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Nor shall His pow’rful grasp relax, 
Through all the way.


281
King of my life, I crown Thee now

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Hussey_E_Jennie&#39;,this)">Jennie E. Hussey</a></div>

KING of my life, I crown Thee now;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thine shall the glory be;
Lest I forget Thy thorn-crowned brow,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lead me to Calvary.

c
Lest I forget Gethsemane,
Lest I forget Thine agony,
Lest I forget Thy love for me,
Lead me to Calvary.

Show me the tomb where Thou wast laid,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Tenderly mourned and wept;
Angels in robes of light arrayed
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Guarded Thee whilst Thou slept. 

c
Lest I forget Gethsemane,
Lest I forget Thine agony,
Lest I forget Thy love for me,
Lead me to Calvary.

Let me, like Mary, through the gloom,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Come with a gift to Thee;
Show to me now the empty tomb -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lead me to Calvary.

c
Lest I forget Gethsemane,
Lest I forget Thine agony,
Lest I forget Thy love for me,
Lead me to Calvary.

May I be willing, Lord, to bear
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Daily my cross for Thee;
Even Thy cup of grief to share -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou hast borne all for me 

c

Lest I forget Thine agony,
Lest I forget Thy love for me,
Lead me to Calvary.

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For all that know not Thee;
Then touch my lips with holy fire,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To speak of Calvary.

c
Lest I forget Gethsemane,
Lest I forget Thine agony,
Lest I forget Thy love for me,
Lead me to Calvary.


282
Lamb of God! our souls adore Thee
Previous BHB #122
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Deck_James_George&#39;,this)">James George Deck  (1841)</a></div>
8.7.8.7.D.

LAMB OF GOD! our souls adore Thee
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;While upon Thy face we gaze;
There the Father’s love and glory
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shine in all their brightest rays.
Thine almighty power and wisdom
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All creation’s works proclaim;
Heaven and earth alike confess Thee
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As the ever great I AM.

Lamb of God! Thy Father’s bosom
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Ever was Thy dwelling-place;
His delight, in Him rejoicing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;One with Him in power and grace:
Oh what wondrous love and mercy!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou didst lay Thy glory by
And for us didst come from heaven,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As the Lamb of God, to die.

Lamb of God! when we behold Thee
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lowly in the manger laid;
Wand’ring as a homeless stranger
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In the world Thy hands had made;
When we see Thee in the garden
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In Thine agony of blood;
At Thy grace we are confounded,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Holy, spotless Lamb of God.

When we see Thee, as the Victim,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Bound to the accursèd tree,
For our guilt and folly stricken,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All our judgment borne by Thee.
Lord, we own, with hearts adoring,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou hast loved us unto blood;
Glory, glory everlasting
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Be to Thee, Thou Lamb of God!

Lamb of God! Thou now art seated
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;High upon Thy Father’s throne;
All Thy gracious work completed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All Thy mighty vict’ry won.
Every knee in heaven is bending
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To the Lamb for sinners slain;
Every voice and harp is swelling,
“Worthy is the Lamb to reign”.

Lord, in all Thy pow’r and glory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Still Thy thoughts and eyes are here,
Watching o’er Thy ransomed people
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Thy gracious heart so dear.
Thou for us art interceding,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Everlasting is Thy love;
And a blessèd rest preparing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In the Father’s house above.

Lamb of God! Thou soon in glory
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Will to this sad earth return;
All Thy foes shall quake before Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All that now despise Thee mourn;
Then Thy saints appearing with Thee
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With Thee in Thy kingdom reign;
Thine the praise, and Thine the glory
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lamb of God, for sinners slain!


283
Let us love and sing and wonder!
Previous BHB #124<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Newton_John&#39;,this)">John Newton  (1774)</a></div>
8.7.8.7.7.7.

LET us love and sing and wonder!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Let us praise the Saviour’s Name!
He has hushed the law’s loud thunder,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He has quenched Mount Sinai’s flame;
He has washed us in His blood,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He has brought us nigh to God.

Let us love the Lord that bought us,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Pitied us when wand’ring far;
Called us by His grace, and taught us
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Where our joys and blessings are;
He has washed us in His blood,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He presents our souls to God.

Let us sing, though fierce temptations
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Threaten hard to bear us down;
For the Lord, our strong salvation,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Holds in view the conq’ror’s crown;
He who washed us in His blood,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Has secured our way to God.

Let us wonder; grace and justice
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Join, and point to mercy’s store;
When through grace in Christ our trust is,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Justice smiles, and asks no more;
He who washed us in His blood
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Soon will bring us home to God.


284
Let us rejoice in Christ the Lord
Previous BHB #125
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Newton_John&#39;,this)">John Newton  (1774)</a></div>
C.M.

LET us rejoice in Christ the Lord
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who claims us for His own;
The hope that’s built upon His Word
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Can ne’er be overthrown.

Though many foes beset us round,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And feeble is our arm,
Our life is hid with Christ in God
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Beyond the reach of harm.

Weak as we are, we shall not faint
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Or fainting, cannot fail;
Jesus, the strength of every saint,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Must in the end prevail.

Though now He’s unperceived by sense,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Faith sees Him always near;
A guide, a glory, a defence,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To save from every fear.

As surely as He overcame,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And conquered death and sin,
So surely those who trust His Name
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Will all His triumph win.


285
Light of the world, shine on our souls
Previous BHB #343
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Bickersteth_Edward&#39;,this)">Edward Bickersteth</a></div>
C.M.

LIGHT of the world, shine on our souls;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy grace to us afford;
And while we meet to learn Thy truth,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Be Thou our teacher, Lord.

As once Thou didst Thy Word expound, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To those who walked with Thee,
So teach us, Lord, to understand,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And its blest fulness see -

May we its riches, power, and depth 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Its holiness discern;
Its joyful news of saving grace
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;By blest experience learn.

Help us each other to assist; 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy Spirit now impart;
Keep humble, but with love inspire,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Thee and Thine, each heart.

Thus may Thy Word be dearer still,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And studied more each day;
And as it richly dwells within,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thyself in it display. 


286
Like a river, glorious is God’s perfect peace
Previous BHB #407
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Havergal_Frances_Ridley&#39;,this)">Frances Ridley Havergal  (©1876)</a></div>
6.5.6.5.D. with Chorus

LIKE a river, glorious
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Is God’s perfect peace,
Over all victorious
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In its bright increase,
Perfect, yet it floweth 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Fuller every day,
Perfect, yet it groweth
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Deeper all the way.

c
Stayed upon Jehovah, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hearts are fully blest;
Finding, as He promised, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Perfect peace and rest.

Hidden in the hollow
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of His blessèd hand,
Never foe can follow,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Never traitor stand;
Not a surge of worry,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Not a shade of care,
Not a blast of hurry
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Touch the spirit there.

c
Stayed upon Jehovah, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hearts are fully blest;
Finding, as He promised, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Perfect peace and rest.

Every joy or trial
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Falleth from above,
Traced upon our dial
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;By the Sun of love:
We may trust Him solely
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All for us to do;
They who trust Him wholly
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Find Him wholly true.

c
Stayed upon Jehovah, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hearts are fully blest;
Finding, as He promised, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Perfect peace and rest.


287
Lo, He comes! with clouds descending
Previous BHB #408
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Cennick_John&#39;,this)">John Cennick</a> tr/ad by <a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Wesley_Charles&#39;,this)">Charles Wesley</a> & <a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Madan_Martin&#39;,this)">Martin Madan</a>  (1752)</div>
8.7.8.7.4.7.

LO, He comes! with clouds descending,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Once for favoured sinners slain;
Thousand thousand saints attending
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Swell the triumph of His train;
Hallelujah! <i>[repeat]</i>
Jesus comes, and comes to reign!

Every eye shall now behold Him
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Robed in brightest majesty;
Those who set at naught and sold Him,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Pierced and nailed Him to the tree,  
Deeply wailing, <i>[repeat]</i>
Shall the true Messiah see.

Lo, the tokens of His passion
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Still His glorious body bears;
Cause of endless exultation
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To His ransomed worshippers;  
Hallelujah! <i>[repeat]</i>
Now the day of Christ appears.

Yea, Amen! let all adore Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;High on Thine eternal throne;
Saviour, take the power and glory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Claim the kingdom for Thine own;  
O come quickly! <i>[repeat]</i>
Hallelujah! come Lord, come!


288
Look, ye saints, the sight is glorious
Previous BHB #127<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kelly_Thomas&#39;,this)">Thomas Kelly</a></div>
8.7.8.7.4.7.

LOOK, ye saints, the sight is glorious;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;See the Man of Sorrows now;
From the fight returned victorious,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Every knee to Him shall bow:
Crown Him! Crown Him!
Crowns become the Victor’s brow.

Crown the Saviour, angels crown Him;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Rich the trophies Jesus brings;
In the seat of power enthrone Him,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;While the vault of heaven rings; 
Crown Him! Crown Him!
Crown the Saviour, King of kings.

Sinners in derision crowned Him;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Mocking thus the Saviour’s claim:
Saints and angels crowd around Him,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Own His title, praise His Name: 
Crown Him! Crown Him!
Spread abroad the Victor’s fame.

Hark, those bursts of acclamation!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hark! those loud triumphant chords!
Jesus takes the highest station;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O what joy the sight affords! 
Crown Him! Crown Him!
King of kings, and Lord of lords.


289
Loosed are the bands of death!

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kirkpatrick_William&#39;,this)">William Kirkpatrick</a></div>
6.6.4.6.6.6.4.

LOOSED are the bands of death!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“Thou art My Son”, God saith
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Jesus raised.
Holden He could not be,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;God’s Holy One is free, 
Firstfruit of victory;
Let God be praised!

Past are the shades of night, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Dawn shows in clearest light
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;An empty tomb.
Risen, the Lord appears, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Calms all His people’s fears, 
Stands in the midst and clears
Away their gloom.

Mighty, stupendous power! 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Unique, triumphant hour!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Alive is He!
Sure proof of righted wrong, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Earnest of ransomed throng,
Spoils taken from the strong
Eternally!

His Father’s glory too, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Enhanced with honour due
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That scene of joy.
So would we now acclaim, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And magnify the Name
Of Him, Whose deathless fame
Our lips employ.


290
Lord and Saviour, we remember

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Hocking_William&#39;,this)">William Hocking</a></div>
8.5.8.3.

LORD and Saviour, we remember,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In Thine hour of shame,
Thou to God Thyself didst render -
Praise Thy name!

Blessèd Saviour, we remember
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou didst meet our foe, 
When the darkness gathered 'round Thee,
And the woe.

Holy Saviour, we remember
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Bitter was Thy cry,
When, for sin by God forsaken,
Wrath was nigh.

Glorious Saviour, we remember 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou didst overcome;
Through Thy victory we, once captive,
Are brought home.

Lord and Saviour, we remember
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And would prize Thy love;
All its fulness do Thou teach us
From above!


291
Lord, e’en to death Thy love could go
Previous BHB #409
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Rossier_Dr_Henri_L&#39;,this)">Henri L. Rossier</a> tr/ad by <div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Wellesley_C_Anne&#39;,this)">C. A. Wellesley</a></div>
C.M.

LORD, e’en to death Thy love could go -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A death of shame and loss;
To vanquish for us every foe,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou didst endure the Cross!

Oh, what a load was Thine to bear,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Alone in that dark hour -
Our sins, in all their terror there,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;God’s wrath, and Satan’s power!

The storm that bowed Thy blessèd head
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Is hushed for ever now,
And rest divine is ours instead,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Whilst glory crowns Thy brow.

Within the Father’s house on high,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We soon shall sing Thy praise;
But here, where Thou didst bleed and die,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We learn that song to raise.


292
Lord, I desire to live as one

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Bancroft_Charitie_Lee&#39;,this)">Charitie Bancroft</a></div>
C.M.

LORD, I desire to live as one,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who bears a blood-bought name,
As one who fears but grieving Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And knows no other shame.

As one by whom Thy walk below
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Should never be forgot;
As one who fain would keep apart
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From all Thou lovest not.

Grant me to live as one who knows,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy fellowship of love;
As one whose eyes can pierce beyond
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The pearl-built gates above. 

As one who daily speaks to Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And hears Thy voice divine
With depths of tenderness declare,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“Belovèd! thou art Mine”.


293
Lord, in Thy form and comeliness

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Pitt_W_F&#39;,this)">F. W. Pitt</a></div>
L.M.

LORD, in Thy form and comeliness
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Father’s brightest glories shine,
Displaying perfect holiness,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Revealing all His love divine.

The world despised, rejected Thee;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We bow in worship, and confess
That all Thy sufferings on the tree
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Proclaim our sin and guiltiness.

Though our transgressions wounded Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy precious blood our pardon sealed;
And, bruised for our iniquity,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy stripes our sinful hearts have healed.

Ours was the guilt that kept us far
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From heaven, from happiness,  from Thee;
Thine was the blood that brought us near,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That cancelled sin, and set us free.

Thine was the blood, the precious blood,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That cleansed each stain and made us meet,
To worship in the courts of God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Redeemed and made in Thee  complete.


294
Lord Jesus, are we one with Thee?
Previous BHB #128
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Deck_James_George&#39;,this)">James George Deck  (1837)</a></div>
C.M.

LORD Jesus, are we one with Thee?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Oh height, oh depth of love!
Once slain for us upon the tree, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We’re one with Thee above. 

Such was Thy grace, that for our sake 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou didst from heaven come down;
With us of flesh and blood partake,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In all our sorrow one. 

Our sins, our guilt, in love divine,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Confessed and borne by Thee;
The gall, the curse, the wrath were Thine,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To set Thy members free.

Ascended now, in glory bright,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lord, one with us Thou art!
Nor life, nor death, nor depth, nor height,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy saints from Thee can part!

Oh, teach us, Lord, to know and own
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;This wondrous mystery,
That Thou with us art truly one,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And we are one with Thee!

Soon, soon shall come that glorious day,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When, seated on Thy throne,
Thou shalt to wondering worlds  display,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That we with Thee are one!


295
Lord Jesus Christ, we seek Thy face
Previous BHB #129<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Stewart_Alexander&#39;,this)">Alexander Stewart</a></div>
L.M.

LORD JESUS CHRIST, we seek Thy face,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Within the veil we bow the knee;
Oh, let Thy glory fill the place,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And bless us while we wait on Thee!

We thank Thee for the precious blood
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That purged our sins and brought us nigh,
All cleansed and sanctified, to God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy holy Name to magnify.

Shut in with Thee, far, far above
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The restless world that wars below,
We seek to learn and prove Thy love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy wisdom and Thy grace to know.

The brow that once with thorns was bound,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy hands, Thy side, we fain would see;
Draw near, Lord Jesus, glory-crowned,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And bless us while we wait on  Thee!


296
Lord Jesus, come!

297
Lord Jesus, Friend unfailing!
Previous BHB #131<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kuster_Samuel_Christian_Gottfried&#39;,this)">Samuel G. Küster</a></div>
7.6.7.6.D.

LORD JESUS, Friend unfailing!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;How dear Thou art to me!
Are cares or fears assailing?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I find my strength in Thee:
Why should my feet grow weary
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of this my pilgrim way?
Rough though the path and dreary,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;It ends in perfect day.

Nought, nought I count as pleasure,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Compared, O Christ, with Thee;
Thy sorrow without measure
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Earned peace and joy for me.
I love to own, Lord Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy claims o’er me divine;
Bought with Thy blood most precious
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Whose can I be but Thine?

What fills my heart with gladness?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;’Tis Thy abounding grace!
Where can I look in sadness,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But, Saviour, on Thy face?
My all is Thy providing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy love can ne’er grow cold;
In Thee, my refuge, hiding,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No good wilt Thou withhold.

Why should I droop in sorrow?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou’rt ever by my side!
Why, trembling, dread the morrow?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What ill can e’er betide?
If I my cross have taken,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;’Tis but to follow Thee;
If scorned, despised, forsaken,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Nought severs Thee from me.

O worldly pomp and glory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Your charms are spread in vain;
I’ve heard a sweeter story,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I’ve found a truer gain.
Where Christ a place prepareth
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;There is my loved abode,
There shall I gaze on Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;There shall I dwell with God.

From every tribulation,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From every sore distress,
In Christ I’ve full salvation,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sure help and quiet rest:
No fear of foes prevailing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I triumph, Lord, in Thee!
Lord Jesus, Friend unfailing!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;How dear Thou art to me.


298
Lord Jesus, gladly do our lips express

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Willey_T&#39;,this)">T. Willey</a></div>
10.10.10.10.

LORD Jesus, gladly do our lips express
Our hearts’ deep sense of all Thy worthiness;
Thou risen One, the Holy and the True,
We give Thee now the praise so justly due.

Thou giv’st us, Lord, once more to taste down here,
The joy Thy presence brings, its warmth and cheer;
With great delight we ’neath Thy shadow rest;
Thy fruit is sweet to those Thy love has blest.

Thou wast alone, till like the precious grain,
In death Thou layest, but did’st rise again;
And in Thy risen life, a countless host
Are “all of one” with Thee, Thy joy  and boast.

We bless Thee, Lord, Thou lov’st to take Thy place, 
Amongst Thine own, who taste Thy boundless grace;
’Tis here we learn Thee, as Thou’rt known above,
In heav’nly glory - home of perfect love.


299
Lord Jesus, I love Thee, I know Thou art mine
Previous BHB #134<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Featherstone_William_Ralph&#39;,this)">William Ralph Featherstone</a> & Adoniram J Gordon</div>
11.11.11.11.

LORD JESUS, I love Thee, I know Thou art mine,
My rock and my fortress, my Surety divine;
My gracious Redeemer, my song shall be now,
’Tis Thou who art worthy, Lord Jesus, ’tis Thou! 

I love Thee because Thou hast first lovèd me,
And purchased my pardon on Calvary’s tree;
I love Thee for wearing the thorns on Thy brow;
’Tis Thou who art worthy, Lord Jesus, ’tis Thou!

I would love Thee in life, I would love Thee in death,
And praise Thee as long as Thou lendest me breath,
And sing, should the death-dew lie cold on my brow,
’Tis Thou who art worthy, Lord Jesus, ’tis Thou!

And when the bright morn of Thy glory shall come,
And the children ascend to the Father’s glad home,
I’ll shout with Thy likeness impressed on my brow,
’Tis Thou who art worthy, Lord Jesus, ’tis Thou!


300
Lord Jesus, in Thy Name alone
Previous BHB #135<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Peters_Mary&#39;,this)">Mary Bowly Peters</a></div>
8.8.8.8.8.8

LORD JESUS, in Thy Name alone
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We soon shall meet around the throne;
With this sole claim, on this sole ground,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy table here we now surround;
What can we mention to our God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Except Thine own most precious blood?

O Jesus, Lord! there is, indeed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Enough in Thee to meet our need,
Enough in Thee to make us glad!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Why should Thy ransomed ones  be sad?
This hope have we before our God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Salvation through Thy precious  blood.

What joy it is to walk with Thee!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But O what joy Thy face to see!
And when our bliss is all complete,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We still shall worship at Thy feet,
And mention nothing to our God
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But that same ever precious  blood.


301
Lord Jesus, my Saviour! How vast Thy love to me
Previous BHB #133<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Anonymous_Hymn_Writers&#39;,this)">Anonymous</a></div>
7.6.7.6.D.

LORD JESUS, my Saviour!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;How vast Thy love to me!
I’ll bathe in its full ocean
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To all eternity;
And wending on to glory
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;This all my song shall be,
I am a feeble sinner,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But Jesus died for me.

O Calvary! O Calvary!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The thorn-crown and the spear,
’Tis here Thy love, Lord Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy flowing wounds appear.
O depths of grace and mercy!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To those dear wounds I flee;
I am a feeble sinner,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But Jesus died for me.

Adore Him! Adore Him!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The glorious work is done;
The Father will not punish me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;’Twas laid upon His Son.
’Tis finished, cried His suff’ring soul,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And I my title see;
I am a feeble sinner,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But Jesus died for me.

In glory, in glory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For ever with the Lord,
I’ll tune my harp, and with the saints
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I’ll sing with sweet accord;
And as I strike the golden strings
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;This all my song shall be,
I was a feeble sinner,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But Jesus died for me.


302
Lord Jesus, Thine
Previous BHB #138<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Midlane_Albert&#39;,this)">Albert Midlane</a></div>
4.6.8.8.4.

LORD JESUS, Thine;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No more this heart of mine
Shall seek its joy apart from Thee;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The world is crucified to me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And I am Thine.

Thine, Thine alone,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My joy, my hope, my crown;
Now earthly things may fade and die,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;They charm my soul no more, for I 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Am Thine alone.

Thine, ever Thine,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For ever to recline
On love eternal, fixed and sure;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Yes, I am Thine for evermore,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lord Jesus, Thine.

Then let me live,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Continual praise to give
To Thy dear Name, my precious Lord;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Henceforth alone, beloved, adored,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Thee I’d live.

Till Thou shalt come
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And bear me to Thy home,
For ever freed from earthly care,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Eternally Thy love to share:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lord Jesus, come!


303
Lord Jesus, Thou by whom the worlds were made

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Hayward_M_A&#39;,this)">A. M. Hayward</a></div>
10.10.10.10.

LORD Jesus, Thou by whom the worlds were made,
As holy Babe wast in a manger laid;
Yet did angelic hosts Thy title own,
And highest glory give to God alone.

Thy lowly pathway grace and truth displayed,
But wicked hands Thy brow with thorns arrayed;
Now glory-crowned, blest answer to Thy shame,
Thy saints with joy Thy worthiness acclaim.

To win Thy bride what depths of woe were Thine,
Scorned and betrayed, beset by powers malign!
Thy piercèd hands, Thy riven side revealed
The strength of love that by Thy death was sealed.

Most blessèd Lord, fain would we learn of Thee
To fill our part, yea, more like Thee to be;
With purpose fixed, Thy path our happy choice,
Thyself our all, until we hear Thy voice.


304
Lord Jesus, Thou who only art
Previous BHB #140<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Anonymous_Hymn_Writers&#39;,this)">Anonymous</a></div>
8.8.8.8.8.8.

LORD JESUS, Thou who only art
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The endless source of purest joy,
Oh, come and fill this longing heart;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;May nought but Thee my thoughts employ;
Teach me on Thee to fix my eye,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For none but Thee can satisfy.

The joys of earth can never fill
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The heart that’s tasted of Thy love;
No portion would I seek until
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I reign with Thee, my Lord, above,
When I shall gaze upon Thy face,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And know more fully all Thy grace.

When from Thy radiant throne on high
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou didst my fall and ruin see,
Thou cam’st on earth for me to die
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That I might share that throne  with Thee.
Loved with an everlasting love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My hopes, my joys are all above.

Oh, what is all that earth can give?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I’m called to share in God’s own joy;
Dead to the world, in Thee I live,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In Thee I’ve bliss without alloy:
Well may I earthly joys resign;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All things are mine, and I am Thine!

Till Thou shalt come to take me home,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Be this my one ambition, Lord,
Self, sin, the world, to overcome,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Fast clinging to Thy faithful Word;
More of Thyself each day to know,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And more into Thine image grow.


305
Lord Jesus, to tell of Thy love
Previous BHB #142<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Haweis_Dr_Thomas&#39;,this)">Thomas Haweis</a></div>
8.8.8.8.

LORD JESUS, to tell of Thy love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our souls shall for ever delight,
And join with the blessèd above
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In praises by day and by night.

Wherever we follow Thee, Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Admiring, adoring, we see
That love which was stronger than death
Flow out without limit, and free.

Descending from glory on high,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With men Thy delight was to dwell;
Contented, our Surety to die,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;By dying to save us from hell.

Enduring the grief and the shame,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And bearing our sins on the Cross,
Oh, who would not boast of Thy love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And count the world’s glory but  dross!


306
Lord, speak to me, that I may speak
Previous BHB #410
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Havergal_Frances_Ridley&#39;,this)">Frances Ridley Havergal  (1872)</a></div>
L.M.

LORD, speak to me, that I may speak
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In living echoes of Thy tone;
As Thou hast sought, so let me seek,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy erring children lost and lone.

Oh, lead me, Lord, that I may lead
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The wand’ring and the  wav’ring feet;
Oh, feed me, Lord, that I may feed
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy hung’ring ones with  manna sweet.

Oh, strengthen me, that while I stand
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Firm on the Rock, and strong in  Thee,
I may stretch out a loving hand
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To wrestlers with the troubled sea.

Oh, teach me, Lord, that I may teach
The precious things Thou dost impart;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And wing my words, that they  may reach
The hidden depths of many a heart.

Oh, give Thine own sweet rest to me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That I may speak with soothing  power
A word in season, as from Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To weary ones in needful hour.

Oh, fill me with Thy fulness, Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Until my very heart o’erflow
In kindling thought and glowing word,
Thy love to tell, Thy praise to show.

Oh, use me, Lord, use even me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Just as Thou wilt, and when,  and where;
Until Thy blessèd face I see,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy rest, Thy joy, Thy glory share.


307
Lord, Thy ransomed Church is waking

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Stock_Sarah_Geraldine&#39;,this)">Sarah Geraldina Stock</a></div>
8.7.8.7.D.

LORD, Thy ransomed Church is waking
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Out of slumber far and near,
Knowing that the morn is breaking
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When the Bridegroom shall appear;
Waking up to claim the treasure
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With Thy precious life-blood bought,
And accept in fuller measure
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All Thy wondrous death hath wrought.

Praise to Thee for this glad shower,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Precious drops of latter rain;
Praise, that by Thy Spirit’s power
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou hast quickened us again,
That Thy gospel’s priceless treasure
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Now is borne from land to land,
And that all the Father’s pleasure
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Prospers in Thy piercèd hand.

Praise to Thee for saved ones yearning
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O’er the lost and wandering throng;
Praise for voices daily learning
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To upraise the glad, new song;
Praise to Thee for sick ones hasting
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Now to touch Thy garment’s hem;
Praise for souls believing, tasting
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All Thy love has won for them.

Set on fire our hearts’ devotion
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With the love of Thy dear name;
Till o’er every land and ocean
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lips and lives Thy Cross proclaim:
Fix our eyes on Thy returning,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Keeping watch till Thou shalt come;
Loins well girt, lamps brightly burning:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Then, Lord, take Thy servants home!


308
Lord, Thy word abideth
Previous BHB #412<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Baker_Sir_Henry_Williams&#39;,this)">Henry Williams Baker  (1861)</a></div>
6.6.6.6.

LORD, Thy word abideth,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And our footsteps guideth;
Who its truth believeth
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Light and joy receiveth.

When our foes are near us,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Then Thy word doth cheer us,
Word of consolation,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Message of salvation.

When the storms are o’er us,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And dark clouds before us,
Then its light directeth,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And our way protecteth.

Who can tell the pleasure,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who recount the treasure,
By Thy word imparted
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To the simple-hearted?

Word of mercy, giving
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Succour to the living;
Word of life, supplying
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Comfort to the dying.

O that we, discerning
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Its most holy learning,
Lord, may love and fear Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Evermore be near Thee.


309
Lord! to Thee my heart ascending
Previous BHB #139<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Codner_Elizabeth&#39;,this)">Elizabeth Harris Codner</a></div>
8.7.8.7.3.

LORD! to Thee my heart ascending
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For Thy mercy full and free,
Thankful sings for grace transcending,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Grace vouchsafed to sinful me,
Even me.
<i>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;[Even me. 
Grace vouchsafed to sinful me.]</i>

Holy Father! who with yearning
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of eternal love didst see
Hatred in my bosom burning,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou didst give Thy Son for me,  
Even me.
<i>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;[Even me. 
Thou didst give Thy Son for me.]</i>

Precious Saviour! Great Redeemer!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Praise, eternal praise to Thee;
Though so long a wand’ring sinner
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou hast kindly welcomed me,  
Even me.
<i>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;[Even me. 
Thou hast kindly welcomed me.]</i>

But I’m lost in joyful wond’ring,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And I say, O can it be
That there will be no more sund’ring
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;’Twixt my blessèd Lord and me?  
Even me.
<i>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;[Even me. 
’Twixt my blessèd Lord and me?]</i>

Can it be that I, an alien,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Now a child shall ever be?
Can it be that, all forgiven,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Glory is prepared for me?  
Even me.
<i>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;[Even me. 
Glory is prepared for me.]</i>

Yes! for Jesus liveth ever,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And His blood hath made me free;
From His love no foe can sever,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For He gave Himself for me,  
Even me.
<i>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;[Even me. 
For He gave Himself for me.]</i>

Lord! I thank Thee for salvation,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Grace so mighty and so free;
Take my all in consecration,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Glorify Thyself in me,  
Even me.
<i>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;[Even me. 
Glorify Thyself in me.]</i>


310
Lord, we are Thine: our God Thou art
Previous BHB #143<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Deck_James_George&#39;,this)">James George Deck  (1841)</a></div>
L.M.D. 

LORD, we are Thine: our God Thou art,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Fashioned and made we are by Thee;
These curious frames! in every part
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy wisdom, power, and love we see;
Each breath we draw, each pulse that beats,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Each organ formed by skill divine,
Each precious sense aloud repeats
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Great God, that we are only Thine.

Lord, we are Thine: in Thee we live,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Supported by Thy tender care;
Thou dost each hourly mercy give;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thine earth we tread, we breathe  Thine air;
Raiment and food Thou dost supply;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy sun’s bright rays around us  shine:
Guarded by Thine all-seeing eye,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We own that we are only Thine.

Lord, we are Thine: bought by Thy blood:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Once the poor guilty slaves of sin;
Thou didst redeem us to our God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And made Thy Spirit dwell within.
Thou hast our sinful wand’rings  borne
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With love and patience all divine:
As brands, then, from the burning  torn
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We own that we are wholly Thine.

Lord, we are Thine: Thy claims we own,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Ourselves to Thee we humbly give;
Reign Thou within our hearts alone,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And make us to Thy glory live.
Here let us each Thy mind display,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In all Thy brilliant virtues shine;
And haste that long-expected day
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When Thou shalt own that we are Thine.


311
Lord, we love to trace Thy footprints

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Bevan_Emma_Frances&#39;,this)">Emma Frances Shuttleworth Bevan</div>
8.7.8.7.D. 

LORD, we love to trace Thy footprints
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Here amidst the desert sand,
Ponder o’er Thy path of suffering -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Wondrous heart and healing hand;
See Thy stoop to Bethlehem’s manger, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hear Thee still the raging wave,
Learn Thy love in all its fulness 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;At the Cross and in the grave.

Lord, we bow in adoration
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As we watch that stream of love;
Find its mighty tide still flowing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From Thee now as crowned above.
’Tis a love no heart can fathom,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Which to us Thou dost unfold,
Inexhaustible and boundless - 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Wondrous theme that ne’er grows old.


312
Lord, we treasure with affection

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Biggs_W_Malcolm&#39;,this)">Malcolm W. Biggs</a></div>
8.7.8.7.

LORD, we treasure with affection
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All Thy path of sorrow here,
And those closing scenes of anguish
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To our hearts Thyself endear.

Deep Thy sorrow then, Lord Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Deeper far than thought can reach;
Grief intense and suff’rings holy,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Far beyond all tongues to teach.

None could follow there, blest Saviour,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When Thou didst for sin atone,
For those suff’rings, deep, unfathomed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Were, Lord Jesus, Thine alone!

Thou didst measure then sin’s distance,
Darkness, wrath and curse were Thine;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Man-betrayed, by God forsaken;
Thus we learn Thy love divine!


313
Lord, we would ne’er forget Thy love
Previous BHB #148<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Deck_James_George&#39;,this)">James George Deck  (1841)</a></div>
L.M.

LORD, we would ne’er forget Thy love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who hast redeemed us by Thy blood;
And now, as our High Priest above,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Dost intercede for us with God.

Lord, we would not forget the pain,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The blood-like sweat, the shameful  tree,
The wrath Thy soul did once sustain
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From sin and death to set us free.

We would remember we are one
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With every saint that loves Thy Name;
United to Thee on the throne,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our life, our hope, our Lord the  same.

Here, in the broken bread and wine,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We hear Thee say, “Remember Me!
I gave My life to ransom thine, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I bore the wrath to set thee free”.

Lord, we are Thine, we praise Thy love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;One with Thy saints, all one in Thee;
We would, until we meet above,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In all our ways remember Thee.


314
Lord, when I think upon the love

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Midlane_Albert&#39;,this)">Albert Midlane</a></div>
C.M.D.

LORD, when I think upon the love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Which Thou to me hast shown,
To die upon the Cross that Thou,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Mayest claim me for Thine own,
I cannot tell why Thou didst show,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Such love to one like me,
Save that it is that I might know
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I owe it all to Thee.

There is no goodness in myself,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To win such precious love;
I loved Thee not - Thou lovest me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And called me from above:
I heard Thy voice, it won my heart,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And bade my doubtings flee;
It gave me rest and peace - O, yes,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I owe it all to Thee.

And still upheld by power divine,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I urge my way along,
In haste to reach the promised rest,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The bright, glad home of song,
And then when glory on me bursts, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And I Thy glory see,
Again I’ll raise the happy song,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“I owe it all to Thee”.


315
Love bound Thee to the altar

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Anonymous_Hymn_Writers&#39;,this)">Author Unknown</a></div>
7.6.7.6.D.

LOVE bound Thee to the altar,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Father’s love and Thine,
For us, O peerless Victim,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That we with Thee might shine.
Thy wealth Thou didst surrender,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thyself didst freely give,
Thy life in grace unbounded,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That we with Thee might live.

In those long hours of anguish,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When men passed scoffing by,
When Satan sore assailed Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When God heard not Thy cry,
Then were our sins Thy burden,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our guilt, our grief, Thy bands;
And then our names were graven
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For ever on Thy hands.

We praise Thee, Lord most holy,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou First-born from the dead;
Now o’er all powers triumphant,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O’er all things Lord and Head;
But ever and for ever
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The memory of Thy pain
Shall raise our songs in worship,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For Thou for us wast slain.


316
Love divine, all loves excelling
Previous BHB #413<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Wesley_Charles&#39;,this)">Charles Wesley  (1747)</a></div>
8.7.8.7.D.

LOVE divine, all loves excelling,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Joy of heaven, to earth come down;
Making us Thy humble dwelling,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All Thy faithful mercies crown:
Jesus, Thou art all compassion;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Pure, unbounded love Thou art,
Visit us with Thine affection,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Enter every longing heart.

Come, Almighty, to deliver!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Let us all Thy grace receive,
Follow in Thy steps, and never,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Nevermore the pathway leave:
Then our hearts will yield their blessing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sweet incense to God above,
Offer praises without ceasing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Glory in Thy perfect love.

Firstfruits of Thy new creation,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Faithful, holy, may we be,
Joyful in Thy great salvation,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Daily more conformed to Thee:
Changed from glory into glory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Till in heaven we take our place,
Then to worship and adore Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lost in wonder, love, and praise.


317
Love divine, so great and wondrous

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Blom_Frederick&#39;,this)">Frederick Blom and Nathaniel Carlson</a></div>
8.7.8.7. with Chorus

LOVE divine, so great and wondrous,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Deep and mighty, pure, sublime!
Coming from the heart of Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Just the same through tests of time.

c
He the pearly gates will open,
So that I may enter in;
For He purchased my redemption,
And forgave me all my sin.

Love divine, so great and wondrous,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All my sins He then forgave!
I will sing His praise forever,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For His blood, His pow’r to save. 

c
He the pearly gates will open,
So that I may enter in;
For He purchased my redemption,
And forgave me all my sin.


318
Loved with everlasting love

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Robinson_Wade_G&#39;,this)">G. Wade Robinson</a></div>
7.7.7.7.D.

LOVED with everlasting love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Led by grace that love to know;
Spirit, breathing from above,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou hast taught me it is so!
Oh, this full and perfect peace!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Oh, this transport all divine!
In a love which cannot cease,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I am His, and He is mine.
In a love which cannot cease,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I am His, and He is mine.

Heaven above is softer blue,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Earth around is sweeter green!
Something lives in every hue
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Christless eyes have never seen:
Birds with gladder songs o’erflow,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Flowers with deeper beauties shine,
Since I know, as now I know,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I am His, and He is mine.
Since I know, as now I know,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I am His, and He is mine.

Things that once were wild alarms
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Cannot now disturb my rest;
Closed in everlasting arms,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Pillowed on the loving breast,
Oh, to lie forever here,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Doubt, and care, and self resign,
While He whispers in my ear -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I am His, and He is mine.
While He whispers in my ear -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I am His, and He is mine.

His for ever, only His;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who the Lord and me shall part?
Ah, with what a rest of bliss
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Christ can fill the loving heart!
Heaven and earth may fade and flee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;First-born light in gloom decline;
But, while God and I shall be,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I am His, and He is mine.
But, while God and I shall be,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I am His, and He is mine.


319
Love’s immensity behold

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kirkpatrick_William&#39;,this)">William Kirkpatrick</a></div>
7.6.7.6.7.7.

LOVE’S immensity behold:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O’er His suff’rings ponder, 
Whose perfections deep are told
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;At Golgotha yonder.
God’s Beloved upon the tree, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Bruised and wounded there we see.

Man of sorrows in this scene,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And with grief acquainted, 
In the midst of men unclean,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Blameless and untainted. 
What He thus in love endures, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Glory to His God secures.

For His Father’s pleasure still,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Freely now He layeth 
Down His life of His own will,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Unto death obeyeth. 
Infinite devotion sealed! 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Love’s immensity revealed!


320
Low in the grave He lay

Waiting the coming day,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus, my Lord!

c

With a mighty triumph o’er His foes;
He arose a victor from the dark domain,
And He lives for ever with His saints to reign;

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hallelujah! Christ arose!

Vainly they seal the dead,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus, my Lord!

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus, my Lord!

c

He arose a victor from the dark domain,
And He lives for ever with His saints to reign;
