321
Low on the ground the Lord of Glory lies

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Ewan_Y_Isaac&#39;,this)">Isaac Y. Ewan</a> and <a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Tait_S_M_J&#39;,this)">J. M. S. Tait</a></div>
10.10.10.10.10.12.

LOW on the ground the Lord of Glory lies, 
Around Him rolls dark sorrow’s fearful flood,
Forth from His soul prayer pours with tears and cries,
Falls to the sod His sweat, like drops of blood.
The awful cup He takes, submissive still,
To drink the last dark drop - it is the Father’s will.

Silent He stands amid the mocking throng,
The thorny crown upon His holy brow,
In patient grace, content to suffer wrong -
It is the Father’s pleasure for Him now.
Meekness majestical! submissive still,
In robe, and reed, and thorn, to do the Father’s will.

Meekly He bows His sacred head to die;
The agony, the shame, are almost o’er,
The parchèd lips have framed their last lone cry,
The tender heart of Christ need bear no more.
But yet He bows the head; submissive still,
E’en in the hour of death, to all the Father’s will.


322
Majestic sweetness sits enthroned
Previous BHB #414
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Stennett_Samuel&#39;,this)">Samuel Stennett  (1787)</a></div>
C.M.

MAJESTIC sweetness sits enthroned
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Upon the Saviour’s brow;
His head with radiant glories crowned,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His lips with grace o’erflow.

Behold the beauties of His face,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And on His glories dwell;
Think of the wonders of His grace, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And all His triumphs tell.

No mortal can with Him compare
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Among the sons of men;
Fairer is He than all the fair
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That fill the heavenly train.

He saw me plunged in deep distress,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He came to my relief;
For me He bore the shameful Cross,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And carried all my grief.

To Him I owe my life and breath,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And all the joys I have;
He makes me triumph over death,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He saves me from the grave.

To heaven, the place of His abode, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He’ll bring my weary feet,
Display the glories of my God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And make my joy complete.

Since from His bounty I receive
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Such proofs of love divine,
Had I a thousand hearts to give,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lord, they should all be Thine!


323
Man of Sorrows! what a name
Previous BHB #147<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Bliss_Philip_P&#39;,this)">Philip P. Bliss  (1875)</a></div>
7.7.7.8.

MAN of Sorrows!” what a name
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For the Son of God, who came
Ruined sinners to reclaim!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hallelujah! what a Saviour!

Bearing shame and scoffing rude,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In my place condemned He stood;
Sealed my pardon with His blood: 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hallelujah! what a Saviour!

Guilty, vile, and helpless, we;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Spotless Lamb of God was He:
“Full atonement”, can it be? 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hallelujah! what a Saviour!

“Lifted up” was He to die,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“It is finished”, was His cry;
Now in heaven exalted high: 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hallelujah! what a Saviour!

When He comes, our glorious King,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All His ransomed home to bring,
Then anew this song we’ll sing: 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hallelujah! what a Saviour!


324
Man’s day is fast receding

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Anonymous_Hymn_Writers&#39;,this)">Author Unknown</a></div>
7.6.7.6.D.

MAN’S day is fast receding,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The day of God will come,
And lingering feet are needing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Oft to be speeded home;
We need to stir affection,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Dull conscience to awake,
Faith’s shield for our protection
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With firmer grasp to take.

The world hath many a wonder,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And many a witching snare;
But see the glory yonder -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What can with that compare?
The Lord a crown is keeping
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For all who faithful stand,
Who, midst a world that’s sleeping,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Watch for the day at hand.

Our labour and our pleasure
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Be this - to do His will;
To fill our little measure
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With loving service still:
The cup of water given
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For Him, will find reward,
Both now and soon in heaven,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Remembered by the Lord.

Lord, may Thy love constrain us
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Through all the “little while”,
Nor fear of man restrain us,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Nor love of praise beguile;
Then, at Thy glorious coming,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Enough, O Lord, if we,
Shall hear Thy voice approving
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Aught we have done for Thee.


325
Master, speak! Thy servant heareth
Previous BHB #415<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Havergal_Frances_Ridley&#39;,this)">Frances Ridley Havergal  (1869)</a></div>
8.7.8.7.7.7.

MASTER, speak! Thy servant heareth,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Waiting for Thy gracious word,
Longing for Thy voice that cheereth,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Master, let it now be heard:
I am listening, Lord, for Thee;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What hast Thou to say to me?

Master, speak! though least and lowest,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Let me not unheard depart;
Master, speak! for oh Thou knowest
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All the yearning of my heart:
Knowest all its truest need;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Speak! and make me blest indeed.

Master, speak! and make me ready
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When Thy voice is truly heard,
With obedience glad and steady
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Still to follow every word:
I am listening, Lord, for Thee:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Master, speak, O speak to me!

Speak to me by name, O Master!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Let me know it is to me;
Speak! that I may follow faster
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With a step more firm and free,
Where the Shepherd leads the flock
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In the shadow of the rock.


326
May the mind of Christ my Saviour
Previous BHB #416
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Wilkinson_Kate_Barcley&#39;,this)">Kate Barclay Wilkinson</a></div>
8.7.8.5.

MAY the mind of Christ my Saviour
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Live in me from day to day,
By His love and power controlling
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All I do and say.

May the word of God dwell richly
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In my heart from hour to hour,
So that all may see I triumph 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Only through His power.

May the peace of God my Father
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Rule my life in everything,
That I may be calm to comfort 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sick and sorrowing.

May the love of Jesus fill me
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As the waters fill the sea;
Him exalting, self abasing, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;This is victory.

May I run the race before me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Strong and brave to face the foe,
Looking only unto Jesus 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As I onward go.

May His beauty rest upon me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As I seek the lost to win,
And may they forget the channel, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Seeing only Him.


327
Meeting in the Saviour’s Name
Previous BHB #149<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kelly_Thomas&#39;,this)">Thomas Kelly</a></div>
7.7.7.7.7.7.

MEETING in the Saviour’s Name,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“Breaking bread” by His command,
To the world we thus proclaim
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On what ground we hope to stand,
When the Lord shall come with clouds,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Joined by heaven’s exulting crowds.

From the Cross our hope we draw
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;’Tis the sinner’s sure resource;
Jesus magnified the law,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus bore its awful curse;
What a joyful truth is this!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O how full of hope it is!

Jesus died, and then arose;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Yes, He rose, He lives to reign;
He will vanquish all His foes
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When to earth He comes again;
His the triumph and the crown,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His the glory and renown.

Sing we, then, of Him who died,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sing of Him who rose again;
By His blood we’re justified,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And with Him we hope to reign:
Yes, we look to see our Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And to share His bright reward.


328
Memorable morning, first day of the week

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Irwin_William&#39;,this)">William Irwin</a></div>
6.5.6.5.D.

MEMORABLE morning, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;First day of the week! 
Loved ones to the garden
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Came their Lord to seek, 
Ready to anoint Him,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thinking all was o’er, 
Heavy in their mourning, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Full of sorrow sore.

But the tomb was empty,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Th’ stone was rolled away;
And as they beheld it
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In perplexity,
Two in shining garments
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Stood where He was laid, 
Said to them affrighted,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“Be ye not afraid”.

“Why the Living seek ye
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thus among the dead? 
He indeed is risen
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Even as He said.”
Then they ran rejoicing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Wondrous news to tell -
That the Lord was living,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And that all was well.

Glorious resurrection!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Past are all His woes.
Hail the mighty Victor
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who in triumph rose. 
From our hearts responsive
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Joyful songs of praise 
Unto Thee, His Father,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Rev’rently we raise.


329
Mid the splendours of the glory
Previous BHB #150<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Reid_William&#39;,this)">William Watkins Reid  (1863)</a></div>
8.7.8.7.4.7.

MID the splendours of the glory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Which we hope ere long to share,
Christ, our Head, and we, His members,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shall appear divinely fair;
Oh, how glorious!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When we meet Him in the air!

From the dateless, timeless, periods,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He has loved us without cause;
And for all His blood-bought myriads,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His is love that knows no pause;  
Matchless Lover!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Changeless as th’ eternal laws!

Oh, what gifts shall yet be granted,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Palms and crowns, and robes of  white,
When the hope for which we panted
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Bursts upon our gladdened sight,  
And our Saviour
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Makes us glorious through His might!

Bright the prospect soon that greets us
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of that longed-for nuptial day,
When our heavenly Bridegroom meets us
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On His kingly, conquering way;  
In the glory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Bride and Bridegroom reign for aye!


330
Midnight shadows close around

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Ewan_Y_Isaac&#39;,this)">Isaac Y. Ewan</a></div>
7.7.7.5.D.

MIDNIGHT shadows close around 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As, in agony profound,
Bowed in grief upon the ground,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Prayer he offers up
With strong crying and with tears;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Mystery to mortal ears
Is the prayer His Father hears,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O’er the awful cup.

Dawn upon the dreadful morn, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shows Him bearing hate and scorn, 
Lowly in His crown of thorn,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Patient in His grace.
To the scourge His back is bare, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From His cheeks they pluck the hair,
Rich and poor, united there,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Mock His marrèd face.

Then, with every sin surpassed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For His vesture lots they cast,
Him they crucify at last,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Human guilt complete.
Pity still His heart within,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Seeks He pardon for their sin
As the nails they hammer in
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Through His hands and feet.

All creation, draped in gloom,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Trembles on the brink of doom
Of an everlasting tomb,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Evermore undone:
But, from out the depth arise
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lonely and mysterious cries, 
And He bows His head and dies,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Endless vict’ry won.


331
Midst the darkness, storm, and sorrow
Previous BHB #155<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Bevan_Emma_Frances&#39;,this)">Emma Frances Shuttleworth Bevan (1898)</div>
8.5.8.5.D.

MIDST the darkness, storm, and sorrow,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;One bright gleam I see;
Well I know the blessèd morrow
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Christ will come for me.
’Midst the light, and peace, and glory
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of the Father’s home,
Christ for me is waiting, watching,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Waiting till I come.

Long the blessèd Guide has led me
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;By the desert road;
Now I see the golden towers,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;City of my God.
There, amidst the love and glory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He is waiting yet;
On His hand a name is graven
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He can ne’er forget.

There, amidst the songs of heaven,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sweeter to His ear
Is the footfall through the desert
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Ever drawing near.
There, made ready are the mansions,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Glorious, bright, and fair;
But the Bride the Father gave Him
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Still is wanting there.

Who is this who comes to meet me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On the desert way,
As the Morning Star foretelling
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;God’s unclouded day?
He it is who came to win me
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On the Cross of shame;
In His glory well I know Him
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Evermore the same.

Oh, the blessèd joy of meeting,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All the desert past!
Oh, the wondrous words of greeting
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He shall speak at last!
He and I together ent’ring
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Those bright courts above;
He and I together sharing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All the Father’s love.

Where no shade nor stain can enter,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Nor the gold be dim;
In that holiness unsullied
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I shall walk with Him.
Meet companion, then, for Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From Him, for Him, made;
Glory of God’s grace for ever
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;There in me displayed.

He, who in the hour of sorrow
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Bore the curse alone;
I, who through the lonely desert
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Trod where He had gone.
He and I in that bright glory
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;One deep joy shall share;
Mine, to be for ever with Him, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His, that I am there.


332
Mine eyes are unto Thee, my God
Previous BHB #417<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Alystyne_Frances_Jane_Van&#39;,this)">Frances (Fanny) Jane Crosby</a></div>
C.M. 

MINE eyes are unto Thee, my God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My soul on Thee doth wait,
My hope is in Thy faithful word,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And in Thy love so great.

Thy promise true my faith receives
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And claims it for its own;
My trusting heart with joy believes
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The covenant-keeping One.

What if Thy love impose a task
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Too hard for me to scan?
I will not question it, nor ask
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The meaning of Thy plan.

O Lord, I only ask for grace
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To work it out with Thee;
And as in faith each step I take
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I’ll more its beauty see.

Its rich unfoldings on my soul
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Each day shall burst anew,
Till glory, bright and endless, dawns
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Upon my ransomed view.

Till then, oh keep me safely, Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Full trusting only Thee,
Till that blest, happy moment come,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When, Lord, Thy face I’ll see.

334
More holiness give me

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Bliss_Philip_P&#39;,this)">Philip P. Bliss</a></div>
6.5.6.5.D.

MORE holiness give me, 
More strivings within;
More patience in suffering, 
More sorrow for sin;
More faith in my Saviour, 
More sense of His care;
More joy in His service, 
More purpose in prayer.

More gratitude give me, 
More trust in the Lord;
More zeal for His glory, 
More hope in His Word;
More tears for His sorrows, 
More pain at His grief;
More meekness in trial, 
More praise for relief.

More purity give me, 
More strength to o’ercome;
More freedom from earth-stains, 
More longings for home;
More fit for the kingdom, 
More useful I’d be;
More blessèd and holy, 
More, Saviour, like Thee.


335
More love to Thee, O Christ

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Prentiss_Elizabeth&#39;,this)">Elizabeth Prentiss</a></div>
6.4.6.4.6.6.4.4. 

MORE love to Thee, O Christ,
More love to Thee!
Hear Thou the prayer I make
On bended knee;
This is my earnest plea -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;More love, O Christ, to Thee!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;More love to Thee!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;More love to Thee!

Once earthly joy I craved,
Sought peace and rest;
Now, Thee alone I seek,
Give what is best;
This all my prayer shall be -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;More love, O Christ, to Thee! 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;More love to Thee! 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;More love to Thee!

Let sorrow do its work,
Send grief or pain;
Sweet are Thy messengers,
Sweet their refrain,
When they can sing with me -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;More love, O Christ, to Thee!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;More love to Thee!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;More love to Thee!

Then shall my latest breath
Whisper Thy praise;
This be the parting cry
My heart shall raise;
This still its prayer shall be -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;More love, O Christ, to Thee! 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;More love to Thee! 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;More love to Thee!


336
My chains are snapt
Previous BHB #152<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Carson_Margaret_Ledley&#39;,this)">Margaret L. Carson</a></div>
11.4.11.4. 

MY chains are snapt, the bonds of sin are broken,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And I am free;
O let the triumphs of His grace be spoken,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who died for me.

O death! O grave! I do not dread your power,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The ransom’s paid;
On Jesus, in that dark and dreadful hour,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My guilt was laid.

Yes, Jesus bore it, bore, in love unbounded,  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What none can know;
He passed through death and gloriously confounded
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our every foe.

And now He’s risen, proclaim the joyful story,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Lord’s on high;
And we in Him are raised to endless glory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And ne’er can die.

We wait to see the Morning Star appearing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In glory bright;
This blessèd hope illumes, with beams most cheering,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The hours of night.


337
My Father’s way may twist and turn

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Overton_M_A&#39;,this)">A. M. Overton</a></div>
C.M. 

MY Father’s way may twist and turn
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My heart may throb and ache,
But in my soul I’m glad to know,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He maketh no mistake.

My cherished plans may go astray,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My hopes may fade away,
But still I’ll trust my Lord to lead,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For He doth know the way.

Tho’ night be dark and it may seem
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That day will never break,
I’ll pin my faith, my all, in Him,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He maketh no mistake.

There’s so much now I cannot see,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My eyesight’s far too dim,
But come what may, I’ll simply trust
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And leave it all to Him.

For by and by the mist will lift,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And plain it all He’ll make,
Through all the way, tho’ dark to me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He made not one mistake.


338
My glorious Victor, Prince divine
Previous BHB #419<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Moule_Handley_Carr_Glyn&#39;,this)">Handley Carr Glyn Moule</a></div>
L.M. 

MY glorious Victor, Prince divine,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Clasp these surrendered hands in Thine;
At length my will is all Thine own,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Glad vassal of a Saviour’s throne.

My Master, lead me to Thy door;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Pierce this now willing ear once  more:
Thy bonds are freedom; let me stay
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With Thee, to toil, endure, obey.

Yes, ear and hand, and thought and will,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Use all in Thy dear slavery still!
Self’s weary liberties I cast
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Beneath Thy feet; there keep them fast.

Tread them still down; and then I know
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;These hands shall with Thy gifts  o’erflow;
And piercèd ears shall hear the tone
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Which tells me Thou and I are one.


339
My God, I am Thine

341
My God, my Father, while I stray
Previous BHB #165
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Elliott_Charlotte&#39;,this)">Charlotte Elliott  (1834)</a></div>
8.8.8.4. 

MY GOD, my Father, while I stray
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Far from my home on life’s rough way,
Oh, teach me from my heart to say -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“Thy will be done!”

c
<i>[** McGranahan tune only: repeat last two lines **]</i>
“Thy will be done!”
“Thy will be done!”
Oh, teach me from my heart to say -
“Thy will be done!”

If dark my path and hard my lot,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;May I be still and murmur not;
But breathe the prayer divinely taught -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“Thy will be done!”

c
<i>[** McGranahan tune only **]</i>
“Thy will be done!”
“Thy will be done!”
But breathe the prayer divinely taught -
“Thy will be done!”

What though in lonely grief I sigh,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For friends beloved, no longer nigh,
Submissive still would I reply -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“Thy will be done!”

If Thou shouldst call me to resign
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What most I prize - it ne’er was  mine,
I only yield Thee what is Thine:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“Thy will be done!”

Should pining sickness waste away
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My life in premature decay,
My Father! help me still to say -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“Thy will be done!”

Renew my will from day to day,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Blend it with Thine, and take away
All that now makes it hard to say -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“Thy will be done!”

And when on earth I breathe no more
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The prayer oft mixed with tears  before,
I’ll sing on heaven’s blissful shore -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“Thy will be done!”


342
My God! what cords of love are Thine
Previous BHB #154
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Doddridge_Philip&#39;,this)">Philip Doddridge</a></div>
C.M. 

MY GOD! what cords of love are Thine,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;How gentle, yet how strong!
Thy truth and grace their power combine
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To draw our souls along.

The guilt of twice ten thousand sins
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;One moment takes away;
And when the fight of faith begins,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our strength is as our day.

Comfort, through all this vale of tears,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In rich profusion flows;
And glory of unnumbered years
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Eternity bestows.

Drawn by such cords we’ll onward move
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Till round the throne we meet,
And, captives in the chain of love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Embrace our Saviour’s feet.


343
Until Then

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Hamblen_Stuart&#39;,this)">Stuart Hamblen</a></div><p>&copy; Copyright 1958, Renewed 1986. Hamblen Music Co./ASCAP (admin. By ClearBox Rights). All rights reserved. Used by permission.</p>
P.M. 

MY heart can sing when I pause to remember,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A heartache here is but a stepping stone,
Along a path that’s winding always upward.
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;This troubled world is not my final home.

c
But until then, my heart will go on singing,
Until then, with joy I’ll carry on,
Until the day my eyes behold that city,
Until the day God calls me home.

The things of earth will dim and lose their value,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;If we recall they’re borrowed for a while;
And things of earth that cause this  heart to tremble,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Remembered there will only bring a smile.

c
But until then, my heart will go on singing,
Until then, with joy I’ll carry on,
Until the day my eyes behold that city,
Until the day God calls me home.


344
My heart is bubbling over, Lord

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Anonymous_Hymn_Writers&#39;,this)">Author Unknown</a></div>
C.M. 

MY heart is bubbling over, Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I will give thanks and sing,
My gladsome sacrifice of praise,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Thy great name I bring.

How fair and lovely, Lord, art Thou,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;How beautiful and sweet,
My grateful heart to Thee would bow,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Before the mercy seat.

Thy name is more than tongue can tell,
Revealed at Calvary,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And ever would my spirit dwell,
On such great love to me.

Grace sought me when I wandered far,
In all my sin and shame,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And living waters brought to me,
All glory to Thy name.

Grace comes indeed from that deep well,
Of God’s great heart of love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Its fullest proof is seen in Him,
Who brought it from above.

My soul attracted to Thyself,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Fresh beauties in Thee see,
And treasures all Thy wondrous words,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“None ever spake like Thee”.

My heart is bubbling over, Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And till Thy face I see,
I fain would praise Thy name for all,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy love and grace to me.


345
My heart is full of Christ, and longs
Previous BHB #420<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Wesley_Charles&#39;,this)">Charles Wesley  (1743)</a></div>
8.8.8.8.8.8. 

MY heart is full of Christ, and longs
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Its glorious matter to declare,
Of Him I make my loftier songs,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And cannot from His praise forbear;
My ready tongue makes haste to sing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The glories of my heavenly King.

Fairer than all the earth-born race,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Perfect in comeliness Thou art;
Replenished are Thy lips with grace,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And full of love Thy tender heart;
God ever blest! we bow the knee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And own all fulness dwells in Thee.

Gird on Thy thigh the Spirit’s sword,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And take to Thee Thy power divine;
Stir up Thy strength, almighty Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All power and majesty are Thine:
Assert Thy worship and renown;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O all-redeeming God, come down!

Come, and maintain Thy righteous cause
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And let Thy glorious toil succeed;
Extend the victory of Thy Cross,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Ride on, and prosper in Thy deed;
Through earth triumphantly ride on,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And reign in every heart alone.


346
My hope is built on nothing less
Previous BHB #158
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Mote_Edward&#39;,this)">Edward Mote</a>  (c.18)</div>
8.8.8.8.8.8. 

MY hope is built on nothing less
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Than Jesus’ blood and righteousness;
I dare not trust the sweetest frame,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But wholly lean on Jesus’ Name.

c
On Christ, the solid Rock, I stand;
All other ground is sinking sand.

When darkness seems to veil His face,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I rest on His unchanging grace;
In every high and stormy gale
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My anchor holds within the veil.

c
On Christ, the solid Rock, I stand;
All other ground is sinking sand.

His oath, His covenant, and blood,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Support me in the whelming flood;
When all around my soul gives way
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He then is all my hope and stay.

c
On Christ, the solid Rock, I stand;
All other ground is sinking sand.

When He shall come with trumpet sound, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Oh, may I then in Him be found; 
Clothed in His righteousness alone,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Faultless to stand before the throne!

c
On Christ, the solid Rock, I stand;
All other ground is sinking sand.


347
My Lord has garments so wondrous fine

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Barraclough_Harry&#39;,this)">Harry Barraclough</a></div>
P.M. 

MY Lord has garments so wondrous fine,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And myrrh their texture fills;
Its fragrance reached to this heart of mine,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With joy my being thrills.

c
Out of the ivory palaces,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Into a world of woe,
Only His great, eternal love
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Made my Saviour go.

His life had also its sorrows sore,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For aloes had a part;
And when I think of the Cross He bore,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My eyes with tear-drops start. 

c
Out of the ivory palaces,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Into a world of woe,
Only His great, eternal love
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Made my Saviour go.

His garments too were in cassia dipped,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With healing in a touch;
Each time my feet in some sin  have slipped,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He took me from its clutch. 

c
Out of the ivory palaces,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Into a world of woe,
Only His great, eternal love
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Made my Saviour go.

In garments glorious He will come,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To open wide the door;
And I shall enter my heavenly home,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To dwell for evermore. 

c
Out of the ivory palaces,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Into a world of woe,
Only His great, eternal love
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Made my Saviour go.


348
My Redeemer! Oh what beauties
Previous BHB #159<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Anonymous_Hymn_Writers&#39;,this)">Anonymous</a></div>
8.7.8.7.4.7. 

MY Redeemer! Oh what beauties
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In that lovely Name appear;
None but Jesus, in His glories,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shall the honoured title wear.
My Redeemer!
Thou hast my salvation wrought.

Sunk in ruin, sin, and misery,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Bound by Satan’s captive chain,
Guided by his artful treachery,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hurrying on to endless pain;
My Redeemer
Plucked me as a brand from hell.

Mine by covenant, mine for ever,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Mine by oath, and mine by blood,
Mine - nor time the bond shall sever,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Mine as an unchanging God.
My Redeemer!
Oh how sweet to call Thee mine!

When in heaven I see Thy glory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When before Thy throne I bow,
Perfected I shall be like Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Fully Thy redemption know.
My Redeemer
Then shall hear me shout His praise.


349
My rest is in heaven, my rest is not here
Previous BHB #160
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Lyte_Henry_Francis&#39;,this)">Henry Francis Lyte</a></div>
11.11.11.11. 

MY rest is in heaven, my rest is not here,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Then why should I murmur when trials are near?
Be hushed my sad spirit, the worst that can come
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But shortens the journey and hastens me home.

It is not for me to be seeking my bliss,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And building my hopes in a region  like this;
I look for a city which hands have not piled;
I pant for a country by sin undefiled.

Afflictions may press me, they cannot destroy, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;One glimpse of His love turns them all into joy; 
And the bitterest tears, if He smile but on them, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Like dew in the sunshine, grow diamond and gem.

Let trial and danger my progress oppose,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;They only make heaven more sweet at the close;
Come joy or come sorrow, whate’er may befall,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A home with my God will make up for it all.

With Christ in my heart, and His Word in my hand,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I travel in haste through an enemy’s land;
The road may be rough, but it cannot be long,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;So I journey on singing the conqueror’s song.


350
My Saviour, I would own Thee

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Taylor_M_H_Rebekah&#39;,this)">Rebekah H. M. Taylor</a></div>
7.6.7.6.D. 

MY Saviour, I would own Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Amid the world’s proud scorn -
The world that mocked and crowned Thee
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With diadem of thorn; 
The world that now rejects Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Makes nothing of Thy love, 
Counts naught the grace and pity 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That brought Thee from above. 

My Lord, my Master, help me 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To walk apart with Thee, 
Outside the camp, where only 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy beauty I can see;
Far from the world’s loud turmoil, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Far from its busy din, 
Far from its praise and honour, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Its unbelief and sin. 

Oh, keep my heart at leisure 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From all the world beside; 
In close communion ever,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With Thee, Lord, to abide,
That I Thy whispered breathings 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of love and truth may hear, 
And hail Thee with rejoicing, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When Thou shalt soon appear.


351
My Shepherd is the Lamb
Previous BHB #156
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Beaumont_John&#39;,this)">John Beaumont</a></div>
6.6.8.4.D. 

MY Shepherd is the Lamb,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The living Lord who died;
With all things good I ever am
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;By Him supplied;
He richly feeds my soul
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With blessings from above;
And leads me where the rivers roll
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of endless love.

My soul He doth restore
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Whene’er I go astray;
He makes my cup of joy run o’er
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From day to day;
His love, so full, so free,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Anoints my head with oil;
Mercy and goodness follow me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Fruit of His toil.

When faith and hope shall cease, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And love abide alone, 
Then shall I see Him face to face,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And know as known;
Still shall I lift my voice,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His praise my song shall be;
And I will in His love rejoice
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who died for me. 


352
My song is love unknown

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Crossman_Samuel&#39;,this)">Samuel Crossman</a></div>
6.6.6.6.8.8. 

MY song is love unknown,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My Saviour’s love to me; 
Love to the loveless shown,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That they might lovely be. 
O who am I, That for my sake, 
My Lord should take Frail flesh and die?

He came from His blest throne 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Salvation to bestow; 
But man made strange, and none 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The longed-for Christ would know. 
But oh, my Friend, My Friend indeed, 
Who at my need His life did spend!

Sometimes they strew His way 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And His sweet praises sing; 
Resounding all the day
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hosannas to their King. 
Then “Crucify!” Is all their breath, 
And for His death They thirst and cry.

Why, what hath my Lord done?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What makes this rage and spite? 
He made the lame to run,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He gave the blind their sight. 
Sweet injuries! Yet they at these
Themselves displease, And ’gainst Him rise.

They rise, and needs will have
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My dear Lord made away;
A murderer they save, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Prince of Life they slay.
Yet cheerful He To suffering goes,
That He His foes From thence might free.

In life, no house, no home
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My Lord on earth might have;
In death, no friendly tomb
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But what a stranger gave.
What may I say? Heav’n was His home,
But mine the tomb Wherein He lay.

Here might I stay and sing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No story so divine;
Never was love, dear King,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Never was grief like Thine.
This is my Friend, In whose sweet praise
I all my days Could gladly spend.


353
My soul, amid this stormy world
Previous BHB #164<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Chapman_Robert_Cleaver&#39;,this)">Robert Cleaver Chapman</a></div>
C.M. 

MY soul, amid this stormy world,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Is like some fluttered dove,
And fain would be as swift of wing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And flee to Him I love.

The cords that bound my heart to earth
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Are loosed by Jesus’ hand;
Before His Cross I now am left
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A stranger in the land.

That visage marred, those sorrows deep,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The thorns, the scourge, the gall,
These were the golden chains  of love
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His captive to enthral!

Fain would I, Saviour, know Thy love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Which yet no measure knows,
Would search the depths of all Thy wounds,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The secret of Thy woes.

Fain would I strike the golden harp,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And wear the promised crown;
And at Thy feet, while bending low,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Would sing what grace has done.


354
My times are in Thy hand
Previous BHB #222
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Lloyd_William_Freeman&#39;,this)">William Freeman Lloyd</a></div>
S.M. 

MY times are in Thy hand;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My God, I wish them there;
My life, my soul, my all, I leave
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Entirely to Thy care.

My times are in Thy hand,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Whatever they may be;
Pleasing or painful, dark or bright,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As best may seem to Thee.

My times are in Thy hand;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Why should I doubt or fear?
My Father’s hand will never cause
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His child a needless tear.

My times are in Thy hand,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus, the Crucified!
The hand my many sins have pierced
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Is now my guard and guide.

My times are in Thy hand,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus, my Advocate!
Nor is that hand outstretched in vain,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For me to supplicate.

My times are in Thy hand,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I’ll always trust in Thee;
Till I have left this weary land,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And all Thy glory see.


355
Nailed to a Cross! those holy hands

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Ewan_Y_Isaac&#39;,this)">Isaac Y. Ewan</a></div>
8.8.8.6. 

NAILED to a Cross! those holy hands
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That healed the helpless in their pain,
That loosed with tender touch their bands 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And brought them life again!

Those holy hands that broke the bread
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When, in His kindness and His care, 
The fainting multitude He fed
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In yonder desert bare!

Nailed to a Cross! those holy feet
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That trod that lowly, lovely way,
Each step a wealth of fragrance sweet,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sweet for eternity!

Those holy feet that never strayed
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Nor stumbled in the path, but trod,
Unfaltering and unafraid,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On in the will of God!

Nailed to a Cross! The Prince of Life!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Lord of Glory’s dying place!
A Cross that leaves a world of strife
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Forever in disgrace!

O thorn-crowned head in death bowed low!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Obedience to surrendered breath! 
O Love most wonderful in woe!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Most beautiful in death!


356
No act of power could e’er atone

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Coates_A_C&#39;,this)">C. A. Coates</a></div>
C.M. 

NO act of power could e’er atone,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No wonder-working word
Could, from the brightness of the throne,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Make love’s sweet voice be heard.

If sinners ever were to know
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The depths of love divine,
All Calv’ry’s weakness and its woe,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Blest Saviour, must be Thine.

God’s righteousness is there proclaimed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His mercy’s depths are known,
While to the full Thou hast maintained
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The glory of His throne.

God now is glorified in Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In Thee, His only Son;
His hand, His house, His heart are free,
Because Thy work is done.

For Thou hast brought again to Him
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;More than by man He lost;
And in the very place of sin
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We see His glory most.

And drawn to Thee in holy love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A song of joy we raise;
In concert with the heav’ns above
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We crown Thee with our praise.


357
No blood, no altar now
Previous BHB #171<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Bonar_Horatius&#39;,this)">Horatius Bonar (1845)</a></div>
6.6.8.6.10.12. 

NO blood, no altar now,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The sacrifice is o’er;
No flame, no smoke ascends on high,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The lamb is slain no more;
But richer blood has flowed from nobler veins
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To purge the soul from guilt, 
and cleanse the reddest stains.

We thank Thee for the blood,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The blood of Christ, Thy Son,
The blood by which our peace is made,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our victory is won:
Great victory o’er hell and sin and woe,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That needs no second fight 
and leaves no second foe.

We thank Thee for the grace
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Descending from above,
That overflows our widest guilt,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Th’ eternal Father’s love,
Love of the Father’s everlasting Son,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Love of the Holy Ghost, 
Jehovah, Three in One.

We thank Thee for the hope,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;So glad, and sure, and clear;
It holds the drooping spirit up
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Till the long dawn appear;
Fair hope! with what a sunshine does it cheer
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our roughest path on earth, 
our dreariest desert here!

We thank Thee for the crown
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of glory and of life;
’Tis no poor with’ring wreath of earth,
Man’s prize in mortal strife;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;’Tis incorruptible as is the throne,
The kingdom of our God 
and His incarnate Son.


358
No bone of Thee was broken
Previous BHB #167<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Chapman_Robert_Cleaver&#39;,this)">Robert Cleaver Chapman</a></div>
7.6.7.6.D. 

NO bone of Thee was broken,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou spotless, paschal Lamb!
Of life and peace a token
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To us who know Thy Name;
The Head, for all the members,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The curse, the vengeance bore,
And God, our God, remembers
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His people’s sins no more.

We, Thy redeemed, are reaping
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What Thou didst sow in tears;
This feast which we are keeping
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy Name to us endears;
It tells of justice hiding
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The face of God from Thee;
Proud men around deriding
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy sorrows on the tree.

Thy death of shame and sorrow
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Was like unto Thy birth,
Which would no glory borrow,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No majesty from earth.
Thy pilgrims, we are hasting
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To our eternal home,
Its joy already tasting
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of vict’ry o’er the tomb.

Thy life and death reviewing, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We tread the narrow way;
Our homeward path pursuing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We watch the dawn of day.
We eat and drink with gladness
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The symbol bread and wine,
And sing with sweetest sadness
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our song of love divine.


359
No condemnation! O my soul
Previous BHB #162
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Chapman_Robert_Cleaver&#39;,this)">Robert Cleaver Chapman</a></div>
C.M. 

NO condemnation!” O my soul
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;’Tis God that speaks the word;
Perfect in comeliness art thou
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In Christ thy glorious Lord.

In God’s own presence now for us
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Saviour doth appear;
His saints as jewels on His heart
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus doth ever bear.

“No condemnation!” precious word!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Consider it, my soul;
Thy sins were all on Jesus laid,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His stripes have made thee whole.

Teach us, O God, to fix our eyes
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On Christ, the spotless Lamb;
So shall we love Thy gracious will
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And glorify Thy Name.


360
No future but glory, Lord Jesus, have we

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Champney_Henry_DArcy&#39;,this)">Henry d'Arcy Champney</a></div>
P.M. 

NO future but glory, Lord Jesus, have we -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;How bright is the prospect of being with Thee! 
O home of all homes, with the Father above! 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O wonderful dwelling of infinite love!
Home, home, bright, bright home!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;How blessèd the prospect, Lord Jesus, of home!

A moment’s affliction, Lord Jesus, is light,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And works for us glory surpassingly bright; 
Whilst viewing not things which are but for a time,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But objects far brighter in glory sublime;
Home, home, bright, bright home!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our future’s eternal in Thy  blessèd home!

“One thing” would we do, we would press toward the goal, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thyself, Lord, in glory, the prize of our soul -
Forget what’s behind, for the bright things before, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Since all they who know Thee would know Thee still more: 
Home, home, bright, bright home!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We press on to know Thee and  reach Thee, at home!

In heaven alone is our city and state, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From thence, Lord, as Saviour,  Thyself we await; 
Our bodies to change and conform them to Thine, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That we in Thine image and glory may shine;
Home, home, bright, bright home!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Soon we shall be with Thee  and like Thee, at home!


361
No gospel like this feast
Previous BHB #421
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Charles_Elizabeth_Rundle&#39;,this)">Elizabeth Rundle Charles</a> or <a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;John_Charles_Ryle&#39;,this)">John Charles Ryle</a></div>
S.M.

NO gospel like this feast
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Spread for us, Lord, by Thee;
No prophets or evangelists
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Preach the glad news more free.

All Thy redemption cost,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All our redemption won;
All it has won for us, the lost,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All it cost Thee, the Son.

Thine was the bitter price,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Ours is the free gift given;
Thine was the blood of sacrifice,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Ours is the wine of heaven.

To Thee, our curse and doom 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Wrapt round Thee with our sin;
The horror of that midday gloom,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The deeper night within.

Here we would rest midway,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As on a sacred height;
That darkest and that brightest day
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Meeting before our sight.

From that dark depth of woes
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy love for us has trod,
We soar to heights of blest repose
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy love prepares with God.

Thus, from self’s chains released,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;One sight alone we see,
Still at the Cross, while at this feast,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We see Thee, only Thee!


362
No lips like Thine, most blessèd Lord

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Anonymous_Hymn_Writers&#39;,this)">Author Unknown</a></div>
C.M.

NO lips like Thine, most blessèd Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;None ever spake like Thee,
As sweetest honey or as myrrh,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Flows fragrant from the tree.

Thy lips “like lilies”! What so pure,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;So lovely in their grace,
With secret power, in hours of grief,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To kindle thoughts of praise!

Forth from those lips rich grace is poured,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To comfort all who mourn;
No tongue like Thine to cheer the  faint,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou Friend of the forlorn!

As softening showers upon the grass,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Gentle as early dew,
Thy speech distils into the soul,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Its graces to renew.

Ages have passed since at Thy voice
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Men marvelled as they heard;
And still our hearts within us burn
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;While listening to Thy word.


363
No room in the inn for the Saviour was found

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Anonymous_Hymn_Writers&#39;,this)">Author Unknown</a></div>
P.M.

NO room in the inn for the Saviour was found, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who from childhood was treated with scorn; 
No place but the manger where cattle were brought,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When in Bethlehem Jesus was born. 

But heaven was opened to give Him the praise,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Denied Him by man on the earth; 
And heavenly hosts broke forth  in their songs,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of wonder and joy at His birth. 

No home but the mountain of Olives was His,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Though the bird of the air had its nest; 
No love but the Father’s, whose  side He had left, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Could give Him refreshment and rest. 

No comforters came, when for comfort He looked, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No pity, when pity He sought; 
For sin He was wounded and  smitten of God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And sinners did set Him at nought!

And angels, who ministered oft to His need,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Were sent to His help from the throne,
When, weary and weak in the  bitterest hour,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His people had left Him alone!

But neither the manger, the Cross, nor the shame, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Are now by this blessèd One known; 
Gethsemane’s sorrows for ever  are past,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And the fruit of them all is His own. 

And now that He dwells in the mansions of bliss, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He has room for each trusting one there;
And His sorrows remembered will heighten the joy,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Which all will eternally share.


364
No shadows yonder! All light and song

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Bonar_Horatius&#39;,this)">Horatius Bonar</a></div>
9.9.9. 

NO shadows yonder! All light and song!
Each day I ponder and say, “How long
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shall time me sunder from that glad throng?”

No weeping yonder! All fled away;
While here I wander, each weary  day,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And sigh as I ponder my long, long stay.

No partings yonder! No space or time
Hearts e’er shall sunder in that fair clime,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Dearer and fonder, friendships sublime!

None wanting yonder bought by the Lamb!
All gathered under the sheltering palm,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Loud as the thunder swells the glad psalm! 


365
No unforeseen event e’er took Him by surprise

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Ewan_Y_Isaac&#39;,this)">Isaac Y. Ewan</a></div>
S.M. 

NO unforeseen event
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;E’er took Him by surprise; 
Toward the Cross with fixed intent 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He moved with open eyes.

Men reckless deeds will vaunt
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And count as courage true 
The daring of the ignorant 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who know not what they do.

He knew the reason why
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Father sent the Son;
He knew that He had come to die,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A Man for men undone.

He weighed that weight of woe
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In each particular;
The length and depth to which He’d go
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He measured from afar.

The scourging and the scorn,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The worst that men could do, 
The meaning of the crown of thorn 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lay open to His view.

He knew! and love can look
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And see in light most sweet 
The glory of the way He took, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The beauty of His feet.


366
None but Thyself, O God, could estimate

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kirkpatrick_William&#39;,this)">William Kirkpatrick</a></div>
10.10.10.10. 

NONE but Thyself, O God, could estimate
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His riches vast, His condescension great,
His stoop from glory’s sphere in grace sublime, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To move in servant form through scenes of time.

His path on earth we rev’rently review,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In every step so faithful and so true, 
Misunderstood by friends, despised by foes, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Yet marked throughout by inward calm repose.

How kind His heart! how full of fragrance sweet!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;How clean His hands! how beautiful His feet!
How pure His words! how gracious every deed!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And all for Thee, the while He met man’s need!

So we remember Him, in all His ways,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Man of Thy good pleasure,  through those days 
Of earthly sojourn, suffering and shame
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And give Thee thanks for Him in His blest Name.


367
None teacheth, Lord, like Thee

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Webley_Daniel&#39;,this)">Daniel Webley</a></div>
S.M. 

None teacheth, Lord, like Thee, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;None can such truth impart, 
Such treasures from Thy Word unfold, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Nor so impress the heart. 

How blest Thy servants were, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When with them on their way 
Thou didst commune, and sweetly chase 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Their sorrows all away! 

So now to us draw near, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And speak to every heart; 
Our light in darkness, joy in grief, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our “All in All”, Thou art.

Open to us Thy Word, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy precious thoughts reveal, 
Thy purposes and ways explain, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And teach us all Thy will. 

So shall our doubt and fear, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our care and grief subside,
And each enraptured heart exclaim, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O Lord, with us abide! 


368
Not all the blood of beasts
Previous BHB #170
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Watts_Isaac&#39;,this)">Isaac Watts  (1709)</a></div>
S.M. 

NOT all the blood of beasts,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On Jewish altars slain,
Could give the guilty conscience peace,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Or wash away one stain.

But Christ, the heavenly Lamb,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Took all our sins away,
A sacrifice of nobler name
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And richer blood than they.

By faith we lay our hand
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On that dear head of Thine;
With broken, contrite hearts we stand,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And there confess our sin.

We now look back to see
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The burden Thou didst bear,
When hanging on the accursèd tree,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And know our guilt was there.

Believing, we rejoice
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To see the curse remove;
We bless the Lamb with cheerful voice,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And sing redeeming love.


369
Not now, but in the coming years

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Cornelius_Maxwell&#39;,this)">Maxwell Cornelius</a></div>
L.M.D. 

NOT now, but in the coming years,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;It may be in the better land,
We’ll read the meaning of our tears,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And there, some time, we’ll understand.

c
Then trust in God through all thy days;
Fear not! for He doth hold thy hand;
Though dark thy way, still sing and praise;
Some time, some time we’ll understand.

We’ll catch the broken threads again,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And finish what we here began;
Heaven will the mysteries explain,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And then, ah then, we’ll understand.

c
Then trust in God through all thy days;
Fear not! for He doth hold thy hand;
Though dark thy way, still sing and praise;
Some time, some time we’ll understand.

We’ll know why clouds instead of sun
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Were over many a cherished plan;
Why song has ceased when scarce begun;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;’Tis there, some time, we’ll understand.

c
Then trust in God through all thy days;
Fear not! for He doth hold thy hand;
Though dark thy way, still sing and praise;
Some time, some time we’ll understand.

Why what we long for most of all,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Eludes so oft our eager hand;
Why hopes are crushed and castles fall,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Up there, some time, we’ll understand.

c
Then trust in God through all thy days;
Fear not! for He doth hold thy hand;
Though dark thy way, still sing and praise;
Some time, some time we’ll understand.

God knows the way, He holds the key,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He guides us with unerring hand;
Some time with tearless eyes we’ll see;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Yes, there, up there, we’ll understand.

c
Then trust in God through all thy days;
Fear not! for He doth hold thy hand;
Though dark thy way, still sing and praise;
Some time, some time we’ll understand.


370
Not what I am, O Lord, but what Thou art
Previous BHB #275
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Bonar_Horatius&#39;,this)">Horatius Bonar</a></div>
10.10.10.10. 

NOT what I am, O Lord, but what Thou art -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That, that alone can be my soul’s true rest;
Thy love, not mine, bids fear and doubt depart,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And stills the tempest of my tossing breast.

It is Thy perfect love that casts out fear;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I know the voice that whispers “It is I”; 
And in Thy well-known words of heavenly cheer,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I find the joy that bids each sorrow fly.

Thy name is Love! I hear it from Thy Cross; 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy name is Love! I read it in Thy tomb;
All meaner love is perishable dross,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But this shall light me through  time’s thickest gloom.

’Tis what I know of Thee, my Lord and God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That fills my soul with peace, my lips with song;
Thou art my health, my joy, my staff and rod;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Leaning on Thee, in weakness I am strong.

More of Thyself, oh, show me, hour by hour,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;More of Thy glory, O my God and Lord;
More of Thyself, in all Thy grace and power;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;More of Thy love and truth, Incarnate Word! 


371
Not what my hands have done

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Bonar_Horatius&#39;,this)">Horatius Bonar</a></div>
S.M.D. 

NOT what my hands have done
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Can save this guilty soul;
Not what my toiling flesh has borne
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Can make my spirit whole.
Not what I feel or do
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Can give me peace with God;
Not all my prayers and sighs and tears
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Can bear my awful load.

Thy work alone, O Christ,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Can ease this weight of sin;
Thy blood alone, O Lamb of God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Can give me peace within.
Thy love to me, O God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Not mine, O Lord, to Thee,
Can rid me of this dark unrest,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And set my spirit free.

Thy grace alone, O God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To me can pardon speak;
Thy power alone, O Son of God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Can this sore bondage break.
No other work, save Thine,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No meaner blood will do;
No strength, save that which is divine,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Can bear me safely through.

I bless the Christ of God;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I rest on love divine;
And with unfaltering lip and heart
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I call this Saviour mine.
His Cross dispels each doubt;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I bury in His tomb
Each thought of unbelief and fear,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Each lingering shade of gloom.

I praise the God of grace;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I trust His truth and might;
He calls me His, I call Him mine,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My God, my joy and light.
In Him is only good,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In me is only ill;
My ill but draws His goodness forth,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And me He loveth still.

’Tis He who saveth me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And freely pardon gives;
I love because He loveth me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I live because He lives.
My life with Him is hid,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My death has passed away,
My clouds have melted into light,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My midnight into day.


372
Now I have found a Friend
Previous BHB #168
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Hope_Henry_Joy_McCracken&#39;,this)">Henry J. M. Hope  (1852)</a></div>
6.4.6.4.6.6.6.4. 

NOW I have found a Friend,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus is mine;
His love shall never end,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus is mine;
Though earthly joys decrease,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Though earthly friendships cease,
Now I have lasting peace,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus is mine.

Though I grow poor and old, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus is mine;
He will my faith uphold, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus is mine;
He will my wants supply,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His precious blood is nigh,
Nought can my hope destroy, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus is mine.

Farewell, mortality! 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus is mine;
Welcome, eternity! 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus is mine.
He my redemption is,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Wisdom and righteousness,
Life, light, and holiness, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus is mine.

Father! Thy Name I bless, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus is mine;
Thine was the sovereign grace, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Praise shall be Thine;
Spirit of holiness,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sealing the Father’s grace,
He made my soul embrace 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus as mine.


373
Now, in a song of grateful praise
Previous BHB #169
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Medley_Samuel&#39;,this)">Samuel Medley  (1776)</a></div>
L.M. 

NOW, in a song of grateful praise,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To our blest Lord the voice we raise;
With all His saints we join to tell -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;<i>Our Saviour hath done all things well!</i>

All worlds His glorious power confess;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His wisdom all His works express;
But oh, His love, what tongue can tell?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;<i>Our Saviour hath done all things well!</i>

And since our souls have known His love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What mercies hath He made us prove!
Mercies which all our praise excel -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;<i>Our Saviour hath done all things well!</i>

Though many a fiery, flaming dart,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The tempter levels at the heart,
With this we all his rage repel -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;<i>Our Saviour hath done all things well!</i>

And when on that bright day we rise,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And join the anthems of the skies,
Among the rest this note shall swell -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;<i>Our Saviour hath done all things well!</i>

c
And above the rest this note shall swell,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;This note shall swell, this note shall swell,
And above the rest this note shall swell,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;<i>Our Saviour hath done all things well!</i>


374
O blessed God! how kind
Previous BHB #181
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kent_John&#39;,this)">John Kent</a></div>
6.6.6.6.8.8. 

O BLESSÈD God! how kind
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Are all Thy ways to me,
Whose dark benighted mind
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Was enmity with Thee.
Yet now, subdued by sovereign grace,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My spirit longs for Thine embrace.

How precious are Thy thoughts
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That o’er my spirit roll!
They rise beyond my faults,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And captivate my soul:
How great their sum, how high they rise,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Can ne’er be known beneath the skies.

Preserved by Jesus, when
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My feet made haste to hell!
And there should I have gone,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But Thou dost all things well:
Thy love was great, Thy mercy free,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Which from the pit delivered me.

Before Thy hands had made
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The sun to rule the day,
Or earth’s foundation laid,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Or fashioned Adam’s clay,
What thoughts of peace and mercy flowed
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In Thy great heart of love, O God.

A monument of grace,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A sinner saved by blood,
The streams of love I trace
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Up to the fountain, God,
And in His sovereign counsels see
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Eternal thoughts of love to me.


375
O blessed God, to Thee we raise

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Ewan_Y_Isaac&#39;,this)">Isaac Y. Ewan</a></div>
L.M. 

O BLESSÈD God, to Thee we raise
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The fruit of lips in priestly praise
Of Him Who bows our hearts as one -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy Holy Son, Thy Holy Son.

Thy Holy Son in lowly guise,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Supremely precious in Thine eyes,
Most fair to Thee apparelled thus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And fair to us, most fair to us.

Thy Holy Son in manhood true,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy heart to please, Thy will  to do,
Thy Name on earth to glorify,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For this to live, for this to die.

Thy Holy Son descending low
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy love to tell, Thy heart to show,
The full expression of Thy mind, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In grace so kind, so wondrous  kind.

Thy Holy Son in human form,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Baring His bosom to the storm
In patient grace for men undone, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy Holy Son! Thy Holy Son!

O Blessèd God, the shame, the scorn,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The cruel Cross, the crown of thorn
We ever will “remember yet”, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And ne’er forget, no, ne’er forget.


376
O blessed Lord, what hast Thou done
Previous BHB #173
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Peters_Mary&#39;,this)">Mary Bowly Peters</a></div>
C.M. 

O BLESSÈD Lord, what hast Thou done!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;How vast a ransom paid!
God’s only well-belovèd Son
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Upon the altar laid!

The Father, in His willing love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Could spare Thee from His side;
And Thou couldst stoop, to bear above,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;At such a cost, Thy Bride.

While our full hearts in faith repose
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Upon Thy precious blood,
Peace in a steady current flows,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Filled from Thy mercy’s flood.

What boundless joy will fill each heart,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our every grief efface,
When we behold Thee as Thou art,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And all Thy love retrace.

Unseen we love Thee, dear Thy Name!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But when our eyes behold,
With joyful wonder we’ll proclaim
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“The half hath not been told!”

For Thou exceedest all the fame
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our ears have ever heard:
Happy are they who know Thy name,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And trust Thy faithful word!


377
O blessed moment, ever drawing nigh

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Ewan_Y_Isaac&#39;,this)">Isaac Y. Ewan</a></div>
10.10.10.10. 

O BLESSÈD moment, ever drawing nigh,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When every shadow shall have passed away, 
When that clear light shall fill the tear-dimmed eye, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And this dark night give place to endless day !

Then all misunderstanding shall have passed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Estrangement shall for evermore have flown, 
And fellowship its fulness reach at last;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We’ll love and know as we are loved and known.

Then everlasting joy we shall obtain,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sorrow and sighing then shall flee away; 
There shall be neither grief, nor cry, nor pain, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To cast a shadow o’er th’ eternal day.

Then shall we evermore be with the Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And love shall rest in endless ecstasy, 
When adoration strikes the  perfect chord 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In full-toned praise of Him eternally.


378
O blessed Saviour! is Thy love so great, so full, so free?
Previous BHB #172
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Stennett_Joseph&#39;,this)">Joseph Stennett</a></div>
C.M. 

O BLESSÈD Saviour! is Thy love
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;So great, so full, so free?
Fain would we give our hearts, our minds,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our lives, our all, to Thee.

We love Thee for the glorious worth
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Which in Thyself we see;
We love Thee for the shameful Cross
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Endured so patiently.

No man of greater love can boast
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Than for his friend to die;
Thou for Thine enemies wast slain!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What love with Thine can vie?

Though in the very form of God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With heavenly glory crowned,
Thou didst partake of human flesh,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Beset with sorrows round.

Thou wouldst like sinful man be made
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In everything but sin,
That we as like Thee might become,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As we unlike have been.

Like Thee in faith, in meekness, love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In every heavenly grace,
From glory thus to glory changed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Till we behold Thy face.

O Lord! we treasure in our souls
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The memory of Thy love;
And ever may Thy Name to us 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A grateful odour prove.


379
O child of God, there is for thee

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Muir_W_D_Thomas&#39;,this)">Thomas D. W. Muir</a></div>
P.M. 

O CHILD of God, there is for thee
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A hope that shines amid the gloom,
A gladsome hope that thou shalt see
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy Lord, for He will surely come.

c
He’ll come, … yes, He’ll come and tarry not;
He’ll come, … yes, He’ll come and tarry not;
He’ll come, … He’ll come, …
He’ll come and tarry not.

When in this world His hands had made,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No room was found for Jesus then;
The mountainside was oft His bed;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Now, glorified, He comes again.

c
He’ll come, … yes, He’ll come and tarry not;
He’ll come, … yes, He’ll come and tarry not;
He’ll come, … He’ll come, …
He’ll come and tarry not.

Exalted now to heaven’s throne,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Saviour there of sinful men;
His loving heart yearns o’er His own,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And for them He will come again.

c
He’ll come, … yes, He’ll come and tarry not;
He’ll come, … yes, He’ll come and tarry not;
He’ll come, … He’ll come, …
He’ll come and tarry not.

O child of God, thy lot may be
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Oft mixed with trial, grief and pain;
Look up, He’ll surely come for thee;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He says, “I’ll quickly come again”.

c
He’ll come, … yes, He’ll come and tarry not;
He’ll come, … yes, He’ll come and tarry not;
He’ll come, … He’ll come, …
He’ll come and tarry not.

Then joy unmingled will be thine,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Earth’s tears and trials all forgot;
So cheer thy heart, no more repine,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His word is sure: He’ll tarry not.

c
He’ll come, … yes, He’ll come and tarry not;
He’ll come, … yes, He’ll come and tarry not;
He’ll come, … He’ll come, …
He’ll come and tarry not.


380
O Christ, in Thee my soul hath found
Previous BHB #178<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author">Unknown tr/ad by <a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Bevan_Emma_Frances&#39;,this)">Emma Frances Bevan</a></div>
C.M.D. 

O CHRIST, in Thee my soul hath found,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And found in Thee alone,
The peace, the joy I sought so long,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The bliss till now unknown.

c
Now none but Christ can satisfy,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;None other Name for me;
There’s love, and life, and lasting joy,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lord Jesus, found in Thee.

I sighed for rest and happiness,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I yearned for them, not Thee;
But while I passed my Saviour by
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His love laid hold on me.

c
Now none but Christ can satisfy,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;None other Name for me;
There’s love, and life, and lasting joy,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lord Jesus, found in Thee.

I tried the broken cisterns, Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But, ah! the waters failed;
E’en as I stooped to drink they fled,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And mocked me as I wailed.

c
Now none but Christ can satisfy,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;None other Name for me;
There’s love, and life, and lasting joy,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lord Jesus, found in Thee.

The pleasures lost I sadly mourned,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But never wept for Thee,
Till grace the sightless eyes received,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy loveliness to see.

c
Now none but Christ can satisfy,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;None other Name for me;
There’s love, and life, and lasting joy,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lord Jesus, found in Thee.


381
O Christ, Thou heavenly Lamb!
Previous BHB #179<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Hurditch_Charles_Russell&#39;,this)">Charles Russell Hurditch</a></div>
S.M. 

O CHRIST, Thou heavenly Lamb!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Joy of the Father’s heart;
Now let Thy love my soul inflame,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Fresh power to me impart.

Power to know the loss
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Suffered, O Lord, by Thee;
Power to glory in the Cross
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou didst endure for me.

Power to feel Thy love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And all its depths to know;
Power to fix the heart above,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And die to all below.

Power to keep the eye
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For ever fixed on Thee;
Power to lift the warning cry
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To souls from wrath to flee.

Power lost souls to win
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From Satan’s mighty hold;
Power the wanderers to bring
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Back to the heavenly fold.

Power to watch and pray,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“Lord Jesus, quickly come!”
Power to hail the happy day
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Destined to bear me home.

Lord Jesus, then to me
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Power divine impart,
To swell redemption’s song to Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For worthy, Lord, Thou art.


382
O Christ, Thou Son of God!
Previous BHB #423<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Bennett_William_Henry&#39;,this)">William Bennett</a></div>
S.M.D. 

O CHRIST, Thou Son of God!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou glorious Lord of all,
Thou living One who once wast slain,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Before Thy face we fall:
To Thee, O Lord, we look,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Thee ourselves we yield;
Be Thou throughout our earthly course
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our refuge and our shield.

Though all around may change
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No change Thou e’er shalt know;
The same art Thou upon the throne
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As Thou wast here below;
The same today Thou art
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As yesterday Thou wast,
The same e’en to eternal days
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As in the wondrous past.

Lord Jesus, take our hearts,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From self-love set them free;
Help us, however dark our path,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To stay our souls on Thee:
Though evil waxes worse,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And many hearts grow cold,
Help us to cleave unto Thy name,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy faithful Word to hold.

Help us to look beyond
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The dark and gloomy night,
To wait for that blest hour when Thou
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Wilt come in glory bright:
When we Thy voice shall hear,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy glorious face shall see,
And, like Thee, in Thy presence stand
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And ever worship Thee.


383
O Christ! we rest in Thee
Previous BHB #175<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Deck_James_George&#39;,this)">James George Deck  (1841)</a></div>
6.6.8.6.8.8. 

O CHRIST! we rest in Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In Thee ourselves we hide;
Laden with guilt and misery,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Where could we rest beside?
’Tis on Thy meek and lowly breast
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our weary souls alone can rest.

Thou Holy One of God!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Father rests in Thee,
And in the savour of that blood
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Once shed on Calvary.
The curse is gone - through Thee we’re blest;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;God rests in Thee - in Thee we rest.

Soon the bright, glorious day,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The rest of God, shall come;
Sorrow and sin shall pass away,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And we shall reach our home;
Then, of the promised land possessed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our souls shall know eternal rest.


384
O Christ, what burdens bowed Thy head!
Previous BHB #176
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Cousin_Ann_Ross&#39;,this)">Anne Ross Cundell Cousin</a></div>
8.6.8.6.8.6. 

O CHRIST, what burdens bowed Thy head!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our load was laid on Thee;
Thou stoodest in the sinner’s stead,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Bear’st all my ill for me.
A victim led, Thy blood was shed;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Now there’s no load for me.

Death and the curse were in our cup,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O Christ, ’twas full for Thee!
But Thou hast drained the last dark drop,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;’Tis empty now for me.
That bitter cup, love drank it up;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Now blessings’ draught for me.

Jehovah lifted up His rod;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O Christ, it fell on Thee!
Thou wast sore stricken of Thy God;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;There’s not one stroke for me.
Thy tears, Thy blood, beneath it flowed;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy bruising healeth me. 

The tempest’s awful voice was heard;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O Christ, it broke on Thee!
Thy open bosom was my ward;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;It braved the storm for me.
Thy form was scarred, Thy visage marred;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Now cloudless peace for me.

Jehovah bade His sword awake;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O Christ, it woke ’gainst Thee!
Thy blood the flaming blade must slake,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy heart its sheath must be;
All for my sake my peace to make,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Now sleeps that sword for me.

For me, Lord Jesus, Thou hast died,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And I have died in Thee;
Thou’rt risen; my bands are all untied,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And now Thou liv’st in me.
The Father’s face of radiant grace
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shines now in light on me.


385
O come, Thou stricken Lamb of God!
Previous BHB #180
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Zinzendorf_Count_Nicolaus_Ludwig&#39;,this)">Nicolaus Ludwig von Zinzendorf</a> (verses 1-2 and 4), Johann Nitschmann (verse 3), Anna C. Nitschmann (verse 8). Tr/ad <a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Wesley_John&#39;,this)">John Wesley</a>. (Note: a minority of sources credit <a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Deszler_Wolfgang_Christopher&#39;,this)">Wolfgang Christoph Dessler</a> instead of Zinzendorf.)</div>
L.M. 

O COME, Thou stricken Lamb of God!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who shed’st for us Thine own life-blood,
And teach us all Thy love: then pain
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Were light, and life or death were gain.

Take Thou our hearts, and let them be
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For ever closed to all but Thee;
Thy willing servants, let us wear
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The seal of love for ever there.

How blest are they who still abide
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Close sheltered by Thy watchful side;
Who life and strength from Thee receive,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And with Thee move, and in Thee live!

Ah, Lord! enlarge our scanty thought
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To know the wonders Thou hast  wrought;
Unloose our stamm’ring tongues to tell
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy love, immense, unsearchable.

First-born of many brethren, Thou!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To whom both heaven and earth must bow!
Heirs of Thy shame and of Thy throne,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We bear the cross, and seek the  crown.


386
O ever homeless Stranger

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Darby_John_Nelson&#39;,this)">John Nelson Darby</a></div>
7.6.7.6. 

O EVER homeless Stranger,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thus, dearest Friend to me;
An outcast in a manger,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That Thou might’st with us be!

Oh, strange yet fit beginning
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of all that life of woe,
In which Thy grace was winning
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Poor man his God to know!

O Love, all thought surpassing!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That Thou should’st with us be,
Nor yet in triumph passing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But human infancy!

Midst sin and all corruption,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Where hatred did abound,
Thy path of true perfection
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Was light on all around.

Yet with all grief acquainted,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Man of Sorrows view,
Unmoved, by ill untainted,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The path of grace pursue.

No eye was found to pity,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No heart to bear Thy woe,
But shame, and scorn, and spitting:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;None cared Thy name to know.

O day of greatest sorrow,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Day of unfathomed grief!
When Thou didst taste the horror
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of wrath without relief.

In death, obedience yielding
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To God His Father’s will,
Love still its power is wielding
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To meet all human ill.

We worship, when we see Thee
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In all Thy sorrowing path;
We long soon to be with Thee
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who bore for us the wrath!


387
O eyes that are weary, and hearts that are sore
Previous BHB #182<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Darby_John_Nelson&#39;,this)">John Nelson Darby</a> & <a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Warner_Anna_Bartlett&#39;,this)">Anna Bartlett Warner</a></div>
11.11.11.11. 

O EYES that are weary, and hearts that are sore,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Look off unto Jesus and sorrow no more;
