The light of His countenance shineth so bright,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That on earth, as in heaven, there need be no night.

Looking off unto Jesus, my eyes cannot see
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The troubles and dangers that throng around me;
They cannot be blinded with sorrowful tears,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;They cannot be shadowed with unbelief fears.

Looking off unto Jesus, my spirit is blest;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In the world I have turmoil, in Him I have rest;
The sea of my life all about me may roar;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When I look unto Jesus I hear it no more.

Looking off unto Jesus, I go not astray;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My eyes are on Him, and He shows me the way;
The path may seem dark, as He leads me along,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But following Jesus, I cannot go wrong.

Looking off unto Jesus, my heart cannot fear,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Its trembling is still, when I see Jesus near;
I know that His power my safeguard will be,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For, “Why are you troubled?” He saith unto me.

Soon, soon shall I know the full beauty and grace
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of Jesus, my Lord, when I stand face to face;
I shall know how His love went before me each day,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And wonder that ever my eyes turned away.


388
Oh! for a closer walk with God!

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Cowper_William&#39;,this)">William Cowper</a></div>
C.M. 

OH! for a closer walk with God!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A calm and heavenly frame!
A light to shine upon the road
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That leads me to the Lamb!

Where is the blessèdness I knew,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When first I saw the Lord?
Where is the soul-refreshing view
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of Jesus and His word?

What peaceful hours I once enjoyed! 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;How sweet their memory still!
But they have left an aching void
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The world can never fill.

The dearest idol I have known,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Whate’er that idol be,
Help me to tear it from Thy throne,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And worship only Thee.

So shall my walk be close with God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Calm and serene my frame;
So purer light shall mark the road
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That leads me to the Lamb.


389
Oh for a thousand tongues to sing
Previous BHB #191
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Wesley_Charles&#39;,this)">Charles Wesley  (1739)</a></div>
C.M. 

OH for a thousand tongues to sing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My great Redeemer’s praise,
The glories of my God and King,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The triumphs of His grace!

My gracious Master and my God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Assist me to proclaim,
To spread through all the earth abroad,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The honours of Thy Name.

Jesus! the Name that charms our fears,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That bids our sorrows cease;
’Tis music in the sinner’s ears,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;’Tis life, and health, and peace.

He breaks the power of cancelled sin,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He sets the prisoner free;
His blood can make the foulest clean,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His blood availed for me.

I felt my Lord’s atoning blood
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Close to my soul applied;
Me, me He loved - the Son of God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For me, for me He died!

He speaks and, listening to His voice,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;New life the dead receive;
The mournful, broken hearts rejoice;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The humble poor believe.

Glory to God, and praise and love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Be ever, ever given;
By saints below and saints above,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Church in earth and heaven.


390
Oh, for the peace that floweth as a river!
Previous BHB #192<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Crewdson_Jane&#39;,this)">Jane Fox Crewdson  (1864)</a></div>
11.10.11.10. 

OH, for the peace that floweth as a river!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Making life’s desert places bloom and smile;
Oh, for the faith to grasp heaven’s bright “for ever”!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Amid the shadows of earth’s “little while”.

“A little while” for patient vigil keeping,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; To face the storm and wrestle with  the strong;
“A little while” to sow the seed with weeping,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Then bind the sheaves and sing the harvest song.

“A little while” the earthen pitcher taking
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To wayside brooks, from far-off fountains fed;
Then the parched lip its thirst for ever slaking
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Beside the fulness of the Fountain-head.

“A little while” to keep the oil from failing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“A little while” faith’s flick’ring lamp to trim;
And then the Bridegroom’s coming footsteps hailing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We’ll haste to meet Him with the bridal hymn.


391
Oh for the robes of whiteness
Previous BHB #193
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Bancroft_Charitie_Lee&#39;,this)">Charatie de Cheney Lees Smith Bancroft</a></div>
7.6.7.6.D. 

OH for the robes of whiteness,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Oh for the tearless eyes;
Oh for the glorious brightness
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of the unclouded skies!
Oh for the no more weeping
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Within the land of love;
The endless joy of keeping
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The bridal feast above!

Oh for the bliss of rising,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My risen Lord to meet;
Oh for the rest of lying
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For ever at His feet!
Oh for the hour of seeing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My Saviour face to face;
The hope of ever being
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In that sweet meeting-place!

Jesus, Thou King of Glory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We soon shall dwell with Thee!
We soon shall sing the story
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of love so rich and free;
Meanwhile our thoughts would enter
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;E’en now before Thy throne,
That all our love might centre
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On Thee, and Thee alone.


392
O glorious Lord! what thoughts Thy mind did fill

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Nunnerley_F_H&#39;,this)">H. F. Nunnerley</a></div>
10.10.10.10. 

O GLORIOUS Lord! what thoughts Thy mind did fill,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When from Thy God Thou cam’st to do His will!
How deep, indeed, the joy that filled Thy heart -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That myriad sons with Thee should find their part!

Thy brethren, Lord, Thine own and one with Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Were in Thy heart when dying on the tree;
Thy church complete and in Thy beauty dressed -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The day of God, and love divine at rest.

O blessèd Lord! those treasured thoughts unfold
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In light divine, as we Thy face behold!
And on our view unbounded  glories break,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That speak Thy fame and songs eternal wake.


393
O God, can we Thy Son forget

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Munro_M_George&#39;,this)">George M. Munro</a></div>
C.M. 

O GOD, can we Thy Son forget,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Forget His agony,
Forget His tears and blood-like sweat
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In dark Gethsemane?

Can we forget Him seized and led,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Forsaken by His own,
When all His friends afar had fled
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And left Him all alone?

Forget that base indignity
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Within the judgment hall, 
Forget Him led, in mockery,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Without the city wall?

Forget those piercèd hands and feet,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That thorn-crowned head bowed low,
Forget that sacrifice most sweet
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Upon that Cross of woe?

Forget Him? Nay! we love to dwell
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In this remembrance meet
Upon the One Who pleased Thee well
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And on His work complete.


394
O God His Father, rev’rently

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kirkpatrick_William&#39;,this)">William Kirkpatrick</a></div>
8.8.8.6. 

O GOD His Father, rev’rently
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We offer up our praise today,
Engaged in Thine appointed way
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With Thy Belovèd Son.

We look on One supremely great, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Ponder o’er ways immaculate, 
As we together meditate
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On Thy Belovèd Son.

That path, with moral glories bright, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Resplendent in Thy holy sight,
Showed nought to mar Thy heart’s delight
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In Thy Belovèd Son.

Performed in perfect purity, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A perfect service do we see, 
Rendered through every day to Thee
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;By Thy Belovèd Son.

Boundless in might, and yet so meek! 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In every circumstance unique! 
None else we see, and only speak
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of Thy Belovèd Son.


395
O God of Bethel! by whose hand
Previous BHB #422<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Doddridge_Philip&#39;,this)">Philip Doddridge</a> & <a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Logan_John_Doddridge_Philip&#39;,this)">John Logan</a> & others  (1737)</div>
C.M. 

O GOD of Bethel! by whose hand
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy people still are fed;
Who through this weary pilgrimage
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hast all our fathers led.

Our vows, our prayers, we now present
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Before Thy throne of grace:
God of our fathers! be the God
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of their succeeding race.

Through each perplexing path of life
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our wandering footsteps guide;
Give us each day our daily bread,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And raiment fit provide.

O spread Thy covering wings around
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Till all our wanderings cease,
And at our Father’s loved abode
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our souls arrive in peace.

Such blessings from Thy gracious hand
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our humble prayers implore;
And Thou shalt be our chosen God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And portion evermore.


396
O God of everlasting days

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Fotheringham_Joseph&#39;,this)">Joseph Fotheringham</a></div>
C.M. 

O GOD of everlasting days,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Through Jesus Christ our Lord, 
In His own Name we gladly raise 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our praise with one accord.

We magnify His Name Who shared 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy glory in the past,
And marvel at His grace Who cared
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His lot with man to cast.

Through Him were made the heavens and earth,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And yet, in lowliness,
He graced a manger at His birth
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy glory to express.

In meekness and obedience through
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His youth’s obscurity,
He in Thy holy favour grew,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In perfect purity.

And when the Adversary sought
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To reach His heart within
That life to mar, in Him was nought 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That could respond to sin.

We thank Thee for the path He trod,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For every kindness shown,
The way He moved for Thee, His God, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And made Thy glory known.


397
O God of glorious majesty

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Pennefather_William&#39;,this)">William Pennefather</a></div>
C.M.D. 

O GOD of glorious majesty,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Messiah, King of grace,
Unveil to us Thy loveliness,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And let us see Thy face!
Obedient to Thy loving voice,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We’ve turned aside awhile,
To wait beside Thy guiding feet,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And rest beneath Thy smile.

Oh, nerve us for the conflict, Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That thickens day by day,
That we, amidst our alien foes,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy banner may display!
We’ve but a little while to fight,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To work, to wait for Thee;
Help us to labour in Thy cause
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With holy energy.

Help us upon our watch to stand,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And never quail for fear,
Till in the glowing eastern sky
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Morning Star appear;
Then with Thy waiting saints above,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thine advent, Lord, we’ll hail,
And over death, and sin, and woe,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We’ll joyfully prevail.


398
O God of matchless grace
Previous BHB #185<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Burlingham_Hannah_Killham&#39;,this)">Hannah Kilham Burlingham</a></div>
6.6.8.6.8.8. 

O GOD of matchless grace,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We sing unto Thy Name!
We stand accepted in the place
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That none but Christ could claim.
Our willing hearts have heard Thy voice,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And in Thy mercy we rejoice.

’Tis meet that Thy delight
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Should centre in Thy Son;
That Thou shouldst place us in Thy sight,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In Him, Thy Holy One.
Thy perfect love has cast out fear;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy favour shines upon us here.

Eternal is our rest,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O Christ of God, in Thee!
Now of Thy peace, Thy joy possessed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We wait Thy face to see.
Now to the Father’s heart received,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We know in whom we have believed.

A sacrifice to God
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In life or death are we;
Then keep us ever, blessèd Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thus set apart to Thee.
Bought with a price, we’re not our own;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We died, we live to God alone!


399
O God our Father, we would come to Thee

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Dick_B_W&#39;,this)">W. B. Dick</a></div>
10.10.10.10.

O GOD our Father, we would come to Thee
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In virtue of our Saviour’s precious blood;
All distance gone, our souls by grace set free,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We worship Thee, our Father and our God.

We would, O God, present before Thy face
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The fragrant name of Thy belovèd Son;
By faith we view Him in that holy  place,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Which, by His dying, He for us has won.

Thy joy in Him who is with Thee we share;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our hearts delight in Thy delight in Him;
Chiefest of thousands, fairer than the fair;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His glory nought can tarnish, nought can dim.

We bow in worship now before Thy throne,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;By faith the Object of Thy love would see;
Who, in the midst, His brethren’s song doth lead.
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Him, our Saviour, shall the glory be!


400
O God, our help in ages past
Previous BHB #207
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Watts_Isaac&#39;,this)">Isaac Watts  (1719)</a></div>
C.M.

O GOD, our help in ages past,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our hope for years to come,
Our shelter from the stormy blast,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And our eternal home.

Under the shadow of Thy throne
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy saints have dwelt secure;
Sufficient is Thine arm alone,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And our defence is sure.

Before the hills in order stood,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Or earth received her frame,
From everlasting Thou art God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To endless years the same.

A thousand ages in Thy sight
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Are like an evening gone;
Short as the watch that ends the night
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Before the rising sun.

Time, like an ever-rolling stream,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Bears all its sons away; 
They fly, forgotten, as a dream
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Dies at the op’ning day.

O God, our help in ages past,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our hope for years to come,
Be Thou our guard while life shall last,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And our eternal home.


401
O God, Thou now hast glorified

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Deck_James_George&#39;,this)">James George Deck</a></div>
8.8.8.8.8.8.

O GOD, Thou now hast glorified
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy holy, Thine eternal Son!
The Nazarene, the Crucified,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Now sits exalted on Thy throne:
To Him, in faith, we cry aloud,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“Worthy art Thou, O Lamb of God!”

Father, Thy holy name we bless,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And gladly hail Thy just decree,
That every tongue shall yet confess 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus the Lord of all to be;
But Thy rich grace has taught us now 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Him as Lord the knee to bow. 

Him as our Lord we gladly own,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Him alone we now would live:
We bow our hearts before Thy throne,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And in His name our praises give;
Our willing voices cry aloud,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“Worthy art Thou, O Lamb of God!”


402
O happy day, that fixed my choice
Previous BHB #188
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Doddridge_Philip&#39;,this)">Philip Doddridge  (c. 1755)</a></div>
P.M.

O HAPPY day, that fixed my choice
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On Thee, my Saviour and my God!
Well may this glowing heart rejoice,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And tell its raptures all abroad.

c
Happy day! happy day!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When Jesus washed my sins away.
He taught me how to watch and pray,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And live rejoicing every day.
Happy day! happy day!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When Jesus washed my sins away.

’Tis done - the great transaction’s done!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I am my Lord’s, and He is mine;
He drew me, and I followed on,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Charmed to confess the voice divine.

c
Happy day! happy day!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When Jesus washed my sins away.
He taught me how to watch and pray,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And live rejoicing every day.
Happy day! happy day!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When Jesus washed my sins away.

Now rest, my long-divided heart,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Fixed on this blissful centre, rest;
Nor ever from Thy Lord depart,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With Him of every good possessed.

c
Happy day! happy day!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When Jesus washed my sins away.
He taught me how to watch and pray,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And live rejoicing every day.
Happy day! happy day!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When Jesus washed my sins away.

High heaven that heard the solemn vow,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That vow renewed shall daily hear,
Till in life’s latest hour I bow
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And bless in death a bond so dear. 

c
Happy day! happy day!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When Jesus washed my sins away.
He taught me how to watch and pray,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And live rejoicing every day.
Happy day! happy day!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When Jesus washed my sins away.


403
O happy day! when first we felt
Previous BHB #189<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Deck_James_George&#39;,this)">James George Deck  (1841)</a></div>
L.M.

O HAPPY day! when first we felt
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our souls with deep contrition melt,
And saw our sins of crimson guilt,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All cleansed by blood on Calvary spilt.

O happy day! when first Thy love
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Began our grateful hearts to move;
And gazing on Thy wondrous Cross,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We saw all else as worthless dross.

O happy day! when we no more
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shall grieve Thee whom our souls  adore,
When sorrows, conflicts, fears shall cease,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And all our trials end in peace.

O happy day! when we shall see
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And fix our longing eyes on Thee,
On Thee, our Light, our Life, our Love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our All below, our heaven above.

O happy day of cloudless light!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Eternal day without a night;
Lord, when shall we its dawning see,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And spend it all in praising Thee?


404
O happy home! O happy home!

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Mackey_Dr_William_Paton&#39;,this)">William Paton Mackay</a></div>
C.M. with Chorus

O HAPPY home! O happy home!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Prepared by Jesus’ hand;
We soon shall be within thy walls,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With all the ransomed band.

c
Where sinners saved by grace,
Redeemed by Jesus’ blood,
Shall see Him face to face -
Their Saviour and their God.

O happy home! O happy home!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Where come no cares nor fears,
Nor death, nor sorrows, for God’s hand
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shall wipe away all tears -

c
Where sinners saved by grace,
Redeemed by Jesus’ blood,
Shall see Him face to face -
Their Saviour and their God.

O happy home! O happy home!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Where we shall sin no more;
Where spotless, sinless, perfect, pure
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We’ll dwell for evermore -

c
Where sinners saved by grace,
Redeemed by Jesus’ blood,
Shall see Him face to face -
Their Saviour and their God.

O happy home! O happy home!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Where Jesus we shall see;
When He appears in glory bright,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Like Him we all shall be -

c
Where sinners saved by grace,
Redeemed by Jesus’ blood,
Shall see Him face to face -
Their Saviour and their God.

O happy home! O happy home!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;May thy bright, glorious ray
Pierce through the clouds, and cheer our steps,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Here on our pilgrim way! - 

c
Where sinners saved by grace,
Redeemed by Jesus’ blood,
Shall see Him face to face -
Their Saviour and their God.


405
O head, so full of bruises
Previous BHB #187
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Gerhardt_Paulus&#39;,this)">Paul Gerhardt</a></div>
7.6.7.6.D.

O HEAD, so full of bruises,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;So full of pain and scorn,
’Midst other sore abuses,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Mocked with a crown of thorn!
O Head, ere now surrounded
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With brightest majesty,
In death once bowed and wounded
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Accursèd on the tree!

Thou Countenance transcendent,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou life-creating Sun
To worlds on Thee dependent,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Yet bruised and spit upon!
O Lord! what Thee tormented
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Was our sin’s heavy load;
We had the debt augmented
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Which Thou didst pay in blood!

Thy grief and Thy compassion
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Were all for sinners’ gain;
Mine, mine was the transgression,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But Thine the deadly pain.
Beneath Thy Cross abiding
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Forever would I rest,
In Thy dear love confiding,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And with Thy presence blest.

And oh, what consolation
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Doth in our hearts take place,
When we Thy toil and passion
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Can gratefully retrace!
Ah! should we, while thus musing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On our Redeemer’s Cross,
E’en life itself be losing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Great gain would be that loss!

We give Thee thanks unfeignèd,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lord Jesus, Friend in need!
For what Thy soul sustainèd
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When Thou for us didst bleed;
Grant us to lean unshaken
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Upon Thy faithfulness,
Until, to glory taken,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We see Thee face to face.


406
Oh, how happy are they

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Wesley_Charles&#39;,this)">Charles Wesley </a></div>
P.M.

OH, how happy are they,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who the Saviour obey,
And have laid up their treasures above!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Tongue can never express
The sweet comfort and peace
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of a soul in its earliest love.

c
It is good to be here,
it is good to be here;
Thy perfect love drives away fear,
And light streaming down makes the pathway all clear:
It is good for us, Lord, to be here.

That sweet comfort was mine,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When the favour divine
I received through the blood of the Lamb; 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When my heart first believed,
What a joy I received, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What a heaven in Jesus’ name!

c
It is good to be here, 
it is good to be here;
Thy perfect love drives away fear,
And light streaming down makes the pathway all clear:
It is good for us, Lord, to be here.

’Twas a heaven below
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My Redeemer to know; 
And the angels could do nothing more
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Than to fall at His feet,
And the story repeat,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And the Lover of sinners adore.

c
It is good to be here, 
it is good to be here;
Thy perfect love drives away fear,
And light streaming down makes the pathway all clear:
It is good for us, Lord, to be here.

Jesus, all the day long
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Was my joy and my song;
Oh, that all His salvation might see!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“He hath loved me”, I cried;
“He hath suffered and died,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To redeem a poor rebel as me”.

c
It is good to be here, 
it is good to be here;
Thy perfect love drives away fear,
And light streaming down makes the pathway all clear:
It is good for us, Lord, to be here.

Oh! the rapturous height
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of that holy delight
Which I felt in the life-giving blood!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of my Saviour possessed,
I was perfectly blest 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As if filled with the fulness of God.

c
It is good to be here, 
it is good to be here;
Thy perfect love drives away fear,
And light streaming down makes the pathway all clear:
It is good for us, Lord, to be here.


407
Oh, it was kind of Him!

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Ewan_Y_Isaac&#39;,this)">Isaac Y. Ewan</a></div>
6.4.6.4.6.6.6.4.

OH, it was kind of Him!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Blest be His Name! 
Unto death binding Him,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Love drawn He came 
Bearing our form below, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Draining our cup of woe, 
Deeper than we could know;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Blest be His Name!

Sorrows gat hold of Him,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Suff’rings untold, 
Darkness enfolded Him;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Over Him rolled 
Billows of judgment dread, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Loosed on His sinless head 
Thorn-crownèd in our stead; 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Blest be His Name!

Earth wore a sullen frown,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Heaven hid its light,
When God’s own Son went down
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Into the night,
Under the chilling wave,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Into the silent grave, 
Sin-stricken souls to save;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Blest be His Name!

Gone is the captive’s chain
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Blest be His Name!
Rent is the veil in twain,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Blest be His Name! 
Deigning for us to die, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To God He brought us nigh 
Over a Jordan dry;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Blest be His Name!


408
O Jesus Christ, grow Thou in me
Previous BHB #424<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Lavater_Johann_Caspar&#39;,this)">Johann Caspar Lavater</a> tr/ad by Elizabeth Lee Allen Smith  (1780)</div>
C.M.

O JESUS CHRIST, grow Thou in me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And all things else recede;
My heart be daily nearer Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From sin be daily freed.

Each day let Thy supporting might
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My weakness still embrace;
My darkness vanish in Thy light,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy life my death efface.

In Thy bright beams which on me fall,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Fade every evil thought;
That I am nothing, Thou art all,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I would be daily taught.

More of Thy glory let me see,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou holy, wise and true;
I would Thy living image be,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In joy and sorrow too.

Fill me with gladness from above,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hold me by strength divine;
Lord, let the glow of Thy great love
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Through my whole being shine.

Make this poor self grow less and less,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Be Thou my life and aim;
Oh make me daily, through Thy grace,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;More meet to bear Thy name.


409
O Jesus, I have promised
Previous BHB #425
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Bode_John_Ernest&#39;,this)">John Ernest Bode  (1868)</a></div>
7.6.7.6.D.

O JESUS, I have promised
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To serve Thee to the end;
Be Thou for ever near me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My Master and my Friend!
I shall not fear the battle,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;If Thou art by my side;
Nor wander from the pathway
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;If Thou wilt be my Guide.

Oh, let me feel Thee near me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The world is ever near;
I see the sights that dazzle,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The tempting sounds I hear;
My foes are ever near me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Around me and within;
But, Jesus, draw Thou nearer,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And shield my soul from sin.

Oh, let me hear Thee speaking,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In accents clear and still,
Above the storms of passion,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The murmurs of self-will.
Oh speak to re-assure me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To hasten or control;
Oh speak, and make me listen,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou Guardian of my soul.

O Jesus, Thou hast promised
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To all who follow Thee,
That where Thou art in glory
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;There shall Thy servant be!
And, Jesus, I have promised
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To serve Thee to the end;
Oh, give me grace to follow,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My Master and my Friend!

Oh, let me see Thy footmarks,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And in them plant mine own;
My hope to follow duly
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Is in Thy strength alone;
Oh, guide me, call me, draw me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Uphold me to the end;
And then in heaven receive me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My Saviour and my Friend!


410
O Jesus, Lord, ’tis Thee alone

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Peters_Mary&#39;,this)">Mary Bowly Peters</a></div>
8.8.8.6.

O JESUS, Lord, ’tis Thee alone
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Holy Spirit would enthrone
In every heart, that we may own
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou art our chiefest joy.

Feebly Thy value we conceive;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Yet with our hearts we do believe,
And would confess from morn to eve,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou art our chiefest joy.

When unbelief its discord flings
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Across our harps, or stills their strings,
Touched by Thy love our spirit sings,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou art our chiefest joy.

Should this world’s dazzling, transient light
Turn from eternal things our sight,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Be Thou than noonday sun more  bright;
Thou art our chiefest joy.

And when our Father’s face we see,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In unveiled brightness shine in Thee,
We’ll sing, in glorious liberty,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou art our chiefest joy.


411
O joy of the justified, joy of the free!
Previous BHB #186
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Bottome_Francis&#39;,this)">Francis (Frank) Bottome</a></div>
P.M.

O JOY of the justified, joy of the free!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I’m washed in that crimson tide opened for me;
In Christ, my Redeemer, rejoicing I stand,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And point to the print of the nail in His hand.

c
O sing of His mighty love,
Sing of His mighty love,
Sing of His mighty love,
Mighty to save!

Lord Jesus, the crucified, now Thou art mine;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Though once a lost sinner, yet now I am Thine;
In conscious salvation I sing of His grace
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who lifts now upon me the smile of His face.

c
O sing of His mighty love,
Sing of His mighty love,
Sing of His mighty love,
Mighty to save!

Oh, bliss of the purified! bliss of the pure!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No wound hath the soul that His  blood cannot cure;
No sorrow-bowed head but may sweetly find rest,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No tears but may dry them upon Jesus’ breast. 

c
O sing of His mighty love,
Sing of His mighty love,
Sing of His mighty love,
Mighty to save!

Lord Jesus, my Saviour, I’ll still sing of Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Yes, sing of Thy precious blood poured out for me;
And when in the mansions of glory above,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I’ll praise and adore Thine unchangeable love.

c
O sing of His mighty love,
Sing of His mighty love,
Sing of His mighty love,
Mighty to save!


412
O Lamb of God! still keep us
Previous BHB #197
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Deck_James_George&#39;,this)">James George Deck  (1841)</a></div>
7.6.7.6.D.

O LAMB of God! still keep us
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Near to Thy wounded side;
’Tis only there in safety
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And peace we can abide!
What foes and snares surround us!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What lusts and fears within!
The grace that sought and found us
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Alone can keep us clean.

’Tis only in Thee hiding
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We know our life secure;
Only in Thee abiding
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The conflict can endure.
Thine arm the victory gaineth
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O’er every hurtful foe;
Thy love each heart sustaineth
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In all its cares and woe.

Soon shall our eyes behold Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With rapture, face to face;
The half hath not been told us
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of all Thy power and grace.
Thy beauty, Lord, and glory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The wonders of Thy love,
Shall be the endless story
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of all Thy saints above!


413
O Lamb of God! ’tis joy to know
Previous BHB #196<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Deck_James_George&#39;,this)">James George Deck  (1841)</a></div>
8.8.6.8.8.6.

O LAMB of God! ’tis joy to know
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That path is o’er of shame and woe,
For us so meekly trod.
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All finished is Thy work of toil;
Thou reapest now the fruit and spoil,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Exalted by our God.

Thy holy head, once bound with thorns,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The crown of glory now adorns;
Thy seat, the Father’s throne.
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Ten thousand thousands sing Thy praise,
Their harps the eternal anthem raise,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Worthy the Lamb alone!

And, Lord, for us Thou sittest there;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy members here Thy fulness  share,
For us Thou dost receive.
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy wisdom, riches, honours,  powers,
Thy boundless love has made all ours,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who in Thy love believe.

We triumph in Thy triumphs, Lord;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy joys our deepest joys afford,
And make our faces shine.
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;While sorrowing, suffering, toiling  here,
How does the thought our spirits cheer,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The throne of glory’s Thine.


414
O Lamb of God, we lift our eyes
Previous BHB #346<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Stewart_Alexander&#39;,this)">Alexander Stewart</a></div>
C.M.

O LAMB of God, we lift our eyes
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Thee amidst the throne;
Shine on us, bid Thy light arise,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And make Thy glory known.

We know Thy work for ever done,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Ourselves alive and free;
Graced with the Spirit of the Son,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Made nigh to God in Thee.

Yet would we prove Thine instant grace,
Thy present power would feel;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lift on us now Thy glorious face,
Thyself, O Lord, reveal.

From Thy high place of purest light,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O Lamb, amidst the throne,
Shine forth upon our waiting sight,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And make Thy glory known.


415
O Lord, how infinite Thy love!

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Lyte_Henry_Francis&#39;,this)">Henry Francis Lyte</a></div>
C.M.

O LORD, how infinite Thy love!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;It spans all time and space,
Enwraps Thy loved ones to the end
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In its encircling grace.

We shew Thy death till Thy return -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou for our sins didst die!
“Remember Me”, Thy parting word;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“Amen”, our hearts reply.

In life and glory one with Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We own no place below,
Save that which links us with Thy death,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Whence life and glory flow.

With Thee, apart from all things here,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We worship, we adore;
While pleasures at our God’s right hand
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Await us evermore.


416
O Lord, how much Thy Name unfolds
Previous BHB #201<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Peters_Mary&#39;,this)">Mary Bowly Peters</a></div>
C.M.

O LORD, how much Thy Name unfolds
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To every opened ear;
The pardoned sinner’s memory holds
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;None other half so dear.

Jesus! it speaks a life of love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And sorrows meekly borne;
It tells of sympathy above,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Whatever griefs we mourn.

It tells us of Thy sinless walk
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In fellowship with God;
And to our ears no tale so sweet
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As Thine atoning blood.

This Name encircles every grace
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That God, as man, could show;
There only can the Spirit trace
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A perfect life below.

The mention of Thy Name shall bow
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our hearts to worship Thee;
The chiefest of ten thousand Thou!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The chief of sinners we.


417
O Lord, I would delight in Thee
Previous BHB #200
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Ryland_John&#39;,this)">John Ryland  (1777)</a></div>
C.M.

O LORD, I would delight in Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And on Thy care depend;
To Thee in every trouble flee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My sure, my steadfast Friend.

When all created streams are dried,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy fulness is the same;
May I with this be satisfied,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And glory in Thy Name.

Why should I thirst for aught below,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;While there’s a fountain near -
A fountain which doth ever flow,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The fainting heart to cheer?

Oh that I may by simple faith
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Abide within the veil,
And rest on what my Saviour saith,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Whose word can never fail!

Thou who hast made my heaven secure,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Wilt here all good provide;
While Christ is rich, can I be poor,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What can I want beside?

O Lord, I cast my care on Thee;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I triumph and adore;
Henceforth my great concern shall be
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To love and praise Thee more.


418
O Lord, it is Thyself; none, none but Thee

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Gresswell_E_J&#39;,this)">J. E. Gresswell</a></div>
10.10.10.10.

O LORD, it is Thyself; none, none but Thee
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Could so call forth response from every heart;
The love that stood the test of Calvary’s tree
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Doth to our longing souls fresh joy impart.

Thou speakest, Lord, of Him Thou hast revealed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy Father, whom Thou lov’st - His glory, Thine.
Thou, His eternal joy, Thyself didst yield
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To bring to pass His thought of love divine.

Thy heart, our God, made known - all, all is told!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The glory of Thy love, all time before,
He to our raptured hearts doth  now unfold,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And moves our souls to worship and adore.

O circle of affections all divine,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The foretaste of eternity’s bright  scene,
Where all the glories of His love shall shine
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In everlasting joy and peace serene!


419
O Lord, it is Thyself to meet
Previous BHB #427<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Thompson_Boethia&#39;,this)">Boethia Thompson</a></div>
C.M.D.

O LORD, it is Thyself to meet,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To this sweet feast we come;
Like Mary, resting at Thy feet,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We learn of Thee alone:
We well remember Thou hast said,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;This do, remembering Me;
So thus we take the wine, the bread,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In memory of Thee.

O Lord, we come, not for our need,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Nor with our grief to Thee;
But on the bread and wine to feed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And to remember Thee:
Yes, to remember all Thy love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Remember all Thy woe,
Remember Thou art now above,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Yet in our midst below.

O Lord, from Thee the bread we take,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From Thy pierced hand the wine;
In rest - accepted for Thy sake -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our meetness, Lord, is Thine:
We praise Thee for this quiet hour
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Spent with Thyself alone,
In which we feel the Spirit’s power,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And all His teachings own.

O Lord, we come, for Thou art here;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Enrich each memory;
Thy faithful promise brings Thee near
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And gathers us to Thee:
O body broken! poured out blood!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Blest memories ever dear;
Thou Son of man! Thou Lamb of God!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;How good to meet Thee here!


420
How Great Thou Art

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Boberg_Carl&#39;,this)">Carl Boberg</a> and <a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Hine_Stuart_K&#39;,this)">Stuart K. Hine</a></div><p>© 1949 and 1953 by the Stuart Hine Trust CIO. All rights in the USA its territories and possessions, except print rights, administered by Capitol CMG Publishing. USA, North, Central and South America print rights and all Canadian rights administered by Hope Publishing Company. All other non US Americas rights administered by the Stuart Hine Trust CIO. Rest of the world rights administered by Integrity Music Europe.</p>
P.M.

O LORD my God! when I in awesome wonder
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Consider all the works Thy hands have made.
I see the stars, I hear the rolling thunder, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy power throughout the universe displayed. 

c
Then sings my soul, My Saviour God, to Thee:
How great Thou art! How great Thou art!
Then sings my soul, My Saviour God, to Thee:
How great Thou art! How great Thou art!

When through the woods and forest glades I wander,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And hear the birds sing sweetly in the trees;
When I look down from lofty mountain grandeur,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And hear the brook and feel the gentle breeze:

c
Then sings my soul, My Saviour God, to Thee:
How great Thou art! How great Thou art!
Then sings my soul, My Saviour God, to Thee:
How great Thou art! How great Thou art!

And when I think that God, His Son not sparing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sent Him to die, I scarce can take it in;
That on the Cross, my burden gladly bearing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He bled and died to take away my sin:

c
Then sings my soul, My Saviour God, to Thee:
How great Thou art! How great Thou art!
Then sings my soul, My Saviour God, to Thee:
How great Thou art! How great Thou art!

When burdens press, and seem beyond endurance,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Bowed down with grief, to Him I lift my face;
And then in love He brings me sweet assurance:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“My child! for thee sufficient is My grace”.

c
Then sings my soul, My Saviour God, to Thee:
How great Thou art! How great Thou art!
Then sings my soul, My Saviour God, to Thee:
How great Thou art! How great Thou art!

When Christ shall come with shout of acclamation,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And take me home, what joy shall fill my heart!
Then I shall bow in humble adoration,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And there proclaim, “My God, how  great Thou art!”

c
Then sings my soul, My Saviour God, to Thee:
How great Thou art! How great Thou art!
Then sings my soul, My Saviour God, to Thee:
How great Thou art! How great Thou art!


421
O Lord of heaven, and earth, and sea!
Previous BHB #426
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Wordsworth_Christopher&#39;,this)">Christopher Wordsworth  (1863)</a></div>
8.8.8.4.

O LORD of heaven, and earth, and sea!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Thee all praise and glory be:
How shall we show our love to Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who givest all?

For peaceful homes and healthful days,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For all the blessings earth displays,
Our God, we owe Thee thanks and praise,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who givest all.

Thou didst not spare Thine only Son,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But gav’st Him for a world undone,
And freely with that Blessèd One  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou givest all.

Thou gav’st the Holy Spirit’s dower, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Spirit of life, of love and power,
And dost His sevenfold graces shower  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Upon us all.

For souls redeemed, for sins forgiven,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For present grace and hopes of  heaven,
Father, what can to Thee be given,  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who givest all?

We lose what on ourselves we spend;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We have as treasure without end
Whatever, Lord, to Thee we lend,  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who givest all.

As from Thyself we all derive -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our life, our gifts, our power to give,
Oh, may we ever to Thee live  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who givest all!


422
O Lord, Thy love’s unbounded!
Previous BHB #206
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Darby_John_Nelson&#39;,this)">John Nelson Darby</a></div>
7.6.7.6.D.

O LORD, Thy love’s unbounded!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;So sweet, so full, so free!
My soul is all transported
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Whene’er I think on Thee.
Yet, Lord, alas! what weakness
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Within myself I find;
No infant’s changing pleasure
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Is like my wandering mind.

And yet Thy love’s unchanging,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And doth recall my heart
To joy in all its brightness,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The peace its beams impart!
Yet, sure, if in Thy presence
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My soul still constant were,
Mine eye would, more familiar,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Its brighter glories bear.

And thus Thy deep perfections
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Much better should I know,
And, with adoring fervour
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Should in Thy likeness grow.
Still, sweet ’tis to discover,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;If clouds have dimmed my sight,
When passed, eternal Lover,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Towards me, as e’er, Thou’rt bright.

Oh, keep my soul, Lord Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Abiding still with Thee;
And if I wander, teach me
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Soon back to Thee to flee,
That all Thy gracious favour
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;May to my soul be known;
And versed in this Thy goodness,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My hopes Thyself shalt crown.


423
O Lord! ’tis joy to look above
Previous BHB #199<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Deck_James_George&#39;,this)">James George Deck  (1841)</a></div>
C.M.

O LORD! ’tis joy to look above,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And see Thee on the throne;
To search the heights and depths of love
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Which Thou to us hast shown.

To look beyond the dark, long night
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And hail the coming day,
When Thou, to all the saints in light,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy glories wilt display.

And, oh, ’tis joy the path to trace
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;By Thee so meekly trod;
Learning of Thee to walk in grace
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And fellowship with God.

Joy to confess Thy blessèd Name,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The virtues of Thy blood,
And to the wearied heart proclaim,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Behold the Lamb of God!


424
O Lord, when we the path retrace
Previous BHB #202<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Deck_James_George&#39;,this)">James George Deck  (1841)</a></div>
C.M.

O LORD, when we the path retrace
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Which Thou on earth hast trod,
To man Thy wondrous love and grace,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy faithfulness to God.

Thy love, by man so sorely tried,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Proved stronger than the grave;
The very spear that pierced Thy side
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Drew forth the blood to save.

Faithful amidst unfaithfulness,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Midst darkness only light,
Thou didst Thy Father’s Name confess,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And in His will delight.

Unmoved by Satan’s subtle wiles,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;By suffering, shame, and loss;
Thy path, uncheered by earthly smiles,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Led only to the Cross.

O Lord, with sorrow and with shame,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We meekly would confess,
How little we, who bear Thy Name,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy mind, Thy ways express.

Give us Thy meek, Thy lowly mind;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We would obedient be;
And all our rest and pleasure find
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In fellowship with Thee.


425
O Lord, where’er Thy people meet
Previous BHB #204
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Cowper_William&#39;,this)">William Cowper</a></div>
L.M.

O LORD, where’er Thy people meet,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;There they behold Thy mercy-seat:
Where’er they seek Thee, Thou art found,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And every place is hallowed ground.

Great Shepherd of Thy chosen few
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy former mercies here renew;
Here to our waiting hearts proclaim
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The sweetness of Thy saving Name.

Here may we prove the power of prayer
To strengthen faith and banish care;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To teach our faint desires to rise,
And bring all heaven before our eyes.

Lord! we are weak, but Thou art near, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Nor short Thine arm, nor deaf  Thine ear;
Oh, fill us with Thy grace divine,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And may our hearts be wholly Thine!


426
O Lord, who art Thy people’s light
Previous BHB #198<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Denny_Sir_Edward&#39;,this)">Sir Edward Denny</a></div>
C.M.

O LORD, who art Thy people’s light,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When shall Thy face be seen?
When wilt Thou meet our longing sight,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Without a cloud between?

We know, Lord Jesus, that Thy heart
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Still for Thy saints doth care;
But we would see Thee as Thou art,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And Thy full image bear.

Thy love sustains us on our way,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;While pilgrims here below;
And grace to help us day by day
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou dost, O Lord, bestow.

But oh, the more we learn of Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And Thy rich mercy prove,
The more we long Thyself to see,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And fully know Thy love.


427
O Lord, who now art seated
Previous BHB #205<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Deck_James_George&#39;,this)">James George Deck  (1841)</a></div>
7.6.7.6.D.

O LORD, who now art seated
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Above the heavens on high,
The gracious work completed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For which Thou cam’st to die.
To Thee our hearts are lifted
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;While pilgrims wand’ring here,
For Thou art truly gifted
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our every weight to bear.

We know that Thou hast bought us,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And cleansed us by Thy blood;
We know Thy grace has brought us
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As kings and priests to God.
We know that soon the morning,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Long looked for, hast’neth near,
When we, at Thy returning,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In glory shall appear.

O Lord, Thy love’s unbounded!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;So full, so vast, so free!
Our thoughts are all confounded
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Whene’er we think on Thee.
For us Thou cam’st from heaven,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For us to bleed and die;
That, purchased and forgiven,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We might ascend on high.

O let this love constrain us
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To give our hearts to Thee;
Let nothing henceforth pain us,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But that which paineth Thee.
Our joy, our one endeavour,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Through suff’ring, conflict, shame,
To serve Thee, gracious Saviour,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And magnify Thy Name.


428
O love divine, how sweet Thou art!
Previous BHB #203
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Wesley_Charles&#39;,this)">Charles Wesley  (1749)</a></div>
8.8.6.8.8.6.

O LOVE divine, how sweet Thou art!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When shall I find my longing heart
All taken up by Thee?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Oh, may I pant and thirst to prove
The greatness of redeeming love!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The love of Christ to me.

God only knows the love of God:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Oh that it more were shed abroad
In this poor longing heart!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For love I sigh, for love I pine;
This only portion, Lord, be mine,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Be mine this better part.

Oh that I may for ever sit,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Like Mary, at the Master’s feet!
Be this my happy choice;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My only care, delight, and bliss,
My joy, my heaven on earth, be this -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To hear the Bridegroom’s voice.

Oh that I may, like favoured John,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Recline my wearied head upon
My dear Redeemer’s breast!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From care, and sin, and sorrow free,
Give me, O Lord, to find in Thee
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My everlasting rest!


429
O love that wilt not let me go
Previous BHB #428<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Matheson_George&#39;,this)">George Matheson  (1882)</a></div>
8.8.8.8.6.

O LOVE that wilt not let me go,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I rest my weary soul in Thee;
I give Thee back the life I owe,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That in Thine ocean depths its flow
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;May richer, fuller be.

O Light that followest all my way,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I yield my flickering torch to Thee;
My heart restores its borrowed ray,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That in Thy sunshine’s blaze its day
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;May brighter, fairer be.

O Joy that seekest me through pain,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I cannot close my heart to Thee;
I trace the rainbow through the rain,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And feel the promise is not vain
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That morn shall tearless be.

O Cross that liftest up my head,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I dare not ask to fly from Thee,
I lay in dust life’s glory dead,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And from the ground there  blossoms red
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Life that shall endless be.


430
O Master! when Thou callest
Previous BHB #429<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Stock_Sarah_Geraldine&#39;,this)">Sarah Geraldina Stock  (1888)</a></div>
7.6.7.6.D.

O MASTER! when Thou callest
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No voice may say Thee nay,
For blest are they that follow
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Where Thou dost lead the way:
In freshest prime of morning,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Or fullest glow of noon,
The note of heavenly warning
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Can never come too soon.

O Master! where Thou callest
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No foot may shrink in fear,
For they who trust Thee wholly
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shall find Thee ever near;
And quiet room and lonely,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Or busy harvest field,
Where Thou, Lord, rulest only,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shall precious produce yield.

O Master! whom Thou callest
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No heart may dare refuse;
’Tis honour, highest honour,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When Thou dost deign to use;
Our brightest and our fairest,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our dearest - all are Thine;
Thou who for each one carest,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We hail Thy love’s design.

They who go forth to serve Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We, too, who serve at home,
May watch and pray together
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Until Thou, Lord, shalt come;
In Thee for aye united
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our song of hope we raise,
Till that blest shore is sighted
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Where all shall turn to praise.


431
O our Saviour, crucified!
Previous BHB #210<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Chapman_Robert_Cleaver&#39;,this)">Robert Cleaver Chapman</a></div>
7.7.7.7.

O OUR Saviour, crucified!
Near Thy Cross would we abide,
There to look with steadfast eye
On Thy dying agony.

Jesus bruised and put to shame
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Tells us all Jehovah’s Name;
God is love, we surely know
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;By the Saviour’s depths of woe.

In His spotless soul’s distress
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We perceive our guiltiness;
O how vile our low estate!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Since our ransom was so great.

Dwelling on Mount Calvary,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Contrite shall our spirits be;
Rest and holiness shall find,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Fashioned like our Saviour’s mind.


432
O patient, spotless One!
Previous BHB #215
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Bernsten_Christian_Andreas&#39;,this)">Christian Andreas Bernstein</a></div>
S.M.

O PATIENT, spotless One!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our hearts in meekness train,
To bear Thy yoke, and learn of Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That we may rest obtain.

Saviour, Thou art enough 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The mind and heart to fill;
Thy patient life - to calm the soul; 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy love - its fear dispel.

O fix our earnest gaze
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;So wholly, Lord, on Thee,
That, with Thy beauty occupied,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We elsewhere none may see.


433
O precious blood, O glorious death
Previous BHB #216<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Toplady_Augustus_Montague&#39;,this)">Augustus Montague Toplady</a></div>
C.M.

O PRECIOUS blood, O glorious death,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;By which the sinner lives!
When stung by sin, this blood we view,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And all our joy revives.

The blood that purchased our release,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And purged our crimson stains,
We challenge earth and hell to show
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A sin it cannot cleanse.

Our scarlet crimes are made as wool,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And we brought nigh to God;
Thanks to that wrath-appeasing death,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That heaven-procuring blood.

The blood that makes His glorious Church 
From every blemish free;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And oh the riches of His love! 
He poured it out for me.

The Father’s everlasting love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And Jesus’ precious blood,
Shall be our endless themes of praise
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In yonder blest abode.

In patience let us then possess
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our souls till He appear;
Our Head already is in heaven,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And we shall soon be there. 


434
O precious exercise to strike the priestly chord

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Ewan_Y_Isaac&#39;,this)">Isaac Y. Ewan</a></div>
S.M.

O PRECIOUS exercise
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To strike the priestly chord, 
To view with clear, anointed eyes 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The beauty of the Lord!

O precious theme for praise,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The moral wealth so vast 
That meets the meditative gaze 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In all the way He passed!

O precious privilege!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The watching cherubim 
Cannot, in priesthood, thus engage 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Converted hearts with Him.

O precious hour to us,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Led by our Lord, to raise
To Thee, His God and Father, thus 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our sacrifice of praise!


435
O precious heavenly Bridegroom

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Young_J_J&#39;,this)">J. J. Young</a></div>
7.6.7.6.D.

O PRECIOUS heavenly Bridegroom,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our hearts run after Thee,
Drawn by Thy love unmeasured
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Displayed at Calvary.
Thy name like precious ointment,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Is fragrant to us now,
Whilst with adoring wonder,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Low at Thy feet we bow.

Within the hallowed circle,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Where Thou art Lord and Head,
We join in deep thanksgiving
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Thee whose blood was shed.
We praise Thee and adore Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For all Thy love and grace,
Whilst with amazing wonder,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy worth and work we trace.

O let my soul remember
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That night of deepest woe,
The bitter dews of sorrow,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Which clung around Thy brow;
Jehovah’s face was hidden,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The sun refused to shine,
Whilst Thou didst bear for sinners,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The storm of wrath divine.

Enraptured with Thy beauty,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My heart would ever gaze,
Upon Thy precious Person
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And ponder all Thy ways,
What fragrance, strength and glory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What grace and truth I see,
How “altogether lovely”,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My Lord art Thou to me.

Then in those many mansions,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Without a veil between,
Our Lord in all His beauty
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shall face to face be seen.
O how our hearts shall praise Him,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O how our tongues will bless,
Throughout eternal ages,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His love and faithfulness.


436
Hiding in Thee
Previous BHB #345<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Cushing_William_Orcutt&#39;,this)">William Orcutt Cushing  (1876)</a></div>
P.M.

OH safe to the Rock that is higher than I
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My soul in its conflicts and sorrows would fly;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;So sinful, so weary, Thine, Thine would I be;
Thou blest Rock of Ages I’m hiding in Thee.

c
Hiding in Thee, hiding in Thee,
Thou blest Rock of Ages,
I’m hiding in Thee.

In the calm of the noontide, in sorrow’s lone hour,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In times when temptation casts o’er me its power;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In the tempests of life, on its wide, heaving sea,
Thou blest Rock of Ages, I’m hiding in Thee.

c
Hiding in Thee, hiding in Thee,
Thou blest Rock of Ages,
I’m hiding in Thee.

How oft in the conflict, when pressed by the foe,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I have fled to my refuge, and breathed out my woe;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;How often, when trials like sea-billows roll,
Have I hidden in Thee, O Thou rock of my soul.

c
Hiding in Thee, hiding in Thee,
Thou blest Rock of Ages,
I’m hiding in Thee.


437
O solemn hour! O hour alone

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Deck_James_George&#39;,this)">James George Deck</a></div>
8.6.8.8.6.8.8.

O SOLEMN hour! O hour alone,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In solitary night,
When God the Father’s only Son,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As Man, for sinners lost, undone,
Expires - amazing sight!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Lord of glory crucified!
The Lord of Life has bled and died!

O mystery of mysteries!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of life and death the tree!
Centre of two eternities,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Which look with rapt, adoring eyes,
Onward, and back to Thee!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O Cross of Christ, where all His pain
And death is our eternal gain.

Oh how our inmost hearts do move,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;While gazing on that Cross!
The death of the incarnate Love!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What shame, what grief, what joy  we prove,
That He should die for us!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our hearts were broken by that cry,
“Eli, lama sabachthani!”

Worthy of death, O Lord, we were;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That vengeance was our due;
In grace Thou, spotless Lamb, didst bear
Thyself our sins, and guilt, and fear;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Justice our Surety slew.
With Thee, our Surety, we have died;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With Thee, we there were crucified.

Quickened with Thee with life divine,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Raised with Thee from the dead;
Thine Own, now human and divine, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shall with Thee in Thy glories shine,
The Church’s living Head!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We, who were worthy but to die,
Now with Thee, “Abba Father”, cry.


438
O soul, are you weary and troubled?

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Lemmel_Helen&#39;,this)">Helen Lemmel</a></div>
P.M.

O SOUL, are you weary and troubled?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No light in the darkness you see?
There’s light for a look at the Saviour
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And life more abundant and free!

c
Turn your eyes upon Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Look full in His wonderful face,
And the things of earth will grow strangely dim,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In the light of His glory and grace.

Through death into life everlasting
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He passed, and we follow Him there;
O’er us sin no more hath dominion -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For more than conquerors we are!

c
Turn your eyes upon Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Look full in His wonderful face,
And the things of earth will grow strangely dim,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In the light of His glory and grace.

His Word shall not fail you - He promised;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Believe Him, and all will be well:
Then go to a world that is dying,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His perfect salvation to tell!

c
Turn your eyes upon Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Look full in His wonderful face,
And the things of earth will grow strangely dim,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In the light of His glory and grace.


439
O spotless Lamb of God, in Thee
Previous BHB #217
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Walker_Mary_Jane_Deck&#39;,this)">Mary Jane Deck Walker</a></div>
L.M.

O SPOTLESS Lamb of God, in Thee 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Father’s holiness we see; 
And with delight Thy children trace, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In Thee, His wondrous love and grace. 

For Thou didst leave Thy throne above
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To teach us that our “God is Love”;
And now we see His glory shine 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In every word and deed of Thine. 

When we behold Thee, Lamb of God, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Beneath our sins’ tremendous load; 
Bearing our curse upon the tree,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;How great our guilt with grief we see. 

There we with joy Thy grace behold; 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Its height and depth can ne’er be told! 
It bursts our chains and sets us free, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And sweetly draws our souls to Thee. 

The Cross reveals Thy love below; 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But better soon our hearts shall know, 
When we behold Thy face above, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The fulness of our Father’s love.


440
Oh, teach me what it meaneth

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Bennett_Lucy_Ann&#39;,this)">Lucy Ann Bennett</a></div>
7.6.7.6.D.

OH, teach me what it meaneth -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That Cross uplifted high,
With Thee, the Man of Sorrows, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Condemned to bleed and die!
Oh, teach me what it cost Thee
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To make a sinner whole;
And teach me, Saviour, teach me -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The value of a soul!

Oh, teach me what it meaneth -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That sacred crimson tide,
The blood and water flowing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From Thine own wounded side:
Teach me that if none other
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Had sinned, but I alone,
Yet still Thy blood, Lord Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thine only, must atone.

Oh, teach me what it meaneth -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy love beyond compare,
The love that reacheth deeper
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Than depths of self-despair!
Yes, teach me, till there gloweth
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In this cold heart of mine
Some faint, yet true reflection,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of that pure love of Thine!

O infinite Redeemer!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I bring no other plea;
Because Thou dost invite me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I cast myself on Thee!
Because Thou dost accept me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I love and I adore;
Because Thy love constraineth,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I’ll praise Thee evermore!


441
Oh, teach us, Lord, Thy searchless love to know

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Boyd_James&#39;,this)">James Boyd</a></div>
10.4.10.4.10.10.

OH, teach us, Lord, Thy searchless love to know,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou, who hast died.
Before our feeble faith, Lord Jesus, show
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy hands and side;
That our glad hearts, responsive unto Thine,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;May wake with all the power of love divine.

Thy death has brought to light the Father’s heart,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And ours has won;
And now we contemplate Thee as  Thou art,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;God’s glorious Son!
And know that we are loved with  that great love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That rests on Thee in those bright courts above.

Thy flesh is meat, Thy blood, blest Saviour, shed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Is drink indeed;
On Thee, the true, the heavenly,  living Bread,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our souls would feed,
And live with Thee in life’s eternal  home,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Where sin, nor want, nor woe, nor death can come.


442
Oh, teach us more of Thy blest ways
Previous BHB #195
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Hutton_James&#39;,this)">James Hutton</a></div>
C.M.

OH, teach us more of Thy blest ways,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou holy Lamb of God!
And fix and root us in Thy grace,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As those redeemed by blood.

Oh, tell us often of Thy love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of all Thy grief and pain!
And let our hearts with joy confess
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From thence comes all our gain. 

For this, oh, may we freely count
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Whate’er we have but loss!
The dearest object of our love
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Compared with Thee but dross!

Engrave this deeply on our hearts,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With an eternal pen,
That we may, in some small degree,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Return Thy love again.


443
Oh, the bitter shame and sorrow
Previous BHB #430
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Monod_Theodore&#39;,this)">Theodore Monod  (1874)</a></div>
8.7.8.8.7.

OH, the bitter shame and sorrow, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That a time could ever be,
When I let the Saviour’s pity
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Plead in vain; and proudly answered,
All of self, and none of Thee!

Yet He found me: I beheld Him 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Bleeding on the accursèd tree,
Heard Him pray, Forgive them, Father!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And my wistful heart said faintly,
Some of self, and some of Thee!

Day by day His tender mercy,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Healing, helping, full and free:
Sweet and strong, and ah! so patient,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Brought me lower, while I whispered,
Less of self, and more of Thee!

Higher than the highest heaven,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Deeper than the deepest sea,
Lord, Thy love at last hath conquered;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Grant me now my spirit’s longing, 
None of self, and all of Thee!


444
Oh, the brightness of the glory

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Trench_Janetta_A&#39;,this)">Janetta Wilbraham Trench</a></div>
8.7.8.7.D.

OH, the brightness of the glory
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shining in the Saviour’s face!
Telling all the blessèd story
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of the ways of God in grace:
Lowly, hated, and rejected
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In the world He came to save,
By the glory of the Father
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Raised triumphant from the grave.

Centre of the Father’s counsels,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He for whom all things were made;
Object of the Father’s pleasure,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who the Father’s name displayed;
All the Father’s will accomplished,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Unto death the path He trod;
Now in highest glory seated,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Centre of the throne of God.

There we see Him crowned with glory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Glory in His unveiled face,
And in peace and rest before Him,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In that glory learn of grace:
For it shineth in the visage
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of the One who for us died,
Bore our sins and all their judgment,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus Christ the crucified.

Called to share the Father’s pleasure
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In His well-belovèd Son,
Seated on His throne in heaven
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For the work on earth well done,
We adore Him, and are waiting
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To behold Him face to face;
In His presence praise the glory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Learn the riches of His grace.


445
O the deep, deep love of Jesus!
Previous BHB #431<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Francis_Samuel_Trevor&#39;,this)">Samuel Trevor Francis (1875)</a></div>
8.7.8.7.D.

O THE deep, deep love of Jesus!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Vast, unmeasured, boundless, free;
Rolling as a mighty ocean
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In its fulness over me:
Underneath me, all around me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Is the current of Thy love,
Leading onward, leading homeward,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To my glorious rest above.

O the deep, deep love of Jesus!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Spread His praise from shore to  shore;
How He loveth, ever loveth,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Changeth never, nevermore:
How He watches o’er His loved ones,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Died to call them all His own;
How for them He intercedeth,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Watcheth o’er them from the throne.

O the deep, deep love of Jesus!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Love of every love the best;
’Tis an ocean vast of blessing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;’Tis a haven sweet of rest:
O such love! the love of Jesus;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;’Tis a heaven of heavens to me;
And it lifts me up to glory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For it lifts me up to Thee.

O the deep, deep love of Jesus!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;’Twould take ages to explore
But a drop of all this ocean
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Or a grain from off its shore:
Yet our hearts are beating highly,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;If our faith is sometimes dim,
For the glory and the rapture
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We shall have when we have Him.


446
O thou, my soul, bless God the Lord
Previous BHB #432
<div class="author">Author Uncertain: <a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Sternhold_Thomas&#39;,this)">Thomas Sternhold</a> or <br>Francis Rous (Rowe) & William Barton (1650)</div>
C.M.

O THOU, my soul, bless God the Lord;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And all that in me is
Be stirrèd up His holy name
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To magnify and bless.

Bless, O my soul, the Lord thy God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And not forgetful be
Of all His gracious benefits
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He hath bestowed on thee:

All thine iniquities who doth
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Most graciously forgive;
Who thy diseases all and pains
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Doth heal, and thee relieve;

Who doth redeem thy life, that thou
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To death may’st not go down;
Who thee with loving kindness doth 
And tender mercies crown.

As far as east is distant from
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The west, so far hath He
From us removèd, in His love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All our iniquity.

Such pity as a father hath
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Unto his children dear;
Like pity shews the Lord to such
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As worship Him in fear.

O bless the Lord, all ye His works,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Wherewith the world is stored
In His dominions everywhere:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My soul, bless thou the Lord!


447
O Thou tender, gracious Shepherd

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Wellesley_Amelia_St_John&#39;,this)">Amelia Wellesley</a></div>
8.7.8.7.D.

O THOU tender, gracious Shepherd,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shedding for us Thy life’s blood,
Unto shame and death delivered,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All to bring us nigh to God!
Now our willing hearts adore Thee;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Now we praise Thy changeless love,
While by faith we come before Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Faith which lifts our souls above.

As our Surety we behold Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Ransoming our souls from death;
As the willing Victim view Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Yielding up to God Thy breath.
In the broken bread we own Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Bruised for us and put to shame;
And this cup, O Lord, we thank Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Speaks our pardon through Thy  name.

Blessèd supper of thanksgiving,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Feast of more than angels’ food!
Bread of life and cup of blessing -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;This is fellowship with God!
Feeble praise we now are bringing;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But when, Lord, we see Thy face,
Better songs of triumph singing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We shall own Thy matchless grace.


448
Oh, what a lonely path were ours
Previous BHB #208
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Denny_Sir_Edward&#39;,this)">Sir Edward Denny</a></div>
C.M.

OH, what a lonely path were ours,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Could we, O Father, see
No home of rest beyond it all,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No guide or help in Thee!

But Thou art near and with us still
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To keep us on the way
That leads along the vale of tears
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To the bright world of day.

There shall Thy glory, O our God!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Break fully on our view;
And we, Thy saints, rejoice to find
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That all Thy Word was true.

There Jesus, on His heavenly throne,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our wondering eyes shall see;
While we the blest associates there
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of all His joy shall be.

Sweet hope! we leave without a sigh
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A blighted world like this;
To bear the cross, despise the shame,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For all that weight of bliss.

Yet little do Thy saints at best,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Endure, O Lord, for Thee;
Whose suffering soul bore all our sins
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And sorrows on the tree:

Who faced our fierce, our ruthless foe,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Unaided and alone;
To win us for Thy crown of joy,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To raise us to Thy throne.


449
Heaven Came Down

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Peterson_W_John&#39;,this)">John W. Peterson</a></div><p>&copy; Copyright 1961 John W. Peterson Music Company. All rights reserved. Used by permission.</p>
P.M.

OH what a wonderful, wonderful day -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Day I will never forget;
After I’d wandered in darkness away,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus my Saviour I met.
Oh what a tender, compassionate friend -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He met the need of my heart;
Shadows dispelling, with joy I am telling,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He made all the darkness depart!

c
Heaven came down and glory filled my soul,
When at the Cross the Saviour made me whole;
My sins were washed away 
And my night was turned to day -
Heaven came down and glory filled my soul!

Born of the Spirit with life from above
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Into God’s family divine,
Justified fully through Calvary’s love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Oh what a standing is mine!
And the transaction so quickly was made
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When as a sinner I came,
Took of the offer of grace He did  proffer -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He saved me, oh praise His dear name!

c
Heaven came down and glory filled my soul,
When at the Cross the Saviour made me whole;
My sins were washed away 
And my night was turned to day -
Heaven came down and glory filled my soul!

Now I’ve a hope that will surely endure
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;After the passing of time;
I have a future in heaven for sure,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;There in those mansions sublime.
And it’s because of that wonderful day
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When at the Cross I believed;
Riches eternal and blessings supernal
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From His precious hand I received.

c
Heaven came down and glory filled my soul,
When at the Cross the Saviour made me whole;
My sins were washed away 
And my night was turned to day -
Heaven came down and glory filled my soul!


450
Oh, what shall we feel in Thy presence when first?
Previous BHB #225
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Grimley_Emily&#39;,this)">Emily Grimley</a></div>
11.11.11.11.

OH, what shall we feel in Thy presence when first
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The visions of glory upon us shall burst!
Our souls now are longing and thirsting for Thee;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Oh, when, blessèd Saviour, Thy face shall we see?

That face, once so marred, we shall gaze on at length,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And fearless behold, as the sun in its strength; 
Those eyes, flames of fire, that so searching we prove,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shall beam on us then inexpressible love.

Thy voice, like great waters, how calmly our soul
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shall hear in the glory its deep waters roll;
Though now it rebukes us, and humbles our pride,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;It shall speak only love to Thy glorified Bride.

O Thou who this world as a lone pilgrim trod,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy Father’s our Father, Thy God is our God;
To Thee we behold the bright seraphim bow:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lord Jesus, what glory doth rest on Thee now!

Thy Spirit has shown God’s deep purpose to be
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To empty, then fill us with glory like Thee;
And now Thou dost wait - Thy full joy to impart,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For that day of espousals - the joy of Thy heart.

Now moment by moment, to answer our needs,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy blood, holy Saviour, in righteousness pleads;
And sheltered by that, how serene and how calm,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our souls on Thy bosom are shielded from harm.

We see Thee, Lord Jesus, with great glory crowned,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And waiting Thy coming, in peace would be found;
The visions of glory have turned all to dross;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For Thee give us grace to count all things but loss.


451
O wonderful, wonderful Word of the Lord

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Alystyne_Frances_Jane_Van&#39;,this)">Frances (Fanny) Jane Crosby</a></div>
P.M.

O WONDERFUL, wonderful Word of the Lord!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;True wisdom its pages unfold;
And though we may read them a thousand times o’er,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;They never, no never, grow old!
Each line hath a treasure, each promise a pearl,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That all if they will may secure;
And we know that when time and the world pass away,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;God’s Word shall for ever endure.

O wonderful, wonderful Word of the Lord!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The lamp that our Father above
So kindly has lighted to teach us  the way
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That leads to the arms of His love!
