Its warnings, its counsels, are  faithful and just;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Its judgments are perfect and pure;
And we know that when time and  the world pass away,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;God’s Word shall for ever endure.

O wonderful, wonderful Word of the Lord!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our only salvation is there;
It carries conviction down deep in  the heart,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And shows us ourselves as we are.
It tells of a Saviour, and points to  the Cross,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Where pardon we now may secure;
For we know that when time and  the world pass away,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;God’s Word shall for ever endure.

O wonderful, wonderful Word of the Lord!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The hope of our friends in the past;
Its truth, where so firmly they  anchored their trust,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Through ages eternal shall last.
O wonderful, wonderful Word of  the Lord!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Unchanging, abiding and sure;
For we know that when time and  the world pass away,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;God’s Word shall for ever endure.


452
Oh, wondrous hour, when Jesus, Thou
Previous BHB #433
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Denny_Sir_Edward&#39;,this)">Edward Denny  (1839)</a></div>
L.M.

OH, wondrous hour, when Jesus, Thou,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Co-equal with the eternal God,
Beneath our sin didst deign to bow,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And in our stead didst bear the rod!

On Thee, the Father’s blessèd Son, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jehovah’s utmost anger fell: 
That all was borne, that all is done,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thine agony, Thy Cross can tell.

When most in Satan’s awful power,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Dear Lord, Thy suffering spirit seemed,
Then, in that dark and fearful hour,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our souls were by Thy blood  redeemed.

’Tis in Thy Cross, Lord, that we learn
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What Thou in all Thy fulness art;
There, through the darkening cloud, discern
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The love of Thy devoted heart.

’Twas mighty love’s constraining power
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That made Thee, blessèd Saviour, die;
’Twas love, in that tremendous hour,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That triumphed in Thy mighty cry.

’Twas all for us - our life we owe,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our hope, our crown of joy, to Thee;
Thy sufferings in that hour of woe,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy victory, Lord, have made us  free.


453
O wondrous Saviour! Jesus, Lord

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Walker_M_S&#39;,this)">S. M. Walker</a></div>
8.8.4.8.8.4.

O WONDROUS Saviour! Jesus, Lord,
Worthy alone to be adored,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We worship Thee!
Thou holy, spotless Son of God,
To Thee the incarnate living Word,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All glory be.

In Thee all human graces blend,
And to Thy Father e’er ascend,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As incense rare;
Fragrant to Him Thou ever art,
Source of rejoicing to His heart,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Most sweet and fair.

Fairer than all the sons of men,
Beyond all praise of tongue or pen,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou peerless One!
In grace, in patient tenderness,
In truth, in holy faithfulness,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thine equal - none!

Matchless, incomparable, divine! - 
In Jesus all perfections shine - 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Oh, blessèd Name!
How shall we tell its worth abroad,
How tell the praises of our Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Or spread His fame?

This, this shall be our endless theme,
When glorified we share with Him
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Father’s home.
And see in blessèd wondrous grace
Our God revealed in Jesus’ face,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lord Jesus, come!


454
Oh worship the King all-glorious above
Previous BHB #434
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kethe_William&#39;,this)">William Kethe</a> & <a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Grant_Sir_Robert&#39;,this)">Robert Grant</a>  (1561)</div>
10.10.11.11.

OH worship the King all-glorious above,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Oh gratefully sing His power and His love;
Our shield and defender, the Ancient of Days,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Pavilioned in splendour and girded with praise.

Oh tell of His might, oh sing of His grace,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Whose robe is the light, whose  canopy space;
His chariots of wrath the deep thunderclouds form,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And dark is His path on the wings of the storm.

The earth, with its store of wonders untold,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Almighty, Thy power hath founded of old;
Hath stablished it fast by a changeless decree,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And round it hath cast, like a mantle, the sea.

Thy bountiful care what tongue can recite?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;It breathes in the air, it shines in the light;
It streams from the hills, it descends to the plain,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And sweetly distils in the dew and the rain.

Frail children of dust, and feeble as frail,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In Thee do we trust, nor find Thee to fail;
Thy mercies how tender, how firm to the end,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our Maker, Defender, Redeemer, and Friend.

O measureless Might! Ineffable Love!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;While angels delight to hymn  Thee above,
The humbler creation, though feeble their lays,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With true adoration shall lisp to Thy praise. 


455
Of Thee, Lord, we would never tire
Previous BHB #184
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Peters_Mary&#39;,this)">Mary Bowly Peters</a></div>
C.M.

OF Thee, Lord, we would never tire;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;This new and living food
Can satisfy our hearts’ desire,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For life is in Thy blood.

If through the night a happy song
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our wearied spirits raise,
What greater joys shall cause ere long
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Eternal bursts of praise!

To look within and see no stain,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Abroad no curse to trace;
To shed no tears, to feel no pain,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To see Thee face to face.

To find each hope of glory gained,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Fulfilled each precious word,
And fully all to have attained
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The image of our Lord.

For this we’re pressing onward still,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And in this hope would be
More subject to the Father’s will,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lord Jesus, more like Thee.


456
On a hill far away stood an old rugged Cross

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Bennard_George&#39;,this)">George Bennard</a></div>
P.M.

ON a hill far away stood an old rugged Cross,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The emblem of suff’ring and shame;
And I love that old Cross where the dearest and best
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For a world of lost sinners was slain.

c
So I’ll cherish the old rugged Cross,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Till my trophies at last I lay down;
I will cling to the old rugged Cross,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And exchange it some day for a crown.

Oh, that old rugged Cross, so despised by the world,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Has a wondrous attraction for me,
For the dear Lamb of God left His  glory above,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To bear it to dark Calvary.

c
So I’ll cherish the old rugged Cross,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Till my trophies at last I lay down;
I will cling to the old rugged Cross,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And exchange it some day for a crown.

In that old rugged Cross, stained with blood so divine, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A wondrous beauty I see;
For ’twas on that old Cross Jesus suffered and died,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To pardon and sanctify me.

c
So I’ll cherish the old rugged Cross,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Till my trophies at last I lay down;
I will cling to the old rugged Cross,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And exchange it some day for a crown.

To that old rugged Cross I will ever be true,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Its shame and reproach gladly bear;
Then He’ll call me some day to  my home far away,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Where His glory forever I’ll share. 

c
So I’ll cherish the old rugged Cross,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Till my trophies at last I lay down;
I will cling to the old rugged Cross,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And exchange it some day for a crown.


457
On Christ salvation rests secure
Previous BHB #212<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Medley_Samuel&#39;,this)">Samuel Medley</a></div>
L.M.

ON Christ salvation rests secure;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Rock of Ages must endure;
Nor can that faith be overthrown
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Which rests upon the “Living Stone”.

No other hope shall intervene;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Him we look, on Him we lean;
Other foundations we disown,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And build on Christ, the “Living  Stone”.

In Him it is ordained to raise
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A temple to Jehovah’s praise,
Composed of all His saints, who own
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No Saviour but the “Living Stone”.

View the vast building, see it rise;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The work, how great! the plan,  how wise!
O wondrous fabric! power unknown,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That rests it on the “Living Stone”.

But most adore His precious Name;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His glory and His grace proclaim;
For us, the lost, condemned, undone,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He gave Himself, the “Living Stone”.


458
On His Father’s throne is seated

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Burlingham_Hannah_Killham&#39;,this)">Hannah Kilham Burlingham</a></div>
8.7.8.7.4.7.

ON His Father’s throne is seated
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Christ the Lord, the Living One;
All His toil on earth completed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All His work for sinners done:
In the glory
See Him, God’s eternal Son!

Every knee shall bow before Him,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Every tongue confess His name;
Ransomed myriads now adore Him,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who endured the sinner’s shame:  
From the glory
God doth now His worth proclaim!

Man the Cross to Him awarded;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Man the Saviour crucified;
Sin’s full judgment stands recorded -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thus was Justice satisfied:  
To the glory
God exalted Him who died.

Son of Man, His incarnation
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Opened first the tale of grace;
Son of Man, in new creation
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Leader of a chosen race!  
Well may glory, 
Give to Him the honoured place!


459
On Jordan’s banks He stands

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kirkpatrick_William&#39;,this)">William Kirkpatrick</a></div>
S.M.

ON Jordan’s bank He stands;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Impressive, lovely sight, 
Obedient to the law’s demands, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Father’s chief delight!

The wilderness, with each
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Deep testing, doth but show
No evil shaft His soul could reach, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No tempter Him o’erthrow.

He serves in harmony
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With all God’s holy will, 
Integrity of heart has He; 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His hand is full of skill.

The Father loves the Son
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who ever pleased Him well; 
Each movement of that Holy One 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Some beauty forth doth tell.

And here, O God, we bow;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In Him do we rejoice;
We worship by the Spirit now, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Praising with heart and voice.

While we remember Him,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our thanks to Thee would flow 
For beauties that shall ne’er grow dim 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Seen in His path below.


460
On merit not my own I stand

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Bonar_Horatius&#39;,this)">Horatius Bonar</a></div>
L.M.

ON merit not my own I stand,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On doings which I have not done,
Merit beyond what I can claim,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Doings more perfect than my own.

Upon a life I have not lived,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Upon a death I did not die,
Another’s life, Another’s death,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I stake my whole eternity.

Not on the tears which I have shed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Not on the sorrows I have known,
Another’s tears, Another’s griefs,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On them I rest, on them alone.

Jesus, O Son of God, I build
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On what Thy Cross has done for me;
There both my death and life I read,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My guilt, my pardon there I see.

Lord, I believe; oh, deal with me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As one who has Thy Word believed!
I take the gift, Lord, look on me
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As one who has Thy gift received.

I taste the love the gift contains,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I clasp the pardon which it brings,
And pass up to the living source
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Above, whence all this fulness  springs.

Here at Thy feast, I grasp the pledge
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Which life eternal to me seals,
Here in the bread and wine I read
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The grace and peace Thy death  reveals.

O fulness of the eternal grace,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O wonders past all wondering!
Here in the hall of love and song,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We sing the praises of our King.


461
On that bright and golden morning

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Alystyne_Frances_Jane_Van&#39;,this)">Frances (Fanny) Jane Crosby</a></div>
P.M.

ON that bright and golden morning when the Son of Man shall come,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And the radiance of His glory we shall see;
When from every clime and nation He shall call His people home -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What a gathering of the ransomed that will be!

c
What a gathering! what a gathering!
What a gathering of the ransomed in the summer land of love!
What a gathering! what a gathering,
Of the ransomed in that happy home above!

When the blest who sleep in Jesus at His bidding shall arise
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From the silence of the grave, and from the sea;
And with bodies all celestial they shall meet Him in the skies -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What a gathering and rejoicing there will be!

c
What a gathering! what a gathering!
What a gathering of the ransomed in the summer land of love!
What a gathering! what a gathering,
Of the ransomed in that happy home above!

When our eyes behold the City, with its “many mansions” bright,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And its river, calm and restful, flowing free -
When the friends that death has parted shall in bliss again unite -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What a gathering and a greeting there will be!

c
What a gathering! what a gathering!
What a gathering of the ransomed in the summer land of love!
What a gathering! what a gathering,
Of the ransomed in that happy home above!

Oh, the King is surely coming, and the time is drawing nigh
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When the blessèd day of promise we shall see;
Then the changing “in a moment, in the twinkling of an eye”,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And for ever in His presence we shall be. 

c
What a gathering! what a gathering!
What a gathering of the ransomed in the summer land of love!
What a gathering! what a gathering,
Of the ransomed in that happy home above!


462
On that most holy morn at Bethlehem

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kirkpatrick_William&#39;,this)">William Kirkpatrick</a></div>
6.4.6.4.6.6.6.4.

ON that most holy morn
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;At Bethlehem,
When Christ the Lord was born
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;At Bethlehem,
He came, O wondrous sight! 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Forth from the realms of light 
Into a scene of night,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;At Bethlehem.

That quiet time He knew  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;At Nazareth
Saw One with purpose true,  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;At Nazareth,
Grow up in all His ways 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To God His Father’s praise. 
Precious indeed those days
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;At Nazareth!

Compassing plain and hill,  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In Galilee,
Serving with subject will,  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In Galilee,
He Whom the Father sent 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Journeyed with fixed intent 
In all the way He went  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In Galilee.

Having the end in view -  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jerusalem,
Onward He moved unto  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jerusalem, 
Gethsemane to face,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To die in wondrous grace 
At the appointed place -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jerusalem.


463
On that same night, Lord Jesus
Previous BHB #435<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Fraser_George_West&#39;,this)">George West Frazer</a></div>
7.6.7.6.D.

ON that same night, Lord Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In which Thou wast betrayed,
When without cause man’s hatred
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Against Thee was displayed,
We hear Thy gracious accents -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“This do; remember Me”;
With joyful hearts responding
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We would remember Thee.

We think of all the darkness
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Which round Thy spirit pressed;
Of all those waves and billows
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Which rolled across Thy breast;
’Tis there Thy grace unbounded,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And perfect love we see;
With joy and yet with sorrow
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We do remember Thee.

We know Thee now as risen,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“The First-born from the dead”;
We see Thee now ascended,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Church’s glorious Head:
In Thee by grace accepted,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With heart and mind set free,
We think of all Thy sorrow,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And thus remember Thee.

Till Thou shalt come in glory
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And call us hence away,
To share with Thee the brightness
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of that unclouded day,
We show Thy death, Lord Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And here would seek to be
More to that death conformèd
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Whilst we remember Thee.


464
On Thy broken body feeding
Previous BHB #214<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Carter_Mary&#39;,this)">Mary Carter</a></div>
8.7.8.7.D.

ON Thy broken body feeding,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lord, our hearts in one unite;
Here our souls behold Thee bleeding,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Put to grief in sinners’ sight.
O that Jesus thus should love us,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Love us unto death and shame!
Let the dear remembrance move us,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;While we meet in His blest Name.

Here the pledge of Thy returning
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Tells of all the joys of home,
And our hearts within us burning,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Cry “Amen, Lord Jesus, come!”
Soon, full soon, we thus together
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In the Father’s house shall meet;
And the heavenly courts for ever
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Tread with undefilèd feet.

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Praise Him for evermore.


466
One day when heaven was filled with His praises

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Chapman_Wilbur_J&#39;,this)">J. Wilbur Chapman</a></div>
11.10.11.10. with Chorus

ONE day when heaven was filled with His praises,
One day when sin was as black as could be,
Jesus came forth to be born of a virgin,
Dwelt among men, my example is He! 

c
Living, He loved me; Dying, He saved me;
Buried, He carried my sins far away;
Rising, He justified freely forever,
One day He’s coming, O glorious day!

One day they led Him up Calvary’s mountain,
One day they nailed Him to die on the tree
Suffering anguish, despised and rejected 
Bearing our sins, my Redeemer is He!

c
Living, He loved me; Dying, He saved me;
Buried, He carried my sins far away;
Rising, He justified freely forever,
One day He’s coming, O glorious day!

One day they left Him alone in the garden,
One day He rested, from suffering free;
Angels came down o’er His tomb to keep vigil
Hope of the hopeless, my Saviour is He!

c
Living, He loved me; Dying, He saved me;
Buried, He carried my sins far away;
Rising, He justified freely forever,
One day He’s coming, O glorious day!

One day the grave could contain Him no longer,
One day the stone rolled away from the door;
Then He arose, over death He had conquered,
Now is ascended, my Lord evermore!

c
Living, He loved me; Dying, He saved me;
Buried, He carried my sins far away;
Rising, He justified freely forever,
One day He’s coming, O glorious day!

One day the trumpet will sound for His coming
One day the skies with His glories will shine; 
Wonderful day, my belovèd ones bringing;
Glorious Saviour, this Jesus is mine!

c
Living, He loved me; Dying, He saved me;
Buried, He carried my sins far away;
Rising, He justified freely forever,
One day He’s coming, O glorious day!


467
One there is above all others
Previous BHB #213
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Dark_Elizabeth_Nunn_Marianne&#39;,this)">Marianne Nunn</a></div>
8.4.8.4.8.8.8.4.

ONE there is above all others;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Oh, how He loves!
His is love beyond a brother’s;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Oh, how He loves!
Earthly friends may fail or leave us,
One day soothe, the next day grieve us,
But this Friend will ne’er deceive us;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Oh, how He loves!

’Tis eternal life to know Him;  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Oh, how He loves!
Think, oh think, how much we owe Him;  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Oh, how He loves!
With His precious blood He bought us,
In the wilderness He sought us,
To His fold He safely brought us;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Oh, how He loves!

Blessèd Jesus! would you know Him?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Oh, how He loves!
Give yourselves entirely to Him;  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Oh, how He loves!
Leave the past for bright tomorrow,
From His Word new courage borrow,
Jesus carries all your sorrow;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Oh, how He loves! 

We have found a friend in Jesus;  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Oh, how He loves!
’Tis His great delight to bless us;  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Oh, how He loves!
How our hearts delight to hear Him -
Bid us dwell in safety near Him:
Why should we distrust or fear Him?  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Oh, how He loves!

Through His Name we are forgiven;  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Oh, how He loves!
Backward shall our foes be driven  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Oh, how He loves!
Best of blessings He’ll provide us,
Naught but good shall e’er betide us,
Safe to glory He will guide us;  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Oh, how He loves!


468
Our Father! Oh, what gracious ways
Previous BHB #219
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Anonymous_Hymn_Writers&#39;,this)">Anonymous</a></div>
L.M.

“OUR Father!” Oh, what gracious ways
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And thoughts of love that Name conveys!
It tells us of the tender care
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Belovèd children ever share.

Our Father! by Thy mercies past,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We learn on Thee our cares to cast;
And while our wants are known to Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We need not fear whate’er they be. 

How oft when wand’ring far away,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy care has hedged up all our way;
So bidding us return and live,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And learn how much Thou canst  forgive.

And though we came with tardy feet,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;It was our Father ran to meet;
It was upon our Father’s breast
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We found again a place of rest.

How precious are Thy thoughts to us!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;How dear Thy Name revealèd thus! 
Oh, make us followers of Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As Thy dear children ought to be! 


469
Our God and Father, we draw near to Thee

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Virgette_N&#39;,this)">Nora F. Vergette</a></div>
10.10.10.10.

OUR God and Father, we draw near to Thee
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In all the worth of Thy belovèd Son;
All Thou hast e’er desired from man we see
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In Him, Thy Christ, Thine own anointed One.

No thought of His e’er moved apart from Thine;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Each holy footstep gave Thee fresh delight;
Perfect expression of Thy will divine
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou hadst in Him - come forth  from glory bright.

Thou ever lovedst Him - ere time began
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He was beside Thee, object of Thy heart;
One with Thyself in Thine eternal plan,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In Godhead glory one in all Thou art.

And now Thou lov’st Him, for Himself He gave;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Theme of our song when time shall cease to be;
Laid down His life that Thou, O God, shouldst have
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Fruit of Thy love, in sons who worship Thee.


470
Our God, whose justice did awake

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Chapman_Robert_Cleaver&#39;,this)">Robert Cleaver Chapman</a></div>
L.M.

OUR God, whose justice did awake,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The sword against Thy Well-beloved,
Thou didst Thine own dear Son forsake,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To mercy by His cries unmoved!

Thy perfect image, Thy delight,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He ever had beheld Thy face;
Thy bosom was, of native right,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His proper, secret dwelling-place.

Yet was the Lord made flesh, and nailed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;By men, His creatures, to the tree;
By all the powers of hell assailed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And bruised, and pierced, and slain by Thee!

In His own majesty arrayed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He spake and built the universe;
Yet to redeem us He was made
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A dying outcast and a curse!

His depths unsearchable of woe
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Alone our utmost guilt proclaim;
Through Jesus’ Cross make us to know
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The utmost glories of Thy name!


471
Our grateful hearts would raise

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Young_J_J&#39;,this)">J. J. Young</a></div>
6.4.6.4.6.6.6.4.

OUR grateful hearts would raise,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Father to Thee,
Their overflowing praise,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Father to Thee.
Brought from the shades of night,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From Satan’s power and might,
Into Thy glorious light,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Father to Thee.

What boundless grace has flowed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Saviour through Thee.
On rebels now bestowed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Saviour in Thee;
Peace through Thy precious blood,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Blest now with every good,
And reconciled to God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Saviour in Thee.

Here in Thy blessèd name,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Gathered to Thee.
Thy death we now proclaim,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Gathered to Thee.
Here in Thy presence sweet,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With saints in light made meet,
Now through Thy work complete,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Gathered to Thee.

Hark how the chorus swells,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lord unto thee,
All fulness in Thee dwells,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Yea, Lord in Thee;
Head of the church art Thou,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In resurrection now,
Low at Thy feet we bow,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lord unto Thee.


472
Our great Redeemer liveth! this assurance
Previous BHB #437<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Alystyne_Frances_Jane_Van&#39;,this)">Frances (Fanny) Jane Crosby</a></div>
11.10.11.10.

OUR great Redeemer liveth! this assurance
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Calms anxious fears and satisfies the heart;
Yes, Jesus lives! at God’s right hand He reigneth,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And in His great salvation we have part.

A living Christ, so strong, so pure, so tender,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Bears every ransomed soul before the throne;
Complete in Him we stand, saved by His merit -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;It was His blood which did for sin atone.

And now we live in Him, our great Redeemer,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who, living, intercedes for us above,
Yet by His Spirit dwells with us,  and keeps us
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Enfolded in His mighty arms of love.

Redeemer, Lord! with gratitude unfeignèd,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We bless Thy holy name that Thou has taught
Our hearts to know Thee, loving, caring, pleading:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Now grant us grace to love Thee as we ought.


473
Our hearts are full, O Father

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Young_J_J&#39;,this)">J. J. Young</a></div>
7.6.7.6.

OUR hearts are full, O Father,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Whilst here we meet as one,
We bring the deep perfection
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of Thy belovèd Son.

What rest, what joy and fragrance,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Ascending up to God,
Rose from His life so holy, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And His most precious blood.

Yea, Thou hast found Thy portion,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy joy, Thy deep delight,
In Him whose every action
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Was pleasing in Thy sight.

We too with joy behold Him,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His wondrous worth we trace,
And know ourselves accepted
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In Him through sovereign grace.

So with full hearts we gather,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Though many yet as one,
To give Thee thanks, O Father,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For Thy belovèd Son.


474
Our hearts are glad, for we have seen the Lord

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Tait_S_M_J&#39;,this)">J. M. S. Tait</a></div>
10.10.10.10.

OUR hearts are glad, for we have seen the Lord;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The living Lord, victorious from the grave. 
O joy to see Him, joy to hear His word,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our Lord and Master, strong to bless and save!

His hands, His feet He showed us, and the place 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Where deeply pierced at last the cruel spear.
O precious scars! O deathless love and grace
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That still in those redeeming wounds appear!

Most wondrous sight! All else is poor and dim
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To eyes that thus have viewed the risen One. 
All power is His, all glories meet in Him, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The sinner’s Saviour, God’s Belovèd Son.

So we are glad, our spirits all aglow,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Filled with a new and strangely  thrilling joy. 
Power from Himself inbreathing, forth we go -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O may His service all our days employ!


475
Our Lord is now rejected
Previous BHB #223<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Whittle_Major_Daniel_Webster&#39;,this)">Daniel Webster Whittle  (1881)</a></div>
P.M.

OUR Lord is now rejected
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And by the world disowned,
By the many still neglected,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And by the few enthroned;
But soon He’ll come in glory!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The hour is drawing nigh,
For the crowning day is coming By and by.

c
Oh, the crowning day is coming!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Is coming by and by!
When our Lord shall come in power
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And glory from on high!
Oh, the glorious sight will gladden
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Each waiting, watchful eye,
In the crowning day that’s coming By and by.

The heavens shall glow with splendour;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But brighter far than they
The saints shall shine in glory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As Christ shall them array:
The beauty of the Saviour
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shall dazzle every eye,
In the crowning day that’s coming By and by.

c
Oh, the crowning day is coming!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Is coming by and by!
When our Lord shall come in power
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And glory from on high!
Oh, the glorious sight will gladden
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Each waiting, watchful eye,
In the crowning day that’s coming By and by.

Our pain shall then be over,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We’ll sin and sigh no more;
Behind us all our sorrow,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And nought but joy before.
A joy in our Redeemer,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As we to Him are nigh,
In the crowning day that’s coming By and by.

c
Oh, the crowning day is coming!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Is coming by and by!
When our Lord shall come in power
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And glory from on high!
Oh, the glorious sight will gladden
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Each waiting, watchful eye,
In the crowning day that’s coming By and by.

Let all who look for, hasten
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The coming joyful day
By earnest consecration
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To walk the narrow way;
By gathering in the lost ones
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For whom our Lord did die,
For the crowning day that’s coming By and by.

c
Oh, the crowning day is coming!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Is coming by and by!
When our Lord shall come in power
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And glory from on high!
Oh, the glorious sight will gladden
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Each waiting, watchful eye,
In the crowning day that’s coming By and by.


476
Our souls are in God’s mighty hand
Previous BHB #221
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Wesley_Charles&#39;,this)">Charles Wesley</a></div>
C.M.D.

OUR souls are in God’s mighty hand,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We’re precious in His sight;
And you and I shall surely stand
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With Him in glory bright.

c
We’ll stem the storm, it won’t be long, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We’ll anchor by and by
In the haven of eternal rest, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With Jesus ever nigh.

Him eye to eye we then shall see,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our face like His shall shine;
Oh, what a glorious company,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Where saints and angels join!

c
We’ll stem the storm, it won’t be long, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We’ll anchor by and by
In the haven of eternal rest, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With Jesus ever nigh.

Oh, what a joyful meeting there!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In robes of white arrayed;
We all shall join in praising Him
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Whose glories never fade.

c
We’ll stem the storm, it won’t be long, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We’ll anchor by and by
In the haven of eternal rest, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With Jesus ever nigh.

Then let us hasten to that day,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When all shall be brought home;
Come, O Redeemer, come, we pray,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lord Jesus, quickly come!

c
We’ll stem the storm, it won’t be long, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We’ll anchor by and by
In the haven of eternal rest, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With Jesus ever nigh.


477
Our voice, with one accord

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Ewan_Y_Isaac&#39;,this)">Isaac Y. Ewan</a></div>
S.M.D.

OUR voice, with one accord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Thee, His God we raise,
While in the midst is He, the Lord, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Leader of the praise, 
The Subject of our song,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our Centre as we sing,
Our Lord His gathered ones among, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Through Whom our praise we bring.

With every heart as one,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As by the Spirit moved,
We muse on Thy Belovèd Son, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Man by Thee approved.
As to the past we turn,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And each His path surveys, 
Our raptured hearts within us burn, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our lips are filled with praise.

A holy priesthood, near,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Through Jesus’ blood, we draw
To Thee, His God and Father here 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With reverence and awe.
As if with feet unshod,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;By Him through Whom we live, 
A sacrifice of praise, O God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The fruit of lips we give.

Remembering Thy Son,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our praise to Thee ascends,
’Tis heaven’s song on earth begun, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The song that never ends.
Our hearts are tuned anew
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As thus, with one accord,
That wealth of grace and truth we view, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Remembering our Lord.


478
Ours are peace and joy divine
Previous BHB #224
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Reid_William&#39;,this)">William Watkins Reid</a></div>
P.M.

OURS are peace and joy divine,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who are one with Christ,
When, like branches in the vine,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We abide in Christ.
As a living grafted shoot,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Nourished from a hidden root,
We may bear all holy fruit
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Through “the love of Christ.”

c
Love of Christ, love of Christ,
Clusters grow on every branch
Through “the love of Christ.”

Christian pity moves our heart
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Through “the love of Christ”;
Others’ woes pierce like a dart
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When there’s love to Christ.
Gospel tidings we must tell,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sinners warn to flee from hell,
Lure and win, alarm, compel,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;By “the love of Christ.”

c
Love of Christ, love of Christ,
Heaven’s ranks we’ll seek to swell
For “the love of Christ.”

We will love with tender care,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Knowing love to Christ,
Brethren who His image bear,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For “the love of Christ”.
Jesus only shall we know,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And our love to all shall flow
In His blood-bought Church below,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For “the love of Christ.”

c
Love of Christ, love of Christ,
We now love all ransomed ones
For “the love of Christ.”

Now we live and walk by faith
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Through “the love of Christ”:
We can triumph over death,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;One in life with Christ.
Rooted, settled, knowing more,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Depths and heights of love explore,
Till we gain the heavenly shore
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Through “the love of Christ.”

c
Love of Christ, love of Christ,
When He comes we then shall know
All “the love of Christ.”


479
Peace, peace is mine! ’Tis peace of Jesus’ making

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Russell_Douglas&#39;,this)">Douglas Russell</a></div>
11.10.11.10.

PEACE, peace is mine! ’Tis peace of Jesus’ making,
The purchase of His own most precious blood;
He bore my sins, and suffered God’s forsaking,
While judgment poured on Him its whelming flood.

Peace, peace is mine, for He who once descended
Has passed in triumph through death’s dark domain;
As Victor He to God’s right hand ascended,
And now is seen, the Lamb who once was slain.

Peace, peace is mine! On high is Christ appearing,
Within the veil my great High Priest is He;
Honour and glory as a crown He’s wearing,
Who wore on earth the crown of thorns for me.

Peace, settled peace - a foretaste here of glory!
May mine e’er as an even river flow!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His love constrains me now to tell  the story -
The story old, whose fulness none can know.

Thus too may others come and share the blessing -
Peace, peace divine - then raise the anthem high,
The name of Jesus “Lord of all” confessing,
And live for Him whose coming draweth nigh!


480
Poor, weak, and worthless though I am
Previous BHB #349
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Newton_John&#39;,this)">John Newton  (1779)</a></div>
L.M.

POOR, weak, and worthless though I am,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I have a rich, almighty Friend;
Jesus, the Saviour, is His name;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He freely loves, and without end.

He ransomed me from hell with blood,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And by His power my foes controlled;
He found me wand’ring far from God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And brought me to His chosen fold.

He cheers my heart, my want supplies,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And says that I shall shortly be
Enthroned with Him above the skies;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Oh, what a friend is Christ to me!

But ah! my inmost spirit mourns, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And well my eyes with tears may  swim,
To think of my perverse returns;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I’ve been a faithless friend to Him.

Sure, were not I most vile and base,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I could not thus my Friend requite!
And were not He the God of grace,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He’d frown and spurn me from  His sight.


481
Praise appreciative, worship adoring

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kirkpatrick_William&#39;,this)">William Kirkpatrick</a></div>
11.12.12.10.

PRAISE appreciative, worship adoring, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Offer we unitedly to Thee, O God, while here 
As a holy priesthood, by the Spirit pouring 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thanks for Thy Son into Thine open ear.

His the condescension outwith our measure; 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Mighty God essentially, He stooped to lowly birth, 
Taking up in manhood, for Thy perfect pleasure, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That servant form, that subject place on earth.

To Thyself attentive, morning by morning,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As the learner Thee He heard with opened ear each day, 
In a scene rebellious, facing hate and scorning,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Holy, and true, and steadfast in His way.

Measureless the glory to Thee accruing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy delight was centred in Thy well-belovèd Son. 
Manifest perfections in Him thus reviewing, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We voice our praise to Thee for such an One.


482
Praise Him! praise Him! Jesus our blessed Redeemer!
Previous BHB #226<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Alystyne_Frances_Jane_Van&#39;,this)">Frances (Fanny) Jane Crosby</a></div>
12.10.12.10.D.

PRAISE Him! praise Him! Jesus, our blessèd Redeemer!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sing, ye saints! His wonderful love proclaim;
Hail Him! hail Him! mightiest angels in glory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Strength and honour give to His holy Name.
Like a shepherd, Jesus will feed His people,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In His arms He carries them all day long;
O ye saints that dwell in the light of His presence
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Praise Him! praise Him! ever in joyful song.

Praise Him! praise Him! Jesus, our blessèd Redeemer!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For our sins He suffered, and bled, and died;
He’s our Rock, our hope of eternal salvation,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hail Him! hail Him! Jesus, the Crucified.
Loving Saviour, meekly enduring  sorrow,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Crowned with thorns that cruelly pierced His brow;
Once for us rejected, despised, and forsaken,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Prince of Glory! He is triumphant now.

Praise Him! praise Him! Jesus, our blessèd Redeemer!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Heavenly portals loud with hosannahs ring!
Jesus, Saviour, reigneth for ever and ever;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Crown Him! crown Him! prophet, and priest, and king.
Death is vanquished! tell it with joy, ye faithful,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Where is now thy victory, boasting grave?
Jesus lives! no longer thy portals are cheerless;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus lives! the mighty and strong to save.


483
Praise, my soul, the King of heaven
Previous BHB #438
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Lyte_Henry_Francis&#39;,this)">Henry Francis Lyte  (1834)</a></div>
8.7.8.7.8.7.

PRAISE, my soul, the King of heaven;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To His feet thy tribute bring;
Ransomed, healed, restored, forgiven,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who like thee His praise shall sing?
Praise Him! praise Him!
Praise the everlasting King.

Praise Him for His grace and favour
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To our fathers in distress;
Praise Him, still the same as ever,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Slow to chide and swift to bless:  
Praise Him! praise Him!
Glorious in His faithfulness.

Father-like He tends and spares us,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Well our feeble frame He knows;
In His hands He gently bears us,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Rescues us from all our foes:  
Praise Him! praise Him!
Widely as His mercy flows.

Frail as summer flower we flourish;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Blows the wind and it is gone;
But, while mortals rise and perish,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;God endures unchanging on:  
Praise Him! praise Him!
Praise the high eternal One.

Angels in the height adore Him!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Ye behold Him face to face;
Saints triumphant bow before Him!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Gathered in from every race:  
Praise Him! praise Him!
Praise with us the God of grace.


484
Praise, praise ye the Name of Jehovah, our God
Previous BHB #228<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Bonar_Horatius&#39;,this)">Horatius Bonar (1845)</a></div>
P.M.

PRAISE, praise ye the Name of Jehovah, our God;
Declare, O declare ye His glories abroad;
Proclaim ye His mercy from nation to nation,
Till the uttermost islands have heard His salvation.

c
For His love floweth on free and full as a river,
And His mercy endureth for ever and ever.

Praise, praise ye the Lamb who for sinners was slain,
Who went down to the grave and ascended again;
And who soon shall return, when these dark days are o’er,
To set up His kingdom in glory and power.

c
For His love floweth on free and full as a river,
And His mercy endureth for ever and ever.

Then the heavens, the earth, and the sea shall rejoice;
The fields and the forests shall lift the glad voice;
The sands of the desert shall flourish in green,
And Lebanon’s glory be shed o’er the scene.

c
For His love floweth on free and full as a river,
And His mercy endureth for ever and ever.

Her bridal attire and her festal array
All Nature shall wear on that glorious day;
For her King cometh down with His people to reign,
And His presence shall bless her with Eden again.

c
For His love floweth on free and full as a river,
And His mercy endureth for ever and ever.


485
Praise the Lord, and leave tomorrow
Previous BHB #229<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Sloan_William_G&#39;,this)">William Sloan</a></div>
8.7.8.7.4.7.

PRAISE the Lord, and leave tomorrow
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In Thy loving Father’s hands;
Burden not thyself with sorrow,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For secure the promise stands.
He is faithful!
Leave thy troubles in His hands.

Trust today, and leave tomorrow,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Each day has enough of care;
Therefore, whatsoe’er thy burden,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;God will give thee strength to bear.  
He is faithful!
Cast on Him thine every care.

Pray today, and let tomorrow
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Bring with it whate’er it may;
Hear thy loving Father promise
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Strength according to thy day.  
He is faithful!
Trust Him, therefore, come what may.

Watch today, and leave tomorrow,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For tomorrow may not come;
For today thy loving Saviour
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;May appear to take thee home.  
He is faithful!
Look for Him, the coming One.

Work today, and leave tomorrow;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All around there’s urgent need;
All around there’s sin and sorrow;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Broadcast, daily sow thy seed.  
God is faithful!
He shall bless thy work indeed.

Thus by trusting, watching, praying,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Each day, as our time rolls on,
We shall find the promised blessing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Daily strength till Jesus come.  
He is faithful!
He will come to take us home.


486
Praise the Lord, who died to save us
Previous BHB #232<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kelly_Thomas&#39;,this)">Thomas Kelly (1806)</a></div>
8.7.8.7.4.7.

PRAISE the Lord, who died to save us,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Praise His ever gracious Name;
Praise Him that He lives to bless us,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Now and evermore the same:
Blessèd Saviour!
We would all Thy love proclaim.

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Brought Thee down to save the lost;
Ye above, His throne surrounding,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Praise Him, praise Him all His host:

Ye are they who owe Him most.

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Objects of such grace alone,
By eternal love elected,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Destined now to share His throne,  
Sing with wonder,
Sing of what our Lord hath done.

Praise His Name, who died to save us;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;’Tis by Him His people live;
And in Him the Father gave us
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All that boundless love could give:  
Life eternal
In our Saviour we receive.


487
Praise the peerless name of Jesus

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Burlingham_Hannah_Killham&#39;,this)">Hannah Kilham Burlingham</a></div>
P.M.

PRAISE the peerless name of Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sing of Him for evermore;
Praise the precious name of Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Tell its value o’er and o’er.
Jesus Christ is God’s salvation;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All who live through Jesus’ name,
Were in death and condemnation,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Heirs of Adam’s sin and shame.
’Tis through Thy death, Lord Jesus,
’Tis through Thy death, Lord Jesus,
’Tis through Thy death, Lord Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Faith can wondrous blessings claim.

Precious blood, the blood of Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Did for all my sins atone;
Sprinkled blood, the blood of Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Speaks for ever from the throne.
Telling how His life was given,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And that He who once was dead -
Son of Man, God’s Son from heaven -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Is the Saviour, as He said.
O, precious blood of Jesus!
O, precious blood of Jesus!
O, precious blood of Jesus!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For a world of sinners shed.

At this name supreme of Jesus
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Every knee, God saith, shall bow;
Lord of all, ’tis this same Jesus
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Whom the world refuses now.
Every eye shall gaze upon Him,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Every tongue confess His name;
Every glory centres in Him,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Wronged of men, and put to shame.
O, blessèd name of Jesus!
O, blessèd name of Jesus!
O, blessèd name of Jesus!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Now His matchless worth proclaim.

Praise the peerless name of Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Tell of Him for evermore;
See Him in God’s glory - Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who the weight of judgment bore.
In the Cross, Thy death, Lord Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;God requirèd what is past;
Thou art Alpha and Omega,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou art First and Thou art Last;
Now in Thy name, Lord Jesus,
Now in Thy name, Lord Jesus,
Now in Thy name, Lord Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All God’s counsel standeth fast.


488
Praise the Saviour, ye who know Him!
Previous BHB #233<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kelly_Thomas&#39;,this)">Thomas Kelly (1806)</a></div>
8.8.8.5.

PRAISE the Saviour, ye who know Him!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who can tell how much we owe Him?
Gladly let us render to Him
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All we are and have.

Jesus is the Name that charms us,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He for conflict fits and arms us;
Nothing moves, and nothing harms us, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When we trust in Him.

Trust in Him, ye saints, for ever;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He is faithful, changing never;
Neither force nor guile can sever 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Those He loves from Him.

Keep us, Lord, oh keep us cleaving
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Thyself, and still believing,
Till the hour of our receiving 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Promised joys in heaven.

Then we shall be where we would be, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Then we shall be what we should be,
That which is not now, nor could be, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Then shall be our own.


489
Praise thy Saviour, O my soul!
Previous BHB #230<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Anonymous_Hymn_Writers&#39;,this)">Anonymous</a></div>
7.7.7.7.

PRAISE thy Saviour, O my soul!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He has drunk the bitter gall,
Paid thy ransom, set thee free;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Praise Him, praise Him cheerfully.

O the wonders of His love!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;See Him coming from above
To atone and die for thee;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Praise Him, praise Him cheerfully.

See the waves and billows roll
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O’er His sinless, spotless soul;
O my soul, it was for thee!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Praise Him, praise Him cheerfully.

Yes, with joy we’ll praise Him now,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Till with saints above we bow,
And to all eternity
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Praise Him, praise Him cheerfully.


490
Praise to the Lord, the Almighty, the King of creation
Previous BHB #439
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Neander_Joachim&#39;,this)">Joachim Neander</a> & Rupert E. Davies tr/ad by <a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Winkworth_Catherine&#39;,this)">Katherine Winkworth</a> & others  (1679)</div>
14.14.4.7.8.

PRAISE to the Lord, the Almighty, the King of creation;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O my soul, praise Him, for He is thy health and salvation;
All ye who hear, Brethren and sisters draw near,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Praise Him in glad adoration.

Praise to the Lord, who o’er all things so wondrously reigneth,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shelters thee under His wings, yea, so gently sustaineth!
Hast thou not seen How thy desires e’er have been
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Granted in what He ordaineth?

Praise to the Lord, who doth prosper thy work and defend thee;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Surely His goodness and mercy here daily attend thee:
Ponder anew What the Almighty can do
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;If with His love He befriend thee.

Praise to the Lord, who, when darkness and sin is abounding,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who, when the godless do triumph, all virtue confounding,
Sheddeth His light, Chaseth the horrors of night,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Saints with His mercy surrounding.

Praise to the Lord, O let all that is in me adore Him;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All that hath life and breath come now with praises before Him;
Let the Amen Sound from His people again:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Gladly for aye we adore Him.


491
Praise ye Jehovah! Praise the Lord most holy

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Cockburn-Campbell_Margaret&#39;,this)">Margaret Cockburn-Campbell</a></div>
11.10.11.10.

PRAISE ye Jehovah! Praise the Lord most holy,
Who cheers the contrite, girds with strength the weak;
Praise Him who will with glory crown the lowly,
And with salvation beautify the meek.

Praise ye the Lord for all His loving-kindness,
And all the tender mercies He has shewn;
Praise Him who pardons all our sin and blindness,
And calls us sons, and seals us for His own. 

Praise ye Jehovah - Source of all our blessing
Before His gifts earth’s richest boons wax dim; 
Resting in Him, His peace and joy possessing,
All things are ours, for we have all in Him.

Praise ye the Father, God the Lord, who gave us,
With full and perfect love, His only Son!
Praise ye the Son, who died Himself to save us!
Praise ye the Spirit! Praise the Three in One!


492
Praise ye the Lord! again, again
Previous BHB #236<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Peters_Mary&#39;,this)">Mary Bowly Peters</a></div>
C.M.

“PRAISE ye the Lord!” again, again,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Spirit strikes the chord;
Nor toucheth He our hearts in vain;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We praise, we praise the Lord.

“Rejoice in Him!” again, again,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Spirit speaks the word;
And faith takes up the happy strain
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our joy is in the Lord.

“Stand fast in Christ!” ah! yet again
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He teaches all the band;
Our best endeavours are in vain,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In Christ alone we stand.

“Clean every whit!” Thou saidst it, Lord;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shall one suspicion lurk?
Thine, surely, is a faithful word,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And Thine a finished work.

For ever be the glory given
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Thee, O Lamb of God!
No joy for us, in earth or heaven,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We owe not to Thy blood.


493
Prayer is the soul’s sincere desire
Previous BHB #234<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Montgomery_James&#39;,this)">James Montgomery  (1818)</a></div>
C.M.

PRAYER is the soul’s sincere desire,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Uttered or unexpressed,
The motion of a hidden fire
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That trembles in the breast.

Prayer is the burden of a sigh,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The falling of a tear,
The upward glancing of an eye
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When none but God is near.

Prayer is the simplest form of speech
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That infant lips can try;
Prayer, the sublimest strains that reach
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Majesty on high.

Prayer is the Christian’s vital breath,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Christian’s native air;
His watchword at the gates of death:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He enters heaven with prayer.

The saints, in prayer, appear as one
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In word, and deed, and mind,
While with the Father and the Son
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sweet fellowship they find.

O Thou by whom we come to God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Life, the Truth, the Way!
The path of prayer Thyself hast trod:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lord, teach us how to pray!


494
Precious is the blood of Jesus
Previous BHB #231<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Furlong_Gordon&#39;,this)">Gordon Furlong</a></div>
8.7.8.7.4.7.

PRECIOUS is the blood of Jesus
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Unto sinners who believe;
From the wrath of God it frees us,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And salvation we receive:
It is finished!
Sounds with joy through earth and heaven.

Jesus now in heaven is seated,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And by faith on Him we rest;
Soon the Church will be completed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And the saints with Him be blest:  
Grace and glory
In our Saviour we receive.

Soon will pass the night of sorrow,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And the Lord in glory come;
We shall see Him on the morrow,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And the Bride be welcomed home:  
Hallelujah!
Glory, glory to the Lamb!


495
Precious thought - my Father knoweth!

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;LW&#39;,this)">L.W.</a></div>
8.5.8.5.D.

PRECIOUS thought - my Father knoweth!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In His love I rest:
For whate’er my Father doeth
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Must be always best;
Well I know the heart that planneth
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Naught but good for me;
Joy and sorrow interwoven,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Love in all I see.

Precious thought - my Father knoweth!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Careth for His child;
Bids me nestle closer to Him,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When the storms beat wild;
Though my earthly hopes are shattered,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And the tear-drops fall,
Yet He is Himself my solace,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Yea, my “all in all”.

Sweet to tell Him all He knoweth!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Roll on Him the care;
Cast upon Himself the burden,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That I cannot bear,
Then, without a care oppressing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Simply to lie still,
Giving thanks to Him for all things,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Since it is His will

Oh, to trust Him then more fully!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Just to simply move
In the conscious calm enjoyment
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of the Father’s love;
Knowing that life’s chequered pathway
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Leadeth to His rest;
Satisfied the way He taketh 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Must be always best.


496
Press forward and fear not!
Previous BHB #235
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Wakefield_Edward&#39;,this)">Edward Wakefield</a></div>
11.11.11.11.

PRESS forward and fear not! Though billows may roll, 
The word of Jehovah Their rage can control; 
Though waves rise in anger, Their tumult shall cease;
One word of His bidding Shall hush them to peace.

Press forward and fear not! Though trial be near;
The Lord is our refuge, Whom, then, shall we fear? 
His staff is our comfort, Our safeguard His rod; 
Then let us be steadfast, And trust in our God.

Press forward and fear not! Be strong in the Lord,
In the power of His promise, The truth of His word; 
Through the sea and the desert Our pathway may tend, 
But He who hath saved us Will save to the end.

Press forward and fear not! Though rough be our way; 
Why should we e’er shrink From our path in dismay? 
We tread but the road Which our Leader has trod; 
Then let us press forward, And trust in our God.


497
Put thou thy trust in God
Previous BHB #440
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Gerhardt_Paulus&#39;,this)">Paul Gerhardt</a> tr/ad by <a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Wesley_John&#39;,this)">John Wesley</a> [Paisley: <a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Wesley_Charles&#39;,this)">Charles Wesley</a>]  (1656)</div>
S.M.

PUT thou thy trust in God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In duty’s path go on;
Walk in His strength with faith and hope,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;So shall thy work be done.

Give to the winds thy fears;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hope, and be undismayed;
God hears thy sighs and counts thy tears,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;God shall lift up thy head.

He everywhere hath sway,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And all things serve His might;
His every act pure blessing is,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His path unsullied light.

Commit thou all thy griefs
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And ways into His hands,
To His sure truth and tender care
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who earth and heaven commands.

Through waves, and clouds, and storms
He gently clears the way;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Wait thou His time; so shall this night
Soon end in joyous day.

When He makes bare His arm
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who shall His work withstand?
When He His people’s cause defends,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who then shall stay His hand?

Leave to His sovereign sway
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To choose and to command;
So shalt thou, wondering, own His way
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;How wise, how strong His hand.

We comprehend Him not
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Yet earth and heaven tell
God sits as Sovereign on the throne
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And ruleth all things well!


498
Redeemed! how I love to proclaim it

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Alystyne_Frances_Jane_Van&#39;,this)">Frances (Fanny) Jane Crosby</a></div>
P.M.

REDEEMED! how I love to proclaim it,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Redeemed by the blood of the Lamb;
Redeemed through His infinite mercy,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His child and for ever I am.

c
Redeemed! Redeemed!
Redeemed by the blood of the Lamb,
Redeemed! Redeemed!
His child and forever I am.

Redeemed, and so happy in Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No language my rapture can tell,
I know that the light of His presence
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With me doth continually dwell.

c
Redeemed! Redeemed!
Redeemed by the blood of the Lamb,
Redeemed! Redeemed!
His child and forever I am.

I think of my blessèd Redeemer,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I think of Him all the day long;
I sing, for I cannot be silent,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His love is the theme of my song.

c
Redeemed! Redeemed!
Redeemed by the blood of the Lamb,
Redeemed! Redeemed!
His child and forever I am.

I know I shall see in His beauty
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The King in whose law I delight,
Who lovingly guardeth my footsteps,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And giveth me songs in the night.

c
Redeemed! Redeemed!
Redeemed by the blood of the Lamb,
Redeemed! Redeemed!
His child and forever I am.

I know there’s a crown that is waiting
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In yonder bright mansions for me, 
And soon, with the spirits made perfect,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;At home with the Lord I shall be.

c
Redeemed! Redeemed!
Redeemed by the blood of the Lamb,
Redeemed! Redeemed!
His child and forever I am.


499
Rejected and despised, as with averted face

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Ewan_Y_Isaac&#39;,this)">Isaac Y. Ewan</a></div>
S.M.

REJECTED and despised,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As with averted face,
He moved ’mong men unrecognised,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And in the outside place.

Through this sad scene of sin,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All pitiful, He passed,
The love that lived His heart within
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To manifest at last.

No form or comeliness
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To please a fallen race, 
Yet lovely in His lowly dress, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And wondrous in His grace!

Thus undesired was He
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Whom all in heaven adored; 
No unanointed eye can see 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The beauty of the Lord.

But One has touched our eyes
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And lips, to see and sing,
Our hearts, this priestly sacrifice
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Thee, O God, to bring.


500
Rejoice! the Lord is King
Previous BHB #441<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Wesley_Charles&#39;,this)">Charles Wesley  (1744)</a></div>
6.6.6.6.8.8.

REJOICE! the Lord is King;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Your Lord and King adore;
Mortals, give thanks and sing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And triumph evermore:
Lift up your heart, lift up your voice;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Rejoice, again I say, rejoice.

Jesus, the Saviour, reigns,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The God of truth and love;
When He had purged our stains
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He took His seat above:
Lift up your heart, lift up your voice;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Rejoice, again I say, rejoice.

His kingdom cannot fail;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He rules o’er earth and heaven;
The keys of death and hell
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Are to our Jesus given:
Lift up your heart, lift up your voice;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Rejoice, again I say, rejoice.

He sits at God’s right hand
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Till all His foes submit,
And bow to His command,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And fall beneath His feet:
Lift up your heart, lift up your voice;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Rejoice, again I say, rejoice.

Rejoice in glorious hope;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus, our Lord, shall come
And take His servants up
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To their eternal home;
We soon shall hear th’ archangel’s voice;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The trump of God shall sound, rejoice!


501
Rest of the saints above

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Darby_John_Nelson&#39;,this)">John Nelson Darby</a></div>
S.M.

REST of the saints above,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jerusalem of God!
Who, in thy palaces of love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy golden streets have trod?

Who shall to me that joy
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of saint-thronged courts declare,
Tell of that constant, sweet employ
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My spirit longs to share?

That rest secure from ill,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No cloud of grief e’er stains;
Unfailing praise each heart doth fill,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And love eternal reigns.

The Lamb is there, my soul!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;There God Himself doth rest
In love divine, diffused through all -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With Him supremely blest.

God and the Lamb! ’tis well;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I know that source divine,
Of joy and love no tongue can tell,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And know that all is mine.

There on the hidden bread,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of Christ - once humbled here -
God’s treasured store - for ever fed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His love my soul shall cheer.

There in effulgence bright,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Saviour and Guide, with Thee
I’ll walk, and in Thy heavenly light
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Whiter my robe shall be!

God and the Lamb shall there
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The light and temple be,
And radiant hosts for ever share
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The unveiled mystery.


502

503
Rise, my soul! behold ’tis Jesus
Previous BHB #238<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Smith_Joseph_Denham&#39;,this)">Joseph Denham Smith</a></div>
8.7.8.7.

RISE, my soul! behold ’tis Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus fills Thy wond’ring eyes;
See Him now in glory seated,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Where thy sins no more can rise.

There, in righteousness transcendent,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lo! He doth in heaven appear,
Shows the blood of His atonement
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As thy title to be there.

All thy sins were laid upon Him,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus bore them on the tree;
God, who knew them, laid them on Him,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And, believing, thou art free.

God now brings thee to His dwelling,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Spreads for thee His feast divine,
Bids thee welcome, ever telling
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What a portion there is thine.

In that circle of God’s favour,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Circle of the Father’s love,
All is rest, and rest for ever,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All is perfectness above.

Blessèd, glorious word “forever”!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Yea, “forever!” is the word;
Nothing can the ransomed sever,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Nought divide them from the Lord.


504
Rise, my soul! thy God directs thee
Previous BHB #239
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Darby_John_Nelson&#39;,this)">John Nelson Darby</a></div>
8.7.8.7.

RISE, my soul! thy God directs thee;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Stranger hands no more impede;
Pass thou on; His hand protects thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Strength that has the captive freed.

Is the wilderness before thee?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Desert lands where drought abides?
Heavenly springs shall there restore thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Fresh from God’s exhaustless tides.

Light divine surrounds thy going;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;God Himself shall mark thy way;
Secret blessings, richly flowing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lead to everlasting day.

Art thou weaned from Egypt’s pleasures? 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;God in secret shall thee keep;
There unfold His hidden treasures,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;There His love’s exhaustless deep.

In the desert God will teach thee
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What the God that thou hast found;
Patient, gracious, powerful, holy,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All His grace shall there abound.

Though thy way be long and dreary,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Eagle strength He’ll still renew;
Garments fresh and foot unweary
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Tell how God hath brought thee  through.

When to Canaan’s long-loved dwelling
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Love divine thy foot shall bring,
There, with shouts of triumph swelling,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Zion’s songs in rest to sing.

There no stranger God shall meet thee;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Stranger thou in courts above!
He who to His rest shall greet thee
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Greets thee with a well-known love.


505
Rise, my soul, with joy and gladness

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Schade_Casper_J&#39;,this)">J. Casper Schade</a></div>
8.7.8.7.4.7.

RISE, my soul, with joy and gladness,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And the praise of Jesus sing;
He removes all cause of sadness -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Only Jesus life could bring;
He redeemed me -
Glory to the eternal King!

Well He knew my lost condition -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sinless offering God must have;
Vain my tears and deep contrition,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Nought that I could do would save:  
He redeemed me,
For His precious life He gave.

Now He lives, He lives for ever,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And for all His people pleads;
One with Him, now nought can sever
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Those for whom He intercedes:  
He redeemed me,
And to glory safely leads.

Bright the prospect of that glory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Seen by faith at God’s right hand;
There we shall recount the story,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In that happy, happy land:  
He redeemed me -
Wondrous all His love has planned!


506
Rock of Ages, cleft for me
Previous BHB #350
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Toplady_Augustus_Montague&#39;,this)">Augustus Montague Toplady  (1776)</a></div>
7.7.7.7.7.7.

ROCK of Ages, cleft for me, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Let me hide myself in Thee!
Let the water and the blood,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From Thy riven side which flowed, 
Be of sin the double cure;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Cleanse me from its guilt and power.

Not the labour of my hands
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Can fulfil Thy law’s demands; 
Could my zeal no respite know,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Could my tears for ever flow,
All for sin could not atone;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou must save, and Thou alone.

Nothing in my hand I bring,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Simply to Thy Cross I cling; 
Naked, come to Thee for dress;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Helpless, look to Thee for grace;
Foul, I to the fountain fly;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Wash me, Saviour, or I die. 

While I draw this fleeting breath,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When mine eyelids close in death, 
When I soar to worlds unknown, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;See Thee on Thy judgment throne,
Rock of Ages, cleft for me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Let me hide myself in Thee. 


507
Round Himself we come so gladly

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kirkpatrick_William&#39;,this)">William Kirkpatrick</a></div>
8.5.8.3.

ROUND Himself we come so gladly
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In this simple way;
Into His blest Name we gather 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Here today.

Glories all-excelling centre
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In that Holy One; 
Infinite effulgence gleaming
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Through the Son!

He Who knows the Father’s bosom
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As His dwelling-place
Now the Father’s heart revealeth
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In His grace;

And a wealth of heav’nly features
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In His ways express,
Day by day, His own inherent
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Preciousness.

Thus before Thyself, His Father,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sweetest hymns of praise, 
Reminiscent of His pathway
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Do we raise.


508
Round His steps the sweetest fragrance

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kirkpatrick_William&#39;,this)">William Kirkpatrick</a></div>
8.4.8.4.8.8.8.4.

ROUND His steps the sweetest fragrance
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Ever shall cling;
With Himself in blest remembrance,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Gladly we sing;
Now in holy convocation, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All our true appreciation
Of the Son, in presentation
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To God we bring.

Excellence surpassing shining  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Through every day, 
Grace and glory intertwining  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His perfect way,
As a tender plant inviting, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Meekness with His might uniting, 
Virtues rare His pathway lighting
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In rich array!

In Him all the Father’s pleasure  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Centred we see; 
Preciousness in boundless measure,  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;True dignity!
His the path of grace excelling, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Works and words beyond all telling,
All the fulness in Him dwelling
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In majesty.


509
Safe in the arms of Jesus
Previous BHB #243<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>

SAFE in the arms of Jesus,
