There by His love o’ershaded, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sweetly my soul shall rest.
Hark! ’tis the voice of angels,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Borne in a song to me,
Over the fields of glory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Over the jasper sea.

c
Safe in the arms of Jesus,
Safe on His gentle breast
There by His love o’ershaded,
Sweetly my soul shall rest.

Safe in the arms of Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Safe from corroding care,
Safe from the world’s temptations,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sin cannot harm me there.
Free from the blight of sorrow,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Free from my doubts and fears;
Only a few more trials,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Only a few more tears!

c
Safe in the arms of Jesus,
Safe on His gentle breast
There by His love o’ershaded,
Sweetly my soul shall rest.

Jesus, my heart’s dear refuge,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus has died for me;
Firm on the Rock of Ages
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Ever my trust shall be.
Here let me wait with patience,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Wait till the night is over;
Wait till I see the morning
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Break on the golden shore.

c
Safe in the arms of Jesus,
Safe on His gentle breast
There by His love o’ershaded,
Sweetly my soul shall rest.


510
Salvation to our God!
Previous BHB #246<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Peters_Mary&#39;,this)">Mary Bowly Peters</a></div>
6.6.8.4.D.

SALVATION to our God!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Salvation to the Lamb!
The shedding of His precious blood
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our only claim.
Our God salvation gives,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And through the Lamb it flows;
Once slain for us - for us He lives,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our sole repose.

The Lamb once slain is seen
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On God’s eternal throne;
And His redeemed are white and clean
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Through Him alone.
Salvation’s joyful sound
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Bursts from the blood-bought throng,
And holy angels all around
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Take up the song.

Our hearts are tuned for this,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Their songs our tongues employ;
The Lamb, the spring of all our bliss,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And God our joy.
Salvation to our God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thanksgiving, power, and might!
And to the Lamb who shed His blood,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our life and light.


511
Satisfied with Thee, Lord Jesus, I am blest

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Bennett_Henry&#39;,this)">Henry Bennett</a> and <a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Wellesley_C_Anne&#39;,this)">C. A. Wellesley</a></div>
P.M.

SATISFIED with Thee, Lord Jesus, I am blest;
Peace which passeth understanding, On Thy breast; 
No more doubting, No more trembling,
No more trembling, Oh, what rest!

Occupied with Thee, Lord Jesus, In Thy grace;
All Thy ways and thoughts about me, Only trace
Deeper stories Of the glories,
Of the glories Of Thy grace.

Taken up with Thee, Lord Jesus, I would be;
Finding joy and satisfaction All in Thee;
Thou the nearest And the dearest,
And the dearest Unto me.

Listening for Thy shout, Lord Jesus, In the air;
When Thy saints shall rise with joy to Meet Thee there.
Oh, what gladness! No more sadness,
No more sadness, Sin nor care.

Longing for the bride, Lord Jesus, Of Thy heart;
To be with Thee in the glory, Where Thou art.
Love so groundless, Grace so boundless,
Grace so boundless Wins my heart.

When Thy blood-bought church, Lord Jesus, Is complete;
When each soul is safely landed At Thy feet;
What a story In the glory,
In the glory She’ll repeat!

Oh, to praise Thee there, Lord Jesus, Evermore! 
Oh, to grieve and wander from Thee Nevermore! 
Earth’s sad story Closed in glory, 
Closed in glory On yon shore!

Then Thy church will be, Lord Jesus, The display 
Of Thy richest grace and kindness In that day; 
Marking pages, Wondrous stages, 
Wondrous stages, O’er earth’s way.


512
Saviour, lead us by Thy power
Previous BHB #444<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Williams_William&#39;,this)">William Williams of Pantycelyn</a></div>
8.7.8.7.D.

SAVIOUR, lead us by Thy power
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Safe into the promised rest;
Choose the path, the way whatever
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Seems to Thee, O Lord, the best:
Be our guide in every peril,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Watch and keep us night and day,
Else our foolish hearts will wander
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From the straight and narrow way.

Since in Thee is our redemption,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And salvation full and free,
Nothing need our souls dishearten
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But forgetfulness of Thee:
Naught can stay our steady progress,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;More than conquerors are we,
If our eyes, whate’er the danger,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Look to Thee, and none but Thee.

In Thy presence we are happy;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In Thy presence we’re secure;
In Thy presence all afflictions
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We can steadfastly endure;
In Thy presence we can conquer,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We can suffer, we can die;
Wandering from Thee we are feeble;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Let Thy love then keep us nigh.


513
Saviour, more than life to me
Previous BHB #244<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Alystyne_Frances_Jane_Van&#39;,this)">Frances (Fanny) Jane Crosby (1868)</a></div>
P.M.

SAVIOUR, more than life to me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I am clinging, clinging close to Thee;
Let Thy precious blood applied,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Keep me ever, ever near Thy side.

c
Every day, every hour,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Let me feel Thy cleansing power;
May Thy tender love to me
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Bind me closer, closer, Lord, to Thee.

Through this changing world below,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lead me gently, gently as I go;
Trusting Thee I cannot stray,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I can never, never lose my way.

c
Every day, every hour,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Let me feel Thy cleansing power;
May Thy tender love to me
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Bind me closer, closer, Lord, to Thee.

Let me love Thee more and more,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Till this fleeting, fleeting life is o’er;
Till my soul is lost in love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In a brighter, brighter world above.

c
Every day, every hour,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Let me feel Thy cleansing power;
May Thy tender love to me
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Bind me closer, closer, Lord, to Thee.


514
Saviour, round these emblems blessed

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kyle_T&#39;,this)">T. Kyle</a></div>
8.7.8.7.

SAVIOUR, round these emblems blessèd,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Here we gather to Thy name;
We remember Thy blest Person,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All Thy suffering, death and shame.

We recall Thy grace in coming,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From Thy Father’s side above,
To this scene of sin and darkness,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To make known His perfect love.

In Thy blessèd Holy Person,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;God and man in one unite;
All the fulness of the Godhead
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Manifested to our sight.

We remember all Thy pathway
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of delight and joy to God;
Bringing glory ever to Him,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Perfect was Thy way, O Lord.

With these emblems thus before us,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We recall Thy death of shame;
Yet remember all the glory
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That it brought to Thy blest name.


515
Saviour, Thou art waiting, waiting

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Francis_Samuel_Trevor&#39;,this)">Samuel Trevor Francis</a></div>
8.7.8.5.D.

SAVIOUR, Thou art waiting, waiting,
Waiting for Thy blood-bought Bride,
Waiting for Thy heart’s deep longing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To be satisfied;
Waiting for the joyous moment,
Watching us with tender care,
Till at Thy command upspringing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We Thy glory share.

Saviour, we are waiting, waiting,
Waiting, blessèd Lord, for Thee,
Waiting for the calling upward,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With our Lord to be:
Long and lone hath been the pathway,
Oft our hearts have failed with fear;
But the guiding star of morning
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Heralds Thee as near.

They are waiting, waiting, waiting,
Loved ones who have gone before -
Waiting for the sweet home-bringing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Parting nevermore,
Oh, the joy, the bliss, the rapture -
All our tears and sorrows past,
Singing, with triumphant praises,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“Safely home at last!”

Keep us, blessèd Jesus, waiting,
Clinging to Thy precious word;
Every footstep of the journey,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Waiting for our Lord;
Waiting till within the mansions
We behold Thy glorious face,
Singing all the wondrous story
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of Thy matchless grace.


516
Saviour, through the desert lead us!
Previous BHB #254
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kelly_Thomas&#39;,this)">Thomas Kelly</a></div>
8.7.8.7.4.7.

SAVIOUR, through the desert lead us!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Without Thee we cannot go;
Thou from cruel chains hast freed us,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou hast laid the tyrant low;
Let Thy presence
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Cheer us all our journey through.

With a price Thy love has bought us;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Saviour, what a love is Thine!
Hitherto Thy power has brought us,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Power and love in Thee combine:
Lord of Glory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Ever on Thy people shine.

Through a desert, waste and cheerless,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Though our destined journey lie,
Rendered by Thy presence fearless,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We may every foe defy:
Nought shall move us
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;While we see Thee, Saviour, nigh.

When we halt, no track discovering,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Fearful lest we go astray,
O’er our path Thy pillar hovering,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Fire by night, and cloud by day,
Shall direct us
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That we may not miss our way.

When we hunger Thou wilt feed us;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Manna shall our camp surround;
Faint and thirsty, Thou wilt heed us,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Streams shall from the rock abound:
Happy people!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What a Saviour we have found!


517
Saviour, Thy dying love Thou gavest me
Previous BHB #443<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Phelps_Dr_Sylvanus_Dryden&#39;,this)">Sylvanus Dryden Phelps  (1862)</a></div>
6.4.6.4.6.6.6.4.

SAVIOUR, Thy dying love
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou gavest me,
Nor should I aught withhold,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My Lord, from Thee;
In love my soul would bow,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My heart fulfil its vow,
Some offering bring Thee now,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Something for Thee.

At the blest mercy-seat
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Pleading for me,
My feeble faith looks up,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus, to Thee;
Help me the cross to bear,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy wondrous love declare,
Some song to raise, or prayer,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Something for Thee.

Give me a faithful heart,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Likeness to Thee;
That each departing day
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Henceforth may see
Some work of love begun,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Some deed of kindness done,
Some wanderer sought and won,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Something for Thee.

All that I am and have,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy gifts so free;
In joy, in grief, through life,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O Lord, for Thee!
And when Thy face I see
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My ransomed soul shall be,
Through all eternity,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Something for Thee.


518
Saviour, we remember Thee!
Previous BHB #251<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Francis_Samuel_Trevor&#39;,this)">Samuel Trevor Francis</a></div>
7.7.7.6.

SAVIOUR, we remember Thee!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy deep woe and agony,
All Thy suffering on the tree:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Saviour, we adore Thee.

Calvary! O Calvary!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Mercy’s vast unfathomed sea,
Love, eternal love to me:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Saviour, we adore Thee.

Darkness hung around Thy head,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When for sin Thy blood was shed,
Victim in the sinner’s stead:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Saviour, we adore Thee.

Jesus, Lord, Thou now art risen!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou hast all our sins forgiven;
Haste we to our home in heaven:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Saviour, we adore Thee.

Soon, with joyful, glad surprise,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We shall hear Thy word - Arise!
Mounting upward to the skies:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Glory, glory, glory!

Saviour, we Thy love adore;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We will praise Thee more and more;
Spread Thy Name from shore to shore:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Saviour, we adore Thee.


519
Show me Thy face - one transient gleam

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Anonymous_Hymn_Writers&#39;,this)">Author Unknown</a></div>
C.M.D.

SHOW me Thy face - one transient gleam
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of loveliness divine,
And I shall never think or dream
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of other love save Thine;
All lesser lights will darken quite,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All lower glories wane,
The beautiful of earth will scarce
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Seem beautiful again.

Show me Thy face - my faith and love
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shall henceforth fixèd be,
And nothing here have power to move 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My soul’s serenity.
My life shall seem a trance, a dream,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And all I feel and see, 
Illusive, visionary -Thou 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The one reality.

Show me Thy face - I shall forget
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The weary days of yore;
The fretting ghosts of vain regret
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shall haunt my soul no more.
All doubts and fears for future years
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In quiet trust subside;
And naught but blest content and calm
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Within my breast abide.

Show me Thy face - the heaviest cross
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Will then seem light to bear;
There will be gain in every loss,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And peace with every care.
With such light feet the years will fleet,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Life will seem brief as blest,
Till I have laid my burden down
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And entered into rest.

Show me Thy face - and I shall be
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In heart and mind renewed,
With wisdom, grace, and energy
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To work Thy work, endued. 
Shine clear, though pale, behind the veil,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Until, the veil removed,
In perfect glory I behold
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The face that I have loved!


520
Show me Thy wounds, exalted Lord!

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Chapman_Robert_Cleaver&#39;,this)">Robert Cleaver Chapman</a></div>
L.M.

SHOW me Thy wounds, exalted Lord!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou hast the power and skill divine,
Since Justice smote Thee with the sword,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To make my heart resemble Thine.

Oh, grant me ever to behold,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With heavenly wisdom’s piercing eye,
Thy pains of death, for they unfold
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy name, Thou Son of God,  Most High!

Show me Thy wounds, and by Thy skill
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;May I, my Saviour, be refined,
To do, like Thee, the Father’s will,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And serve Him with a perfect mind.


521
Simply trusting every day
Previous BHB #248<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Stites_Edgar_Page&#39;,this)">Edgar Page Stites  (1876)</a></div>
7.7.7.7.D.

SIMPLY trusting every day,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Trusting through a stormy way;
Even when my faith is small,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Trusting Jesus, that is all.

c
Trusting Him while life shall last,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Trusting Him till earth is past,
Till within the jasper wall;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Trusting Jesus, that is all.

Brightly doth His Spirit shine
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Into this poor heart of mine;
While He leads I cannot fall,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Trusting Jesus, that is all.

c
Trusting Him while life shall last,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Trusting Him till earth is past,
Till within the jasper wall;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Trusting Jesus, that is all.

Singing, if my way is clear,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Praying, if the path is drear;
If in danger, for Him call;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Trusting Jesus, that is all.

c
Trusting Him while life shall last,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Trusting Him till earth is past,
Till within the jasper wall;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Trusting Jesus, that is all.

Trusting as the moments fly,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Trusting as the days go by;
Trusting Him whate’er befall,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Trusting Jesus, that is all.

c
Trusting Him while life shall last,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Trusting Him till earth is past,
Till within the jasper wall;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Trusting Jesus, that is all.

Sing the wondrous love of Jesus

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He’ll prepare for us a place.

c
When we all get to heaven,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What a day of rejoicing that will be!
When we all see Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We’ll sing and shout the victory!

While we walk the pilgrim pathway,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Clouds will overspread the sky;
But when trav’lling days are over,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Not a shadow, not a sigh. 

c

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What a day of rejoicing that will be!

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We’ll sing and shout the victory!

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Trusting, serving every day;

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Will the toils of life repay. 

c

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What a day of rejoicing that will be!
When we all see Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We’ll sing and shout the victory!

Onward to the prize before us!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Soon His beauty we’ll behold;
Soon the pearly gates will open;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We shall tread the streets of gold.

c
When we all get to heaven,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What a day of rejoicing that will be!
When we all see Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We’ll sing and shout the victory!


523
Sing to God my spirit sing

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Blane_William&#39;,this)">William Blane</a></div>
7.7.7.6.

SING to God, my spirit, sing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Joyful praise and worship bring!
He whom sinners mocked as King - 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He shall bear the glory! 

He in lowly guise who came
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Bore the spitting and the shame,
His the highest place and name -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He shall bear the glory.

He who wept above the grave,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He who stilled the raging wave,
Meek to suffer, strong to save -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He shall bear the glory.

He who sorrow’s pathway trod,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He that every good bestowed,
Son of Man and Son of God -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He shall bear the glory.

He who bled with scourging sore, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thorns and scarlet meekly wore, 
He who every sorrow bore - 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He shall bear the glory. 

Monarch of the smitten cheek, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Scorn of Jew and scorn of Greek, 
Priest and King, divinely meek - 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He shall bear the glory. 

On the rainbow-circled throne 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Mid the myriads of His own, 
Nevermore to weep alone - 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He shall bear the glory. 

Man of slighted Nazareth -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;King who wore the thorny wreath -
Son obedient unto death -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He shall bear the glory.

His the grand eternal weight, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His the priestly-regal state; 
Him the Father maketh great - 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He shall bear the glory.

He who died to set us free, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He who lives and loves e’en me, 
He who comes, whom I shall see - 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He shall bear the glory.


524
So Send I You

<p>Copyright © 1964 New Spring Publishing Inc. (ASCAP) (adm. at CapitolCMGPublishing.com) All rights reserved. Used by permission.</p><div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Clarkson_Margaret_E&#39;,this)">Margaret E. Clarkson</a></div>
11.10.11.10.

SO send I you to labour unrewarded,
To serve unpaid, unloved, unsought, unknown,
To bear rebuke, to suffer scorn and scoffing -
So send I you to toil for Me alone.

So send I you - by grace made strong to triumph 
O’er hosts of hell, o’er darkness, death and sin,
My name to bear and in that name to conquer - 
So send I you, My victory to win.

So send I you to bind the bruised and broken,
O’er wand’ring souls to work, to weep, to wake,
To bear the burdens of a world a-weary -
So send I you to suffer for My sake.

So send I you - to take to souls in bondage
The Word of Truth that sets the captive free,
To break the bonds of sin, to loose death’s fetters - 
So send I you, to bring the lost to Me.

So send I you to loneliness and longing,
With heart a-hung’ring for the loved and known,
Forsaking home and kindred, friend and dear one -
So send I you to know My love alone.

So send I you - My strength to know in weakness,
My joy in grief, My perfect peace in pain,
To prove My pow’r, My grace, My promised presence - 
So send I you, eternal fruit to gain.

So send I you to leave your life’s ambition,
To die to dear desire, self-will resign,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To labour long, and love where  men revile you -
So send I you to lose your life in Mine.

So send I you - to bear My cross with patience,
And then one day with joy to lay it down,
To hear My voice, “Well done, My faithful servant - 
Come share My throne, My kingdom and My crown!”


525
Some day the silver cord will break

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Alystyne_Frances_Jane_Van&#39;,this)">Frances (Fanny) Jane Crosby</a></div>
L.M.D.

SOME day the silver cord will break,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And I no more as now shall sing;
But, oh, the joy when I shall wake
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Within the palace of the King!

c
And I shall see Him face to face,
And tell the story - Saved by grace;
And I shall see Him face to face,
And tell the story - Saved by grace.

Some day my earthly house will fall,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I cannot tell how soon ’twill be,
But this I know - my All in All
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Has now a place in heav’n for me. 

c
And I shall see Him face to face,
And tell the story - Saved by grace;
And I shall see Him face to face,
And tell the story - Saved by grace.

Some day, when fades the golden sun 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Beneath the rosy-tinted west,
My blessèd Lord shall say, “Well done!”
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And I shall enter into rest. 

c
And I shall see Him face to face,
And tell the story - Saved by grace;
And I shall see Him face to face,
And tell the story - Saved by grace.

Some day; till then I’ll watch and wait,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My lamp all trimmed and burning  bright,
That when my Saviour ope’s the gate,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My soul to Him may take its flight. 

c
And I shall see Him face to face,
And tell the story - Saved by grace;
And I shall see Him face to face,
And tell the story - Saved by grace.


526
Some golden daybreak

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Blackmore_Carl&#39;,this)">Carl Blackmore</a></div><p>&copy; The Rodeheaver Co/Word Music LLC (Adm Song Solutions www.songsolutions.org). All rights reserved. Used by permission.</p>
9.9.9.9. with Chorus

SOME glorious morning, sorrow will cease,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Some glorious morning, all will be peace;
Heartaches all ended, labour all done, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Heaven will open - Jesus will come.

c
Some golden daybreak, Jesus will come;
Some golden daybreak, battles all won,
He’ll shout the vict’ry, break through the blue,
Some golden daybreak, for me, for you.

Sad hearts will gladden, all shall be bright,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Goodbye forever to earth’s dark night;
Changed in a moment, like Him  to be,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Oh, glorious daybreak, Jesus I’ll see!

c
Some golden daybreak, Jesus will come;
Some golden daybreak, battles all won,
He’ll shout the vict’ry, break through the blue,
Some golden day-break, for me, for you.

Oh, what a meeting, there in the skies,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No tears nor crying shall dim our eyes;
Loved ones united, eternally,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Oh, what a daybreak, that morn will be!

c
Some golden daybreak, Jesus will come;
Some golden daybreak, battles all won,
He’ll shout the vict’ry, break through the blue,
Some golden day-break, for me, for you.


527
Sometimes a light surprises

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Cowper_William&#39;,this)">William Cowper</a></div>
7.6.7.6.D.

SOMETIMES a light surprises
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Christian while he sings;
It is the Lord who rises
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With healing in His wings;
When comforts are declining,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He grants the soul again
A season of clear shining,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To cheer it after rain.

In holy contemplation
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We sweetly then pursue
The theme of God’s salvation,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And find it ever new;
Set free from present sorrow,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We cheerfully can say -
E’en let the unknown morrow
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Bring with it what it may.

It can bring with it nothing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But He will bear us through;
Who gives the lilies clothing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Will clothe His people too:
Beneath the spreading heavens
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No creature but is fed;
And He who feeds the ravens,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Will give His children bread.

Though vine nor fig tree neither
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Their wonted fruit shall bear;
Though all the fields should wither
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Nor flocks nor herds be there;
Yet God the same abiding,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His praise shall tune my voice,
For, while in Him confiding,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I cannot but rejoice.


528
Son of God, exalted now

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Withy_John&#39;,this)">John Withy</a></div>
7.7.7.7.

SON of God, exalted now,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Highest honours crown Thy brow;
On the Father’s throne divine,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All the Conqueror’s triumphs Thine.

Here Thy cup was grief and shame,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Here, despised Thy lowly name;
Hour of darkness, power of hell,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On Thy spotless soul then fell.

Now Thy travail all is o’er,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou shalt humbled be no more;
Joy to Thee shall ever flow
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From Thy toil and shame below.

Son of God, exalted now,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thee we worship, bending low;
This the Father claims for Thee -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Reverent lip and bowèd knee.

Gather now full many a gem,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Saviour, for Thy diadem -
Trophies of Thy toil and love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Meet to shine in courts above.

Speed the bright, millennial day,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Call Thy Bride to come away;
Through the earth let joy and song
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy glad triumph roll along!


529
Son of God, ’twas love that made Thee
Previous BHB #250<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Anonymous_Hymn_Writers&#39;,this)">Anonymous</a></div>
8.7.8.7.D.

SON of God, ’twas love that made Thee
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Die, our ruined souls to save;
’Twas our sins’ vast load that laid Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lord of Life, within the grave;
But Thy glorious resurrection
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Showed Thee conqueror o’er the tomb;
So the saints by Thy protection
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Through Thy work shall overcome.

Thou to heaven hast now ascended,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Ent’ring there by Thine own blood;
All Thy work of suff’ring ended,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Fully wrought the will of God.
For Thy Church Thou still art caring,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For us pleading in Thy love;
And our place of rest preparing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In the Father’s house above.

Now the Holy Ghost doth gather
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Unto God Thy people here;
We, as sons, cry, “Abba, Father!”
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His great love excluding fear:
What a debt of love we owe Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Love that we can ne’er express,
Since we, through the Spirit, know Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Christ the Lord, our righteousness.


530
Son of God! with joy we praise Thee

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Tregelles_Samuel_Prideaux&#39;,this)">Samuel Tregelles</a></div>
8.7.8.7.D.

SON of God! with joy we praise Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On the Father’s throne above;
All Thy wondrous work displays Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Full of grace, and full of love:
Lord, accept our adoration -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For our sins Thou once wast slain;
Through Thy blood we have salvation,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And with Thee we soon shall reign.

God, in Thee His love unfolding,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shows how rich has been His grace;
We are blest, with joy beholding
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All His glory in Thy face;
In His counsel, ere creation,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All the Church He chose in Thee;
And our Surety for salvation
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou wast then ordained to be.

When it seemed that sin must sever
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All the chosen heirs from God,
Thou, with love which faileth never,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Didst redeem us by Thy blood:
Oh, the mercy which hath blest us,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Purposed thus ere time began -
Mercy which in Thee hath kept us,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Mercy vast, like heaven’s span!


531
Soon shall our Master come, our toil and sorrow cease

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Deck_James_George&#39;,this)">James George Deck</a></div>
S.M.

SOON shall our Master come,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our toil and sorrow cease;
He’ll call His waiting servants home
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To endless joy and peace.

Now may we do His will,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In all His footsteps tread;
And, in a world of evil, still
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To grieve Him only dread.

May we His name confess
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;’Midst suffering, shame, and loss;
Stand forth His faithful witnesses,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And glory in the Cross.

Watchful may we be found,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our loins well girded be;
In works of faith and love abound,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Till we our Master see.

Then shall we soar above,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Nor cease our sweet employ,
And hear Him say, with tenderest love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“Enter thy Master’s joy”.


532
Soon will the Master come – soon pass away

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Deck_James_George&#39;,this)">James George Deck</a></div>
10.10.10.10.10.10.

SOON will the Master come - soon pass away
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our times of conflict, grief and suffering here;
Our night of weeping end in cloudless day,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And sorrow’s moment like a dream appear:
Eternity with Jesus, in the skies -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;How soon that Sun of righteousness may rise!

We shall behold Him whom unseen we love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We shall be with Him whom we long to see;
We shall be like Him, fit for realms above -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With Him, and like Him, for eternity:
If now to sit at Jesus’ feet our  choice,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;How will fruition then our souls rejoice!


533
Sorrows abounding, beyond human sounding

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Ewan_Y_Isaac&#39;,this)">Isaac Y. Ewan</a></div>
11.10.10.10.

SORROWS abounding, Beyond human sounding, 
Darkness surrounding, Low He prayed and wept; 
Deep were they sleeping Who watch should have kept, 
While He, with weeping, Prayed the while they slept.

Sorrows exceeding, Beyond human heeding, 
Burdened His pleading In that gloomy glade; 
Prayer with strong crying And tears there He made 
On the ground, lying Prostrate as He prayed.

Sorrows unsparing, Beyond human bearing,
Too deep for sharing, Bowed His holy head;
Darkness besetting, Of prospects so dread,
With blood-like sweating Tearfully He pled.

Sorrows o’erflowing, Beyond human knowing,
Too deep for showing, Unto agony!
There, the cup taking, He rose to obey
Unto forsaking, In humility.


534
Sovereign grace! o’er sin abounding
Previous BHB #249<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kent_John&#39;,this)">John Kent</a></div>
8.7.8.7.4.7.

SOVEREIGN grace! o’er sin abounding,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Ransomed souls, the tidings swell;
’Tis a deep that knows no sounding;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who its breadth or length can tell?
On its glories
Let my soul for ever dwell!

What from Christ the soul can sever,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Bound by everlasting bands?
Once in Him, in Him for ever,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thus the eternal covenant stands:  
None shall pluck thee
From the Strength of Israel’s hands.

Heirs of God, joint-heirs with Jesus
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Long ere time its race began;
To His Name eternal praises,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O what wonders love hath done!  
One with Jesus;
By eternal union one.

On such love, my soul, still ponder,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Love so great, so rich, so free!
Say, while lost in holy wonder,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Why, O Lord, such love to me?  
Hallelujah!
Grace shall reign eternally.


535
Sowing the seed by the daylight fair

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Oakey_S_Emily&#39;,this)">Emily S. Oakey</a></div>
P.M.

SOWING the seed by the daylight fair,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sowing the seed by the noonday glare,
Sowing the seed by the fading light,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sowing the seed in the solemn night;
Oh, what shall the harvest be?

c
Sown in the darkness or sown in the light,
Sown in our weakness or sown in our might;
Gathered in time or eternity,
Sure, ah, sure will the harvest be!

Sowing the seed by the wayside high,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sowing the seed on the rocks to die,
Sowing the seed where the thorns will spoil,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sowing the seed in the fertile soil; 
Oh, what shall the harvest be?

c
Sown in the darkness or sown in the light,
Sown in our weakness or sown in our might;
Gathered in time or eternity,
Sure, ah, sure will the harvest be!

Sowing the seed with an aching heart,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sowing the seed while the  teardrops start,
Sowing in hope till the reapers come
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Gladly to gather the harvest home;
Oh, what shall the harvest be?

c
Sown in the darkness or sown in the light,
Sown in our weakness or sown in our might;
Gathered in time or eternity,
Sure, ah, sure will the harvest be!


536
Speak, Lord, in the stillness
Previous BHB #445
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Crawford_Emily_May&#39;,this)">Emily May Grimes Crawford  (1920)</a></div>
6.5.6.5.

SPEAK, Lord, in the stillness,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;While I wait on Thee;
Hush my heart to listen
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In expectancy.

Speak, O blessèd Master,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In this quiet hour;
Let me see Thy face, Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Feel Thy touch of power.

For the words Thou speakest,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;They are life indeed;
Living Bread from heaven
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Now my spirit feed.

All to Thee is yielded,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I am not my own;
Blissful, glad surrender,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I am Thine alone.

Speak, Thy servant heareth,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Be not silent, Lord;
Waits my soul upon Thee
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For the quickening word.

Fill me with the knowledge
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of Thy glorious will;
All Thine own good pleasure
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In Thy child fulfil.

Like a watered garden,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Full of fragrance rare,
Lingering in Thy presence
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Let my life appear.


537
Stricken, smitten and afflicted

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kelly_Thomas&#39;,this)">Thomas Kelly</a></div>
8.7.8.7.

“STRICKEN, smitten and afflicted”,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;See Him dying on the tree!
’Tis the Christ, by man rejected;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Yes, my soul, ’tis He! ’tis He!

Many hands were raised to wound Him,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;None would interpose to save;
But the awful stroke that bowed Him
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Was the stroke that Justice gave.

Mark the Sacrifice appointed!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;See Who bears the awful load!
’Tis the Word, the Lord’s Anointed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Son of Man and Son of God!

Here we have a firm foundation;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Here the refuge of the lost:
Christ, the rock of our salvation:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His the name in which we boast.

Lamb of God, for sinners wounded!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sacrifice to cancel guilt!
None shall ever be confounded
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who on Thee their hopes have built.


538
Sweet are the seasons when we wait
Previous BHB #253
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kelly_Thomas&#39;,this)">Thomas Kelly</a></div>
L.M.

SWEET are the seasons when we wait
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To hear what God our Lord will say,
For they who watch at Wisdom’s gate
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Are never empty sent away.

Behold us, Lord, a few of Thine,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who hither come to seek Thy face;
In mercy on Thy people shine
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And let Thy presence fill the place.

How sweet, how blessèd is the thought
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That Thou dost hear Thy people’s cries!
And whether Thou dost give, or not,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;’Tis love that grants, and love denies.

O teach us, Lord, to wait Thy will,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To be content with all Thou dost;
For us, Thy grace sufficient still,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With most supplied when needing  most.

Till life shall end, thus let it be,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And, O sustain us in that hour;
That conflict past, we hope to see
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Saviour whom we here adore.

We hope at length to take our part
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With yonder host, through trouble brought;
We hope to see Thee as Thou art
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And then to praise Thee as we ought.


539
Sweet feast of love divine!
Previous BHB #252<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Denny_Sir_Edward&#39;,this)">Edward Denny  (1839)</a></div>
S.M.

SWEET feast of love divine!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;’Tis grace that makes us free
To feed upon this bread and wine,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In memory, Lord, of Thee.

Here every welcome guest
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Waits, Lord, from Thee to learn
The secrets of Thy Father’s breast,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And all Thy grace discern.

Here conscience ends its strife,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And faith delights to prove
The sweetness of the Bread of Life,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The fulness of Thy love.

That blood that flowed for sin
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In symbol here we see,
And feel the blessèd pledge within
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That we are loved of Thee.

Oh! If this glimpse of love
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Is so divinely sweet,
What will it be, O Lord, above,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy gladdening smile to meet!

To see Thee face to face,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy perfect likeness wear,
And all Thy ways of wondrous grace
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Through endless years declare.


540
Sweet hour of prayer!

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Walford_William&#39;,this)">William Walford</a></div>
L.M.D.

SWEET hour of prayer! sweet hour of prayer!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That calls me from a world of care,
And bids me at my Father’s throne
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Make all my wants and wishes known.
In seasons of distress and grief,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My soul has often found relief,
And oft escaped the tempter’s snare,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;By thy return, sweet hour of prayer!

Sweet hour of prayer! sweet hour of prayer!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The joy I feel, the bliss I share,
Of those whose anxious spirits burn
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With strong desires for thy return!
With such I hasten to the place
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Where God, my Saviour, shows His face,
And gladly take my station there,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And wait for thee, sweet hour of  prayer!

Sweet hour of prayer! sweet hour of prayer!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy wings shall my petition bear
To Him whose truth and faithfulness
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Engage the waiting soul to bless;
And since He bids me seek His face,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Believe His Word and trust His grace,
I’ll cast on Him my every care,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And wait for thee, sweet hour of prayer!

Sweet hour of prayer! sweet hour of prayer!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;May I thy consolation share,
Till, from Mount Pisgah’s lofty height,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I view my home and take my flight.
This robe of flesh I’ll drop, and rise
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To seize the everlasting prize,
And shout, while passing through  the air,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“Farewell, farewell, sweet hour of prayer!”


541
Sweet is the savour of His Name
Previous BHB #256<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kelly_Thomas&#39;,this)">Thomas Kelly</a></div>
L.M.

SWEET is the savour of His Name
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who suffered in His people’s stead;
His portion here, reproach and shame:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He liveth now; He once was dead.

He once was dead; the very same
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who sits on yonder throne above;
Who bears in heaven the greatest Name,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Whom angels serve, whom angels love.

He once was dead; the very same
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who made the worlds - a work of  power,
Who now upholds the mighty frame,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And keeps it till the final hour.

He once was dead; but now He lives,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His glory fills all heaven above;
Its blessèdness to heaven He gives,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The fountain He of joy and love.

His people shall His triumph share,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With Him shall live, and with Him  reign;
In heaven their joy is full, for there
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;They see the Lamb for sinners slain.


542
Sweet the moments, rich in blessing
Previous BHB #258<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Allen_James&#39;,this)">James Allen</a> adapted by <a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Shirley_Sir_Walter&#39;,this)">William Walter Shirley</a>   (1761)</div>
8.7.8.7.

SWEET the moments, rich in blessing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Which before the Cross we spend,
Life, and health, and peace possessing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From the sinner’s dying Friend.

Here we rest, in wonder viewing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All our sins on Jesus laid,
And a full redemption flowing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From the sacrifice He made.

Here we find the dawn of heaven,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;While upon the Cross we gaze,
See our trespasses forgiven,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And our songs of triumph raise.

Oh that near the Cross abiding,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We may to the Saviour cleave!
Nought with Him our hearts dividing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All for Him content to leave.

May we still, the Cross discerning,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;There for peace and comfort go;
There new wonders daily learning,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All the depths of mercy know.


543
Sweet the theme of Jesus’ love

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Midlane_Albert&#39;,this)">Albert Midlane</a></div>
7.7.7.7.

SWEET the theme of Jesus’ love!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sweet the theme, all themes above;
Love, unmerited and free,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our triumphant song shall be.

Love so vast that nought can bound;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Love too deep for thought to sound;
Love which made the Lord of all
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Drink the wormwood and the gall.

Love which led Him to the Cross,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Bearing there unuttered loss;
Love which brought Him to the gloom
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of the cold and darksome tomb.

Love which made Him thence arise
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Far above the starry skies;
There, with tender, loving care,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All His people’s griefs to share.

Love which will not let Him rest
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Till His chosen all are blest;
Till they all for whom He died
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Live rejoicing at His side.


544
Sweeter sounds than music knows
Previous BHB #257<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Newton_John&#39;,this)">John Newton  (1779)</a></div>
7.7.7.7.

SWEETER sounds than music knows
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Charm me in Immanuel’s Name;
All her hopes my spirit owes
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To His birth, His Cross, His shame.

When He came, the angels sang,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“Glory be to God on high!”
Lord, unloose my stammering tongue;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who should louder sing than I?

Did the Lord a man become
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That He might the law fulfil,
Bleed and suffer in my room;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And canst thou, my tongue, be still?

No! I must my praises bring,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Though they worthless are and weak,
For should I refuse to sing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sure the very stones would speak.

O my Saviour, Shield, and Sun,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shepherd, Counsellor, and Friend!
Every precious name in one;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I will love Thee without end.


545
Take my life, and let it be
Previous BHB #446<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Havergal_Frances_Ridley&#39;,this)">Frances Ridley Havergal  (1874)</a></div>
7.7.7.7.

TAKE my life, and let it be
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Consecrated, Lord, to Thee;
Take my moments and my days,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Let them flow in ceaseless praise.

Take my hands, and let them move
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;At the impulse of Thy love;
Take my feet, and let them be
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Swift and beautiful for Thee.

Take my voice, and let me sing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Always, only, for my King;
Take my lips, and let them be
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Filled with messages from Thee.

Take my silver and my gold,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Not a mite would I withhold;
Take my intellect, and use
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Every power as Thou shalt choose.

Take my will, and make it Thine,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;It shall be no longer mine;
Take my heart - it is Thine own,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;It shall be Thy royal throne.

Take my love; my Lord, I pour
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;At Thy feet its treasure-store:
Take myself, and I will be
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Ever, only, all for Thee.


546
Take the name of Jesus with you

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Baxter_Lydia&#39;,this)">Lydia Baxter</a></div>
8.7.8.7. with Chorus

TAKE the name of Jesus with you,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Child of sorrow and of woe;
It will joy and comfort give you -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Take it, then, where’er you go.

c

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hope of earth and joy of heaven;
Precious name, Oh, how sweet!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hope of earth and joy of heaven.

If temptations round you gather,

Precious name, Oh, how sweet!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hope of earth and joy of heaven.

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;How it thrills our souls with joy,
When His loving arms receive us,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And His songs our tongues employ!

c

Precious name, Oh, how sweet!

At the name of Jesus bowing,

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When our journey is complete. 

c

547

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Oh, the length and breadth of love!
Oh, the fulness of redemption,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Pledge of endless life above.

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sweetest comfort of my soul;
With my Saviour watching o’er me
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I can sing, though billows roll.

c

Oh, the fulness of redemption,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Pledge of endless life above.

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Let me see His constant smile;
Then throughout my pilgrim journey
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Light will cheer me all the while.

Oh, the fulness of redemption,

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In His Cross my trust shall be,
Till, with clearer, brighter vision,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Face to face my Lord I see.

c
Oh, the height and depth of mercy!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Oh, the length and breadth of love!
Oh, the fulness of redemption,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Pledge of endless life above.


548
Take time to be holy, speak oft with thy Lord

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Longstaff_William&#39;,this)">William Longstaff</a></div>
11.11.11.11.

TAKE time to be holy, speak oft with thy Lord;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Abide in Him always, and feed on His Word.
Make friends of God’s children, help those who are weak;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Forgetting in nothing His blessing to seek.

Take time to be holy, the world rushes on;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Spend much time in secret, with Jesus alone.
By looking to Jesus, like Him thou shalt be;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy friends in thy conduct His likeness shall see.

Take time to be holy, let Him be thy Guide;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And run not before Him, whatever betide.
In joy or in sorrow, still follow thy Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And, looking to Jesus, still trust in  His Word.

Take time to be holy, be calm in thy soul;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Each thought and each motive beneath His control. 
Thus led by His Spirit to fountains of love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou soon shalt be fitted for service above.


549
Teach me Thy way, O Lord
Previous BHB #447
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Ramsey_Benjamin_Mansell&#39;,this)">Benjamin Mansell Ramsey  (1919)</a></div>
6.4.6.4.6.6.6.4.

TEACH me Thy way, O Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Teach me Thy way!
Thy gracious aid afford,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Teach me Thy way!
Help me to walk aright,
More by faith, less by sight;
Lead me with heavenly light,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Teach me Thy way!

When I am sad at heart,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Teach me Thy way!
When earthly joys depart,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Teach me Thy way!
In hours of loneliness,
In times of dire distress,
In failure or success,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Teach me Thy way!

When doubts and fears arise,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Teach me Thy way!
When storms o’erspread the skies
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Teach me Thy way!
Shine through the cloud and rain,
Through sorrow, toil and pain;
Make Thou my pathway plain,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Teach me Thy way!

Long as my life shall last,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Teach me Thy way!
Wher’er my lot is cast,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Teach me Thy way!
Until the race is run,
Until the journey’s done,
Until the crown is won,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Teach me Thy way!


550
That night in which He was betrayed

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Ewan_Y_Isaac&#39;,this)">Isaac Y. Ewan</a></div>
C.M.

THAT night in which He was betrayed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In love’s attention sweet,
And with the lowly towel arrayed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He washed His followers’ feet.

That night in love the loaf He took,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He blessed, He brake, He gave, 
And mem’ry gives the backward look 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That parting love would crave.

That night the cup He took, anew
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Gave thanks, and lovingly,
He poured it out and said, “This do
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In memory of Me”.

That night His thoughtful love was shown,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When He provision made,
Not for Himself, but for His own,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And to His Father prayed.

That night the last few steps He took
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of all that wondrous way,
And passed o’er Kidron’s wintry brook
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Into Gethsemane.

That night, that sad and solemn night,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Alone, He wept and prayed;
That night, that memorable night
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In which He was betrayed.


551
That sight of love unveiled

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kirkpatrick_William&#39;,this)">William Kirkpatrick</a></div>
S.M.

“THAT sight” of love unveiled
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our vision fills anew -
Upon a Cross, by foes assailed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Son of God we view.

In such a scene of hate,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With scorn upon Him cast,
His grace appears, His meekness great,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His condescension vast.

Deep calleth unto deep,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;While sorrows that appal 
Like raging floods around Him sweep;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Unmoved, He bears it all.

We see His purpose true,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Scriptures to fulfil,
In absolute obedience to
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His Father’s holy will.

The gathered fragrance sweet
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From all the path He trod,
Now at His death, ascends complete
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As incense unto God.

Our sacrifice of praise
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Thee, O God, we bring;
We worship as on Him we gaze,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And as of Him we sing.


552
The atoning work is done
Previous BHB #260<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kelly_Thomas&#39;,this)">Thomas Kelly  (1806)</a></div>
6.6.6.6.8.8.

THE atoning work is done,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Victim’s blood is shed;
And Jesus now has gone
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His people’s cause to plead.
He stands in heaven, their great High Priest,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And bears their names upon His breast.

He sprinkled with His blood
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The mercy-seat above;
For justice had withstood
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The purposes of love:
But justice now withstands no more,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And mercy yields its boundless  store.

No temple made with hands
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His place of service is;
In heaven itself He stands,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A heavenly priesthood His:
In Him the shadows of the law
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Are all fulfilled, and now withdraw.

And though awhile He be
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hid from the eyes of men,
His people look to see
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Their great High Priest again:
In brightest glory He will come
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And take His waiting people home.


553
The bread and wine are spread upon the board
Previous BHB #448<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Goodman_George&#39;,this)">George Goodman</a></div>
10.10.10.10.

THE bread and wine are spread upon the board,
The guests are here, invited by the Lord;
Why come they thus? Why tarry for a space?
But for Thy presence, O Thou King of grace.

Hush, O our hearts, as in the sacred name
We bow in worship and the promise claim -
Where two or three are gathered there am I,
Unseen, yet present to faith’s opened eye.

Here in our midst art Thou, O risen Lord;
Worthy, O Lamb once slain, to be adored;
Here in our midst to lead Thy people’s praise,
And incense sweet unto the Father raise.

We do remember Thee, as Thou hast said,
And think upon Thee as we break the bread,
Recall Thy dying love, Thy Cross and shame,
Drinking the cup of blessing in Thy name.

Thus do we show the death of our dear Lord,
While in our hearts His love is shed abroad;
So is faith quickened for the conflict here,
Till in a little while He shall appear.

Only a little while we pilgrims stay 
To spread the table on our desert way;
Soon will He come, and coming take us home,
Amen, e’en so, Lord Jesus quickly come!


554
The Church’s one foundation
Previous BHB #449
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Stone_Samuel_John&#39;,this)">Samuel John Stone  (1866)</a></div>
7.6.7.6.D.

THE Church’s one foundation
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Is Jesus Christ her Lord:
She is His new creation
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;By water and the Word;
From heaven He came and sought her
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To be His holy bride;
With His own blood He bought her,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And for her life He died.

Elect from every nation,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Yet one o’er all the earth,
Her charter of salvation
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;One Lord, one faith, one birth:
One holy name she blesses,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Partakes one holy food,
And to one hope she presses,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With every grace endued.

Though with a scornful wonder
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Men see her sore oppressed,
By schisms rent asunder,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;By heresies distressed,
Yet saints their watch are keeping,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Their cry goes up, “How long?”
And soon the night of weeping
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shall be the morn of song.

’Mid toil and tribulation,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And tumult of her war,
She waits the consummation
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of peace for evermore,
Till, with the vision glorious,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Her longing eyes are blest,
And the great Church victorious
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shall be the Church at rest.

Yet she on earth hath union
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With God the Three in One,
And mystic sweet communion
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With those whose rest is won;
Oh, happy ones and holy!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lord, give us grace that we,
Like them, the meek and lowly,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On high may dwell with Thee!


555
The cloudless day is nearing
Previous BHB #262<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;H_C_A&#39;,this)">C. A. H.</a></div>
7.6.7.6.D.

THE cloudless day is nearing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When Thou, O Lord, wilt come,
Thy radiant beauty wearing, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To take Thy people home!
Bright hosts on hosts around Thee
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shall catch Thy living rays,
And all who once have found Thee
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Breathe out new songs of praise.

But how shall I then know Thee
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Amid those hosts above?
What tokens true will show me
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The object of my love?
Thy glories, all excelling,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In pure effulgence shine;
But glory in Thee dwelling
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Will ne’er proclaim Thee mine.

Thy wounds, Thy wounds, Lord Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Those deep, deep wounds will tell
The sacrifice that frees us
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From self, and death, and hell!
These link Thee once for ever
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With all who own Thy grace;
No hand these bonds can sever,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No hand these scars efface.

O Jesus! Lord most blessèd,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou Lamb of God divine,
Thou standest forth confessèd,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I wondering, claim Thee mine.
I worship and adore Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Transported with Thy love;
Prostrate myself before Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And dwell in light above.


556
The countless multitude on high
Previous BHB #265
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Rutherford_Archibald_J&#39;,this)">Archibald Rutherford</a> & <a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Sandeman_Robert&#39;,this)">Robert Sandeman  (1775)</a></div>
L.M.

THE countless multitude on high,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who tune their songs to Jesus’ name,
All merit of their own deny,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And Jesus’ worth alone proclaim.

Firm on the ground of sovereign grace
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;They stand before Jehovah’s throne;
The only song in that blest place
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Is - “Thou art worthy, Thou alone!”

“Salvation’s glory all be paid
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Him who sits upon the throne,
And to the Lamb whose blood was shed;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou! Thou art worthy, Thou alone.”

“For Thou wast slain, and in Thy blood
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;These robes were washed so  spotless pure!
Thou mad’st us kings and priests to God:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For ever let Thy praise endure!”

Let us with joy adopt the strain
We soon shall sing for ever there -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“Worthy’s the Lamb for sinners slain
Worthy alone the crown to wear!”

Without one thought that’s good to plead, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Oh, what could shield us from despair,
But this, though we are vile indeed, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Lord our righteousness is there!


557
The Cross! the Cross! the blood-stained Cross!
Previous BHB #261<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Stockton_John_Hart&#39;,this)">John Hart Stockton</a></div>
C.M.

THE Cross! the Cross! the blood-stained Cross!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Cross of Christ I see,
It tells me of that precious blood
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That once was shed for me.

The wrath! the wrath! the awful wrath
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That Jesus felt for me!
When bearing my sin’s heavy load
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He died on Calvary.

But Jesus lives! the Saviour lives!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In heaven He pleads for me;
And boldly I approach to God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His blood my only plea.

The crown! the crown! the glorious crown!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The crown of victory!
The crown of life! it shall be mine
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When I the Saviour see.

He comes! He comes! the Saviour comes!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who bled and died for me;
Then will I sing, with rapture sing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When gazing, Lord, on Thee.


558
The day of glory bearing
Previous BHB #266
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Deck_James_George&#39;,this)">James George Deck  (1841)</a></div>
7.6.7.6.D.

THE day of glory bearing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Its brightness far and near 
The day of Christ’s appearing, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We now no longer fear;
For we shall rise to meet Him
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Triumphant in the sky,
And every heart shall greet Him
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With songs of victory.

He once, a spotless Victim,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For us, on Calvary bled;
Jehovah did afflict Him,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And bruise Him in our stead; 
To Him by grace united,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We joy in Him alone;
And now, by faith, delighted,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Behold Him on the throne.

There He is interceding
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For all who on Him rest; 
And grace from Him proceeding
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Tells how in Him we’re blest:
Soon, to His place in glory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His waiting saints He’ll raise,
To chant their joyful story
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In songs of loudest praise!


559
The day returns, into His Name

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Murray_George&#39;,this)">George Murray</a></div>
C.M.

THE day returns, into His Name,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O God, we gather thus
To give to Thee through Him Who came 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To make Thee known to us.

His glories great, His matchless worth,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In all His path we trace,
His life recall, His death tell forth 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And praise His love and grace.

Sweet are the accents to Thine ear
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That praise that Worthy One 
Whom we remember, gathered here, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy well-belovèd Son.

The loaf and cup before us spread
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The precious truths declare, 
His body giv’n, His blood thus shed, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His grace beyond compare.

With such sweet symbols of His love
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Laid thus before our gaze,
Our hearts we lift to Thee above 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In sacrifice of praise.


560
The day Thou gavest, Lord, is ended
Previous BHB #450<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Ellerton_John&#39;,this)">John Ellerton  (1870)</a></div>
9.8.9.8.

THE day Thou gavest, Lord, is ended;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The darkness falls at Thy behest;
To Thee our morning hymns ascended,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy praise shall sanctify our rest.

We thank Thee that Thy Church unsleeping,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;While earth rolls onward into light,
Through all the world her watch is  keeping,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And rests not now by day or night.

As o’er each continent and island
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The dawn leads on another day,
The voice of prayer is never silent,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Nor dies the strain of praise away.

The sun that bids us rest is waking
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our brethren ’neath the western sky;
And hour by hour fresh lips are making
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy wondrous doings heard on high.

So be it, Lord! Thy throne shall never,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Like earth’s proud empires, pass  away;
Thy kingdom stands, and grows for ever,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Till all Thy creatures own Thy sway.


561
The gloom of dark Gethsemane

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Ewan_Y_Isaac&#39;,this)">Isaac Y. Ewan</a></div>
L.M.

THE gloom of dark Gethsemane, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The sore amaze, the mystery, 
The blood-like sweat, the agony, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Remembering! Remembering!

The holy calm, the clam’rous cry,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of “crucify!” yea, “crucify!”
The silent Man led forth to die, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Remembering! Remembering!

The shame, the pain, the darkness dread, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The lonely cry, the bowèd head,
The Prince of Life becoming dead, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Remembering! Remembering!

Rememb’ring Him, Whom we adore, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Whose griefs, O God, we ponder o’er, 
The loaf we break, the cup we pour, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Remembering! Remembering!


562
The glory shines before me
Previous BHB #263
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Burlingham_Hannah_Killham&#39;,this)">Hannah Kilham Burlingham</a></div>
7.6.7.6.D.

THE glory shines before me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I cannot linger here;
Though clouds may darken o’er me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My Father’s house is near:
If through this barren desert
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A little while I roam,
The glory shines before me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I am not far from home.

Beyond the storms I’m going,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Beyond this vale of tears,
Beyond the floods o’erflowing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Beyond the changing years:
I’m going to the better land,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;By faith long since possessed:
The glory shines before me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For this is not my rest.

The Lamb is there the glory!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Lamb is there the light!
Affliction’s grasp but tears me
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From phantoms of the night:
The voice of Jesus calleth me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My race will soon be run;
The glory shines before me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The prize will soon be won.

The glory shines before me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I know that all is well;
My Father’s care is o’er me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His praises I would tell:
The love of Christ constraineth me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His blood hath washed me white;
Where Jesus is in glory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;’Tis home, and love, and light.


563
The God of Abraham praise
Previous BHB #273
<div class="author">Daniel ben Judah tr/ad by <a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Olivers_Thomas&#39;,this)">Thomas Olivers</a>  (c.14)</div>
6.6.8.4.D.

THE God of Abraham praise,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who reigns enthroned above,
Ancient of everlasting days,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And God of love.
Jehovah, great I AM!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;By earth and heaven confessed,
I bow and bless the sacred Name,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For ever blessed.

The God of Abraham praise,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;At whose supreme command
From earth I rise, and seek my joys
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;At His right hand.
He calls me to forsake
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Earth’s wisdom, fame, and power,
And Him my only portion make,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My shield and tower.

The God of Abraham praise,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Whose all-sufficient grace
Shall guide me all my pilgrim days,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In all my ways.
He calls a worm His friend,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He calls Himself my God;
And He shall save me to the end
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Through Jesus’ blood.

He by Himself hath sworn,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I on His oath depend,
I shall, on eagle-wings upborne,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To heaven ascend:
I shall behold His face,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I shall His power adore,
And sing the wonders of His grace
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For evermore.

The whole triumphant host 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Give thanks to God on high;
“Hail, Father, Son, and Holy Ghost!”
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;They ever cry.
Hail, Abraham’s God, and mine!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I join the heavenly lays;
All might and majesty are Thine,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And endless praise!

The head that once was crowned with thorns

565
The holiest now we enter
Previous BHB #270<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Peters_Mary&#39;,this)">Mary Bowly Peters</a></div>
7.6.7.6.D.

THE holiest now we enter
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In perfect peace with God;
Regaining our lost centre
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Through Christ’s atoning blood:
Though great may be our dulness
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In thought, and word, and deed,
We glory in the fulness
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of Him who meets our need.

Much incense is ascending
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Before the eternal throne;
God graciously is bending
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To hear each feeble groan.
To all our prayers and praises
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Christ adds His sweet perfume,
And love the censer raises
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Their odours to consume.

O God, we come with singing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Because the great High Priest
Our names to Thee is bringing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Nor e’er forgets the least:
For us He wears the mitre
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Where holiness shines bright;
For us His robes are whiter
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Than heaven’s unsullied light.


566
The hour arrives; they reach the place

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kirkpatrick_William&#39;,this)">William Kirkpatrick</a></div>
8.6.8.4.

“THE hour” arrives; they reach “the place”,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Cross they raise on high, 
The Lord of glory and of grace
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;They leave to die.

With holy awe we call to mind
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That solemn scene of woe,
The depth to which, in love so kind,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He there would go.

Surrounding Him, the wicked throng
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Relentless rage disclose: 
Against the One Who did no wrong 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Their wrath arose.

And His the pain, the bitter smart,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The ignominious place,
The parchèd lips, the melted heart,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The marrèd face.

There His perfection would we view,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Displayed in such a death -
Devoted, meek, submissive, true 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To His last breath!

So for Himself we offer now
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our thanks, O God, to Thee; 
In glad response our hearts we bow 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Adoringly.


567
The King of love my Shepherd is
Previous BHB #451<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Baker_Sir_Henry_Williams&#39;,this)">Henry Williams Baker  (1868)</a></div>
8.7.8.7.

THE King of love my Shepherd is,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Whose goodness faileth never;
I nothing lack if I am His
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And He is mine for ever.

Where streams of living water flow
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My ransomed soul He leadeth,
And where the verdant pastures grow
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With food celestial feedeth.

Perverse and foolish oft I strayed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But yet in love He sought me,
And on His shoulder gently laid,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And home, rejoicing, brought me.

In death’s dark vale I fear no ill
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With Thee, dear Lord, beside me;
Thy rod and staff my comfort still,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy Cross before to guide me.

Thou spread’st a table in my sight;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy unction grace bestoweth;
And O what transport of delight
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From Thy pure chalice floweth.

And so through all the length of days
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy goodness faileth never;
Good Shepherd, may I sing Thy praise
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Within Thy house for ever!


568
The Lamb of God to slaughter led
Previous BHB #271<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Chapman_Robert_Cleaver&#39;,this)">Robert Cleaver Chapman</a></div>
C.M.

THE Lamb of God to slaughter led,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The King of Glory see!
The crown of thorns upon His head,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;They nail Him to the tree!

The Father gives His only Son;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Lord of Glory dies
For us, the guilty and undone,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A spotless Sacrifice!

Thy Name is holy, O our God!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Before Thy throne we bow;
Thy bosom is Thy saints’ abode,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We call Thee Father now!

Enthroned with Thee, now sits the Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And in Thy bosom dwells;
Justice, that smote Him with the sword,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our perfect pardon seals.

Eternal death was once our doom;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Now death has lost its sting;
We rose with Jesus from the tomb,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jehovah’s love to sing.


569
The Lord Himself shall come
Previous BHB #272
(Nearer Home tune: sing verse 4 again at end)<br><br><div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Montgomery_James&#39;,this)">James Montgomery</a> & John Russell Darbyshire</div>
S.M.

THE Lord Himself shall come,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And shout a quickening word; 
Thousands shall answer from the tomb -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“For ever with the Lord”.

Then, as we upward fly,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That resurrection word
Shall be our shout of victory -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“For ever with the Lord”.

How shall I meet those eyes? 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Mine on Himself I cast,
And own myself the Saviour’s prize -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Mercy from first to last.

Knowing as I am known! 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;How shall I love that word!
How oft repeat before the throne -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“For ever with the Lord!”

That resurrection word, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That shout of victory - 
Once more - “For ever with the Lord”, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Amen, so let it be!


570
The Lord is risen: the Red Sea’s judgment flood

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Mackey_Dr_William_Paton&#39;,this)">William Paton Mackay</a></div>
10.10.10.10.

THE Lord is risen: the Red Sea’s judgment flood
Is passed in Him who bought us with His blood.
The Lord is risen: we stand beyond the doom
Of all our sin, through Jesus’ empty tomb.

The Lord is risen: with Him we also rose,
And in His grave see all our vanquished foes.
The Lord is risen: beyond the judgment land
In Him, in resurrection-life, we stand.

The Lord is risen: we’re now redeemed to God,
And tread the desert which His feet have trod.
The Lord is risen: the sanctuary’s our place,
Where now we dwell before the Father’s face.

The Lord is risen: the Lord is gone before;
We long to see Him and to sin no more!
The Lord is risen: our trumpet shout shall be,
“Thou hast prevailed! Thy people, Lord, are free!”


571
The Lord of Glory, being found in fashion as a man

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kirkpatrick_William&#39;,this)">William Kirkpatrick</a></div>
C.M.

THE Lord of Glory, being found
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In fashion as a Man,
With moral glory fitly crowned,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His path on earth began.

The form of God to Him pertained;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Complete equality
Was not an object to be gained, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For very God was He.

Not to His own things did He look,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But, in amazing grace,
Emptied Himself, and stooping, took
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The servant’s form and place.

And faithfulness, and tender grace
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Adorned the words He spoke; 
He needed not e’er to retrace 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;One step, or word revoke.

In Him the Father found delight,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All inexpressible,
Radiance of moral glory bright, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Fragrance ineffable.

Self-humbled One Whose preciousness
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Was told in every breath,
He served in full devotedness, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Obedient unto death.


572
The Lord of Glory! Who is He?
Previous BHB #276<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Chapman_Robert_Cleaver&#39;,this)">Robert Cleaver Chapman</a></div>
8.7.8.7.8.8.7.

THE Lord of Glory! Who is He?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who is the King of Glory?
Only the Son of God can be
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Christ, the King of Glory:
Consider all His wounds, and see
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;How Jesus’ death upon the tree
Proclaims Him King of Glory.

Above all heavens, at God’s right hand,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Now sits the King of Glory;
The angels, by His favour, stand
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Before the throne of Glory;
Swiftly they fly at His command
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To guard His own of every land,
To keep the heirs of glory.

Death and the grave confess the Lamb
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To be the King of Glory;
The powers of darkness dread His Name,
Creation shows His glory.
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He said: “E’er Abram was, I am”;
Jesus is evermore the same,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Almighty King of Glory.

Thrice happy who in Him believe,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;They soon will share His glory;
Born of His Spirit, they receive
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His sacred pledge of glory;
Taught by His Cross, for sin they grieve,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He calls them brethren, and they  cleave
To Him, their hope of glory.


573
The Lord will perfect that which doth concern me
Previous BHB #453<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Iverson_Winifred_A&#39;,this)">Winifred A. Iverson</a></div>
11.10.11.10.

THE Lord will perfect that which doth concern me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His way is perfect, so His goal must be;
All life’s events in harmony are working
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For those clear issues which I yet shall see.

The Lord will perfect that which doth concern me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And will complete each half-formed thing He sends:
His rich designs most carefully are woven,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;There are with Him no loose or broken ends.

The Lord will perfect that which doth concern me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And finish what His grace has here begun;
He gathers up life’s fragments, losing nothing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And turns to good account each single one.

The Lord will perfect that which doth concern me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Will do it in so many different ways;
By loss or gain, by high success  or failure,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;By steady course or unexpected phase.

The Lord will perfect that which doth concern me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Expecting me to show Him on my part
A trust intelligent and full of interest
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In all these purposes of His great heart.


574
The Lord’s my Shepherd, I’ll not want:
Previous BHB #454<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Rous_Francis&#39;,this)">Francis Rous (Rowe)</a> & (1650) Scottish Psalter  (1650)</div>
C.M.

THE Lord’s my Shepherd, I’ll not want:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He makes me down to lie
In pastures green; He leadeth me
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The quiet waters by.

My soul He doth restore again;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And me to walk doth make
Within the paths of righteousness,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;E’en for His own name’s sake.

Yea, though I walk in death’s dark vale,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Yet will I fear none ill;
For Thou art with me; and Thy rod
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And staff me comfort still.

My table Thou hast furnishèd
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In presence of my foes;
My head Thou dost with oil anoint,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And my cup overflows.

Goodness and mercy all my life
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shall surely follow me;
And in God’s house for evermore
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My dwelling-place shall be.


575
The love of God is greater far

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Lehman_M_F&#39;,this)">F. M. Lehman and Meir Ben Isaac Nehorai</a></div>
P.M.

THE love of God is greater far
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Than tongue or pen can ever tell;
It goes beyond the highest star,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And reaches to the lowest hell;
The guilty pair, bowed down with care,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;God gave His Son to win;
His erring child He reconciled,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And pardoned from his sin. 

c
Oh, love of God, how rich and pure!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;How measureless and strong!
It shall forevermore endure -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The saints’ and angels’ song. 

When hoary time shall pass away,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And earthly thrones and kingdoms  fall,
When men who here refuse to pray,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On rocks and hills and mountains  call,
God’s love, so sure, shall still endure,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All measureless and strong;
Redeeming grace to Adam’s race -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The saints’ and angels’ song. 

c
Oh, love of God, how rich and pure!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;How measureless and strong!
It shall forevermore endure -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The saints’ and angels’ song. 

Could we with ink the ocean fill,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And were the skies of parchment  made,
Were every stalk on earth a quill,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And every man a scribe by trade;
To write the love of God above
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Would drain the ocean dry;
Nor could the scroll contain the whole,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Though stretched from sky to sky. 

c
Oh, love of God, how rich and pure!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;How measureless and strong!
It shall forevermore endure -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The saints’ and angels’ song. 


576
