His love is more than tongue can tell!

That I might live for evermore,

c
His love is more than tongue can tell!
His love is more than tongue can tell!
The love that Jesus had for me

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Is more than tongue can tell!

His love is more than tongue can tell!

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The rest He gives, so free from fear,
The Hope in Him, so bright and clear,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Is more than tongue can tell!

c
His love is more than tongue can tell!
His love is more than tongue can tell!
The love that Jesus had for me
Is more than tongue can tell!


577
The Name of Jesus is so sweet

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Martin_C_William&#39;,this)">William C. Martin</a></div>
P.M.

THE Name of Jesus is so sweet,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I love its music to repeat;
It makes my joys full and complete,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The precious Name of Jesus!

c
“Jesus”, oh, how sweet the name!
“Jesus”, every day the same;
“Jesus”, let all saints proclaim
Its worthy praise forever!

I love the Name of Him whose heart
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Knows all my griefs and bears a part;
Who bids all anxious fears depart -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I love the Name of Jesus!

c
“Jesus”, oh, how sweet the name!
“Jesus”, every day the same;
“Jesus”, let all saints proclaim
Its worthy praise forever!

That Name I fondly love to hear,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;It never fails my heart to cheer;
Its music dries the falling tear,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Exalt the Name of Jesus!

c
“Jesus”, oh, how sweet the name!
“Jesus”, every day the same;
“Jesus”, let all saints proclaim
Its worthy praise forever!

No word of man can ever tell
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;How sweet the Name I love so well;
Oh, let its praises ever swell!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Oh, praise the Name of Jesus!

c
“Jesus”, oh, how sweet the name!
“Jesus”, every day the same;
“Jesus”, let all saints proclaim
Its worthy praise forever!


578
The night is wearing fast away
Previous BHB #278<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Hoare_Mary_Jane&#39;,this)">Mary Jane Hoare</a></div>
8.7.8.7.D.

THE night is wearing fast away,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The glorious day is dawning,
When Christ shall all His grace display,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The fair millennial morning.
Gloomy and dark the night hath been,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And long the way, and dreary;
And sad the weeping saints are seen,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And faint, and worn, and weary.

Ye mourning pilgrims, dry your tears,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And hush each sigh of sorrow;
The light of that bright morn appears,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The long Sabbatic morrow.
Lift up your heads! behold from far
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A flood of splendour streaming!
It is the bright and Morning Star
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In living lustre beaming.

And see that star-like host around
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of angel bands attending;
Hark! hark! the trumpet’s gladdening sound,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;’Mid shouts triumphant blending.
He comes! the Bridegroom  promised long;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Go forth with joy to meet Him,
And raise the new and nuptial song,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In cheering strains to greet Him.


579
The perfect righteousness of God

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Midlane_Albert&#39;,this)">Albert Midlane</a></div>
L.M.

THE perfect righteousness of God
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Is witnessed in the Saviour’s blood;
’Tis in the Cross of Christ we trace
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His righteousness, yet wondrous grace.

God could not pass the sinner by;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Justice demands that he must die;
But in the Cross of Christ we see
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;How God can save, yet righteous be.

The judgment fell on Jesus’ head; 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;’Twas in His blood sin’s debt was paid; 
Stern justice can demand no more,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And mercy can dispense her store.

The sinner who believes is free,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Can say, “The Saviour died for me”;
Can point to the atoning blood
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And say, “This made my peace  with God”.


580
The sands of time are sinking
Previous BHB #190
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Cousin_Ann_Ross&#39;,this)">Anne Ross Cundell Cousin</a></div>
7.6.7.6.D.

THE sands of time are sinking,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The dawn of heaven breaks;
The summer morn I’ve sighed for -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The fair, sweet morn awakes:
Dark, dark hath been the midnight,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But dayspring is at hand,
And glory, glory dwelleth
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In Immanuel’s land.

O Christ! He is the fountain,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The deep, sweet well of love;
The streams on earth I’ve tasted,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;More deep I’ll drink above;
There, to an ocean’s fulness,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His mercy doth expand,
And glory, glory dwelleth
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In Immanuel’s land.

I’ve wrestled on towards heaven,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;‘Gainst storm, and wind, and tide,
Now, like a weary traveller
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That leaneth on his guide,
Amid the shades of evening,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;While sinks life’s lingering sand,
I hail the glory dawning
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From Immanuel’s land.

With mercy and with judgment
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My web of time He wove,
And aye the dews of sorrow
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Were lustred with His love.
I’ll bless the hand that guided,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I’ll bless the heart that planned,
When throned where glory dwelleth
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In Immanuel’s land.

Oh, I am my Beloved’s
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And my Belov’d is mine;
He brings a poor vile sinner
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Into His “house of wine”.
I stand upon His merit;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I know no other stand,
Not e’en where glory dwelleth
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In Immanuel’s land.

The Bride eyes not her garment,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But her dear Bridegroom’s face;
I will not gaze at glory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But on the King of grace.
Not at the crown He giveth,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But on His piercèd hand;
The Lamb is all the glory
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of Immanuel’s land.


581
The Saviour comes! No outward pomp

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Robertson_William&#39;,this)">William Robertson</a></div>
C.M.D.

THE Saviour comes! No outward pomp
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Bespeaks His presence nigh;
No earthly beauty shines in Him
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To draw the carnal eye.

c
All beauty may we ever see,
In God’s belovèd Son,
The chiefest of ten thousand He,
The only lovely One!

Rejected and despised of men,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Behold a Man of woe!
Grief was His close companion here 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Through all His life below.

c
All beauty may we ever see,
In God’s belovèd Son,
The chiefest of ten thousand He,
The only lovely One!

Yet all the griefs He felt were ours,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Ours were the woes He bore;
Pangs, not His own, His spotless soul
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With bitter anguish tore.

c
All beauty may we ever see,
In God’s belovèd Son,
The chiefest of ten thousand He,
The only lovely One!

His sacred blood hath washed our souls 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From sin’s polluting stain;
His stripes have healed us, and His death
Revived our souls again.

c
All beauty may we ever see,
In God’s belovèd Son,
The chiefest of ten thousand He,
The only lovely One!

We all, like sheep, have gone astray,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In ruin’s fatal road;
On Him were our transgressions laid;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He bore the mighty load.

c
All beauty may we ever see,
In God’s belovèd Son,
The chiefest of ten thousand He,
The only lovely One!

He died to bear the guilt of men, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That sin might be forgiven;
He lives to bless them and defend,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And plead their cause in heaven.

c
All beauty may we ever see,
In God’s belovèd Son,
The chiefest of ten thousand He,
The only lovely One!


582
The Saviour lives, no more to die
Previous BHB #280
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Medley_Samuel&#39;,this)">Samuel Medley</a></div>
L.M.

THE Saviour lives, no more to die;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He lives our Head, enthroned on high;
He lives triumphant o’er the grave;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He lives eternally to save.

He lives to still His people’s fears;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He lives to wipe away their tears;
He lives their mansions to prepare;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He lives to bring them safely there.

The chief of sinners He receives;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His saints He loves, and never  leaves;
He’ll guard us safe from every ill,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And all His promises fulfil.

Abundant grace will He afford,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Till we are present with the Lord;
And prove what we have sung before,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That Jesus lives for evermore.

Then let our souls in Him rejoice,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And sing His praise with cheerful  voice;
Our doubts and fears for ever gone,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For Christ is on the Father’s throne.


583
The Saviour’s blood, on Calvary shed

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Russell_Douglas&#39;,this)">Douglas Russell</a></div>
P.M.

THE Saviour’s blood, on Calvary shed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Has full atonement made;
Captivity He captive led,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My ransom price He paid;
The crown has found a worthy brow;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As “Lord of all” behold Him now!

c
Ever, my heart, keep cling…ing …
Cling to the Lord alone …
His work is done, His victory won;
Well may my heart keep singing,
Well may my heart keep singing!

In Him “accepted” and “complete”,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As He is, so am I;
The judgment storm on Him did beat
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He lives, no more to die!
Atoning blood - I hear its voice!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;It speaketh peace! my heart, rejoice!

c
Ever, my heart, keep cling…ing …
Cling to the Lord alone …
His work is done, His victory won;
Well may my heart keep singing,
Well may my heart keep singing!

The great High Priest within the veil,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Both Priest and Sacrifice,
To keep His own will never fail,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Made His at such a price!
They soon will see Him coming out;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With rapture greet His welcome  shout!

c
Ever, my heart, keep cling…ing …
Cling to the Lord alone …
His work is done, His victory won;
Well may my heart keep singing,
Well may my heart keep singing!


584
The sorrows of the daily life
Previous BHB #281
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Anonymous_Hymn_Writers&#39;,this)">Anonymous</a></div>
L.M.

THE sorrows of the daily life,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The shadows o’er my path which fall,
Too oft obscure the glory’s light
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Until I rise above them all.

Until upon the mountain height
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I stand, my God, with Thee alone,
Bathed in the fullest, clearest light,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The glory that surrounds the throne.

Calm in Thy secret presence, Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I rest this weary soul of mine;
Feed on the fulness of Thy word,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And die to all the things of time.

Oh! take my fevered hands in Thine,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And keep me, Master, nearer Thee,
Walking above the things of time,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In closest fellowship with Thee.

The child of God must walk alone,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;If he would live and walk with Thee;
And only to such hearts are known
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The joys of Thy blest company.

Alone with Thee, O Master, where
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The light of earthly glory dies,
Misunderstood by all, I dare
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To do what Thine own heart will  prize.

Such be my path through life down here,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;One long, close, lonely walk with Thee,
Until past every doubt and fear,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy face in light above I see.


585
The veil is rent: Lo! Jesus stands
Previous BHB #283<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Deck_James_George&#39;,this)">James George Deck  (1841)</a></div>
C.M.

THE veil is rent: Lo! Jesus stands
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Before the throne of grace;
And clouds of incense from His hands
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Fill all that glorious place.

His precious blood is sprinkled there,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Before and on the throne;
And His own wounds in heaven declare
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His work on earth is done.

“’Tis finished!” on the Cross He said,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In agonies and blood;
’Tis finished! now He lives to plead
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Before the face of God.

’Tis finished! here our souls can rest,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His work can never fail;
By Him, our Sacrifice and Priest,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We enter through the veil.

Within the holiest of all,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Cleansed by His precious blood,
Before Thy throne Thy children fall,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And worship Thee, our God.

Boldly our heart and voice we raise,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His Name, His blood, our plea;
Assured our prayers and songs of praise
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Ascend by Him to Thee.


586
The wanderer no more will roam
Previous BHB #286
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Walker_Mary_Jane_Deck&#39;,this)">Mary Jane Deck Walker</a></div>
8.8.8.6.

THE wanderer no more will roam,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The lost one to the fold hath come,
The prodigal is welcomed home,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O Lamb of God, in Thee!

Though clad in rags, by sin defiled,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Father hath embraced His child;
And I am pardoned, reconciled,  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O Lamb of God, in Thee!

It is the Father’s joy to bless,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His love provides for me a dress,
A robe of spotless righteousness,  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O Lamb of God, in Thee!

Now shall my famished soul be fed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A feast of love for me is spread,
I feed upon the children’s bread,  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O Lamb of God, in Thee!

Yea, in the fulness of His grace,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He puts me in the children’s place,
Where I may gaze upon His face,  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O Lamb of God, in Thee!

I cannot half His love express,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Yet, Lord, with joy my lips confess,
This blessèd portion I possess,  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O Lamb of God, in Thee!

Thy precious name it is I bear, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In Thee I am to God brought near,
And all the Father’s love I share,  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O Lamb of God, in Thee!

And when I in Thy likeness shine,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The glory and the praise be Thine,
That everlasting joy is mine,  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O Lamb of God, in Thee!


587
Thee we praise, our God and Father

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Fleming_Inglis&#39;,this)">Inglis Fleming</a></div>
8.5.8.3.

THEE we praise, our God and Father,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou Thy love hast shown;
Ere the world was, Thou didst choose us
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For Thine own.

Thou Thine only Son hast given,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou art glorified,
For, in love to bring us near Thee,  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He has died.

By the Spirit now indwelling
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We with Christ have part;
Father! we Thy sons now call Thee,  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From our heart.

Love divine, our present portion,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Heaven’s choicest store;
Thee we worship, God and Father,  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thee adore!

Soon in Thine own house around Thee
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Still our praise shall swell;
Sons before Thee ever joying  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We shall dwell.

For His praise who glorified Thee
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We like Him shall be;
Firstborn He of many brethren  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Praising Thee.


588
There have been names that I have loved to hear

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Long_B_Lela&#39;,this)">Lela B. Long</a></div>
P.M.

THERE have been names that I have loved to hear,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;But never has there been a name so dear
To this heart of mine, as the name divine,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The precious, precious name of Jesus.

c
Jesus is the sweetest name I know,
And He’s just the same as His lovely name,
And that’s the reason why I love Him so;
Oh, Jesus is the sweetest name I know.

There is no name in earth or heaven above,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That we should give such honour and such love
As the blessèd name, let us all acclaim,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That wondrous, glorious name of Jesus. 

c
Jesus is the sweetest name I know,
And He’s just the same as His lovely name,
And that’s the reason why I love Him so;
Oh, Jesus is the sweetest name I know.

And some day I shall see Him face to face
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To thank and praise Him for His wondrous grace,
Which He gave to me, when He made me free,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The blessèd Son of God called Jesus. 

c
Jesus is the sweetest name I know,
And He’s just the same as His lovely name,
And that’s the reason why I love Him so;
Oh, Jesus is the sweetest name I know.


589
There is a fold whence none can stray
Previous BHB #279<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;East_John&#39;,this)">John East</a></div>
C.M.

THERE is a fold whence none can stray,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And pastures ever green,
Where sultry sun, or stormy day,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Or night is never seen.

There is a Shepherd living there,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Firstborn from the dead,
Who tends with sweet, unwearied care
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The flock for which He bled.

There congregate the sons of light,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Fair as the morning sky,
And taste of infinite delight
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Beneath their Saviour’s eye.

Their joy bursts forth in strains of love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In one harmonious song,
And through the heavenly courts above
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The echoes roll along.

O may our faith take up that sound
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Though toiling here below!
’Midst trials may our joys abound,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And songs amidst our woe.

Until we reach that happy shore,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And join to swell their strain,
And from our God go out no more,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And never weep again.


590
There is a fountain filled with blood
Previous BHB #274
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Cowper_William&#39;,this)">William Cowper</a> & Unknown  (1771)</div>
C.M.

THERE is a fountain filled with blood,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Drawn from Emmanuel’s veins;
And sinners plunged beneath that flood,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lose all their guilty stains.

The dying thief rejoiced to see
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That fountain in his day;
And there have I, as vile as he,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Washed all my sins away!

Dear dying Lamb, Thy precious blood
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shall never lose its power,
Till all the ransomed church of God
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Be saved to sin no more.

E’er since, by faith, I saw the stream
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy flowing wounds supply,
Redeeming love has been my theme,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And shall be till I die. 

Then in a nobler, sweeter song,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I’ll sing Thy power to save;
When this poor lisping, stammering tongue
Lies silent in the grave.

Lord, I believe Thou hast prepar’d
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;(Unworthy though I be)
For me a blood-bought free reward,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A golden harp for me!

’Tis strung, and tun’d, for endless years,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And form’d by power divine;
To sound in God the Father’s ears
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No other name but Thine.


591
There is a green hill far away
Previous BHB #455<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Alexander_Cecil_Frances&#39;,this)">Cecil Frances (Humphreys) Alexander  (1852)</a></div>
C.M.

THERE is a green hill far away,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Without a city wall,
Where the dear Lord was crucified,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who died to save us all.

We may not know, we cannot tell
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What pains He had to bear;
But we believe it was for us
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He hung and suffered there.

He died that we might be forgiven,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He died to make us good,
That we might go at last to heaven,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Saved by His precious blood.

There was no other good enough
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To pay the price of sin;
He only could unlock the gate
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of heaven, and let us in.

O dearly, dearly has He loved,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And we must love Him too,
And trust in His redeeming blood,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And try His works to do.


592
There is a Name I love to hear
Previous BHB #285<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Whitfield_Frederick&#39;,this)">Frederick Whitfield  (©1855)</a></div>
C.M.

THERE is a Name I love to hear,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I love to speak its worth;
It sounds like music in mine ear,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The sweetest Name on earth.

It tells me of a Saviour’s love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who died to set me free;
It tells me of His precious blood,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The sinner’s perfect plea.

It tells me of a Father’s smile
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Beaming upon His child;
It cheers me through this “little while”,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Through desert, waste, and wild.

It bids my trembling soul rejoice,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;It dries each rising tear;
It tells me in a “still, small voice”
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To trust and never fear.

Jesus! the Name I love so well,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Name I love to hear;
No saint on earth its worth can tell,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No heart conceive how dear.

This Name shall shed its fragrance still
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Along life’s thorny road,
Shall sweetly smooth the rugged hill
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That leads me up to God.

And there, with all the blood-bought throng,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From sin and sorrow free,
I’ll sing the new, eternal song
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of Jesus’ love to me.


593
There is an eye that never sleeps

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Wallace_J&#39;,this)">J. Wallace</a></div>
C.M.

THERE is an eye that never sleeps
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Beneath the wing of night;
There is an ear that never shuts
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When sink the beams of light.

There is an arm that never tires
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When human strength gives way;
There is a love that never fails
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When earthly loves decay.

That eye is fixed on seraph throngs,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That arm upholds the sky,
That ear is filled with heavenly songs,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That love is throned on high.

But there’s a power which faith can wield,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When mortal aid is vain,
That eye, that arm, that love to reach,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That listening ear to gain.

That power is prayer, which soars on high,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Through Jesus to the throne,
And moves the hand which moves  the world,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To bring deliverance down.


594
What a Day That Will Be

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Hill_James&#39;,this)">James Hill</a> and Tilly McIntyre</div><p>&copy; Copyright 1955, Renewed 1983. Ben Speer Music/SESAC (admin. By ClearBox Rights). All rights reserved. Used by permission.</p>
P.M.

THERE is coming a day When no heartaches shall come;
No more clouds in the sky, No more tears to dim the eye;
All is peace for evermore On that happy, golden shore,
What a day, glorious day, that will be!

c
What a day that will be When my Jesus I shall see!
And I look upon His face, The One who saved me by His grace;
When He takes me by the hand, 
And leads me through the Promised Land,
What a day, glorious day, that will be!

There’ll be no sorrow there, No more burdens to bear;
No more sickness, no pain, No more parting over there;
And forever I will be With the One who died for me,
What a day, glorious day, that will be!

c
What a day that will be When my Jesus I shall see!
And I look upon His face, The One who saved me by His grace;
When He takes me by the hand, 
And leads me through the Promised Land,
What a day, glorious day, that will be!

There will be reunion there In that upper garden fair,
Loved ones gathered round His feet, What a joy again to meet!
There where faith gives way to sight, In that city of delight,
What a day, glorious day, that will be!

c
What a day that will be When my Jesus I shall see!
And I look upon His face, The One who saved me by His grace;
When He takes me by the hand, 
And leads me through the Promised Land,
What a day, glorious day, that will be!


595
Isn't the love of Jesus Something Wonderful

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Peterson_W_John&#39;,this)">John W. Peterson</a></div><p>&copy; Copyright 1961 John W. Peterson Music Company. All rights reserved. Used by permission.</p>
P.M.

THERE will never be a sweeter story, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Story of the Saviour’s love divine, 
Love that brought Him from the realms of glory 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Just to save a sinful soul like mine.

c
Isn’t the love of Jesus 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Something wonderful,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Wonderful, wonderful!
Oh, isn’t the love of Jesus 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Something wonderful,
Wonderful it is to me!

Boundless as the universe around me, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Reaching to the farthest soul away, 
Saving, keeping love it was that found me 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That is why my heart can truly say -

c
Isn’t the love of Jesus 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Something wonderful,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Wonderful, wonderful!
Oh, isn’t the love of Jesus 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Something wonderful,
Wonderful it is to me!

Love beyond our human comprehending, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Love of God in Christ, how can  it be!
This will be my theme and never ending -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Great redeeming love of Calvary

c
Isn’t the love of Jesus 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Something wonderful,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Wonderful, wonderful!
Oh, isn’t the love of Jesus 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Something wonderful,
Wonderful it is to me!


596
There’s a call comes ringing

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Gabriel_Charles&#39;,this)">Charles H. Gabriel</a></div>
P.M.

THERE’S a call comes ringing o’er the restless wave, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Send the light! … Send the light! 
There are souls to rescue, there are souls to save,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Send the light! … Send the light!

c
Send the light … the blessèd Gospel light;
Let it shine … from shore to shore!
Send the light … and let its radiant beams 
Light the world for evermore! 

We have heard the Macedonian call today,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Send the light! … Send the light!
And our grateful off’ring at the  Cross we lay, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Send the light! … Send the light!

c
Send the light … the blessèd Gospel light;
Let it shine … from shore to shore!
Send the light … and let its radiant beams 
Light the world for evermore! 

May the grace of Jesus ev’rywhere abound, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Send the light! … Send the light!
And a Christ-like spirit ev’rywhere  be found,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Send the light! … Send the light! 

c
Send the light … the blessèd Gospel light;
Let it shine … from shore to shore!
Send the light … and let its radiant beams 
Light the world for evermore! 

Let us not grow weary in the work of love, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Send the light! … Send the light!
Let us gather jewels for a crown  above,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Send the light! … Send the light! 

c
Send the light … the blessèd Gospel light;
Let it shine … from shore to shore!
Send the light … and let its radiant beams 
Light the world for evermore! 


597
There’s a land that is fairer than day

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Bennett_F_Sanford&#39;,this)">Sanford F. Bennett</a></div>
P.M.

THERE’S a land that is fairer than day,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And by faith we can see it afar;
For the Father waits over the way,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To prepare us a dwelling-place there.

c
In the sweet … by-and-by
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We shall meet on that beautiful shore;
In the sweet … by-and-by
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We shall meet on that beautiful shore.

We shall sing on that beautiful shore
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The melodious songs of the blest;
And our spirits shall sorrow no more -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Not a sigh for the blessing of rest.

c
In the sweet … by-and-by
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We shall meet on that beautiful shore;
In the sweet … by-and-by
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We shall meet on that beautiful shore.

To our bountiful Father above,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We will offer our tribute of praise,
For the glorious gift of His love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And the blessings that hallow our  days.

c
In the sweet … by-and-by
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We shall meet on that beautiful shore;
In the sweet … by-and-by
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We shall meet on that beautiful shore.

We shall rest on that beautiful shore -
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In the joys of the saved we shall  share:
All our pilgrimage-toil will be o’er,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And the conqueror’s crown we  shall wear.

c
In the sweet … by-and-by
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We shall meet on that beautiful shore;
In the sweet … by-and-by
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We shall meet on that beautiful shore.


598
They nailed my Lord upon the tree

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Miles_Austin_C&#39;,this)">C. Austin Miles</a></div>
P.M.

THEY nailed my Lord upon the tree
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And left Him, dying, there:
Through love He suffered there for me;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;’Twas love beyond compare.

c
Crucified! Crucified!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And nailed upon the tree!
With piercèd hands and feet and side!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For you! … For me!

Upon His head a crown of thorns,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Upon His heart my shame;
For me He prayed, for me He died,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And, dying, spoke my name. 

c
Crucified! Crucified!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And nailed upon the tree!
With piercèd hands and feet and side!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For you! … For me!

“Forgive them, O forgive!” He cried, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Then bowed His sacred head;
“O Lamb of God! my sacrifice!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For me Thy blood was shed.”

c
Crucified! Crucified!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And nailed upon the tree!
With piercèd hands and feet and side!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For you! … For me!

His voice I hear, His love I know;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I worship at His feet;
And kneeling there, at Calvary’s Cross,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Redemption is complete. 

c
Crucified! Crucified!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And nailed upon the tree!
With piercèd hands and feet and side!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For you! … For me!


599
Thine be the glory, risen, conquering Son

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Budry_Edmond&#39;,this)">Edmond Budry tr. Richard Hoyle</a></div>
P.M.

THINE be the glory, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Risen, conquering Son;
Endless is the victory 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou o’er death hast won.
Angels in bright raiment 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Rolled the stone away,
Kept the folded grave-clothes 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Where Thy body lay.

c
Thine be the glory, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Risen, conquering Son;
Endless is the victory 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou o’er death hast won!

Lo! Jesus meets us, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Risen from the tomb;
Lovingly He greets us, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Scatters fear and gloom;
Let the church with gladness
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hymns of triumph sing,
For her Lord now liveth; 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Death hath lost its sting.

c
Thine be the glory, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Risen, conquering Son;
Endless is the victory 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou o’er death hast won!

No more we doubt Thee, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Glorious Prince of life!
Life is nought without Thee; 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Aid us in our strife;
Make us more than conquerors, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Through Thy deathless love;
Bring us safe through Jordan 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Thy home above.

c
Thine be the glory, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Risen, conquering Son;
Endless is the victory 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou o’er death hast won!


600
Thine ever – loved and chosen

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Anonymous_Hymn_Writers&#39;,this)">Author Unknown</a></div>
7.6.7.6.D.

THINE ever - loved and chosen
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In Thy deep thoughts of grace,
Before the world’s foundation,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Or dayspring knew its place;
Thine only - sought and followed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When in the far-off land;
Then kept, and fed, and guided,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;By Thine unwearied hand.

Thine, only Thine, Lord Jesus!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Whom need we now beside?
For ever in Thy presence
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our weary souls we hide:
All other refuge faileth,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All other springs run dry,
Thou, Lord, alone art changeless,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And Thou art ever nigh.

Thine ever, Lord, Thine only,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;E’en in the glory-light,
When bursts the dawn of heaven
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Upon our raptured sight,
One deep joy shall enfold us,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Shall swell our highest song -
That we are Thine, Thine only,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;’Mid all the gathered throng!

Thine only, Lord; oh, keep us
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;More closely at Thy side!
While here we wait and worship,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our hearts would there abide:
We crave no other gladness,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We seek no other rest,
Till raisèd in Thy likeness,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We shall with Thee be blest!


601
Thine is the love, Lord, that draws us together

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Harding_M_A&#39;,this)">A. M. Harding</a></div>
11.10.11.10.

THINE is the love, Lord, that draws us together,
Guiding our steps from the wilderness ways;
Soon face to face we’ll adore Thee for ever,
Now our glad hearts would be filled with Thy praise.

Faithful Thy grace o’er our pathway has waited,
Deep the delight we have found, Lord, in Thee;
Now with this treasure our spirits are freighted,
Bowed at Thy feet, and the fragrance set free.

For us, Lord Jesus, Thyself Thou hast given;
Suff’rings unfathomed for us hast Thou known;
Now, in accord with the homage of heaven,
Rises a song from the hearts of Thine own.

Jesus, Lord Jesus, we love and adore Thee;
Glorious Thy Name, all our praises above;
Peerless Thy beauty, we worship before Thee;
Hushed are our spirits, at rest in Thy love.


602
This world is a wilderness wide
Previous BHB #298
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Darby_John_Nelson&#39;,this)">John Nelson Darby</a></div>
8.8.8.8.

THIS world is a wilderness wide;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I have nothing to seek nor to choose;
I’ve no thought in the waste to abide; 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I have nought to regret nor to lose. 

The Lord is Himself gone before;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He has marked out the path that  I tread;
It’s as sure as the love I adore;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I have nothing to fear nor to dread.

The path where my Saviour is gone 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Has led up to His Father and God, 
To the place where He’s now on the throne;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And His strength shall be mine on the road.

And with Him shall my rest be on high,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When in holiness bright I sit down,
In the joy of His love ever nigh,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In the peace that His presence  shall crown.

’Tis the treasure I’ve found in His love
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That has made me a pilgrim below;
And ’tis there, when I reach Him above,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As I’m known, all His fulness I’ll  know.

And, Saviour! ’tis Thee from on high 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I await till the time Thou shalt come,
To take him Thou hast led by Thine eye
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Thyself in Thy heavenly home.

Till then, ’tis the path Thou hast trod
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My delight and my comfort shall be;
I’m content with Thy staff and Thy rod,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Till with Thee all Thy glory I see.


603
Thou art coming, O our Saviour!
Previous BHB #288<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Havergal_Frances_Ridley&#39;,this)">Frances Ridley Havergal</a></div>
8.7.8.7.D.

THOU art coming, O our Saviour!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Coming, God’s anointed King!
Every tongue Thy Name confessing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Well may we rejoice and sing.
Thou art coming! Rays of glory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Through the veil Thy death has rent,
Gladden now our pilgrim pathway,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Glory from Thy presence sent.

c
Thou art coming! Thou art coming!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We shall meet Thee on Thy way;
Thou art coming! We shall see Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And be like Thee on that day:
Thou art coming! Thou art coming!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus, our belovèd Lord!
O the joy to see Thee reigning,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Worshipped, glorified, adored!

Thou art coming! Not a shadow,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Not a mist, and not a tear,
Not a sin, and not a sorrow
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On that sunrise grand and clear:
Thou art coming: Blessèd Saviour,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Nothing else seems worth a  thought;
O how marvellous the glory,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And the bliss Thy pain hath bought!

c
Thou art coming! Thou art coming!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We shall meet Thee on Thy way;
Thou art coming! We shall see Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And be like Thee on that day:
Thou art coming! Thou art coming!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus, our belovèd Lord!
O the joy to see Thee reigning,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Worshipped, glorified, adored!

Thou art coming! We are waiting
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With a “hope” that cannot fail,
Asking not the day or hour,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Anchored safe within the veil:
Thou art coming! At Thy table
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We are witnesses for this,
As we meet Thee in communion,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Earnest of our coming bliss.

c
Thou art coming! Thou art coming!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We shall meet Thee on Thy way;
Thou art coming! We shall see Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And be like Thee on that day:
Thou art coming! Thou art coming!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Jesus, our belovèd Lord!
O the joy to see Thee reigning,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Worshipped, glorified, adored!


604
Thou art the Everlasting Word
Previous BHB #352<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Condor_Josiah&#39;,this)">Josiah Condor  (1836)</a></div>
8.6.8.6.8.8.

THOU art the Everlasting Word,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Father’s only Son,
God manifestly seen and heard,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And heaven’s belovèd One.

c
Worthy, O Lamb, of God, art Thou!
That every knee to Thee should bow.

In Thee, most perfectly expressed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Father’s glories shine,
Of the full Deity possessed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Eternally divine!

c
Worthy, O Lamb, of God, art Thou!
That every knee to Thee should bow.

True image of the Infinite,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Whose essence is concealed;
Brightness of uncreated light,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The heart of God revealed.

c
Worthy, O Lamb, of God, art Thou!
That every knee to Thee should bow.

But the high myst’ries of Thy name 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;An angel’s grasp transcend;
The Father only (glorious claim!)
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Son can comprehend.

c
Worthy, O Lamb, of God, art Thou!
That every knee to Thee should bow.

Yet loving Thee, on whom His love
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Ineffable doth rest,
Thy members all, in Thee, above,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As one with Thee are blest.

c
Worthy, O Lamb, of God, art Thou!
That every knee to Thee should bow.

Throughout the universe of bliss,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The centre Thou, and Sun,
Th’ eternal theme of praise is this,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To heaven’s belovèd One.

c
Worthy, O Lamb, of God, art Thou!
That every knee to Thee should bow.


605
Thou hidden love of God, whose height
Previous BHB #290<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Tersteegen_Gerhard&#39;,this)">Gerhard Tersteegen tr/ad by John Wesley  (1729)</a></div>
8.8.8.8.8.8.

THOU hidden love of God, whose height,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Whose depth unfathomed no man knows,
I see from far Thy beauteous light,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And inly sigh for Thy repose:
My heart is pained, nor can it be
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;At rest till it finds rest in Thee.

Is there a thing beneath the sun
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That strives with Thee my heart  to share?
O tear it thence, and reign alone
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Lord of every motion there!
Then shall my heart from earth be free,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When it has found repose in Thee.

O hide this self from me that I
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No more, but Christ in me shall live!
My vile affections mortify,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Nor let one darling sin survive:
In all things nothing may I see,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Nothing desire, or seek, but Thee.

Each moment calls from earth away
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My heart which lowly waits Thy call;
Speak to my inmost soul and say,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“I am thy Life, thy God, thy All!”
To know Thy power, to hear Thy voice,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To feel Thy love, be all my choice.


606
Thou life of my life, blessed Saviour

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Homburg_Ernst_Christoph&#39;,this)">Ernst Homburg</a> and <a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Bevan_Emma_Frances&#39;,this)">Emma F. Bevan</a></div>
9.8.9.8.9.8. with Chorus

THOU life of my life, blessèd Saviour 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy death was the death that was mine,
For me was Thy Cross and Thine anguish, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy love and Thy sorrow divine; 
Thou_hast suffered the Cross and the judgment,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That I might forever go free - 

c
A thousand, a thousand thanksgivings, 
I bring, my Lord Jesus, to Thee!

For me hast Thou borne the reproaches, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The mockery, hate, and disdain, 
The blows and the spittings of  sinners, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The scourging, the shame and the pain; 
To save me from bondage and judgment, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou gladly hast suffered for me - 

c
A thousand, a thousand thanksgivings, 
I bring, my Lord Jesus, to Thee!

O Lord, from my heart do I thank Thee 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For all Thou hast borne in my room, 
Thine agony, dying unsolaced, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Alone in the darkness and gloom, 
That I, in the glory of heaven, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For ever and ever might be - 

c
A thousand, a thousand thanksgivings, 
I bring, my Lord Jesus, to Thee!


607
Thou, my everlasting portion
Previous BHB #294
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Alystyne_Frances_Jane_Van&#39;,this)">Frances (Fanny) Jane Crosby (1874)</a></div>
P.M.

THOU, my everlasting portion!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;More than friend or life to me,
All along my pilgrim journey,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Saviour, let me walk with Thee!
Close to Thee, close to Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Close to Thee, close to Thee,
All along my pilgrim journey,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Saviour, I would walk with Thee!

Not for ease or worldly pleasure,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Nor for fame my prayer shall be,
Gladly will I toil and suffer,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Only let me walk with Thee.
Close to Thee, close to Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Close to Thee, close to Thee,
Gladly will I toil and suffer,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;So that I may walk with Thee.

Lead me through the vale of shadows,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Bear me o’er life’s fitful sea;
Then the gate of life eternal
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I shall enter, Lord, with Thee.
Close to Thee, close to Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Close to Thee, close to Thee,
Then the gate of life eternal
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I shall enter, Lord, with Thee.


608
Thou who did’st come to die
Previous BHB #456<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Elders_K_H&#39;,this)">K. H. Elders</a></div>
6.6.4.6.6.6.4.

THOU who did’st come to die
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From the bright realms on high,
Thou art our peace:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Peace Thou dost now impart
To each believing heart,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Peace that shall ne’er depart;
Thou art our peace.

Thou who did’st rise again,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy tomb was sealed in vain, 
Thou art our life:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All power to Thee is given,
Exalted Lord in heaven,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And we with Thee are risen, 
Thou art our life.

Thou who wilt surely come
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To take Thy loved ones home, 
Thou art our hope:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What joys and bliss untold
Will to our gaze unfold
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When we Thy face behold! 
Thou art our hope.

Peace, life and hope, O Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou giv’st us in Thy Word, 
Blest be Thy name:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We would our praise outpour,
Would worship and adore
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Now and for evermore; 
Blest be Thy name.


609
Thou whose almighty word
Previous BHB #457<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Marriott_John&#39;,this)">John Marriott  (1813)</a></div>
6.6.4.6.6.6.4.

THOU whose almighty word
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Chaos and darkness heard,
And took their flight,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Hear us, we humbly pray,
And, where the gospel day
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Sheds not its glorious ray,
Let there be light.

Thou who didst come to bring,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On Thy redeeming wing, 
Healing and sight,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Health to the sick in mind,
Sight to the inly blind,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Oh now to all mankind 
Let there be light.

Spirit of truth and love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Life-giving, holy Dove, 
Speed forth Thy flight;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Move on the water’s face,
Bearing the lamp of grace,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And in earth’s darkest place 
Let there be light.

Blessèd and holy Three,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Glorious Trinity, 
Wisdom, Love, Might,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Boundless as ocean’s tide
Rolling in fullest pride,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Through the world, far and wide, 
Let there be light.


610
Though faint, yet pursuing, we go on our way

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Darby_John_Nelson&#39;,this)">John Nelson Darby</a></div>
11.11.11.11.

THOUGH faint, yet pursuing, we go on our way;
The Lord is our leader, His strength is our stay;
Though suff’ring, and sorrow, and trial be near,
The Lord is our refuge - why then should we fear?

He raiseth the fallen, He cheereth the faint;
If the weak are opprest, He hears their complaint;
The way may be dreary, and thorny the road,
But let us not falter; our help is in God. 

And to His green pastures our footsteps He leads;
His flock in the desert how kindly He feeds!
The lambs in His bosom He tenderly bears,
And brings back the wanderer safe from the snares.

Though clouds may surround us, our God is our light;
Though foes are around us, our God is our might;
So faint, yet pursuing, whatever may come;
The Lord is our Leader, and heaven our home! 

And there shall His people eternally dwell,
With Him who has led them so safely and well;
The toilsome way over, the wilderness passed,
And Canaan the blessèd is theirs at the last.


611
Thought-views that ne’er grow dim

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Ewan_Y_Isaac&#39;,this)">Isaac Y. Ewan</a></div>
6.6.6.6.8.8.

THOUGHT-VIEWS that ne’er grow dim
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Stir in our hearts, O God,
As we remember Him
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In all the path He trod:
Thy Holy Son, in manhood grace, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Whose holy steps again we trace.

Thoughts of those holy feet,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Steadfast from day to day, 
Most beautiful and sweet,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Treading that lowly way,
On for the glory of Thy Name, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And down toward a Cross of shame!

Thoughts of His human tears,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His sympathy of heart,
’Mid human griefs and fears
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His perfect human part;
Ready to weep with them that weep,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In human pity, kind and deep!

Thoughts of His wondrous grace;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Lord of Glory He,
Yet, in the lowest place,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Veiling His Majesty!
As one that serveth moving through 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy heart to show, Thy will to do!


612
Thrice blessed hope that cheers the weary soul

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Ewan_Y_Isaac&#39;,this)">Isaac Y. Ewan</a></div>
10.10.10.10.

THRICE blessèd hope that cheers the weary soul,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As through this scene of sin we sadly roam, 
That “this same Jesus” Who has made us whole 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Is coming back again to take us home!

“This same” the men in white apparel said
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To those who upward gazed where He had gone; 
Thus hope within their troubled hearts was shed, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And holy joy upon their faces shone.

We muse upon the measure of His love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The manner of His words, His works, His ways, 
Until we yearn to be with Him above,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And on the fulness of His glory gaze.

What joy it gives to dwell upon the thought
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That soon He’ll come and take His own away, 
And we shall upward to Himself be caught, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To be with Him for all eternity!

Meanwhile we’ll turn us to the daily task,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And try to do it as He’d have it done, 
And answer for our hope to all who ask; 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We’re working, waiting for the Coming One.


613
Through Him to Thee, His God

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Ewan_Y_Isaac&#39;,this)">Isaac Y. Ewan</a></div>
S.M.D.

THROUGH Him to Thee, His God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Adoring thanks we raise:
The grace of Aaron’s budded rod
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Has touched our lips with praise, 
And taught us thus to be
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Through Him, this holy hour, 
A holy priesthood unto Thee
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In resurrection power.

Rememb’ring Him, we each,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In praise to Thee, express
The most our feeble thoughts can reach
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of all His preciousness. 
And thus, through Him, we seek
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To rise, with hearts as one, 
To this, the privilege to speak 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Thee about Thy Son.

We seek Thy pleasure here,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We know Thy heart’s delight, 
We would be pleasing to Thine ear
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And pleasing in Thy sight,
And move in this, the mind
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That marked the perfect One:
For Thou art pleased with them that find
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Their pleasure in Thy Son.

Our hearts are drawn to Him
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Whose life was all for Thee: 
The light that nought on earth could dim,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Veiled in humanity, 
Revealed in perfect grace
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Through all that lowly way, 
A glory nought shall e’er efface, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Fresh for eternity!


614
Through the love of God our Saviour
Previous BHB #296<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Peters_Mary&#39;,this)">Mary Bowly Peters  (1847)</a></div>
8.4.8.4.8.8.8.4.

THROUGH the love of God our Saviour
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All will be well.
Free and changeless is His favour;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All, all is well.
Precious is the blood that healed us,
Perfect is the grace that sealed us,
Strong the hand stretched forth to shield us,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All must be well.

Though we pass through tribulation,  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All will be well.
Ours is such a full salvation;  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All, all is well.
Happy, still in God confiding,
Fruitful, if in Christ abiding,
Holy, through the Spirit’s guiding,  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All must be well.

We expect a bright tomorrow;  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All will be well.
Faith can sing through days of sorrow,  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All, all is well.
On our Father’s love relying,
Christ our every need supplying,
Whether living now, or dying,  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All must be well.


615
Through Thy precious body broken
Previous BHB #289
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Dark_Elizabeth_Nunn_Marianne&#39;,this)">Elizabeth Dark</a></div>
8.4.8.4.8.8.8.4.

THROUGH Thy precious body broken
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Inside the veil;
O what words to sinners spoken
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Inside the veil!
Precious as the blood that bought us,
Perfect as the love that sought us,
Holy as the Lamb that brought us
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Inside the veil.

When we see Thy love unshaken  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Outside the camp;
Scorned by man, by God forsaken,  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Outside the camp;
Thine own love alone can charm us,
Shame need now no more alarm us,
Glad we follow, nought can harm us  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Outside the camp.

Lamb of God, through Thee we enter  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Inside the veil;
Cleansed by Thee, we boldly venture  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Inside the veil:
Not a stain; a new creation;
Ours is such a full salvation;
Low we bow in adoration  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Inside the veil.

Unto Thee, the homeless stranger  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Outside the camp,
Forth we hasten, fear no danger  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Outside the camp.
Thy reproach, far richer treasure
Than all Egypt’s boasted pleasure;
Drawn by love that knows no measure,  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Outside the camp.

Soon Thy saints shall all be gathered  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Inside the veil:
All at home, no more be scattered,  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Inside the veil.
Nought from Thee our hearts shall sever;
We shall see Thee, grieve Thee never;
“Praise the Lamb!” shall sound for ever  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Inside the veil.


616
Thy broken body, gracious Lord
Previous BHB #291<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Tregelles_Samuel_Prideaux&#39;,this)">Samuel Prideaux Tregelles</a></div>
L.M.

THY broken body, gracious Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Is shadowed by this broken bread:
The wine, which in this cup is poured,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Points to the blood which Thou hast shed.

And while we meet together thus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We show that we are one in Thee;
Thy precious blood was shed for us,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy death, O Lord, has set us free.

Brethren! in Thee, in union sweet,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For ever be Thy grace adored!
’Tis in Thy Name that now we meet,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And know Thee with us, gracious Lord.

We have one hope that Thou wilt come;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thee in the air, we wait to see,
When Thou wilt take Thy people home,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And we shall ever reign with Thee.


617
Thy grace, O Lord, that measured once the deep

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Coates_A_C&#39;,this)">C. A. Coates</a></div>
10.10.10.10.

THY grace, O Lord, that measured once the deep
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of Calv’ry’s woe, to seek and save Thy sheep,
Has touched our hearts and made them long for Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thyself our treasure and our all to be.

Thy glory, Lord, at God’s right hand above,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Supreme of all in that blest scene of love,
In sonship tells our hearts their wondrous place,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In Thee accepted by the Father’s grace.

Thy fulness, Lord, of light and love divine,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No thought can grasp, nor human mind define.
The whole vast scene of glory will display
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That fulness in a quickly-coming day.

When all things filled by Thee are wholly blest,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And God’s deep love eternally shall rest
In that which ever speaks to Him of Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy greatness, Lord, the universe shall see.

Thy beauties, Lord, Thy holy precious worth,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Surpassing far the deepest joys of earth,
Attract our hearts - our joy Thy constant love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thyself our object in those scenes above.


618
Thy life was given for me
Previous BHB #458
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Havergal_Frances_Ridley&#39;,this)">Frances Ridley Havergal</a> tr/ad by Church Hymns  (1858)</div>
6.6.6.6.6.6.

THY life was given for me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy blood, O Lord, was shed
That I might ransomed be,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And quickened from the dead;
Thy life was given for me;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What have I given for Thee?

Long years were spent for me
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In weariness and woe,
That through eternity
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy glory I might know;
Long years were spent for me:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Have I spent one for Thee?

Thy Father’s home of light,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy rainbow-circled throne,
Were left for earthly night,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For wanderings sad and lone.
Yea, all was left for me:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Have I left aught for Thee?

Thou, Lord, hast borne for me
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;More than my tongue can tell
Of bitterest agony,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To rescue me from hell;
Thou sufferedst all for me:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What have I borne for Thee?

And Thou hast brought to me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Down from Thy home above,
Salvation full and free,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy pardon and Thy love;
Great gifts Thou broughtest me;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What have I brought to Thee?

O let my life be given,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My years for Thee be spent;
World-fetters all be riven,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And joy with suffering blent;
Thou gavest Thyself for me;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I give myself to Thee.


619
Thy love we own, Lord Jesus
Previous BHB #295<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Yerbury_W&#39;,this)">W. Yerbury</a></div>
7.7.8.7.D.

THY love we own, Lord Jesus;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In service unremitting,
Within the veil Thou dost prevail,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Each soul for worship fitting.
Encompassed here with failure,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Each earthly refuge fails us;
Without, within, beset with sin,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy Name alone avails us.

Thy love we own, Lord Jesus!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For though Thy toils are ended,
Thy tender heart doth take its part
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With those Thy grace befriended.
Thy sympathy, most precious!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou succourest in sorrow,
And bidst us cheer while pilgrims here,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And haste the hopeful morrow.

Thy love we own, Lord Jesus!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy way is traced before Thee;
Thou wilt descend, and we ascend,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To meet in heavenly glory.
Soon shall the blissful morning
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Call forth Thy saints to meet Thee,
Our only Lord, alone adored,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With gladness then we’ll greet Thee.

Thy love we own, Lord Jesus!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And wait to see Thy glory,
To know as known, and fully own
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy perfect grace before Thee:
We plead Thy parting promise,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Come, Saviour, to release us;
Then endless praise our lips shall raise
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For love like Thine, Lord Jesus.


620
Thy Name we bless, Lord Jesus!
Previous BHB #287
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Tregelles_Samuel_Prideaux&#39;,this)">Samuel Prideaux Tregelles</a></div>
7.7.8.7.D.

THY Name we bless, Lord Jesus!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That Name all names excelling:
How great Thy love, all praise above,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Should every tongue be telling.
The Father’s loving-kindness
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In giving Thee was shown us;
Now by Thy blood redeemed to God,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As children He doth own us.

From that eternal glory
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou hadst with God the Father,
He gave His Son, that He in one
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His children all might gather.
Our sins were all laid on Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;God’s wrath Thou hast endurèd;
It was for us Thou suffer’dst thus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And hast our peace securèd.

Thou from the dead wast raisèd,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And from all condemnation
Thy saints are free, as risen in Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Head of the new creation!
On high Thou hast ascended
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To God’s right hand in heaven;
The Lamb once slain, alive again,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Thee all power is given.

Thou hast bestowed the earnest
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of that we shall inherit;
Till Thou shalt come to take us home,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We’re sealed by God the Spirit.
We wait for Thine appearing,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When we shall know more fully
The grace divine that made us Thine,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou Lamb of God most holy!


621
Till He come! Oh let the words
Previous BHB #299<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Bickersteth_Edward_Henry&#39;,this)">Edward Henry Bickersteth  (1861)</a></div>
7.7.7.7.7.7.

“'TILL He come!” Oh let the words
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Linger on the trembling chords;
Let the “little while” between
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In their golden light be seen;
Let us think how heaven and home
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Lie beyond that “Till He come!”

When the weary ones we love
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Enter on their rest above,
When their words of love and cheer
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Fall no longer on our ear,
Hush! be every murmur dumb,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;It is only “Till He come!”

Clouds and darkness round us press;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Would we have one sorrow less?
All the sharpness of the Cross,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All that tells the world is loss,
Death, and darkness, and the tomb,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Pain us only “Till He come!”

Sweet the feast of love divine,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Broken bread and outpoured wine;
Sweet memorials, till the Lord
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Call us round His heavenly board,
Some from earth, from glory some,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Severed only “Till He come!”


622
Tis finished all: our souls to win
Previous BHB #303
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Denny_Sir_Edward&#39;,this)">Sir Edward Denny</a></div>
L.M.

TIS finished all: our souls to win
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His life the blessèd Jesus gave, 
Then rising, left His people’s sin 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Behind Him in His opening grave.

Past suff’ring now, the tender heart
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of Jesus, on His Father's throne,
Still in our sorrow bears a part,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And feels it as He felt His own.

Sweet thought! we have a Friend above,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our weary, faltering steps to guide, 
Who follows with the eye of love
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The little flock for which He died.

O Jesus, teach us more and more
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On Thee alone to cast our care;
And, gazing on Thy Cross, adore
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The wondrous grace that brought Thee there.


623
Tis past, the dark and dreary night
Previous BHB #304
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Denny_Sir_Edward&#39;,this)">Sir Edward Denny</a></div>
C.M.

TIS past, the dark and dreary night:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And, Lord, we hail Thee now,
Our Morning Star, without a cloud 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of sadness on Thy brow.

Thy path on earth, the Cross, the grave,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy sorrows all are o'er;
And, oh, sweet thought! Thine eye shall weep,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy heart shall break no more.

Deep were those sorrows, deeper still
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The love that brought Thee low,
That bade the streams of life from Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A lifeless Victim, flow. 

The soldier, as he pierced Thee, proved,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Man’s hatred, Lord, to Thee;
While in the blood that stained the  spear,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Love, only love, we see.

Drawn from Thy pierced and bleeding side,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That pure and cleansing flood
Speaks peace to every heart that knows
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The virtues of Thy blood.

Yet ’tis not that we know the joy
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of cancelled sin alone,
But, happier far, Thy saints are called
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To share Thy glorious throne.

So closely are we linked in love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;So wholly one with Thee,
That all Thy bliss and glory then
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our bright reward shall be.

Yes, when the storm of life is calmed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The dreary desert past, 
Our way-worn hearts shall find in Thee
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Their full repose at last.


624
Blessed hour of prayer
Previous BHB #302
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Alystyne_Frances_Jane_Van&#39;,this)">Frances (Fanny) Jane Crosby (1880)</a></div>
P.M.

TIS the blessèd hour of prayer,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;when our hearts lowly bend,
And we gather to Jesus,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;our Saviour and Friend;
If we come to Him in faith,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His protection to share,
What a balm for the weary!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O how sweet to be there!

c
Blessèd hour of prayer,
Blessèd hour of prayer, 
What a balm for the weary!
O how sweet to be there!

’Tis the blessèd hour of prayer,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;when the Saviour draws near
With tender compassion
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;His people to hear;
When He tells us we may cast
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;at His feet every care:
What a balm for the weary! 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O how sweet to be there!

c
Blessèd hour of prayer,
Blessèd hour of prayer, 
What a balm for the weary!
O how sweet to be there!

’Tis the blessèd hour of prayer, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;when the tempted and tried,
To the Saviour who loves them,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;their sorrows confide;
With a sympathising heart
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He removes every care:
What a balm for the weary! 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O how sweet to be there!

c
Blessèd hour of prayer,
Blessèd hour of prayer, 
What a balm for the weary!
O how sweet to be there!

At the blessèd hour of prayer, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;trusting Him we believe
That the blessing we’re needing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;we’ll surely receive;
In the fulness of delight
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;we shall lose every care;
What a balm for the weary! 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O how sweet to be there! 

c
Blessèd hour of prayer,
Blessèd hour of prayer, 
What a balm for the weary!
O how sweet to be there!


625
Tis we, O Lord, whom Thou hast shown
Previous BHB #300<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Peters_Mary&#39;,this)">Mary Bowly Peters</a></div>
L.M.

TIS we, O Lord, whom Thou hast shown
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The deadly bitterness of sin;
We, who forgiving love have known,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;May fitly bring thank-offerings in.

Thy presence called for Israel’s praise,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Encompassed by their mortal foes;
And when in death they met their gaze,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;What songs of glorious triumph rose.

And we have known redemption, Lord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From bondage worse than theirs  by far;
Sin held us by a stronger cord,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Yet by Thy mercy free we are.

O blesséd Lord! Thy groans and tears,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy death the power of darkness  broke;
Bursting the chains we wore for years
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;It freed us from the iron yoke.

Divine Deliverer! Thou alone
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy people from the deep couldst  bring;
The glorious triumph all Thine own:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy Name, Thy might, Thy grace,  we sing.


626
To Calvary, Lord, in spirit now
Previous BHB #305
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Denny_Sir_Edward&#39;,this)">Sir Edward Denny</a></div>
C.M.

TO Calvary, Lord, in spirit now,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our weary souls repair,
To dwell upon Thy dying love,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And taste its sweetness there.

Sweet resting-place of every heart,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That feels the plague of sin,
Yet knows that deep mysterious joy,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The peace with God, within.

There, through Thine hour of deepest woe,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy suffering spirit passed;
Grace there its wondrous victory  gained,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And love endured its last.

Dear suffering Lamb! Thy bleeding wounds,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With cords of love divine,
Have drawn our willing hearts to  Thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And linked our life with Thine.

Our longing eyes would fain behold
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That bright and blessèd brow,
Once wrung with bitterest anguish, wear
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Its crown of glory now.

Thy sympathies and hopes are ours:
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We long, O Lord, to see
Creation, all below, above,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Redeemed and blest by Thee. 


627
To God be the glory, great things He hath done

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Alystyne_Frances_Jane_Van&#39;,this)">Frances (Fanny) Jane Crosby</a></div>
11.11.11.11. with Chorus

TO God be the glory, great things He hath done,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;So loved He the world that He gave us His Son,
Who yielded His life an atonement for sin,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And opened the Life Gate that all may go in.

c
Praise the Lord, praise the Lord, Let the earth hear His voice!
Praise the Lord, praise the Lord, Let the people rejoice!
Oh, come to the Father, through Jesus the Son,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And give Him the glory, great things He hath done.

Oh, perfect redemption, the purchase of blood,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To every believer the promise of God;
The vilest offender who truly  believes,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That moment from Jesus a pardon receives. 

c
Praise the Lord, praise the Lord, Let the earth hear His voice!
Praise the Lord, praise the Lord, Let the people rejoice!
Oh, come to the Father, through Jesus the Son,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And give Him the glory, great things He hath done.

Great things He hath taught us, great things He hath done,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And great our rejoicing through Jesus the Son;
But purer, and higher, and greater will be
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Our wonder, our transport, when Jesus we see.

c
Praise the Lord, praise the Lord, Let the earth hear His voice!
Praise the Lord, praise the Lord, Let the people rejoice!
Oh, come to the Father, through Jesus the Son,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And give Him the glory, great things He hath done.


628
To Thee, His God, we bring, remembering

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Ewan_Y_Isaac&#39;,this)">Isaac Y. Ewan</a></div>
6.4.6.4.6.6.6.4.

TO Thee, His God, we bring, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Remembering,
This our thank-offering,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Remembering,
Rememb'ring Him as He
Never again shall be:
His journey to the tree
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Remembering! 

Those nights on Olivet  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Remembering, 
Locks with the night dews wet  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Remembering,
Remembering those days 
Calling for constant praise,
Ours unto Thee we raise,  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Remembering!

His path of grace most sweet  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Remembering,
The fragrance of His feet  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Remembering,
Remembering His face
Set like a flint in grace
To reach at last that place,  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Remembering! 

That prospect to His soul  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Remembering,
That road to such a goal  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Remembering,
Remembering Thy Son,
And all His work well done
That worthy, worthy One  
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Remembering!


629
To Thee, O gracious Saviour!
Previous BHB #301<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Monsell_John_Samuel_Bewley&#39;,this)">John Samuel Bewley Monsell</a></div>
7.6.7.6.D.

TO Thee, O gracious Saviour!
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My spirit turns for rest,
My peace is in Thy favour,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My pillow on Thy breast:
Though all the world deceive me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;I know that I am Thine,
And Thou wilt never leave me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;O blessèd Saviour mine!

In Thee my trust abideth,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;On Thee my hope relies,
O Thou whose love provideth
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For all beneath the skies:
O Thou whose mercy found me,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From bondage set me free,
And then for ever bound me
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With threefold cords to Thee.

My grief is in the dullness
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With which this sluggish heart
Doth open to the fulness
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of all Thou dost impart:
My joy is in Thy merit,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And holiness divine,
My comfort in Thy Spirit
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That binds my life to Thine.

Alas, that I should ever
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Have failed in love to Thee,
The only One who never
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Forgot or slighted me!
Oh for a heart to love Thee
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;More truly as I ought,
And nothing place above Thee
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In deed, or word, or thought!

Oh for that choicest blessing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of living in Thy love!
And thus on earth possessing
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The peace of heaven above:
Oh for the bliss that by it
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The soul securely knows,
The holy calm and quiet
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Of faith’s serene repose!


630
Together at the feast

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Munro_M_George&#39;,this)">George M. Munro</a></div>
S.M.

TOGETHER at the feast,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A sacrifice we raise
To Thee, O God, through our Great Priest
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who leads our hymn of praise.

Our hearts are occupied
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With Thy Belovèd Son,
In Whom Thou hast been glorified, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;By Whom Thy will was done.

Our eyes afresh survey
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That path of purest grace, 
And mark the beauties of His way, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That time shall ne'er efface.

Our minds recall again
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Those words of grace so free, 
Those mighty deeds of power, as when 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;He quelled the storm at sea.

Our hands we thus would fill
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With gathered incense sweet - 
The fragrance inexpressible 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That lingered round His feet.

Through Him, O God, we raise
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In worship rev'rently,
United sacrifice of praise,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Acceptable to Thee.


631
Twas love that sought Gethsemane
Previous BHB #297<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Dickinson_William&#39;,this)">William Dickinson</a></div>
8.7.8.7.

TWAS love that sought Gethsemane,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Or Judas ne’er had found Thee;
’Twas love that nailed Thee to the tree,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Or iron ne’er had bound Thee.

’Twas love that lived, ’twas love that died,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With endless life to bless us;
Well hast Thou won Thy blood- bought Bride,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Worthy art Thou, Lord Jesus!


632
Twas on that night, when doomed to know
Previous BHB #459<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Morison_John&#39;,this)">John Morrison  (1781)</a></div>
L.M.

TWAS on that night, when doomed to know
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The eager rage of every foe,
That night in which He was betrayed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Saviour of the world took bread.

And after thanks and glory given
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To Him that rules in earth and  heaven,
That symbol of His flesh He broke,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And thus to all His followers spoke.

My broken body thus I give
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For you, for all; take, eat, and live:
And oft the sacred rite renew
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That brings My wondrous love to  view.

Then in His hands the cup He raised, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And God anew He thanked and  praised,
While kindness in His bosom glowed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And from His lips salvation flowed.

My blood I thus pour forth, He cries,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;To cleanse the soul in sin that lies;
In this the covenant is sealed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And heaven’s eternal grace revealed.

With love to man this cup is fraught,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Let all partake the sacred draught;
Through latest ages let it pour
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In memory of My dying hour.


633
Until He come

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Kirkpatrick_William&#39;,this)">William Kirkpatrick</a></div>
4.6.8.8.4.

UNTIL He come,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And we shall be at home,
The feast we keep, His death proclaim, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And spread abroad His precious Name, 
Until He come.

As sons of light,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;We look beyond the night,
With eagerness to hail the dawn, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;When bursts the Morning Star upon
Our waiting sight!

Our hearts would be
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;From every hindrance free, 
And centred on the Coming One, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Whose every glory as the Son
We long to see.

For Him we wait,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And watch at wisdom's gate,
In readiness to do His will,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For Him, each day with service fill  
While thus we wait.


634
Unto Him who loved us, gave us
Previous BHB #307<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Waring_Samuel_Miller&#39;,this)">Samuel Miller Waring</a></div>
8.7.8.7.8.7.

UNTO Him who loved us, gave us
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Every pledge that love could give;
Freely shed His blood to save us;
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Gave His life that we might live;
Be the kingdom, and dominion,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And the glory evermore!


635
Unto the end with even pace

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Ewan_Y_Isaac&#39;,this)">Isaac Y. Ewan</a></div>
C.M.

UNTO the end with even pace,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Through such a scene as this, 
With not one thing done out of place,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And not one word amiss!

Unto the end His kindness such
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;As never need ignored: 
Malchus received that healing touch, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And Peter sheathed his sword.

Unto the end He loved His own
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With an unchanging love,
True as the everlasting throne, 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;All other loves above.

Unto the end, calm and serene,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That lowly path He trod: 
There moral Majesty was seen 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Marking the Son of God.

O Blessèd God those steps we trace
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Through such a scene as this; 
Sing of that priceless wealth of grace 
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;With priestly emphasis.


636
Walk in the light, so shalt thou know
Previous BHB #309<span class="far fa-pause-circle fa-rotate-90"></span>
<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Barton_Bernard&#39;,this)">Bernard Barton  (1826)</a></div>
C.M.

WALK in the light, so shalt thou know
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;That fellowship of love
His Spirit only can bestow,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Who reigns in light above.

Walk in the light, o’er sin abhorred
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thou shalt the victory gain;
The blood of Jesus Christ Thy Lord
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Cleanseth from every stain.

Walk in the light, and thou shalt find
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Thy heart made truly His,
Who dwells in cloudless light enshrined,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;In whom no darkness is.

Walk in the light, and e’en the tomb
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;No fearful shade shall wear;
Glory shall chase away the gloom,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;For Christ hath conquered there.

Walk in the light, and thine shall be
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;A path, though thorny, bright;
For God, by grace, shall dwell in thee,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;And God Himself is light!


637
Was it for me, for me alone?

<div class="author"><a href="javascript:;" onClick="showCompanion(&#39;Whyte_M_John&#39;,this)">John M. Whyte</a></div>
L.M.D.

WAS it for me, for me alone,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Saviour left His glorious throne;
The dazzling splendours of the sky?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Was it for me He came to die?

c
It was for me, Yes, all for me, …
O love of God, So great, so free …
O wondrous love, I'll shout and sing, …
He died for me, My Lord and King!

Was it for me sweet angel strains
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Came floating o'er Judea's plains,
That starlight night so long ago?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Was it for me God planned it so? 

c
It was for me, Yes, all for me, …
O love of God, So great, so free …
O wondrous love, I'll shout and sing, …
He died for me, My Lord and King!

Was it for me the Saviour said,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;“Pillow thy weary, aching head,
Trustingly on thy Saviour's breast”?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Was it for me? Can I thus rest? 

c
It was for me, Yes, all for me, …
O love of God, So great, so free …
O wondrous love, I'll shout and sing, …
He died for me, My Lord and King!

Was it for me He wept and prayed,
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;My load of sin before Him laid; 
That night within Gethsemane?
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Was it for me, that agony? 

c
It was for me, Yes, all for me, …
O love of God, So great, so free …
O wondrous love, I'll shout and sing, …
He died for me, My Lord and King!

Was it for me He bowed His head
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Upon the Cross, and freely shed
